a.def: used before countable or singular nouns referring to people or things that have not already been mentioned

abandon.def: to leave somebody, especially somebody you are responsible for, with no intention of returning
abandon.ex: The baby had been abandoned by its mother.

ability.def: the fact that somebody/something is able to do something
ability.ex: People with the disease may lose their ability to communicate.

able.def: to have the skill, intelligence, opportunity, etc. needed to do something
able.ex: You must be able to speak French for this job.

abolish.def: to officially end a law, a system or an institution

abortion.def: the deliberate ending of a pregnancy at an early stage

about.def: a little more or less than; a little before or after
on the subject of somebody/something; in connection with somebody/something

above.def: at or to a higher place
above.ex: Put it on the shelf above.

above.def: at or to a higher place or position than something/somebody
above.ex: The water came above our knees.

abroad.def: in or to a foreign country

absence.def: the fact of somebody being away from a place where they are usually expected to be; the occasion or period of time when somebody is away
absence.ex: The decision was made in my absence (= while I was not there).

absent.def: not in a place because of illness, etc.
absent.ex: He was absent from work for two weeks.

absolute.ex: I've joined a class for absolute beginners.

absolutely.def: used to emphasize that something is completely true

absorb.def: to take in a liquid, gas or other substance from the surface or space around
absorb.ex: Plants absorb carbon dioxide from the air.

abstract.def: based on general ideas and not on any particular real person, thing or situation

absurd.def: extremely silly; not logical and sensible
absurd.ex: That uniform makes the guards look absurd.

abundance.def: a large quantity that is more than enough
abundance.ex: The brochure promised beautiful walks with an abundance of wildlife.

abuse.def: the use of something in a way that is wrong or harmful
to make bad use of something, or to use so much of something that it harms your health

academic.def: connected with education, especially studying in schools and universities
a person who teaches and/or does research at a university or college

academy.def: a school or college for special training
academy.ex: She trained at the Royal Academy of Music.

accelerate.def: to happen faster or earlier; to make something happen faster or earlier

accent.def: a way of pronouncing the words of a language that shows which country, area or social class a person comes from; how well somebody pronounces a particular language

accept.def: to take willingly something that is offered; to say ‘yes’ to an offer, invitation, etc.
accept.ex: He asked me to marry him and I accepted.

acceptable.def: agreed or approved of by most people in a society
acceptable.ex: Children must learn socially acceptable behaviour.

acceptance.def: the act of accepting a gift, an invitation, an offer, etc.
acceptance.ex: Please confirm your acceptance of this offer in writing.

access.def: the opportunity or right to use something or to see somebody/something
access.ex: High-speed internet access has become a necessity.

access.def: to open a computer file or use a computer system
access.ex: Most people use their phones to access the internet.

accessible.def: that can be reached, entered, used, seen, etc.
accessible.ex: The remote desert area is accessible only by helicopter.

accident.def: an unpleasant event, especially in a vehicle, that happens unexpectedly and causes injury or damage

accidentally.def: by chance; in a way that was not planned
accidentally.ex: I accidentally hit him in the face.

accommodate.def: to provide somebody with a room or place to sleep, live or sit
accommodate.ex: The hotel can accommodate up to 500 guests.

accommodation.def: a place to live, work or stay in

accompany.def: to travel or go somewhere with somebody/something
accompany.ex: His wife accompanied him on the trip.

accomplish.def: to succeed in doing or completing something
accomplish.ex: The first part of the plan has been safely accomplished.

accomplishment.def: an impressive thing that is done or achieved after a lot of work
accomplishment.ex: It was one of the President's greatest accomplishments.

accordance.def: according to a rule or the way that somebody says that something should be done

according to.def: as stated or reported by somebody/something
according to.ex: According to Mick, it's a great movie.

accordingly.def: in a way that is appropriate to what has been done or said in a particular situation
accordingly.ex: We have to discover his plans and act accordingly.

account.def: an arrangement that somebody has with a bank, etc. to keep money there, take some out, etc.
account.ex: I don't have a bank account.

account.def: to have the opinion that somebody/something is a particular thing
account.ex: In English law a person is accounted innocent until they are proved guilty.

accountability.def: the fact of being responsible for your decisions or actions and expected to explain them when you are asked

accountable.def: responsible for your decisions or actions and expected to explain them when you are asked
accountable.ex: The state spends taxpayers’ money and should be held accountable.

accountant.def: a person whose job is to keep or check financial accounts
accountant.ex: We talked to the company’s chief accountant.

accumulate.def: to gradually get more and more of something over a period of time
accumulate.ex: I seem to have accumulated a lot of books.

accumulation.def: the process of gradually increasing or getting more and more of something over a period of time

accuracy.def: the state of being exact or correct; the ability to do something with skill and without making mistakes
accuracy.ex: They questioned the accuracy of the information in the file.

accurate.def: correct and true in every detail

accurately.def: in a way that is correct and true in every detail
accurately.ex: The article accurately reflects public opinion.

accusation.def: a statement saying that you think a person is guilty of doing something wrong or illegal; the fact of accusing somebody
accusation.ex: I don't want to make an accusation until I have some proof.

accuse.def: to say that somebody has done something wrong or is guilty of something
accuse.ex: accuse somebody of something, to accuse somebody of murder/a crime

accused.def: a person who is on trial for committing a crime

achieve.def: to succeed in reaching a particular goal, status or standard, especially by making an effort for a long time

achievement.def: a thing that somebody has done successfully, especially using their own effort and skill
achievement.ex: the greatest scientific achievement of the decade

acid.def: that contains acid or has the essential characteristics of an acid; that has a pH of less than seven
acid.ex: Rye is tolerant of poor, acid soils.

acid.def: a chemical, usually a liquid, that contains hydrogen and has a pH of less than seven. The hydrogen can be replaced by a metal to form a salt. Acids usually have a bitter sharp taste and can often burn holes in or damage things they touch.
acid.ex: The acid burned a hole in her coat.

acknowledge.def: to accept that something is true
acknowledge.ex: She refuses to acknowledge the need for reform.

acquire.def: to gain something by your own efforts, ability or behaviour
acquire.ex: She has acquired a good knowledge of English.

acquisition.def: the act of getting something, especially knowledge, a skill, etc.

acre.def: a unit for measuring an area of land; 4 840 square yards or about 4 050 square metres

across.def: from one side to the other side
across.ex: It's too wide. We can't swim across.

across.def: from one side to the other side of something

act.def: a particular thing that somebody does
act.ex: You have committed a serious criminal act.

act.def: to do something for a particular purpose or in order to deal with a situation
act.ex: It is vital that we act to stop the destruction of the rainforests.

action.def: the process of doing something in order to make something happen or to deal with a situation
action.ex: The time has come for action if these beautiful animals are to survive.

activate.def: to make something such as a device or chemical process start working
activate.ex: The burglar alarm is activated by movement.

activation.def: the act of making something such as a device or chemical process start working
activation.ex: The activation of several target genes results in two major effects.

active.def: always busy doing things, especially physical activities
active.ex: Staying physically active in later years can also keep you feeling younger.

activist.def: a person who works to achieve political or social change, especially as a member of an organization with particular aims

activity.def: a thing that you do for interest or pleasure

actor.def: a person who performs on the stage, on television or in films, especially as a profession
actor.ex: Both lead actors (= the ones who play the main parts) are outstanding.

actress.def: a woman who performs on the stage, on television or in films, especially as a profession
actress.ex: The Oscar-winning actress has taken a break from acting.

actual.def: used to emphasize something that is real or exists in fact
actual.ex: James looks younger than his wife but in actual fact (= really) he is five years older.

actually.def: used in speaking to emphasize a fact or a comment, or that something is really true

acute.ex: There is an acute shortage of water.

ad.def: a notice, picture or film telling people about a product, job or service
ad.ex: The TV ads were first run last year.

adapt.def: to change your behaviour in order to deal more successfully with a new situation
adapt.ex: It's amazing how soon you adapt.

adaptation.def: the action or process of changing something, or of being changed, to suit a new purpose or situation
adaptation.ex: the adaptation of buildings for military purposes

add.def: to put something together with something else so as to increase the size, number, amount, etc.

addiction.def: the condition of being unable to stop using or doing something as a habit, especially something harmful

addition.def: a thing that is added to something else
addition.ex: All of these technologies are fairly recent additions.

additional.def: more than was first mentioned or is usual

additionally.def: in a way that is more than was first mentioned or is usual
additionally.ex: Additionally, the bus service will run on Sundays, every two hours.

address.def: details of where somebody lives or works and where letters, etc. can be sent
to think about a problem or a situation and decide how you are going to deal with it
address.ex: Your essay does not address the real issues.

adequate.def: enough in quantity, or good enough in quality, for a particular purpose or need
adequate.ex: They'll need an adequate supply of hot water.

adequately.def: in a way that is enough in quantity, or good enough in quality, for a particular purpose or need

adhere.ex: Once in the bloodstream, the bacteria adhere to the surface of the red cells.

adjacent.ex: The planes landed on adjacent runways.

adjust.def: to change something slightly to make it more suitable for a new set of conditions or to make it work better
adjust.ex: Watch out for sharp bends and adjust your speed accordingly.

adjustment.def: a small change made to something in order to correct or improve it
adjustment.ex: I've made a few adjustments to the design.

administer.def: to manage and organize the affairs of a company, an organization, a country, etc.

administration.def: the activities that are done in order to plan, organize and run a business, school or other institution
administration.ex: Administration costs are passed on to the customer.

administrative.def: connected with organizing the work of a business or an institution

administrator.def: a person whose job is to manage and organize the public or business affairs of a company or an institution, or a person who works in an office dealing with records, accounts, etc.
administrator.ex: Such organizational decisions are made by the hospital administrators.

admire.def: to respect somebody for what they have done or to respect their qualities

admission.def: the act of accepting somebody into an institution, organization, etc.; the right to enter a place or to join an institution or organization
admission.ex: Hospital admission is not necessary in most cases.

admit.def: to agree, often unwillingly, that something is true
admit.ex: It was a stupid thing to do, I admit.

adolescent.def: a young person who is developing from a child into an adult
adolescent.ex: adolescents between the ages of 13 and 18 and the problems they face

adopt.def: to take somebody else’s child into your family and become its legal parent(s)
adopt.ex: a campaign to encourage childless couples to adopt

adoption.def: the act of adopting a child; the fact of being adopted
adoption.ex: She put the baby up for adoption.

adult.ex: preparing young people for adult life

adult.def: a fully grown person who is legally responsible for their actions
adult.ex: Children must be accompanied by an adult.

advance.def: done or given before something is going to happen
advance.ex: Please give us advance warning of any changes.

advance.def: progress or a development in a particular activity or area of understanding
advance.ex: We live in an age of rapid technological advance.

advance.def: if knowledge, technology, etc. advances, it develops and improves
advance.ex: Technology is advancing at an incredibly rapid pace.

advanced.def: having the most modern and recently developed ideas, methods, etc.
advanced.ex: Scientists are working on highly advanced technology to replace fossil fuels.

advantage.def: a thing that helps you to be better or more successful than other people

adventure.def: an unusual, exciting or dangerous experience, journey or series of events

adverse.def: negative and unpleasant; not likely to produce a good result

advertise.def: to tell the public about a product or a service in order to encourage people to buy or to use it
advertise.ex: If you want to attract customers you need to advertise.

advertisement.def: a notice, picture or film telling people about a product, job or service

advertising.def: the activity and industry of advertising things to people on television, in newspapers, on the internet, etc.

advice.def: an opinion or a suggestion about what somebody should do in a particular situation

advise.def: to tell somebody what you think they should do in a particular situation
advise.ex: I would strongly advise against going out on your own.

advocate.def: a person who supports or speaks in favour of somebody or of a public plan or action
advocate.ex: advocate for something/somebody, an advocate for hospital workers
The group does not advocate the use of violence.

aesthetic.def: connected with beauty and art and the understanding of beautiful things
aesthetic.ex: the aesthetic appeal of the songs

affair.def: events that are of public interest or political importance

affect.def: to produce a change in somebody/something
affect.ex: How will these changes affect us?

affection.def: the feeling of liking or loving somebody/something very much and caring about them
affection.ex: Children need lots of love and affection.

afford.def: to have enough money to be able to buy or do something
afford.ex: Can we afford a new car?

affordable.def: cheap enough that people can afford to pay it or buy it
affordable.ex: We offer quality products at affordable prices.

afraid.def: feeling fear; frightened because you think that you might be hurt or suffer

after.def: at a time later than something; when something has finished
after.ex: I'll call you after I've spoken to them.

after.def: later than something; following something in time

aftermath.def: the situation that exists as a result of an important (and usually unpleasant) event, especially a war, an accident, etc.
aftermath.ex: A lot of rebuilding took place in the aftermath of the war.

afternoon.def: the period of time from 12 o'clock in the middle of the day until about 6 o’clock in the evening

afterwards.def: at a later time; after an event that has already been mentioned
afterwards.ex: Afterwards she was sorry for what she'd said.

again.def: one more time; on another occasion

age.def: the number of years that a person has lived or a thing has existed
age.ex: You're the same age as my brother.
As he aged, his memory got worse.

aged.ex: They have two children aged six and nine.

agency.def: a business or an organization that provides a particular service especially when representing other businesses or organizations
agency.ex: She works for an advertising agency.

agenda.def: a list of items to be discussed at a meeting
agenda.ex: We have a very full agenda of issues to discuss.

agent.def: a person whose job is to act for, or manage the affairs of, other people in business, politics, etc.
agent.ex: Our agent in New York deals with all US sales.

aggression.def: feelings of anger and hate that may result in threatening or violent behaviour
aggression.ex: The research shows that computer games may cause aggression.

aggressive.def: angry, and behaving in a threatening way; ready to attack
aggressive.ex: Seals have been known to exhibit aggressive behaviour towards swimmers.

ago.def: used in expressions of time with the simple past tense to show how far in the past something happened

agree.def: to have the same opinion as somebody; to say that you have the same opinion

agreement.def: an arrangement, a promise or a contract made with somebody

agricultural.def: connected with the science or practice of farming

agriculture.def: the science or practice of farming
agriculture.ex: The number of people employed in agriculture has fallen in the last decade.

ah.def: used to express surprise, pleasure or sympathy, or when you disagree with somebody

ahead.def: further forward in space or time; in front
ahead.ex: I'll run ahead and warn them.

aid.def: money, food, etc. that is sent to help countries in difficult situations
to help somebody/something to do something, especially by making it easier
aid.ex: The test is designed to aid in the diagnosis of various diseases.

aide.def: a person who helps another person, especially a politician, in their job

aids.def: a serious illness caused by a virus called HIV that attacks the body’s ability to resist infection (the abbreviation for ‘Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome’)

aim.def: the purpose of doing something; what somebody is trying to achieve
aim.ex: the stated aims of the study

aim.def: to try or plan to achieve something
aim.ex: He has always aimed high (= tried to achieve a lot).

air.def: the mixture of gases that surrounds the earth and that we breathe
air.ex: Let's go out for some fresh air.

aircraft.def: any vehicle that can fly and carry goods or passengers

airline.def: a company that provides regular flights to take passengers and goods to different places

airport.def: a place where planes land and take off and that has buildings for passengers to wait in

alarm.def: a loud noise or a signal that warns people of danger or of a problem
alarm.ex: She decided to sound the alarm (= warn people that the situation was dangerous).

alarm.def: to make somebody anxious or afraid
alarm.ex: I can only guess that they don't want to alarm the public yet.

albeit.ex: He finally agreed, albeit reluctantly, to help us.

album.def: a book in which you keep photographs, stamps, etc.

alcohol.def: drinks such as beer, wine, etc. that can make people drunk

alert.def: able to think quickly; quick to notice things
alert.ex: Suddenly he found himself awake and fully alert.

alert.def: a situation in which people are watching for danger and ready to deal with it
alert.ex: Police are warning the public to be on the alert for suspicious packages.

alert.def: to warn somebody about a dangerous situation or one that requires immediate action
alert.ex: Neighbours quickly alerted the emergency services.

alien.def: strange and frightening; different from what you are used to
a person who is not a citizen of the country in which they live or work

align.def: to arrange something in the correct position, or to be in the correct position, in relation to something else, especially in a straight line
align.ex: Make sure the shelf is aligned with the top of the cupboard.

alignment.ex: the alignment of the sun, moon and earth at a particular time

alike.ex: My sister and I do not look alike.
They tried to treat all their children alike.

all.ex: She was dressed all in white.
All horses are animals, but not all animals are horses.
All of the food has gone.

all right.def: used to check that somebody agrees or understands
all right.ex: We've got to get up early, all right?

allegation.def: a public statement that is made without giving proof, accusing somebody of doing something that is wrong or illegal

allege.def: to state something as a fact but without giving proof
allege.ex: The prosecution alleges (that) she was driving carelessly.

allegedly.def: expressed as though something is a fact but without giving any proof
allegedly.ex: crimes allegedly committed during the war

alliance.def: an agreement between countries, political parties, etc. to work together in order to achieve something that they all want

allocate.def: to give something officially to somebody/something for a particular purpose
allocate.ex: A large sum has been allocated for buying new books for the library.

allocation.def: an amount of money, space, etc. that is given to somebody for a particular purpose
allocation.ex: We have spent our entire allocation for the year.

allow.def: to let somebody/something do something; to let something happen or be done
allow.ex: His parents won't allow him to stay out late.

allowance.def: an amount of money that is given to somebody regularly or for a particular purpose

ally.def: a country that has agreed to help and support another country, especially in case of a war

almost.ex: I like almost all of them.

alone.ex: Tom is not alone in finding Rick hard to work with.

along.ex: I was just walking along singing to myself.

along.def: from one end to or towards the other end of something
along.ex: They walked slowly along the road.

alongside.def: next to or at the side of something
alongside.ex: A police car pulled up alongside us.

already.def: before now or before a particular time in the past

also.ex: She's fluent in French and German. She also speaks a little Italian.

alter.def: to become different; to make somebody/something different
alter.ex: Prices did not alter significantly during 2019.

alternative.def: that can be used instead of something else
alternative.ex: The road was closed so we had to find an alternative route.

alternative.def: a thing that you can choose to do or have out of two or more possibilities
alternative.ex: The car is too expensive so we're trying to find a cheaper alternative.

although.def: used for introducing a statement that makes the main statement in a sentence seem surprising
although.ex: Although the sun was shining, it wasn't very warm.

altogether.def: (used to emphasize something) completely; in every way
altogether.ex: The train went slower and slower until it stopped altogether.

aluminium.def: a chemical element. Aluminium is a light, silver-grey metal used for making pans, etc.

always.def: at all times; on every occasion
always.ex: There's almost always somebody at home in the evenings.

amateur.def: doing something for pleasure or interest, not as a job
a person who takes part in a sport or other activity for pleasure, not as a job
amateur.ex: The tournament is open to both amateurs and professionals.

amazing.def: very surprising, especially in a way that you like or admire

ambassador.def: an official who lives in a foreign country as the senior representative there of their own country
ambassador.ex: the British Ambassador to Italy/in Rome

ambition.def: something that you want to do or achieve very much
ambition.ex: She had fulfilled her lifelong ambition.

ambitious.def: determined to be successful, rich, powerful, etc.

ambulance.def: a vehicle with special equipment, used for taking sick or injured people to a hospital

amend.def: to change a law, document, statement, etc. slightly in order to correct a mistake or to improve it
amend.ex: He asked to see the amended version.

amendment.def: a small change or improvement that is made to a document or proposed new law; the process of changing a document or proposed new law
amendment.ex: to introduce/propose/table an amendment (= to suggest it)

amid.def: in the middle of or during something, especially something that causes excitement or fear
amid.ex: He finished his speech amid tremendous applause.

among.def: surrounded by somebody/something; in the middle of somebody/something

amount.ex: I was amazed at the amount he could eat.

analogy.def: a comparison of one thing with another thing that has similar features; a feature that is similar
analogy.ex: The teacher drew an analogy between the human heart and a pump.

analyse.def: to examine the nature or structure of something, especially by separating it into its parts, in order to understand or explain it
analyse.ex: The job involves collecting and analysing data.

analysis.def: the detailed study or examination of something in order to understand more about it; the result of the study

analyst.def: a person whose job involves examining facts or materials in order to give an opinion on them

ancestor.def: a person in your family who lived a long time ago
ancestor.ex: His ancestors had come to America from Ireland.

anchor.def: a heavy metal object that is attached to a rope or chain and dropped over the side of a ship or boat to keep it in one place

ancient.def: belonging to a period of history that is thousands of years in the past
ancient.ex: Archaeologists are excavating the ruined temples of this ancient civilization.

angel.def: a spirit who is believed to be a servant of God, and is sent by God to deliver a message or perform a task. Angels are often shown dressed in white, with wings.

anger.def: the strong feeling that you have when something has happened that you think is bad and unfair
anger.ex: She had to find a way to express her pent-up anger.

angle.def: the space between two lines or surfaces that join, measured in degrees

angry.def: having strong feelings about something that you dislike very much or about an unfair situation
angry.ex: Her behaviour really made me angry.

animal.def: a creature that is not a bird, a fish, a reptile, an insect or a human
animal.ex: the animals and birds of South America

animation.def: the process of making films, videos and computer games in which drawings, models or images of people and animals seem to move

ankle.def: the joint connecting the foot to the leg; the narrow part of the leg just above the ankle joint

anniversary.def: a date that is an exact number of years after the date of an important or special event
anniversary.ex: on the anniversary of his wife’s death

announce.def: to tell people something officially, especially about a decision, plans, etc.
announce.ex: He officially announced his intention to resign at today's press conference.

announcement.def: a spoken or written statement that informs people about something

annoy.ex: His constant joking was beginning to annoy her.

annoyed.ex: He was beginning to get very annoyed with me about my carelessness.

annual.def: happening or done once every year

anonymous.def: with a name that is not known or that is not made public

another.def: one more; an extra thing or person

answer.def: something that you say, write or do to react to a question or situation
answer.ex: I rang the bell, but there was no answer.

answer.def: to say, write or do something as a reaction to a question or situation
answer.ex: I repeated the question, but she didn't answer.

anticipate.ex: We don't anticipate any major problems.

anxiety.def: the state of feeling nervous or worried that something bad is going to happen

anxious.ex: The bus was late and Sue began to get anxious.

any.def: used to emphasize an adjective or adverb in negative sentences or questions, meaning ‘at all’
any.ex: He wasn't any good at French.

any.def: used with uncountable or plural nouns in negative sentences and questions, after if or whether, and after some verbs such as prevent, ban, forbid, etc. to refer to an amount or a number of something, however large or small
used in negative sentences and in questions and after if or whether to refer to an amount or a number, however large or small
any.ex: We need some more paint; there isn't any left.

any more.def: often used at the end of negative sentences and at the end of questions, to mean ‘any longer’
any more.ex: She doesn't live here any more.

anybody.def: used instead of somebody in negative sentences and in questions after if/whether, and after verbs such as prevent, forbid, avoid, etc.
anybody.ex: Is there anybody who can help me?

anyone.def: used instead of someone in negative sentences and in questions after if/whether, and after verbs such as prevent, forbid, avoid, etc.

anything.def: used instead of something in negative sentences and in questions; after if/whether; and after verbs such as prevent, ban, avoid, etc.

anyway.def: used when adding something to support an idea or argument
anyway.ex: It's too expensive and anyway the colour doesn't suit you.

anywhere.def: used in negative sentences and in questions instead of somewhere

apart.def: separated by a distance, of space or time
apart.ex: The two houses stood 500 metres apart.

apartment.def: a set of rooms for living in, usually on one floor of a building

apologize.def: to say that you are sorry for doing something wrong or causing a problem

apology.def: a word or statement saying sorry for something that has been done wrong or that causes a problem

app.def: a piece of software that you can download to a device such as a smartphone or tablet, for example to look up information or to play a game
app.ex: You first need to install the app on your device.

apparatus.def: the tools or other pieces of equipment that are needed for a particular activity or task

apparently.def: according to what you have heard or read; according to the way something appears
apparently.ex: Apparently they are getting divorced soon.

appeal.def: a deeply felt request for money, help or information that is needed immediately, especially one made by a charity or by the police
appeal.ex: appeal for something, to launch a TV appeal for donations to the charity

appeal.def: to make a deeply felt request, especially for something that is needed immediately
appeal.ex: I am appealing (= asking for money) on behalf of the famine victims.

appealing.ex: Spending the holidays in Britain wasn't a prospect that I found particularly appealing.

appear.ex: Three days later a rash appeared.

appearance.def: the way that somebody/something looks on the outside; what somebody/something seems to be

appetite.ex: He suffered from headaches and loss of appetite.

applaud.def: to show your approval of somebody/something by clapping your hands (= hitting your open hands together several times)
applaud.ex: He started to applaud and the others joined in.

apple.def: a round fruit with shiny red or green skin that is fairly hard and white inside

applicable.def: that can be said to be true in the case of somebody/something
applicable.ex: Give details of children where applicable (= if you have any).

applicant.def: a person who makes a formal request for something (= applies for it), especially for a job, a place at a college or university, etc.
applicant.ex: There were over 500 applicants for the job.

application.def: a formal (often written) request for something, such as a job, permission to do something or a place at a college or university

apply.def: to make a formal request, usually in writing, for something such as a job, a loan, permission for something, a place at a university, etc.
apply.ex: You should apply in person/by letter.

appoint.def: to choose somebody for a job or position of responsibility
appoint.ex: They have appointed a new head teacher at my son's school.

appointment.def: a formal arrangement to meet or visit somebody at a particular time, especially for a reason connected with their work
appointment.ex: I've got a dental appointment at 3 o'clock.

appreciate.def: to recognize the good qualities of somebody/something
appreciate.ex: Over the years he came to appreciate the beauty and tranquillity of the river.

appreciation.def: pleasure that you have when you recognize and enjoy the good qualities of somebody/something
appreciation.ex: She shows little appreciation of good music.

approach.def: a way of dealing with somebody/something; a way of doing or thinking about something such as a problem or a task
approach.ex: She took the wrong approach in her dealings with them.

approach.def: to come near to somebody/something in distance or time
approach.ex: We could hear the train approaching.

appropriate.def: suitable, acceptable or correct for the particular circumstances

appropriately.def: in a way that is suitable, acceptable or correct for the particular circumstances
appropriately.ex: The government has been accused of not responding appropriately to the needs of the homeless.

approval.def: the feeling that somebody/something is good or acceptable; a positive opinion of somebody/something
approval.ex: She desperately wanted to win her father's approval.

approve.def: to think that somebody/something is good, acceptable or suitable
approve.ex: I told my mother I wanted to leave school but she didn't approve.

approximately.def: used to show that something is almost, but not completely, accurate or correct
approximately.ex: The journey took approximately seven hours.

april.def: the fourth month of the year, between March and May

arbitrary.def: not seeming to be based on a reason, system or plan and sometimes seeming unfair
arbitrary.ex: The choice of players for the team seemed completely arbitrary.

architect.def: a person whose job is designing buildings
architect.ex: The house was designed by architect Louis Kahn.

architecture.def: the art and study of designing buildings

archive.def: a collection of historical documents or records of a government, a family, a place or an organization; the place where these records are stored

area.def: part of a place, town, etc., or a region of a country or the world
area.ex: to improve access to services in rural areas

arena.def: a place with a flat open area in the middle and seats around it where people can watch sports and entertainment

arguably.def: used (often before a comparative or superlative adjective) when you are stating an opinion that you believe you could give reasons to support
arguably.ex: He is arguably the best actor of his generation.

argue.def: to speak angrily to somebody because you disagree with them

argument.def: a conversation or discussion in which two or more people disagree, often angrily

arise.def: to happen; to start to exist
arise.ex: An opportunity arose to work in the United States.

arm.def: either of the two long parts that stick out from the top of the body and connect the shoulders to the hands
arm.ex: He escaped with only a broken arm.

arm.def: to provide weapons for yourself/somebody in order to fight a battle or a war
arm.ex: The country was arming against the enemy.

arms.def: weapons, especially as used by the army, navy, etc.

army.def: a large organized group of soldiers who are trained to fight on land
army.ex: The two opposing armies faced each other across the battlefield.

around.def: surrounding somebody/something; on each side of something
around.ex: The house is built around a central courtyard.

arrange.def: to plan or organize something in advance

arrangement.def: a plan or preparation that you make so that something can happen
arrangement.ex: Travel and accommodation arrangements have been finalized.

array.def: a group or collection of things or people, often one that is large or impressive
array.ex: a vast array of bottles of different shapes and sizes

arrest.def: if the police arrest somebody, the person is taken to a police station and kept there because the police believe they may be guilty of a crime
arrest.ex: A man has been arrested in connection with the robbery.

arrival.def: an act of coming or being brought to a place
arrival.ex: We apologize for the late arrival of the train.

arrive.def: to get to a place, especially at the end of a journey

arrow.def: a thin stick with a sharp point at one end, which is shot from a bow

art.def: the use of the imagination to express ideas or feelings, particularly in painting, drawing or sculpture

article.def: a piece of writing about a particular subject in a newspaper or magazine, on a website, etc.

articulate.def: to express or explain your thoughts or feelings clearly in words
articulate.ex: She struggled to articulate her thoughts.

artificial.def: made or produced to copy something natural; not real

artist.def: a person who creates works of art, especially paintings or drawings
artist.ex: an exhibition of work by contemporary British artists

artistic.ex: the artistic works of the period

artwork.def: pictures and photographs prepared for books, magazines, etc.
artwork.ex: Can you let me have the finished artwork for the poster by Friday?

as.def: used when you are comparing two people or things, or two situations
as.ex: You're as tall as your father.
He sat watching her as she got ready.

as.def: used to describe somebody/something appearing to be somebody/something else
as.ex: They were all dressed as clowns.

ash.def: the grey or black powder that is left after something, especially tobacco, wood or coal, has burnt

ashamed.def: feeling shame or feeling embarrassed about somebody/something or because of something you have done
ashamed.ex: She was deeply ashamed of her behaviour at the party.

aside.def: to one side; out of the way

ask.def: to say or write something in the form of a question, in order to get information
ask.ex: How old are you—if you don’t mind me/my asking?

asleep.ex: I waited until they were all fast asleep (= sleeping deeply).

aspect.def: a particular part or feature of a situation, an idea, a problem, etc.; a way in which it may be considered
aspect.ex: The book aims to cover all aspects of city life.

aspiration.def: a strong desire to have or do something
aspiration.ex: I didn't realize you had political aspirations.

aspire.def: to have a strong desire to achieve or to become something
aspire.ex: She aspired to a scientific career.

assassination.def: the murder of an important or famous person, especially for political reasons
assassination.ex: The president survived a number of assassination attempts.

assault.def: the crime of attacking somebody physically
assault.ex: Both men were charged with assault.

assault.def: to attack somebody violently, especially when this is a crime
assault.ex: He has been charged with assaulting a police officer.

assemble.def: to come together as a group; to bring people or things together as a group
assemble.ex: All the students were asked to assemble in the main hall.

assembly.def: a group of people who have been elected to meet together regularly and make decisions or laws for a particular region or country

assert.def: to state clearly and definitely that something is true
assert.ex: She continued to assert that she was innocent.

assertion.def: a statement saying that you strongly believe something to be true
assertion.ex: He was correct in his assertion that the minister had been lying.

assess.def: to make a judgement about the nature or quality of somebody/something
assess.ex: Accurately assessing environmental impacts is very complex.

assessment.def: an opinion or a judgement about somebody/something that has been thought about very carefully
assessment.ex: a detailed assessment of the risks involved

asset.def: a person or thing that is valuable or useful to somebody/something
asset.ex: In his job, patience is an invaluable asset.

assign.def: to give somebody something that they can use, or some work or responsibility
assign.ex: The teacher assigned a different task to each of the children.

assignment.def: a task or piece of work that somebody is given to do, usually as part of their job or studies
assignment.ex: Students are required to complete all homework assignments.

assist.def: to help somebody to do something
assist.ex: Anyone willing to assist can contact this number.

assistant.def: having a rank below that of a senior person and helping them in their work
a person who helps or supports somebody, usually in their job
assistant.ex: My assistant will now demonstrate the machine in action.

associate.def: to make a connection between people or things in your mind
associate.ex: I always associate the smell of baking with my childhood.

associated.def: if one thing is associated with another, the two things are connected because they happen together or one thing causes the other
associated.ex: associated with doing something, the risks associated with taking drugs

association.def: an official group of people who have joined together for a particular purpose
association.ex: Do you belong to any professional or trade associations?

assume.def: to think or accept that something is true but without having proof of it
assume.ex: It is reasonable to assume (that) the economy will continue to improve.

assumption.def: a belief or feeling that something is true or that something will happen, although there is no proof

assurance.def: a statement that something will certainly be true or will certainly happen, particularly when there has been doubt about it
assurance.ex: They called for assurances that the government is committed to its education policy.

assure.def: to tell somebody that something is definitely true or is definitely going to happen, especially when they have doubts about it
assure.ex: You think I did it deliberately, but I assure you (that) I did not.

astonishing.ex: She ran 100m in an astonishing 10.6 seconds.

asylum.def: protection that a government gives to people who have left their own country, usually because they were in danger for political reasons

at.def: used to say where something/somebody is or where something happens
at.ex: at the corner of the street

athlete.def: a person who competes in sports

atmosphere.def: the mixture of gases that surrounds the earth
atmosphere.ex: Wind power doesn't release carbon dioxide into the atmosphere.

atrocity.def: a cruel and violent act, especially in a war
atrocity.ex: In the war, both sides committed atrocities.

attach.def: to fasten or join one thing to another
attach.ex: I attach a copy of my notes for your information.

attachment.def: a document that you send to somebody using email

attack.def: an act of using violence to try to hurt or kill somebody
attack.ex: Five people were killed in the attacks that took place last night.

attack.def: to use violence to try to hurt or kill somebody
attack.ex: Most dogs will not attack unless provoked.

attain.def: to succeed in getting something, usually after a lot of effort
attain.ex: Most of our students attained five ‘A’ grades in their exams.

attempt.def: an act of trying to do something, especially something difficult, often with no success
attempt.ex: I passed my driving test at the first attempt.

attempt.def: to make an effort or try to do something, especially something difficult
attempt.ex: I will attempt to answer all your questions.

attend.def: to be present at an event
attend.ex: We'd like as many people as possible to attend.

attendance.def: the act of being present at a place, for example at school
attendance.ex: Attendance at these lectures is not compulsory.

attention.def: used for asking people to listen to something that is being announced
attention.ex: Attention, please! Passengers for flight KL412 are requested to go to gate 21 immediately.

attention.def: the act of listening to, looking at or thinking about something/somebody carefully; interest that people show in somebody/something

attitude.def: the way that you think and feel about somebody/something; the way that you behave towards somebody/something that shows how you think and feel
attitude.ex: These societies have to change their attitudes towards women.

attorney.def: a lawyer, especially one who can act for somebody in court
attorney.ex: The prosecuting attorney began with a short opening statement.

attract.def: if you are attracted by something, it interests you and makes you want it; if you are attracted by somebody, you like or admire them
attract.ex: I had always been attracted by the idea of working abroad.

attraction.def: an interesting or lively place to go or thing to do
attraction.ex: Buckingham Palace is a major tourist attraction.

attractive.def: pleasant to look at, especially in a sexual way

attribute.def: a quality or feature of somebody/something
attribute.ex: Patience is one of the most important attributes in a teacher.

attribute.def: to say or believe that something is the result of a particular thing
attribute.ex: She attributes her success to hard work and a little luck.

auction.def: a public sale in which things are sold to the person who offers the most money for them

audience.def: the group of people who have gathered to watch or listen to something (a play, concert, somebody speaking, etc.)
audience.ex: The audience was/were clapping for 10 minutes.

audio.def: connected with sound that is recorded

audit.def: an official examination of business and financial records to see that they are true and correct

august.def: the 8th month of the year, between July and September

aunt.def: the sister of your father or mother; the wife of your uncle or aunt

authentic.def: known to be real and what somebody claims it is and not a copy
authentic.ex: I don't know if the painting is authentic.

author.def: a person who writes books or the person who wrote a particular book

authority.def: the power to give orders to people

authorize.def: to give official permission for something, or for somebody to do something
authorize.ex: I can authorize payments up to £5 000.

automatic.def: having controls that work without needing a person to operate them

automatically.def: without needing a person to operate controls

autonomy.def: the freedom for a country, a region or an organization to govern itself independently
autonomy.ex: a campaign in Wales for greater autonomy

autumn.def: the season of the year between summer and winter, when leaves change colour and the weather becomes colder
autumn.ex: in the autumn of something, in the autumn of 2010

availability.def: the fact that something is possible to get, buy or find

available.def: that you can get, buy or find

average.def: calculated by adding several amounts together, finding a total, and dividing the total by the number of amounts
average.ex: The average age of participants was 52 years.

average.def: the result of adding several amounts together, finding a total, and dividing the total by the number of amounts
to be equal to a particular amount as an average
average.ex: Economic growth is expected to average 2% next year.

avoid.def: to prevent something bad from happening

await.ex: He is in custody awaiting trial.

award.def: a prize such as money, etc. for something that somebody has done
award.ex: He was nominated for the best actor award.

award.def: to make an official decision to give something to somebody as a payment, prize, contract, etc.
award.ex: Knowing why and how corporations award contracts is vitally important.

aware.ex: As you're aware, this is not a new problem.

awareness.def: knowing something; knowing that something exists and is important
awareness.ex: awareness of something, an awareness of the importance of eating a healthy diet

away.def: to or at a distance from somebody/something in space or time
away.ex: The beach is a mile away.

baby.def: a very young child or animal

back.def: located behind or at the back of something
back.ex: We were sitting in the back row

back.def: to or into the place, condition, situation or activity where somebody/something was before
back.ex: When is he coming back to work?

back.def: the part of the human body that is on the opposite side to the chest, between the neck and the tops of the legs; the part of an animal’s body that corresponds to this
back.ex: He was lying on his back on the sofa.

back.def: to encourage somebody or give them help; to give financial support to somebody/something
back.ex: Her parents backed her in her choice of career.

backdrop.def: everything that can be seen around an event or scene
backdrop.ex: The mountains provided a dramatic backdrop for our picnic.

background.def: the details of a person’s family, education, experience, etc.

backing.def: help and support from somebody to do something; financial support for something
backing.ex: The police gave the proposals their full backing.

backup.def: extra help or support that you can get if necessary
backup.ex: The police had backup from the army.

backwards.def: towards a place or position that is behind
backwards.ex: I lost my balance and fell backwards.

bacteria.def: the simplest and smallest forms of life. Bacteria exist in large numbers in air, water and soil, and also in living and dead creatures and plants, and are often a cause of disease.
bacteria.ex: Neither chilling nor freezing kills all bacteria.

bad.ex: I have some bad news for you, I'm afraid.

badge.def: a small piece of metal or plastic, with a design or words on it, that a person wears or carries to show that they belong to an organization, support something, have achieved something, have a particular rank, etc.
badge.ex: She wore a badge saying ‘Vote for Coates’.

bag.def: a container made of cloth, leather, plastic or paper, used to carry things in, especially when shopping or travelling
bag.ex: He was carrying a heavy bag of groceries.

bail.def: money that somebody agrees to pay if a person accused of a crime does not appear at their trial. When bail has been arranged, the accused person is allowed to go free until the trial.
bail.ex: Can anyone put up bail for you?

bake.def: to cook food in an oven without extra fat or liquid; to be cooked in this way

balance.def: a situation in which different things exist in equal, correct or good amounts
balance.ex: This newspaper maintains a good balance in its presentation of different opinions.

balance.def: to put your body or something else into a position where it is steady and does not fall
balance.ex: How long can you balance on one leg?

balanced.def: keeping or showing a balance so that different things or different parts of something exist in equal or correct amounts
balanced.ex: The programme presented a balanced view of the two sides of the conflict.

ball.def: a round object used for throwing, hitting or kicking in games and sports

ballet.def: a style of dancing that tells a dramatic story with music but no talking or singing
ballet.ex: She wants to be a ballet dancer.

balloon.def: a small bag made of very thin rubber that becomes larger and rounder when you fill it with air or gas. Balloons are brightly coloured and used as decorations or toys.

ballot.def: the system of voting in writing and usually in secret; an occasion on which a vote is held
ballot.ex: The chairperson is chosen by secret ballot.

ban.def: an official rule that says that something is not allowed
to decide or say officially that something is not allowed
ban.ex: The law effectively bans smoking in all public places.

banana.def: a long curved fruit with a thick yellow skin and that is soft inside, which grows on trees in hot countries

band.def: a small group of musicians who play popular music together, often with a singer or singers

bank.def: an organization that provides various financial services, for example keeping or lending money
bank.ex: I don't have much money in the bank at the end of the month.

banner.def: a long piece of cloth with a message on it that is carried between two poles or hung in a public place to show support for something
banner.ex: A huge banner over the street said ‘Welcome home’.

bar.def: a place where you can buy and drink alcoholic and other drinks
bar.ex: We arranged to meet in a bar called the Flamingo.

bar.def: to ban or prevent somebody from doing something
bar.ex: Prisoners are barred by law from voting in general elections.

bare.ex: She likes to walk around in bare feet.

barely.def: in a way that is just possible but only with difficulty
barely.ex: He could barely read and write.

bargain.def: a thing bought for less than the usual price
bargain.ex: I picked up a few good bargains in the sale.

barrel.def: a large round container, usually made of wood or metal, with flat ends and, usually, curved sides

barrier.def: an object like a fence that prevents people from moving forward from one place to another
barrier.ex: The crowd had to stand behind barriers.

base.def: the lowest part of something, especially the part or surface on which it rests or stands
base.ex: The lamp has a heavy base.

base.def: to use a particular city, town, etc. as the main place for a business, holiday, etc.
base.ex: The organization is now based in Geneva.

baseball.def: a game played especially in the US by two teams of nine players, using a bat and ball. Each player tries to hit the ball and then run around four bases before the other team can return the ball.

based.def: if one thing is based on another, it uses it or is developed from it
based.ex: The movie is based on a real-life incident.

basement.def: a room or rooms in a building, partly or completely below the level of the ground
basement.ex: Kitchen goods are sold in the basement.

basic.def: forming the part of something that is most necessary and from which other things develop

basically.def: in the most important ways, without considering things that are less important
basically.ex: I think we are basically saying the same thing.

basis.def: the way things are organized or arranged; how often something happens
basis.ex: We are in contact on a regular basis.

basket.def: a container for holding or carrying things. Baskets are made of long thin pieces of material that bends and twists easily, for example plastic, wire or wicker.

basketball.def: a game played by two teams of five players, using a large ball which players try to throw into a high net hanging from a ring

bass.def: the lowest tone or part in music, for instruments or voices
bass.ex: He always plays his stereo with the bass turned right up.

bat.def: a piece of wood with a handle, made in various shapes and sizes, and used for hitting the ball in games such as baseball, cricket and table tennis
to hit a ball with a bat, especially in a game of baseball or cricket

bath.def: a large, long container that you put water in and then get into to wash your whole body

bathroom.def: a room in which there is a bath, a washbasin and often a toilet
bathroom.ex: Go and wash your hands in the bathroom.

battery.def: a device that is placed inside a car engine, clock, radio, etc. and that produces the electricity that makes it work

battle.def: a fight between armies, ships or planes, especially during a war; a violent fight between groups of people
battle.ex: Napoleon was defeated at the Battle of Waterloo.

battle.def: to try very hard to achieve something difficult or to deal with something unpleasant or dangerous

battlefield.def: a place where a battle is being fought or has been fought

bay.def: a part of the sea, or of a large lake, partly surrounded by a wide curve of the land

be.def: used when you are naming people or things, describing them or giving more information about them
used with a present participle to form progressive tenses

beach.def: an area of sand or small stones (called shingle), next to the sea or a lake
beach.ex: on the beach, tourists sunbathing on the beach

beam.def: a line of light, electromagnetic waves or particles

bean.def: a seed, or pod containing seeds, of a climbing plant, eaten as a vegetable. There are several types of bean and the plants that they grow on are also called beans.

bear.def: a heavy wild animal with thick fur and sharp claws (= pointed parts on the ends of its feet). There are many types of bear.
to be able to accept and deal with something unpleasant
bear.ex: The pain was almost more than he could bear.

beast.def: an animal, especially one that is large or dangerous, or one that is unusual

beat.def: a single hit to something, such as a drum, or a movement of something, such as your heart; the sound that this makes
beat.ex: several loud beats on the drum

beat.def: to defeat somebody in a game or competition

beautiful.def: having beauty; giving pleasure to the senses or to the mind

beauty.def: the quality of giving pleasure to the senses or to the mind
beauty.ex: beauty of something, the beauty of the sunset/of poetry/of his singing

because.ex: I did it because he told me to.

become.ex: It soon became apparent that no one was going to come.

bed.def: a piece of furniture for sleeping on

bee.def: a black and yellow flying insect that can sting (= touch your skin and make you feel a sharp pain).Bees live in large groups and make honey (= a sweet sticky substance that is good to eat).
bee.ex: Bees were buzzing in the clover.

beef.def: meat that comes from a cow

beer.def: an alcoholic drink made from malt with hops added to give it taste. There are many types of beer.

before.def: at an earlier time; in the past; already
before.ex: You should have told me so before.

beg.def: to ask somebody for something especially in an anxious way because you want or need it very much
beg.ex: Now you have to beg and plead.

begin.def: to start doing something; to do the first part of something

beginning.def: the time when something starts; the first part of an event, a story, etc.
beginning.ex: A story has to have a beginning, middle, and end.

behalf.ex: He began a petition in behalf of the farmers.

behave.def: to do things in a particular way

behaviour.def: the way that somebody behaves, especially towards other people

behind.def: at or towards the back of somebody/something; further back
behind.ex: She rode off down the road with the dog running behind.

behind.def: at or towards the back of somebody/something, and often hidden by it or them
behind.ex: Who's the girl standing behind Jan?

being.ex: The Irish Free State came into being in 1922.

belief.def: a strong feeling that something/somebody exists or is true; confidence that something/somebody is good or right
belief.ex: I admire his passionate belief in what he is doing.

believe.def: to feel certain that something is true or that somebody is telling you the truth

bell.def: a hollow metal object, often like a cup in shape, that makes a ringing sound when hit by a small piece of metal inside it; the sound that it makes
bell.ex: A peal of church bells rang out in the distance.

belong.def: to be in the right or suitable place
belong.ex: Where do these plates belong (= where are they kept)?

beloved.ex: in memory of our dearly beloved son, John

below.def: at or to a lower level, position or place
below.ex: They live on the floor below.

below.def: at or to a lower level or position than somebody/something
below.ex: He dived below the surface of the water.

belt.def: a long narrow piece of leather, cloth, etc. that you wear around the middle part of your body

bench.def: a long seat for two or more people, usually made of wood

benchmark.def: something that can be measured and used as a standard that other things can be compared with
benchmark.ex: Tests at the age of seven provide a benchmark against which the child's progress at school can be measured.

bend.def: a curve or turn, especially in a road or river
bend.ex: a sharp bend in the road

bend.def: to lean, or make something lean, in a particular direction

beneath.def: in or to a lower position than somebody/something; under somebody/something
beneath.ex: They found the body buried beneath a pile of leaves.

beneficial.def: improving a situation; having a helpful or useful effect
beneficial.ex: A good diet is beneficial to health.

beneficiary.def: a person who gains as a result of something
beneficiary.ex: Who will be the main beneficiary of the cuts in income tax?

benefit.def: an advantage that something gives you; a helpful and useful effect that something has
benefit.ex: Freedom of information brings great benefits.

benefit.def: to be useful to somebody or improve their life in some way
benefit.ex: We should spend the money on something that will benefit everyone.

beside.def: next to or at the side of somebody/something
beside.ex: He sat beside her all night.

besides.def: used for making an extra comment that adds to what you have just said
besides.ex: I don't really want to go. Besides, it's too late now.

besides.def: in addition to somebody/something; apart from somebody/something
besides.ex: We have lots of things in common besides music.

best.def: of the most excellent type or quality
best.ex: That's the best movie I've ever seen!
Which one do you like best?

best.def: the most excellent thing or person
best.ex: We all want the best for our children.

bet.def: an arrangement to risk money, etc. on the result of a particular event; the money that you risk in this way
to risk money on a race or an event by trying to predict the result
bet.ex: You have to be over 16 to bet.

betray.def: to give information about somebody/something to an enemy
betray.ex: He was offered money to betray his colleagues.

better.def: of a higher standard or less poor quality; not as bad as something else
better.ex: We're hoping for better weather tomorrow.

better.def: in a more excellent or pleasant way; not as badly
better.ex: She sings much better than I do.
the better of the two books

between.def: in the space or period of time separating two or more points, objects, etc. or two dates, events, etc.
between.ex: The house was near a park but there was a road in between.

between.def: in or into the space separating two or more points, objects, people, etc.
between.ex: Q comes between P and R in the English alphabet.

beyond.def: on the other side; further on
beyond.ex: Snowdon and the mountains beyond were covered in snow.

beyond.def: on or to the further side of something
beyond.ex: The road continues beyond the village up into the hills.

bias.def: a strong feeling in favour of or against one group of people, or one side in an argument, often not based on fair judgement
bias.ex: accusations of political bias in news programmes (= that reports are unfair and show favour to one political party)

bicycle.def: a road vehicle with two wheels that you ride by pushing the pedals with your feet
bicycle.ex: He got on his bicycle and rode off.

bid.def: an offer by a person or a company to pay a particular amount of money for something
bid.ex: A German firm launched a takeover bid for the company.

bid.def: to offer to pay a particular price for something, especially at an auction

big.def: large in size, degree, amount, etc.

bike.ex: I used to ride my bike around the neighbourhood for hours.

bill.def: a document that shows how much you owe somebody for goods or services
to send somebody a bill for something
bill.ex: Please bill me for the books.

bin.def: a container that you put waste in

bind.def: to tie somebody/something with rope, string, etc. so that they/it cannot move or are held together strongly
bind.ex: She was bound to a chair.

biography.def: the story of a person’s life written by somebody else; this type of writing

biological.def: connected with the science of biology

biology.def: the scientific study of the life and structure of plants and animals

bird.def: a creature that is covered with feathers and has two wings and two legs. Most birds can fly.
bird.ex: a bird’s nest with two eggs in it

birth.def: the time when a baby is born; the process of being born
birth.ex: The baby weighed three kilos at birth.

birthday.def: the day in each year which is the same date as the one on which you were born

biscuit.def: a small flat dry cake for one person, usually sweet, and baked until hard

bishop.def: a senior priest in charge of the work of the Church in a city or district

bit.ex: These trousers are a bit tight.

bite.ex: The dog gave me a playful bite.

bite.def: to use your teeth to cut into or through something

bitter.def: having a strong, unpleasant taste; not sweet
bitter.ex: Black coffee leaves a bitter taste in the mouth.

black.def: having the very darkest colour, like coal or the sky at night
the very darkest colour, like night or coal
black.ex: the black of the night sky

blade.def: the flat part of a knife, tool or machine, which has a sharp edge or edges for cutting
blade.ex: The machine comes with a plastic guard over the blade to protect the operator.

blame.def: responsibility for doing something badly or wrongly; saying that somebody is responsible for something
blame.ex: Why do I always get the blame for everything that goes wrong?

blame.def: to think or say that somebody/something is responsible for something bad
blame.ex: She doesn't blame anyone for her father's death.

blank.def: empty, with nothing written, printed or recorded on it
blank.ex: Sign your name in the blank space below.

blank.def: an empty space on a printed form or document for you to write answers, information, etc. in

blanket.def: a large cover, often made of wool, used especially on beds to keep people warm
blanket.ex: It’s cold tonight—can I have another blanket?

blast.def: an explosion or a powerful movement of air caused by an explosion
to violently destroy or break something into pieces, using explosives
blast.ex: They blasted a huge crater in the runway.

bleed.def: to lose blood, especially from a wound or an injury

blend.def: a mixture of different types of the same thing
to mix two or more substances together
blend.ex: Blend the flour with the milk to make a smooth paste.

bless.def: to ask God to protect somebody/something
bless.ex: They brought the children to Jesus and he blessed them.

blessing.def: God’s help and protection, or a prayer asking for this

blind.ex: Doctors think he will go blind.

block.def: a large piece of a solid material that is square or rectangular in shape and usually has flat sides
block.ex: The houses are made of concrete blocks with tin roofs.

block.def: to stop something from moving or flowing through a pipe, a passage, a road, etc. by putting something in it or across it
block.ex: After today's heavy snow, many roads are still blocked.

blog.def: a website where an individual person, or people representing an organization, write regularly about recent events or topics that interest them, usually with photos and links to other websites that they find interesting
blog.ex: This is a link to the museum's blog.

blood.def: the red liquid that flows through the bodies of humans and animals
blood.ex: He lost a lot of blood in the accident.

blow.def: a hard hit with the hand, a weapon, etc.
blow.ex: She received a severe blow on the head.

blow.def: to send out air from the mouth

blue.def: having the colour of a clear sky or the sea on a clear day
the colour of a clear sky or the sea on a clear day

board.def: a long thin piece of strong hard material, especially wood, used, for example, for making floors, building walls and roofs and making boats
board.ex: He had ripped up the carpet, leaving only the bare boards.

board.def: to get on a ship, train, plane, bus, etc.

boast.def: to talk in a way that shows you are too proud of something that you have or can do
boast.ex: I don't want to boast, but I can actually speak six languages.

boat.def: a vehicle (smaller than a ship) that travels on water, moved by oars, sails or a motor

body.def: the whole physical structure of a human or an animal
body.ex: The human body is a complex structure.

boil.def: when a liquid boils or when you boil it, it is heated to the point where it forms bubbles and turns to steam or vapour
boil.ex: The water was bubbling and boiling away.

bold.def: brave and confident; not afraid to say what you feel or to take risks
bold.ex: It was a bold move on their part to open a business in France.

bomb.def: a weapon designed to explode at a particular time or when it is dropped or thrown
to attack something/somebody by leaving a bomb in a place or by dropping bombs from a plane
bomb.ex: Warplanes bombed targets in and around the capital.

bombing.def: an occasion when a bomb is dropped or left somewhere; the act of doing this

bond.def: something that forms a connection between people or groups, such as a feeling of friendship or shared ideas and experiences

bone.def: any of the hard parts that form the skeleton of the body of a human or an animal
bone.ex: He survived the accident with no broken bones.

bonus.def: an extra amount of money that is added to a payment, especially to somebody’s wages or salary as a reward

book.def: a set of printed pages that are fastened inside a cover so that you can turn them and read them
book.ex: His desk was covered with piles of books.

book.def: to arrange to have or use something on a particular date in the future; to buy a ticket in advance

booking.def: an arrangement that you make in advance to buy a ticket to travel somewhere, go to the theatre, etc.

boom.def: a sudden increase in trade and economic activity; a period of wealth and success
boom.ex: Living standards improved rapidly during the post-war boom.

boost.def: something that helps or encourages somebody/something
to make something increase, or become better or more successful

boot.def: a strong shoe that covers the foot and ankle and often the lower part of the leg

border.def: the line that divides two countries or areas; the land near this line
border.ex: I live in a small town in the US, near the Canadian border.

border.def: to share a border with another country or area

bored.def: feeling tired and impatient because you have lost interest in somebody/something or because you have nothing to do
bored.ex: There was a bored expression on her face.

boring.def: not interesting; making you feel tired and impatient

born.def: to come out of your mother’s body at the beginning of your life

borrow.def: to take and use something that belongs to somebody else, and return it to them at a later time

boss.def: a person who is in charge of other people at work and tells them what to do
boss.ex: I'll ask my boss if I can have the day off.

both.def: used with plural nouns to mean ‘the two’ or ‘the one as well as the other’

bother.def: to spend time and/or energy doing something

bottle.def: a glass or plastic container, usually round with straight sides and a narrow neck, used especially for storing liquids

bottom.def: in the lowest, last or furthest place or position

bounce.def: if something bounces or you bounce it, it moves quickly away from a surface it has just hit or you make it do this
bounce.ex: The ball bounced twice before he could reach it.

bound.def: certain or likely to happen, or to do or be something
bound.ex: There are bound to be changes when the new system is introduced.

boundary.def: a real or imagined line that marks the limits or edges of something and separates it from other things or places; a dividing line
boundary.ex: After the war the national boundaries were redrawn.

bow.def: the act of bending your head or the upper part of your body forward in order to say hello or goodbye to somebody or to show respect
bow.ex: She gave a slight bow of her head in greeting.

bow.def: to move your head or the top half of your body forwards and downwards as a sign of respect or to say hello or goodbye
bow.ex: He bowed low to the assembled crowd.

bowl.def: a deep round dish with a wide open top, used especially for holding food or liquid

box.def: a container made of wood, thick card, metal, etc. with a flat stiff base and sides and often a lid (= cover), used especially for holding solid things
box.ex: Everything we owned was neatly packed in cardboard boxes.

boy.def: a male child or a young male person

boyfriend.def: a man or boy that somebody has a romantic or sexual relationship with

brain.def: the organ inside the head that controls movement, thought, memory and feeling
brain.ex: The human brain is a complex organ.

branch.def: a part of a tree that grows out from the main stem and on which leaves, flowers and fruit grow
branch.ex: She climbed the tree and hid in the branches.

brand.def: a type of product, service, etc. made or offered by a particular company under a particular name
to apply a brand name, image or identity to something
brand.ex: The website was not doing a very good job of branding the company.

brave.def: willing to do things that are difficult, dangerous or painful; not afraid

breach.def: a failure to do something that must be done by law
to not keep to an agreement or not keep a promise
breach.ex: The government is accused of breaching the terms of the treaty.

bread.def: a type of food made from flour, water and usually yeast mixed together and baked

break.def: a short period of time when you stop what you are doing and rest, eat, etc.
break.ex: She was on her lunch break.

break.def: to be damaged and separated into two or more parts, as a result of force; to damage something in this way
break.ex: All the windows broke with the force of the blast.

breakdown.def: an occasion when a vehicle or machine stops working

breakfast.def: the first meal of the day
breakfast.ex: They were having breakfast when I arrived.

breakthrough.def: an important development that may lead to an agreement or achievement

breast.def: either of the two round soft parts at the front of a woman’s body that produce milk when she has had a baby
breast.ex: She put the baby to her breast.

breath.def: the air that you take into your lungs and send out again

breathe.def: to take air into your lungs and send it out again through your nose or mouth
breathe.ex: He breathed deeply before speaking again.

breathing.def: the action of taking air into the lungs and sending it out again
breathing.ex: Her breathing became steady and she fell asleep.

breed.def: a particular type of animal that has been developed by people in a certain way, especially a type of dog, cat or farm animal
breed.ex: Labradors and other large breeds of dog

breed.def: to have sex and produce young
breed.ex: Many animals breed only at certain times of the year.

brick.def: baked clay used for building walls, houses and other buildings; an individual block of this
brick.ex: The school is built of brick.

bride.def: a woman on her wedding day, or just before or just after it
bride.ex: a toast to the bride and groom

bridge.def: a structure that is built over a road, railway, river, etc. so that people, vehicles, etc. can cross from one side to the other
bridge.ex: We crossed the bridge over the River Windrush.

brief.def: lasting only a short time; short

briefly.ex: He had spoken to Emma only briefly.

bring.def: to come to a place with somebody/something
bring.ex: Don't forget to bring your books with you.

broadband.def: a way of connecting to the internet that allows you to receive information, including pictures, etc., very quickly and that is always active (so that the user does not have to connect each time)
broadband.ex: plans to provide rural areas with fast broadband

broadcast.ex: (British English), a party political broadcast (= for example, before an election)

broadcast.def: to send out programmes on television or radio
broadcast.ex: The concert will be broadcast live (= at the same time as it takes place) tomorrow evening.

broadcaster.def: a person whose job is presenting or talking on television or radio programmes
broadcaster.ex: She is a writer and broadcaster on environmental matters.

broken.def: that has been damaged or injured; no longer whole or working correctly

brother.def: a boy or man who has the same parents as another person

brown.def: having the colour of earth or coffee
the colour of earth or coffee
brown.ex: leaves of various shades of brown

browser.def: a computer program that lets you look at or read documents on the World Wide Web
browser.ex: What do you use as your default browser?

brush.def: an object made of short stiff hairs (called bristles) or wires set in a block of wood or plastic, usually attached to a handle. Brushes are used for many different jobs, such as cleaning, painting and tidying your hair.
to clean, polish or make something smooth with a brush
brush.ex: brush something, to brush your hair/teeth

bubble.def: a ball of air or gas in a liquid, or a ball of air inside a solid substance such as glass

buck.def: a US, Australian or New Zealand dollar; a South African rand; an Indian rupee

buddy.ex: an old college buddy of mine

budget.def: the money that is available to a person or an organization and a plan of how it will be spent over a period of time

buffer.def: a thing or person that reduces a shock or protects somebody/something against difficulties
buffer.ex: Support from family and friends acts as a buffer against stress.

bug.ex: There's a bug crawling up your arm.

build.def: to make something, especially a building, by putting parts together
build.ex: They have permission to build 200 new homes.

building.def: a structure such as a house or school that has a roof and walls

bulk.def: the main part of something; most of something
bulk.ex: The bulk of the population lives in cities.

bullet.def: a small metal object that is fired from a gun
bullet.ex: He was found to have a single bullet wound in his chest.

bunch.def: a number of things of the same type which are growing or fastened together
bunch.ex: a bunch of bananas, grapes, etc.

burden.def: a duty, responsibility, etc. that causes worry, difficulty or hard work

bureaucracy.def: the system of official rules and ways of doing things that a government or an organization has, especially when these seem to be too complicated

burial.def: the act or ceremony of burying a dead body

burn.def: an injury or a mark caused by fire, heat or acid

burn.ex: A welcoming fire was burning in the fireplace.

burst.def: to break open or apart, especially because of pressure from inside; to make something break in this way
burst.ex: That balloon will burst if you blow it up any more.

bury.def: to place a dead body in the ground
bury.ex: They killed her and buried her body.

bus.def: a large road vehicle that carries passengers, especially one that travels along a fixed route and stops regularly to let people get on and off
bus.ex: Shall we walk or go by bus?

bush.def: a plant that grows thickly with several hard stems coming up from the root

business.def: the activity of making, buying, selling or supplying goods or services for money
business.ex: It's been a pleasure to do business with you.

businessman.def: a man who works in business, especially at a high level
businessman.ex: a group of visiting Italian politicians and businessmen
