busy.def: having a lot to do; perhaps not free to do something else because you are working on something

but.def: used to introduce a word or phrase that contrasts with what was said before
but.ex: I got it wrong. It wasn't the red one but the blue one.
We've had nothing but trouble with this car.

butter.def: a soft yellow food made from cream, used in cooking and for spreading on bread

button.def: a small round piece of metal, plastic, etc. that is sewn onto a piece of clothing and used for fastening two parts together
button.ex: (British English), to do up/undo your buttons

buy.def: to obtain something by paying money for it
buy.ex: Where did you buy that dress?

by.def: used for showing how or in what way something is done
by.ex: The house is heated by gas.

cabin.def: a small room on a ship in which you live or sleep
cabin.ex: I lay in my cabin feeling miserably seasick.

cabinet.def: a group of senior members of a government that is responsible for advising and deciding on government policy

cable.def: a set of wires, covered in plastic or rubber, that carries electricity, phone signals, etc.

cafe.def: a place where you can buy drinks and simple meals. Alcohol is not usually served in British or American cafes.
cafe.ex: There are small shops and pavement cafes around every corner.

cake.def: a sweet food made from a mixture of flour, eggs, butter, sugar, etc. that is baked in an oven. Cakes are made in various shapes and sizes and are often decorated, for example with cream or icing.

calculate.def: to use numbers to find out a total number, amount, distance, etc.
calculate.ex: An independent valuer will calculate the value of your property.

calculation.def: the act or process of using numbers to find out an amount

call.def: the act of speaking to somebody on the phone
call.ex: to get/receive a call from somebody

call.def: to give somebody/something a particular name; to use a particular name or title when you are talking to somebody
call.ex: They decided to call the baby Mark.

calm.ex: It is important to keep calm in an emergency.

calm.def: a quiet and peaceful time or situation
to make somebody/something become quiet and more relaxed, especially after strong emotion or excitement
calm.ex: Have some tea; it'll calm your nerves.

camera.def: a piece of equipment for taking photographs, moving pictures or television pictures. It can be a separate item or part of another device.
camera.ex: Just point the camera and press the button.

camp.def: a place where people live temporarily in tents or temporary buildings
to put up a tent and live in it for a short time
camp.ex: I camped overnight in a field.

campaign.def: a series of planned activities that are intended to achieve a particular social, commercial or political aim
to take part in or lead a campaign, for example to achieve social or political change, or in order to win an election
campaign.ex: The party campaigned vigorously in the north of the country.

camping.def: living in a tent, etc. on holiday

campus.def: the buildings of a university or college and the land around them

can.def: a metal container in which food and drink is sold
can.ex: can of something, a can of beans/beer/soda

can.def: used to say that it is possible for somebody/something to do something, or for something to happen

canal.def: a long straight passage dug in the ground and filled with water for boats and ships to travel along; a smaller passage used for carrying water to fields, crops, etc.

cancel.def: to decide that something that has been arranged will not now take place
cancel.ex: All flights have been cancelled because of bad weather.

cancer.def: a serious disease in which growths of cells, also called cancers, form in the body and kill normal body cells. The disease often causes death.

candidate.def: a person who is trying to be elected or is applying for a job

candle.def: a round stick of wax with a piece of string (called a wick) through the middle that is lit to give light as it burns

cannot.def: the negative of can; can not
cannot.ex: I cannot believe the price of the tickets!

canvas.def: a strong heavy rough material used for making tents, sails, etc. and by artists for painting on

cap.def: a type of soft flat hat with a peak (= a hard curved part sticking out in front). Caps are worn especially by men and boys, often as part of a uniform

capability.def: the ability or qualities necessary to do something
capability.ex: Age affects the range of a person's capabilities.

capable.def: having the ability or qualities necessary for doing something
capable.ex: You are capable of better work than this.

capacity.def: the number of things or people that a container or space can hold
capacity.ex: a fuel tank with a maximum capacity of 50 litres

capital.def: having the form and size used at the beginning of a sentence or a name
capital.ex: English is written with a capital ‘E’.

capital.def: the most important town or city of a country or region, where the government operates from
capital.ex: Cairo is the capital of Egypt.

capitalism.def: an economic system in which a country’s businesses and industry are controlled and run for profit by private owners rather than by the government
capitalism.ex: the growth of industrial capitalism in the West

capitalist.def: based on the principles of capitalism

captain.def: the person in charge of a ship or commercial aircraft

capture.def: the act of capturing somebody/something or of being captured
capture.ex: He evaded capture for three days.

capture.def: to catch a person or an animal and keep them as a prisoner or shut them in a space that they cannot escape from
capture.ex: Allied troops captured over 300 enemy soldiers.

car.def: a road vehicle with an engine and four wheels that can carry a small number of passengers
car.ex: Paula got into the car and drove off.

carbon.def: a chemical element. Carbon is found in all living things, existing in a pure state as diamond and graphite.

card.def: thick, stiff paper; a piece of this for writing on

care.def: the process of caring for somebody/something and providing what they need for their health or protection
care.ex: Some people were badly injured and needed medical care.

care.def: to feel that something is important and worth worrying about
care.ex: I don't care (= I will not be upset) if I never see him again!

career.def: the series of jobs that a person has in a particular area of work, usually involving more responsibility as time passes

careful.def: giving attention or thought to what you are doing so that you avoid hurting yourself, damaging something or doing something wrong

carefully.def: with a lot of attention or thought, so that you avoid hurting yourself, damaging something or doing something wrong

careless.def: not giving enough attention and thought to what you are doing, so that you make mistakes

cargo.def: the goods carried in a ship, an aircraft or a motor vehicle
cargo.ex: The tanker began to spill its cargo of oil.

carpet.def: a piece of thick woven material made of wool, etc., used to cover the floor of a room or stairs; the material used for carpets
carpet.ex: He spent the morning laying the new carpet.

carriage.def: a separate section of a train for carrying passengers

carrot.def: a long pointed orange root vegetable

carry.def: to support the weight of somebody/something and take them or it from place to place; to take somebody/something from one place to another
carry.ex: He was carrying a large bag.

cartoon.def: a humorous drawing in a newspaper or magazine, especially one about politics or events in the news

carve.def: to make objects, patterns, etc. by cutting away material from a piece of wood or stone, or another hard material

case.def: a particular situation or a situation of a particular type
case.ex: In some cases people have had to wait several weeks for an appointment.

cash.def: money in the form of coins or notes
cash.ex: How much cash do you have on you?

casino.def: a public building or room where people play gambling games for money
casino.ex: a new hotel and casino in Las Vegas

cast.def: all the people who act in a play or film
cast.ex: an all-star cast (= including many well-known actors)

cast.def: to look, smile, etc. in a particular direction
cast.ex: She cast a welcoming smile in his direction.

castle.def: a large strong building with thick, high walls and towers, built in the past by kings or queens, or other important people, to defend themselves against attack

casual.ex: casual clothes (= comfortable clothes that you choose to wear in your free time)

casualty.def: a person who is killed or injured in war or in an accident
casualty.ex: Our primary objective is reducing road casualties.

cat.def: a small animal with soft fur that people often keep as a pet. Cats catch and kill birds and mice.

catalogue.def: a complete list of items, for example of things that people can look at or buy
catalogue.ex: a mail-order catalogue (= a book showing goods for sale to be sent to people’s homes)

catch.def: an act of catching something, for example a ball
catch.ex: Roger made some brilliant catches at today's game.

catch.def: to stop and hold a moving object or person, especially in your hands
catch.ex: She managed to catch the keys as they fell.

category.def: a group of people or things with particular features in common
category.ex: These are the nominees from each category.

cater.def: to provide food and drinks for a social event
cater.ex: Most of our work now involves catering for weddings.

cattle.def: cows and bulls that are kept as farm animals for their milk or meat

cause.def: the person or thing that makes something happen
cause.ex: Unemployment is a major cause of poverty.

cause.def: to make something happen, especially something bad or unpleasant; to make somebody do something
cause.ex: Do they know what caused the fire?

caution.def: care that you take in order to avoid danger or mistakes; the fact of not taking any risks

cautious.def: being careful about what you say or do, especially to avoid danger or mistakes; not taking any risks
cautious.ex: The government has been cautious in its response to the report.

cave.def: a large hole in the side of a hill or cliff or under the ground
cave.ex: the mouth (= the entrance) of the cave

cd.def: a small disc on which sound or information is recorded. CDs can be played or read on various types of machines, including CD players and computers. (the abbreviation for ‘compact disc’)
cd.ex: His albums are available on CD and online.

cease.def: to stop happening or existing; to stop something from happening or existing
cease.ex: Welfare payments cease as soon as an individual starts a job.

ceiling.def: the top inside surface of a room
ceiling.ex: a large room with a high ceiling

celebrate.def: to show that a day or an event is important by doing something special on it
celebrate.ex: Jake's passed his exams. We're going out to celebrate.

celebration.def: a special event that people organize in order to celebrate something
celebration.ex: The occasion was the 40th anniversary celebrations of the orchestra.

cell.def: the smallest unit of living matter that can exist on its own. All plants and animals are made up of cells.

cemetery.def: an area of land used for burying dead people, especially one that is not next to a church
cemetery.ex: He was buried in a private cemetery.

cent.def: a coin and unit of money worth 1% of the main unit of money in many countries, for example of the US dollar or of the euro
cent.ex: A one-minute phone call to the UK cost 10 cents.

central.def: in the centre of an area or object

centre.def: the middle point or part of something
centre.ex: He walked to the centre of the circle.

centre.def: to be the person or thing around which most activity takes place; to make somebody/something the central person or thing
centre.ex: State occasions always centred around the king.

century.ex: Valparaiso was the country's main port.

ceremony.def: a public or religious occasion that includes a series of formal or traditional actions
ceremony.ex: More than 1 000 people attended the ceremony.

certain.def: strongly believing something; having no doubts
certain.ex: I think it was him, but I can't be certain.

certainly.ex: Without treatment, she will almost certainly die.

certainty.ex: There is no certainty that the president's removal would end the civil war.

certificate.def: an official document that may be used to prove that the facts it states are true

chain.def: a series of connected metal rings, used for pulling or fastening things; a length of chain used for a particular purpose
chain.ex: She wore a heavy gold chain around her neck.

chain.def: to fasten something with a chain; to fasten somebody/something to another person or thing with a chain, so that they do not escape or get stolen
chain.ex: The doors were always locked and chained.

chair.def: a piece of furniture for one person to sit on, with a back, a seat and four legs
to act as the chairman or chairwoman of a meeting, discussion, etc.

chairman.def: the person in charge of a meeting, who tells people when they can speak, etc.
chairman.ex: Sir Herbert took it upon himself to act as chairman.

challenge.def: a new or difficult task that tests somebody’s ability and skill
to question whether a statement or an action is right, legal, etc.; to refuse to accept something
challenge.ex: His legal team immediately sought to challenge the decision.

challenging.def: difficult in an interesting way that tests your ability

chamber.def: a hall in a public building that is used for formal meetings
chamber.ex: The members left the council chamber.

champion.def: a person, team, etc. that has won a competition, especially in a sport

championship.def: a competition to find the best player or team in a particular sport

chance.def: a possibility of something happening, especially something that you want
chance.ex: She only has a slim chance of passing the exam.

change.def: the act or result of something becoming different
change.ex: We're hoping for a change in the weather.
Rick hasn't changed. He looks exactly the same as he did at school.

chapter.def: a separate section of a book, usually with a number or title

character.def: a person or an animal in a book, play or film
character.ex: the main character in the film

characteristic.def: very typical of something or of somebody’s character
characteristic.ex: Community support of families is characteristic of many societies.

characteristic.def: a typical feature or quality that something/somebody has
characteristic.ex: There were few similarities in the brothers' physical characteristics.

characterize.def: to be typical of a person, place or thing
characterize.ex: the rolling hills that characterize this part of England

charge.def: the amount of money that somebody asks for goods and services
to ask an amount of money for goods or a service
charge.ex: The fees charged by some companies are excessive.

charity.def: an organization for helping people in need

charm.def: the power of pleasing or attracting people
charm.ex: He was a man of great charm.

charming.ex: The cottage is tiny, but it's charming.

chart.def: a diagram, lists of figures, etc. that shows information
chart.ex: Some shares have lost two-thirds of their value since being issued (see chart).

chart.def: to record or follow the progress or development of somebody/something
chart.ex: The exhibition charts the history of the palace.

charter.def: a written statement describing the rights that a particular group of people should have
charter.ex: the European Social Charter of workers’ rights

chase.def: an act of running or driving after somebody/something in order to catch them or it
chase.ex: The thieves were caught by police after a short chase.

chase.def: to run, drive, etc. after somebody/something in order to catch them or it

chat.ex: I just called in for a chat.

chat.def: to talk in a friendly, informal way to somebody
chat.ex: I'm sorry I can't stop to chat.

cheap.def: costing little money or less money than you expected

cheap.ex: I got this dress cheap in a sale.

cheat.def: a person who cheats, especially in a game
to trick somebody or make them believe something that is not true
cheat.ex: She is accused of attempting to cheat the taxman.

check.def: an act of making sure that something is safe, correct or in good condition by examining it
check.ex: Could you give the tyres a check?

check.def: to examine something to see if it is correct, safe or acceptable
check.ex: She gave me the minutes of the meeting to read and check.

cheek.def: either side of the face below the eyes

cheer.def: a shout of joy, support or praise
cheer.ex: A great cheer went up from the crowd.

cheer.def: to shout loudly, in order to show support or praise for somebody, or to encourage them
cheer.ex: We all cheered as the team came on to the field.

cheerful.def: happy, and showing it by the way that you behave
cheerful.ex: You're not your usual cheerful self today.

cheese.def: a type of food made from milk that can be either soft or hard and is usually white or yellow in colour; a particular type of this food

chef.def: a person whose job is to cook, especially the most senior person in a restaurant, hotel, etc.
chef.ex: a new book by celebrity chef Jamie Oliver

chemical.ex: changes in the chemical composition of the atmosphere

chemical.def: a substance obtained by or used in a chemical process

chemistry.def: the scientific study of the structure of substances, how they react when combined or in contact with one another, and how they behave under different conditions

chest.def: the top part of the front of the body, between the neck and the stomach

chicken.def: a large bird that is often kept for its eggs or meat
chicken.ex: They keep chickens in the back yard.

chief.def: a person with a high rank or the highest rank in a company or an organization

child.def: a young human who is not yet an adult

childhood.def: the period of somebody’s life when they are a child

chip.def: a long thin piece of potato fried in oil or fat
chip.ex: He was eating a burger and chips.

chocolate.def: a hard brown sweet food made from cocoa beans, used in cooking to add taste to cakes, etc. or eaten as a sweet

choice.def: an act of choosing between two or more possibilities; something that you can choose
choice.ex: We are faced with a difficult choice.

choir.def: a group of people who sing together, for example in church services or public performances
choir.ex: She sings in the school choir.

choose.def: to decide which thing or person you want out of the ones that are available

chop.def: to cut something into pieces with a sharp tool such as a knife
chop.ex: He was chopping logs for firewood.

chronic.def: lasting for a long time; difficult to cure

chunk.def: a thick, solid piece that has been cut or broken off something

church.def: a building where Christians go to attend services, pray, etc.
church.ex: The procession moved into the church.

cigarette.def: a thin tube of paper filled with tobacco, for smoking

cinema.def: a building in which films are shown

circle.def: to draw a circle around something
circle.ex: Luke circled a date on the kitchen calendar.

circuit.def: a line, route or journey around a place
circuit.ex: The race ended with eight laps of a city centre circuit.

circulate.def: when a liquid, gas or air circulates or is circulated, it moves continuously around a place or system
circulate.ex: The condition prevents the blood from circulating freely.

circulation.def: the movement of blood around the body
circulation.ex: Regular exercise will improve blood circulation.

circumstance.def: the conditions and facts that are connected with and affect a situation, an event or an action
circumstance.ex: Police said there were no suspicious circumstances surrounding the boy's death.

cite.def: to mention something as a reason or an example, or in order to support what you are saying
cite.ex: She cited examples of companies the city has helped relocate or expand.

citizen.def: a person who has the legal right to belong to a particular country
citizen.ex: The defeat of the president did not change the lives of ordinary citizens for the better.

citizenship.def: the legal right to belong to a particular country
citizenship.ex: They were granted full French citizenship.

city.ex: Parking is difficult in the city centre.

civic.def: officially connected with a town or city

civil.def: connected with the people who live in a country
civil.ex: civil unrest (= that is caused by groups of people within a country)

civilian.def: connected with people who are not members of the armed forces or the police
civilian.ex: He left the army and returned to civilian life.

civilian.def: a person who is not a member of the armed forces or the police
civilian.ex: Two soldiers and one civilian were killed in the explosion.

civilization.def: a state of human society that is very developed and organized

claim.def: a statement that something is true although it has not been proved and other people may not agree with or believe it
claim.ex: The company had made false claims about its products.

claim.def: to say that something is true although it has not been proved and other people may not believe it
claim.ex: He claims (that) he was not given a fair hearing.

clarify.def: to make something clearer or easier to understand
clarify.ex: clarify something, to clarify a situation/problem/issue

clarity.def: the quality of being expressed clearly
clarity.ex: a lack of clarity in the law

clash.def: a short fight between two groups of people
clash.ex: Clashes broke out between police and demonstrators.

class.def: a group of students who are taught together
class.ex: We were in the same class at school.

classic.def: accepted or deserving to be accepted as one of the best or most important of its kind
a book, film or song which is well known and considered to be of very high quality, setting standards for other books, etc.
classic.ex: The film is now a cult classic.

classification.def: the act or process of putting people or things into a group or class (= of classifying them)
classification.ex: a style of music that defies classification (= is like no other)

classify.def: to arrange something in groups according to features that they have in common
classify.ex: The books in the library are classified according to subject.

classroom.def: a room where a class of children or students is taught

clause.def: a group of words that includes a subject and a verb, and forms a sentence or part of a sentence
clause.ex: There are languages that require the subject to come before the object in a clause.

clean.def: to remove the dirt or dust from something
clean.ex: I spent all day cooking and cleaning.

clear.def: easy to understand and not confusing
clear.ex: She gave me clear and precise directions.

clear.def: to remove things that are not wanted or needed from something
clear.ex: The settlers cleared the land and planted crops.

clearly.def: in a way that is easy to see or hear
clearly.ex: Please speak clearly after the tone.

clerk.def: a person whose job is to serve customers in a shop
clerk.ex: The clerk at the counter gave me too little change.

clever.def: quick at learning and understanding things

click.def: the act of pressing the button on a computer mouse or touchpad
to choose a particular function or item on a computer screen, etc., by pressing one of the buttons on a mouse or touchpad
click.ex: Click here to add your opinion to the survey.

client.def: a person who uses the services or advice of a professional person or organization
client.ex: She's a well-known lawyer with many famous clients.

cliff.def: a high area of rock with a very steep side, often at the edge of the sea or ocean

climate.def: the regular pattern of weather conditions of a particular place

climb.def: an act of climbing up a mountain, rock or large number of steps; a period of time spent climbing
to go up something towards the top
climb.ex: climb (up) something, to climb a mountain/tree

cling.def: to hold on tightly to somebody/something
cling.ex: cling to somebody/something, survivors clinging to a raft

clinic.def: a building or part of a hospital where people can go for special medical treatment or advice
clinic.ex: Your local family planning clinic can give you advice about birth control.

clinical.def: relating to the examination and treatment of patients and their illnesses
clinical.ex: clinical research (= done on patients, not just considering theory)

clip.def: a short part of a film that is shown separately
clip.ex: Here is a clip from her latest movie.

clock.def: an instrument for measuring and showing time, in a room, on the wall of a building or on a computer screen (not worn or carried like a watch)

close.ex: I had no idea the beach was so close.

close.def: the end of a period of time or an activity
close.ex: at the close of the seventeenth century

close.def: to put something into a position so that it covers an opening; to get into this position

closely.ex: He walked into the room, closely followed by the rest of the family.

closure.def: the situation when a factory, school, hospital, etc. shuts permanently

cloth.def: material made by weaving or knitting cotton, wool, silk, etc.

clothes.def: the things that you wear, such as trousers, dresses and jackets
clothes.ex: I bought some new clothes for the trip.

clothing.def: clothes, especially a particular type of clothes
clothing.ex: Workers at the factory wear protective clothing.

cloud.def: a grey or white mass that floats in the sky, made of very small drops of water
cloud.ex: Dark clouds were gathering in the west.

club.def: an organization for people who share an interest or do a sport or activity together

clue.def: an object, a piece of evidence or some information that helps the police solve a crime

cluster.def: a group of things of the same type that grow or appear close together
cluster.ex: The telescope is focused on a dense cluster of stars at the edge of the galaxy.

coach.def: a person who trains a person or team in sport
to train somebody to play a sport, to do a job better, or to improve a skill
coach.ex: He has coached the team for five years.

coal.def: a hard black mineral that is found below the ground and burnt to produce heat
coal.ex: I put more coal on the fire.

coalition.def: a government formed by two or more political parties working together
coalition.ex: The two parties have formed a coalition.

coast.def: the land next to or near to the sea or ocean

coat.def: a piece of outdoor clothing that is worn over other clothes to keep warm or dry. Coats have sleeves (= parts covering the arms) and may be long or short.

cocktail.def: a drink usually made from a mixture of one or more spirits (= strong alcoholic drinks) and fruit juice. It can also be made without alcohol.

code.def: a system of words, letters, numbers or symbols that represent a message or record information secretly or in a shorter form
code.ex: to break/crack a code (= to understand and read the message)

coffee.def: the roasted seeds (called coffee beans) of a tropical bush; a powder made from them

cognitive.def: connected with mental processes of understanding

coin.def: a small flat piece of metal used as money

coincide.def: to take place at the same time
coincide.ex: It's a pity our trips to New York don't coincide.

coincidence.def: the fact of two things happening at the same time by chance, in a surprising way

cold.def: having a lower than usual temperature; having a temperature lower than the human body
cold.ex: I'm cold. Turn the heating up.

cold.def: a common illness that affects the nose and/or throat, making you cough, sneeze, etc.

collaborate.def: to work together with somebody in order to produce or achieve something
collaborate.ex: Researchers around the world are collaborating to develop a new vaccine.

collaboration.def: the act of working with another person or group of people to create or produce something
collaboration.ex: It was a collaboration that produced extremely useful results.

collapse.def: a sudden failure of something, such as an institution, a business or a course of action
collapse.ex: the collapse of the Soviet Union

collapse.def: to fall down or fall in suddenly, often after breaking apart
collapse.ex: The roof collapsed under the weight of snow.

colleague.def: a person that you work with, especially in a profession or a business

collect.def: to bring things together from different people or places

collection.def: a group of objects, often of the same sort, that have been collected
collection.ex: He wanted to share his vast art collection with the world.

collective.def: done or shared by all members of a group of people; involving a whole group or society

collector.def: a person who collects things, either as a hobby or as a job

college.def: (in the UK) a place where students go to study or to receive training after they have left school

collision.def: an accident in which two vehicles or people crash into each other
collision.ex: collision between A and B, a collision between two trains

colonial.def: connected with or belonging to a country that controls another country

colony.def: a country or an area that is governed by people from another, more powerful, country

colour.def: the appearance that things have that results from the way in which they reflect light. Red, orange and green are colours.

coloured.def: having a particular colour or different colours

colourful.def: full of bright colours or having a lot of different colours

column.def: one of the straight sections from top to bottom into which text on a page or screen is divided
column.ex: a dictionary with two columns per page

columnist.def: a journalist who writes regular articles for a newspaper or magazine

combat.def: fighting or a fight, especially during a time of war
to stop something unpleasant or harmful from happening or from getting worse

combination.def: two or more things joined or mixed together to form a single unit
combination.ex: The tragedy was due to a combination of factors.

combine.def: to come together to form a single thing or group; to join two or more things or groups together to form a single one
combine.ex: Hydrogen and oxygen combine to form water.

come.def: to move to or towards a person or place
come.ex: He left and said he was never coming back.

comedy.def: a play, film or TV show that is intended to be funny, usually with a happy ending; plays, films and TV shows of this type

comfort.def: the state of being physically relaxed and free from pain; the state of having a pleasant life, with everything that you need
comfort.ex: The hotel offers a high standard of comfort and service.

comfort.def: to make somebody who is worried or unhappy feel better by being kind and showing sympathy to them
comfort.ex: The victim's widow was today being comforted by family and friends.

comfortable.def: making you feel physically relaxed; pleasant to wear, sit on, etc.

comic.def: a magazine, usually for children, that tells stories through pictures

command.def: an order given to a person or an animal
command.ex: Begin when I give the command.

command.def: to tell somebody to do something
command.ex: He commanded his men to retreat.

commander.def: a person who is in charge of something, especially an officer in charge of a particular group of soldiers or a military operation

commence.def: to begin to happen; to begin something
commence.ex: The meeting is scheduled to commence at noon.

comment.def: something that you say or write that gives an opinion on or explains somebody/something
comment.ex: She made helpful comments on my work.

comment.def: to express an opinion about something
comment.ex: He refused to comment until after the trial.

commentary.def: a spoken description of an event that is given while it is happening, especially on the radio or television

commentator.def: a person who describes an event while it is happening, especially on television or radio

commerce.def: trade, especially between countries; the buying and selling of goods and services
commerce.ex: Leaders of industry and commerce met at the summit in Paris.

commercial.def: connected with the buying and selling of goods and services
an advertisement on television, on the radio or on a website

commission.def: an official group of people who have been given responsibility to control something, or to find out about something, usually for the government
commission.ex: The commission is expected to report its findings next month.

commission.def: to officially ask somebody to write, make or create something or to do a task for you
commission.ex: She has been commissioned to write a new national anthem.

commissioner.def: a member of a commission (= an official group of people who are responsible for controlling something or finding out about something)
commissioner.ex: the Church Commissioners (= the group of people responsible for controlling the financial affairs of the Church of England)

commit.def: to do something wrong or illegal

commitment.def: a promise to do something or to behave in a particular way; a promise to support somebody/something; the fact of committing yourself
commitment.ex: I'm not ready to make a long-term commitment.

committee.def: a group of people who are chosen, usually by a larger group, to make decisions or to deal with a particular subject

commodity.def: a product or a raw material that can be bought and sold
commodity.ex: rice, flour and other basic commodities

common.def: happening often; existing in large numbers or in many places

commonly.def: usually; very often; by most people
commonly.ex: Christopher is commonly known as Kit.

communicate.def: to share or exchange information, news, ideas, feelings, etc.

communication.def: the activity or process of expressing ideas and feelings or of giving people information
communication.ex: Good communication between team leaders and members is essential.

community.def: all the people who live in a particular area, country, etc. when talked about as a group
community.ex: The local community supported us from the start.

companion.def: a person or an animal that travels with you or spends a lot of time with you

company.def: a business organization that makes money by producing or selling goods or services

comparable.def: similar to somebody/something else and able to be compared
comparable.ex: A comparable house in the south of the city would cost twice as much.

comparative.def: measured or judged by how similar or different it is to something else
comparative.ex: Then he was living in comparative comfort (= compared with others or with his own life at a previous time).

compare.def: to examine people or things to see how they are similar and how they are different
compare.ex: It is interesting to compare their situation and ours.

comparison.def: the process of comparing two or more people or things
comparison.ex: For Durkheim, comparison was the most important method of analysis in sociology.

compassion.def: a strong feeling of sympathy for people or animals who are suffering and a desire to help them

compel.def: to force somebody to do something; to make something necessary
compel.ex: The law can compel fathers to make regular payments for their children.

compelling.def: that makes you pay attention to it because it is so interesting and exciting
compelling.ex: Her latest book makes compelling reading.

compensate.def: to provide something good to balance or reduce the bad effects of damage, loss, etc.
compensate.ex: Nothing can compensate for the loss of a loved one.

compensation.def: something, especially money, that somebody gives you because they have hurt you, or damaged something that you own; the act of giving this to somebody

compete.def: to take part in a contest or game
compete.ex: The games were an outstanding success, with almost 2 400 athletes competing.

competence.def: the ability to do something well
competence.ex: to gain a high level of competence in English

competent.def: having enough skill or knowledge to do something well or to the necessary standard
competent.ex: He's very competent in his work.

competition.def: an event in which people compete with each other to find out who is the best at something

competitive.def: used to describe a situation in which people or organizations compete against each other

competitor.def: a person who takes part in a competition

compile.def: to produce a book, list, report, etc. by bringing together different items, articles, songs, etc.; to collect information in order to produce a book, list, etc.
compile.ex: We are trying to compile a list of suitable people for the job.

complain.def: to say that you are annoyed, unhappy or not satisfied about somebody/something
complain.ex: She never complains, but she's obviously exhausted.

complaint.def: a reason for not being satisfied; a statement that somebody makes saying that they are not satisfied

complement.def: to add to something in a way that improves it or makes it more attractive
complement.ex: The excellent menu is complemented by a good wine list.

complete.def: including all the parts, etc. that are necessary; whole
to finish making or doing something

completely.def: in every way possible; in every part
completely.ex: It was completely different from how I had imagined it.

completion.def: the act or process of finishing something; the state of being finished and complete
completion.ex: the completion of the new hospital building

complex.def: made of many different things or parts that are connected; difficult to understand
a group of buildings of a similar type together in one place

complexity.def: the state of being formed of many parts; the state of being difficult to understand
complexity.ex: the increasing complexity of modern telecommunication systems

compliance.def: the practice of obeying rules or requests made by people in authority
compliance.ex: compliance (with something), procedures that must be followed to ensure full compliance with the law

complicated.def: made of many different things or parts that are connected; difficult to understand

complication.def: a thing that makes a situation more complicated or difficult
complication.ex: The bad weather added a further complication to our journey.

comply.def: to obey a rule, an order, etc.; to meet particular standards
comply.ex: They refused to comply with the UN resolution.

component.def: one of several parts of which something is made
component.ex: the different organizations involved in the design of the various components

compose.ex: Mozart composed his last opera shortly before he died.

composer.def: a person who writes music, especially classical music
composer.ex: Verdi was a prolific composer of operas.

composition.def: the different parts that something is made of; the way in which the different parts are organized
composition.ex: the chemical composition of the soil

compound.def: a thing consisting of two or more separate things combined together

comprehensive.def: including all, or almost all, the items, details, facts, information, etc., that may be involved

comprise.def: to have somebody/something as parts or members

compromise.def: an agreement made between two people or groups in which each side gives up some of the things they want so that both sides are happy at the end
compromise.ex: After lengthy talks the two sides finally reached a compromise.

compromise.def: to give up some of your demands in order to reach an agreement after disagreeing with somebody
compromise.ex: Neither side is prepared to compromise.

compulsory.def: that must be done because of a law or a rule
compulsory.ex: English is a compulsory subject at this level.

compute.ex: The losses were computed at £5 million.

computer.def: an electronic machine that can store, organize and find information, do processes with numbers and other data, and control other machines

conceal.ex: The paintings were concealed beneath a thick layer of plaster.

concede.def: to admit that something is true, logical, etc. after first denying it or resisting it

conceive.def: to form an idea, a plan, etc. in your mind
conceive.ex: He conceived the idea of transforming the old power station into an arts centre.

concentrate.def: to give all your attention to something and not think about anything else
concentrate.ex: I can't concentrate with all that noise going on.

concentration.def: the ability to direct all your effort and attention on one thing, without thinking of other things
concentration.ex: This book requires a great deal of concentration.

concept.def: an idea or a principle that is connected with something abstract
concept.ex: concept of something, the concept of social class

conception.def: an understanding or a belief of what something/somebody is or what something/somebody should be

concern.def: a feeling of worry, especially one that is shared by many people
concern.ex: Villagers expressed concern about the level of traffic.

concern.def: to affect somebody/something; to involve somebody/something
concern.ex: Don't interfere in what doesn't concern you.

concerned.def: worried and feeling concern about something/somebody
concerned.ex: Concerned citizens can have a huge impact.

concession.def: something that you allow or do, or allow somebody to have, in order to end an argument or to make a situation less difficult
concession.ex: The firm will be forced to make concessions if it wants to avoid a strike.

conclude.def: to decide or believe something as a result of what you have heard or seen
conclude.ex: It is too early for us to conclude anything.

conclusion.def: something that you decide when you have thought about all the information connected with the situation
conclusion.ex: We can safely draw some conclusions from our discussion.

concrete.def: building material that is made by mixing together cement, sand, small stones and water

condemn.def: to say very strongly that you think something is bad, usually for moral reasons
condemn.ex: The government issued a statement condemning the killings.

condition.def: the state that something is in
condition.ex: in… condition, to be in pristine/excellent/perfect condition

conduct.def: a person’s behaviour in a particular place or in a particular situation
conduct.ex: The sport has a strict code of conduct.

conduct.def: to organize and/or do a particular activity

confer.def: to discuss something with somebody, in order to exchange opinions or get advice
confer.ex: He wanted to confer with his colleagues before reaching a decision.

conference.def: a large official meeting, usually lasting for a few days, at which people with the same work or interests come together to discuss their views
conference.ex: The hotel is used for exhibitions, conferences and social events.

confess.def: to admit, especially formally or to the police, that you have done something wrong or illegal
confess.ex: After hours of questioning, the suspect confessed.

confession.def: a statement that a person makes, admitting that they are guilty of a crime; the act of making such a statement
confession.ex: After hours of questioning by police, she made a full confession.

confidence.def: the feeling that you can trust, believe in and be sure about the abilities or good qualities of somebody/something
confidence.ex: The president's actions hardly inspire confidence.

confident.def: feeling sure about your own ability to do things and be successful
confident.ex: She was in a relaxed, confident mood.

configuration.def: an arrangement of the parts of something or a group of things; the form or shape that this arrangement produces
configuration.ex: The design is based on four configurations of squares.

confine.def: to keep somebody/something inside the limits of a particular activity, subject, area, etc.
confine.ex: The work will not be confined to the Glasgow area.

confirm.def: to state or show that something is definitely true or correct, especially by providing evidence
confirm.ex: His guilty expression confirmed my suspicions.

confirmation.def: a statement, letter, etc. that shows that something is true, correct or definite
confirmation.ex: I'm still waiting for confirmation of the test results.

conflict.def: a situation in which people, groups or countries disagree strongly or are involved in a serious argument
conflict.ex: The violence was the result of political conflicts.

conflict.def: if two ideas, beliefs, stories, etc. conflict, it is not possible for them to exist together or for them both to be true
conflict.ex: Reports conflicted on how much of the aid was reaching the famine victims.

confront.def: to appear and need to be dealt with by somebody
confront.ex: What is to be done about the economic problems confronting the country?

confrontation.def: a situation in which there is anger between people or groups who disagree because they have different opinions
confrontation.ex: She wanted to avoid another confrontation with her father.

confuse.def: to make somebody unable to think clearly or understand something
confuse.ex: These two sets of statistics are guaranteed to confuse the public.

confused.def: unable to think clearly or to understand what is happening or what somebody is saying

confusing.ex: The instructions on the box are very confusing.

confusion.def: a state of not being certain about what is happening, what you should do, what something means, etc.
confusion.ex: The announcement caused a lot of confusion.

congratulate.def: to tell somebody that you are pleased about their success or achievements
congratulate.ex: I congratulated them all on their results.

congregation.def: a group of people who are gathered together in a church for a religious service, not including the priest and choir
congregation.ex: The congregation stood to sing the hymn.

congressional.def: related to or belonging to a congress or the Congress in the US

connect.def: to join together two or more things; to be joined together
connect.ex: The towns are connected by train and bus services.

connected.def: (of two or more things or people) having a link between them
connected.ex: We do not believe that the incidents are connected.

connection.def: something that connects two facts, ideas, etc.
connection.ex: Scientists have established a connection between cholesterol levels and heart disease.

conquer.def: to take control of a country or city and its people by force

conscience.def: the part of your mind that tells you whether your actions are right or wrong
conscience.ex: to have a clear/guilty conscience (= to feel that you have done right/wrong)

conscious.ex: She's very conscious of the problems involved.

consciousness.def: the state of being able to use your senses and mental powers to understand what is happening
consciousness.ex: I can't remember any more—I must have lost consciousness.

consecutive.def: following one after another in a continuous series
consecutive.ex: She was absent for nine consecutive days.

consensus.def: an opinion that all members of a group agree with
consensus.ex: She is skilled at achieving consensus on sensitive issues.

consent.def: permission to do something, especially given by somebody in authority
consent.ex: The written consent of a parent is required.

consent.def: to agree to something or give your permission for something
consent.ex: When she told them what she intended, they readily consented.

consequence.def: a result of something that has happened, especially an unpleasant result

consequently.ex: This poses a threat to agriculture and the food chain, and consequently to human health.

conservation.def: the protection of the natural environment
conservation.ex: to be interested in wildlife conservation

conservative.def: opposed to great or sudden social change; showing that you prefer traditional styles and values
conservative.ex: the conservative views of his parents

conserve.def: to protect something and prevent it from being changed or destroyed
conserve.ex: new laws to conserve wildlife in the area

consider.def: to think about something carefully, especially in order to make a decision
consider.ex: I'd like some time to consider.

considerable.def: great in amount, size, importance, etc.
considerable.ex: The project wasted a considerable amount of time and money.

considerably.ex: The need for sleep varies considerably from person to person.

consideration.def: the act of thinking carefully about something
consideration.ex: An employer is legally bound to give due consideration to the request.

consistency.def: the quality of always behaving in the same way or of having the same opinions, standard, etc.; the quality of being consistent
consistency.ex: She has played with great consistency all season.

consistent.def: always behaving in the same way, or having the same opinions, standards, etc.
consistent.ex: a consistent approach to the problem

consistently.ex: Her work has been of a consistently high standard.

consolidate.def: to make a position of power or success stronger so that it is more likely to continue
consolidate.ex: With this new movie he has consolidated his position as the country's leading director.

conspiracy.def: a secret plan by a group of people to do something harmful or illegal
conspiracy.ex: conspiracy (to do something), a conspiracy to overthrow the government

constant.def: happening all the time or repeatedly

constituency.def: a district that elects its own representative to parliament
constituency.ex: Unemployment is high in her constituency.

constitute.def: to be considered to be something
constitute.ex: Does such an activity constitute a criminal offence?

constitution.def: the system of laws and basic principles that a state, a country or an organization is governed by
constitution.ex: your right to vote under the constitution

constraint.def: a thing that limits something, or limits your freedom to do something

construct.def: to build or make something such as a road, building or machine

construction.def: the process or method of building or making something, especially roads, buildings, bridges, etc.
construction.ex: Construction began this year and will take approximately 18 months.

consult.def: to go to somebody for information or advice
consult.ex: If the pain continues, consult your doctor.

consultant.def: a person who knows a lot about a particular subject and is employed to give advice about it to other people

consultation.def: the act of discussing something with somebody or with a group of people before making a decision about it

consume.def: to use something, especially fuel, energy or time
consume.ex: The electricity industry consumes large amounts of fossil fuels.

consumer.def: a person who buys goods or uses services

consumption.def: the act of using energy, food or materials; the amount used
consumption.ex: the production of fuel for domestic consumption (= to be used in the country where it is produced)

contact.def: the act of communicating with somebody, especially regularly
contact.ex: I don't have much contact with my uncle.

contact.def: to communicate with somebody, for example by phone, letter or email
contact.ex: I've been trying to contact you all day.

contain.def: if something contains something else, it has that thing inside it or as part of it
contain.ex: This drink doesn't contain any alcohol.

container.def: a box, bottle, etc. in which something can be stored or transported

contemplate.def: to think about whether you should do something, or how you should do something
contemplate.ex: You're too young to be contemplating retirement.

contemporary.ex: We have no contemporary account of the battle (= written near the time that it happened).

contempt.def: the feeling that somebody/something is without value and deserves no respect at all
contempt.ex: She looked at him with contempt.

contend.def: to say that something is true, especially in an argument
contend.ex: I would contend that the minister's thinking is flawed on this point.

contender.def: a person or team with a chance of winning a competition
contender.ex: contender (for something), a contender for a gold medal in the Olympics

content.def: happy and satisfied with what you have
content.ex: He seemed more content, less bitter.

content.def: the things that are contained in something
content.ex: He tipped the contents of the bag onto the table.

contention.ex: One area of contention is the availability of nursery care.

contest.def: a competition in which people try to win something
to take part in a competition, election, etc. and try to win it

context.def: the situation in which something happens and that helps you to understand it
context.ex: Such databases are being used in a wide range of contexts.

continent.def: one of the large land masses of the earth such as Europe, Asia or Africa

continually.def: in a way that is repeated many times so that it is annoying

continue.def: to keep existing or happening without stopping
continue.ex: If the current trend continues, that number will increase 165 per cent by 2050.

continuous.def: happening or existing for a period of time without being interrupted
continuous.ex: Recovery after the accident will be a continuous process that may take several months.

contract.ex: All employees have a written contract of employment.

contract.def: to become less or smaller; to make something become less or smaller

contractor.def: a person or company that has a contract to do work or provide goods or services for another company

contradiction.def: a lack of agreement between facts, opinions, actions, etc.
contradiction.ex: There is a contradiction between the two sets of figures.

contrary.def: the opposite fact, event or situation
contrary.ex: In the end the contrary was proved true: he was innocent and she was guilty.

contrast.def: a difference between two or more people or things that you can see clearly when they are compared or put close together; the fact of comparing two or more things in order to show the differences between them
contrast.ex: The two cities make an interesting contrast.

contrast.def: to compare two things in order to show the differences between them
contrast.ex: The poem contrasts youth and age.

contribute.def: to give something, especially money or goods, to help achieve or provide something

contribution.def: a gift or payment that is made to a person or an organization in order to help pay for something
contribution.ex: All contributions will be gratefully received.

contributor.def: a person who writes articles for a magazine, book or website, or who talks on a radio or television programme or at a meeting
contributor.ex: a regular contributor to this magazine

control.def: the power to make decisions about how a country, an area, an organization, etc. is run
control.ex: The party expects to gain control of the council in the next election.

control.def: to have power over a person, company, country, etc. so that you are able to decide what they must do or how it is run
control.ex: By the age of 21 he controlled the company.

controversial.def: causing a lot of angry public discussion and disagreement

controversy.def: public discussion and argument about something that many people strongly disagree about, think is bad, or are shocked by

convenience.def: the quality of being useful, easy or suitable for somebody
convenience.ex: We have provided seats for the convenience of our customers.

convenient.def: useful, easy or quick to do; not causing problems
convenient.ex: Fruit is a convenient source of vitamins and energy.

convention.def: the way in which something is done that most people in a society expect and consider to be polite or the right way to do it
convention.ex: the rigid social conventions of Victorian Britain

conventional.def: tending to follow what is done or considered acceptable by society in general; normal and ordinary, and perhaps not very interesting

conversation.def: an informal talk involving a small group of people or only two; the activity of talking in this way

conversion.def: the act or process of changing something from one form, use or system to another
conversion.ex: Their main business is the conversion of farm buildings into family homes.

convert.def: to change or make something change from one form, purpose, system, etc. to another
convert.ex: They took just nine months to convert the building.

convey.def: to make ideas, feelings, etc. known to somebody
convey.ex: Colours like red convey a sense of energy and strength.

convict.def: to decide and state officially in court that somebody is guilty of a crime

conviction.def: the act of finding somebody guilty of a crime in court; the fact of having been found guilty
conviction.ex: He plans to appeal against his conviction.

convince.def: to make somebody/yourself believe that something is true
convince.ex: Are the prime minister's assurances enough to convince the public?

convinced.ex: Sam nodded but he didn't look convinced.

convincing.def: that makes somebody believe that something is true

cook.def: a person who cooks food or whose job is cooking
cook.ex: John is a very good cook (= he cooks well).

cook.def: to prepare food by heating it, for example by boiling, baking or frying it
cook.ex: Where did you learn to cook?

cooker.def: a large piece of equipment for cooking food, containing an oven and gas or electric rings on top

cooking.def: the process of preparing food by heating it
cooking.ex: My husband does all the cooking.

cool.def: fairly cold; not hot or warm
to become cool or cooler; to make somebody/something become cool or cooler

cooperate.def: to work together with somebody else in order to achieve something
cooperate.ex: The two groups agreed to cooperate with each other.

cooperative.def: involving doing something together or working together with others towards a shared aim
cooperative.ex: Cooperative activity is essential to effective community work.

coordinate.def: to organize the different parts of an activity and the people involved in it so that it works well
coordinate.ex: They appointed a new manager to coordinate the work of the team.

coordination.def: the act of making parts of something, groups of people, etc. work together in an efficient and organized way
coordination.ex: The aim was to improve the coordination of services.

coordinator.def: a person who organizes the different parts of an activity and the people involved in it so that it works well
coordinator.ex: The campaign needs an effective coordinator.

cope.def: to deal successfully with something difficult
cope.ex: I got to the stage where I wasn't coping any more.

copper.def: a chemical element. Copper is a soft red-brown metal used for making electric wires, pipes and coins.

copy.def: a thing that is made to be the same as something else, especially a document or a work of art
copy.ex: The thieves replaced the original painting with a copy.

copy.def: to make something that is exactly like something else
copy.ex: He taught himself by copying paintings in the Louvre.

copyright.def: if a person or an organization holds the copyright on a piece of writing, music, etc., they are the only people who have the legal right to publish, broadcast, perform it, etc., and other people must ask their permission to use it or any part of it
copyright.ex: Copyright expires seventy years after the death of the author.

core.ex: core subjects (= subjects that all the students have to study) such as English and mathematics

core.def: the most important or central part of something
core.ex: This report goes to the core of the argument.

corner.def: a part of something where two or more sides, lines or edges join
corner.ex: the four corners of a square

corporate.def: connected with a large business company

correct.def: accurate or true, without any mistakes
correct.ex: Do you have the correct time?

correct.def: to make something right or accurate, for example by changing it or removing mistakes
correct.ex: correct something, to correct an error

correction.def: a change that makes something more accurate than it was before
correction.ex: I've made a few small corrections to your report.

correctly.def: in a way that is accurate or true, without any mistakes

correlate.def: if two or more facts, figures, etc. correlate or if a fact, figure, etc. correlates with another, the facts are closely connected and affect or depend on each other
correlate.ex: The figures do not seem to correlate.

correlation.def: a connection between two things in which one thing changes as the other does
correlation.ex: There is a direct correlation between exposure to sun and skin cancer.

correspond.def: to be the same as or match something
correspond.ex: Your account and hers do not correspond.

correspondence.def: the letters, emails, etc. a person sends and receives

correspondent.def: a person who reports news from a particular country or on a particular subject for a newspaper or a television or radio station

corresponding.def: matching or connected with something that you have just mentioned
corresponding.ex: A change in the money supply brings a corresponding change in expenditure.

corridor.def: a long narrow passage in a building, with doors that open into rooms on either side
corridor.ex: His room is along the corridor.

corrupt.def: willing to use their power to do dishonest or illegal things in return for money or to get an advantage
corrupt.ex: It was seen as the only way to overthrow a corrupt regime.

corruption.def: dishonest or illegal behaviour, especially of people in authority

cost.def: the amount of money that you need in order to buy, make or do something
cost.ex: cost of something, the high/low cost of housing

cost.def: if something costs a particular amount of money, you need to pay that amount in order to buy, make or do it

costly.def: costing a lot of money, especially more than you want to pay
costly.ex: Buying new furniture may prove too costly.

costume.def: the clothes worn by people from a particular place or during a particular historical period

cottage.def: a small house, especially in the country

cotton.def: a plant grown in warm countries for the soft white hairs around its seeds that are used to make cloth and thread
cotton.ex: From the age of six she worked in the fields, picking cotton.

could.def: used as the past tense of ‘can’
could.ex: She said that she couldn't come.

council.def: a group of people who are elected to govern an area such as a city or county

councillor.def: a member of a city or county council

counselling.ex: The couple decided to go for relationship counselling.

counsellor.def: a person who has been trained to advise people with problems, especially personal problems
counsellor.ex: I went to see a debt counsellor and she agreed to come to the bank with me.

count.def: an act of counting to find the total number of somebody/something
count.ex: If the election result is close, there will be a second count.

count.def: to say numbers in the correct order

counter.def: a long flat surface over which goods are sold or business is done in a shop, bank, etc.
counter.ex: I asked the woman behind the counter if they had any postcards.

counter.def: to reply to somebody by trying to prove that what they said is not true
counter.ex: Such arguments are not easily countered.

counterpart.def: a person or thing that has the same position or function as somebody/something else in a different place or situation
counterpart.ex: The Foreign Minister held talks with his Chinese counterpart.

countless.def: very many; too many to be counted or mentioned

country.def: an area of land that has or used to have its own government and laws

countryside.def: land outside towns and cities, with fields, woods, etc.
countryside.ex: The surrounding countryside is windswept and rocky.

county.def: one of a number of areas that some countries are divided into, each with its own local government
county.ex: rural counties west of the Mississippi

coup.def: a sudden change of government that is illegal and often violent
coup.ex: He seized power in a military coup in 2008.

couple.ex: I saw a couple of men get out.

courage.def: the ability to do something dangerous, or to face pain or opposition, without showing fear
courage.ex: He showed great courage and determination.

course.def: a series of lessons or lectures on a particular subject

court.def: the place where legal trials take place and where crimes, etc. are judged

courtesy.def: polite behaviour that shows respect for other people
courtesy.ex: I was treated with the utmost courtesy by the staff.

cousin.def: a child of your aunt or uncle

cover.def: a thing that is put over or on another thing, usually to protect it or to decorate it
cover.ex: Brighten up your room with some colourful cushion covers.

cover.def: to place something over or in front of something in order to hide, protect or decorate it
cover.ex: Cover the chicken loosely with foil.

coverage.def: the reporting of news and sport in the media

covered.def: having a layer or amount of something on it
covered.ex: His face was covered in blood.

cow.def: a large animal kept on farms to produce milk or beef

crack.def: a line on the surface of something where it has broken but not split into separate parts
crack.ex: This cup has a crack in it.

crack.def: to break without dividing into separate parts; to break something in this way
crack.ex: The ice cracked as I stepped onto it.

craft.def: an activity involving a special skill at making things with your hands
to make something using special skills, especially with your hands
craft.ex: All the furniture is crafted from natural materials.

crash.def: an accident in which a vehicle hits something, for example another vehicle, usually causing damage and often injuring or killing the passengers
if a vehicle crashes or the driver crashes it, it hits an object or another vehicle, causing damage
crash.ex: I was terrified that the plane would crash.

crawl.def: to move forward on your hands and knees or with your body close to the ground
crawl.ex: Our baby is just starting to crawl.

crazy.ex: Are you crazy? We could get killed doing that.

cream.def: between yellow and white in colour
the thick white or pale yellow fatty liquid that rises to the top of milk, used in cooking or as a type of sauce to put on fruit, etc.
cream.ex: We had strawberries and cream for dessert.

create.def: to make something happen or exist
create.ex: Scientists disagree about how the universe was created.

creation.def: the act or process of making something that is new, or of causing something to exist that did not exist before
creation.ex: Job creation needs to be the top priority.

creative.def: involving the use of skill and the imagination to produce something new or a work of art
creative.ex: a course on creative writing (= writing stories, plays and poems)

creativity.def: the use of skill and imagination to produce something new or to produce art
creativity.ex: Creativity and originality are more important than technical skill.

creator.def: a person who has made or invented a particular thing
creator.ex: Walt Disney, the creator of Mickey Mouse

creature.def: a living thing, real or imaginary, that can move around, such as an animal

credibility.def: the quality that somebody/something has that makes people believe or trust them

credible.def: that can be believed or trusted

credit.def: an arrangement that you make, with a shop for example, to pay later for something you buy
to add an amount of money to somebody’s bank account
credit.ex: They credited my account two days later.

creep.def: to move slowly, quietly and carefully, because you do not want to be seen or heard
creep.ex: I crept up the stairs, trying not to wake my parents.

crew.def: all the people working on a ship, plane, etc.

crime.def: activities that involve breaking the law
crime.ex: This month's figures show an increase in violent crime.

criminal.def: a person who commits a crime

crisis.def: a time of great danger, difficulty or doubt when problems must be solved or important decisions must be made

criterion.def: a standard or principle by which something is judged, or with the help of which a decision is made
criterion.ex: The main criterion is value for money.

critic.def: a person who expresses opinions about the good and bad qualities of books, music, etc.

critical.def: saying what you think is bad about somebody/something
critical.ex: You should just ignore any critical comments.

critically.def: in a way that says what you think is bad about somebody/something
critically.ex: She spoke critically of her father.

criticism.def: the act of expressing disapproval of somebody/something and opinions about their faults or bad qualities; a statement showing this disapproval

criticize.def: to say that you think somebody/something is bad; to say what you do not like or think is wrong about somebody/something
criticize.ex: All you ever do is criticize!

critique.def: a piece of written criticism of a set of ideas, a work of art, etc.
critique.ex: She wrote a feminist critique of Freud's theories.

crop.def: a plant that is grown in large quantities, especially as food
crop.ex: Sugar is an important crop on the island.

cross.def: a mark or an object formed by two lines crossing each other (X or +); the mark (X) is often used on paper to show something
cross.ex: I've put a cross on the map to show where the hotel is.

cross.def: to go across; to pass or stretch from one side to the other

crowd.def: a large number of people gathered together in a public place, for example in the streets or at a sports game
crowd.ex: He pushed his way through the crowd.

crowded.def: having a lot of people or too many people
crowded.ex: We made our way through the crowded streets.

crown.def: an object in the shape of a circle, usually made of gold and precious stones, that a king or queen wears on his or her head on official occasions
crown.ex: The crown was placed upon the new monarch's head.

crucial.def: extremely important, because it will affect other things
crucial.ex: The next few weeks are going to be crucial.

crude.def: in its natural state, before it has been processed or refined

cruel.def: having a desire to cause physical or mental pain and make somebody suffer
cruel.ex: He was known to be a cruel dictator.

cruise.def: a journey by sea, visiting different places, especially as a holiday
cruise.ex: I'd love to go on a round-the-world cruise.

cruise.def: to travel in a ship or boat visiting different places, especially as a holiday

crush.def: to press something so hard that it is damaged or injured, or loses its shape
crush.ex: The car was completely crushed under the truck.

cry.def: a loud sound without words that expresses a strong feeling
cry.ex: cry of something, to give a cry of anguish/despair/pain/joy/alarm, etc.

cry.def: to produce tears from your eyes because you are unhappy or hurt

crystal.def: a small piece of a substance with many even sides, that is formed naturally when the substance becomes solid

cue.def: an action or event that is a signal for somebody to do something
cue.ex: Jon's arrival was a cue for more champagne.

cult.def: very popular with a particular group of people
a way of life, an attitude, an idea, etc. that has become very popular

cultivate.def: to prepare and use land for growing plants or crops
cultivate.ex: The land around here has never been cultivated.

cultural.def: connected with the culture of a particular society or group, its customs, beliefs, etc.
cultural.ex: Teachers need to be aware of cultural differences.

culture.def: the customs and beliefs, art, way of life and social organization of a particular country or group

cup.def: a small container that is like a bowl in shape, usually with a handle, used for drinking tea, coffee, etc.
cup.ex: He filled the cup with water.

cupboard.def: a piece of furniture with doors and shelves used for storing dishes, food, clothes, etc.

cure.def: a medicine or medical treatment that cures an illness
cure.ex: cure for something, the search for a cure for cancer

cure.def: to make a person or an animal healthy again after an illness
cure.ex: Will you be able to cure him, Doctor?

curiosity.def: a strong desire to know about something

curious.def: having a strong desire to know about something
curious.ex: He is such a curious boy, always asking questions.

curly.def: having a lot of curls or a curved shape

currency.def: the system of money that a country uses

current.def: happening now; of the present time
current.ex: The necklace would be worth over $5 000 at current prices.

current.def: the movement of water in the sea or a river; the movement of air in a particular direction
current.ex: It's easier to go with the current.

curriculum.def: the subjects that are included in a course of study or taught in a school, college, etc.
curriculum.ex: The school curriculum should be as broad as possible.

curtain.def: a piece of cloth that is hung to cover a window
curtain.ex: to draw/pull/close the curtains (= to pull them across the window so they cover it)

curve.def: a line or surface that bends gradually; a smooth bend
curve.ex: He admired the delicate curve of her ear.

curve.def: to move or make something move in the shape of a curve; to be in the shape of a curve

custody.def: the legal right or duty to take care of or keep somebody/something; the act of taking care of something/somebody
custody.ex: Who will have custody of the children?

custom.def: an accepted way of behaving or of doing things in a society or a community

customer.def: a person or an organization that buys goods or services from a shop or business
customer.ex: marketing strategies to target potential customers

cut.def: a wound caused by something sharp
cut.ex: cuts and bruises on the face

cut.def: to make an opening or a wound in something, especially with a sharp tool such as a knife or scissors
cut.ex: She cut her finger on a piece of glass.

cutting.def: an article or a story that you cut from a newspaper or magazine and keep

cycle.ex: He was riding his cycle home when he was hit by a lorry.

cycle.def: to ride a bicycle; to travel by bicycle
cycle.ex: measures to encourage more people to cycle

cynical.def: believing that people only do things to help themselves rather than for good or honest reasons

daily.def: happening, done or produced every day
daily.ex: Many people still read a daily newspaper.
The machines are inspected twice daily.

dairy.def: milk, cheese and other milk products
dairy.ex: The doctor told me to eat less red meat and dairy.

dam.def: a barrier that is built across a river in order to stop the water from flowing, used especially to make a reservoir (= a lake for storing water) or to produce electricity

damage.def: physical harm caused to something which makes it less attractive, useful or valuable
to have a bad or harmful effect on something/somebody
damage.ex: The church was badly damaged by the 1997 earthquake.

damaging.def: causing damage; having a bad effect on somebody/something

dance.def: a series of movements and steps that are usually performed to music; a particular example of these movements and steps
dance.ex: Do you know any other Latin American dances?

dance.def: to move your body to the sound and rhythm of music

dancer.def: a person who dances or whose job is dancing

dancing.ex: There was music and dancing till two in the morning.

danger.def: the possibility of something happening that will injure, harm or kill somebody, or damage or destroy something

dangerous.def: likely to injure or harm somebody, or to damage or destroy something

dare.def: to be brave enough to do something
dare.ex: She said it as loudly as she dared.

dark.def: with no or very little light, especially because it is night
the lack of light in a place, especially because it is night
dark.ex: Are the children afraid of the dark?

darkness.def: the state of being dark, without any light
darkness.ex: After a few minutes our eyes got used to the darkness.

data.def: facts or information, especially when examined and used to find out things or to make decisions
data.ex: We collected publicly available data over a 10-day period.

database.def: an organized set of data that is stored in a computer and can be looked at and used in various ways

date.def: a particular day of the month, sometimes in a particular year, given in numbers and words
to write or print the date on something
date.ex: Thank you for your letter dated 24th March.

daughter.ex: We have two sons and a daughter.

dawn.def: the time of day when light first appears

dead.ex: My mother's dead; she died in 2017.

deadline.def: a point in time by which something must be done
deadline.ex: I prefer to work to a deadline.

deadly.def: causing or likely to cause death

deal.def: an agreement, especially in business, on particular conditions for buying or doing something
to give cards to each player in a game of cards
deal.ex: Whose turn is it to deal?

dealer.def: a person whose business is buying and selling a particular product

dear.def: used at the beginning of a letter before the name or title of the person that you are writing to
used in expressions that show that you are surprised, upset, annoyed or worried
dear.ex: Oh dear! I think I've lost my purse!

death.def: the fact of somebody dying or being killed
death.ex: the anniversary of his wife’s death

debate.def: a formal discussion of an issue at a public meeting or in a parliament. In a debate two or more speakers express opposite views and then there is often a vote on the issue.
debate.ex: the first ever televised presidential debate

debate.def: to discuss something, especially formally, before making a decision or finding a solution
debate.ex: Politicians will be debating the bill later this week.

debris.def: pieces of wood, metal, building materials, etc. that are left after something has been destroyed
debris.ex: Emergency teams are still clearing the debris from the plane crash.

debt.def: a sum of money that somebody owes

debut.def: the first public appearance of a performer or sports player
debut.ex: He will make his debut for the first team this week.

decade.def: a period of ten years, especially a continuous period, such as 1910–1919 or 2000–2009
decade.ex: the early decades of the nineteenth century

december.def: the 12th and last month of the year

decent.def: of a good enough standard or quality

decide.def: to think carefully about the different possibilities that are available and choose one of them
decide.ex: It's up to you to decide.

decision.def: a choice or judgement that you make after thinking and talking about what is the best thing to do
decision.ex: I think I've made the right decision.

decision-making.def: the process of deciding about something important, especially in a group of people or in an organization

decisive.def: very important for the final result of a particular situation

deck.def: the top outside floor of a ship or boat
deck.ex: I was the only person on deck at that time of night.

declaration.def: an official or formal statement, especially about the plans of a government or an organization; the act of making such a statement

declare.def: to say something officially or publicly
declare.ex: The government has declared a state of emergency.

decline.def: a continuous decrease in the number, value, quality, etc. of something
