decline.ex: There has been a 5 per cent decline in student numbers.

decline.def: to become smaller, fewer, weaker, etc.
decline.ex: Support for the party continues to decline.

decorate.def: to make something look more attractive by putting things on it
decorate.ex: They decorated the room with flowers and balloons.

decoration.def: a thing that makes something look more attractive on special occasions

decrease.def: the process of reducing something; the amount that something is reduced by
decrease.ex: Sales for May show a decrease compared with the same month last year.

decrease.def: to become smaller in size, number, etc.; to make something smaller in size, number, etc.
decrease.ex: Donations have decreased significantly over the past few years.

dedicated.def: working hard at something because it is very important to you

dedication.def: the hard work and effort that somebody puts into an activity or a purpose because they think it is important

deed.def: a thing that somebody does that is usually very good or very bad
deed.ex: It's a stirring tale of heroic deeds.

deem.def: to have a particular opinion about somebody/something
deem.ex: The evening was deemed a great success.

deep.def: having a large distance from the top or surface to the bottom
a long way below the surface of something or a long way inside or into something

default.def: what happens or appears if you do not make any other choice or change, especially in a computer program

defeat.def: failure to win or to be successful
defeat.ex: The party faces defeat in the election.

defeat.def: to win against somebody in a war, competition, sports game, etc.
defeat.ex: He defeated the champion in three sets.

defect.def: a fault in something or in the way it has been made that means that it is not perfect

defence.def: the act of protecting somebody/something from attack, criticism, etc.
defence.ex: in defence of somebody/something, soldiers who died in defence of their country

defend.ex: The role of the military is to defend the country.

defender.def: a player who must stop the other team from scoring in games such as football (soccer), hockey, etc.
defender.ex: a passionate defender of human rights

defensive.ex: As a defensive measure he built a series of coastal forts and watchtowers.

deficiency.def: the state of not having, or not having enough of, something that is essential
deficiency.ex: Vitamin deficiency in the diet can cause illness.

deficit.def: the amount by which money spent or owed is greater than money earned in a particular period of time

define.def: to say or explain what the meaning of a word or phrase is
define.ex: The term ‘normal’ is difficult to define.

definite.def: sure or certain; unlikely to change
definite.ex: Can you give me a definite answer by tomorrow?

definitely.def: a way of emphasizing that something is true and that there is no doubt about it
definitely.ex: I definitely remember sending the letter.

definition.def: an explanation of the meaning of a word or phrase, especially in a dictionary
definition.ex: The dictionary provides clear, simple definitions.

defy.def: to refuse to obey or show respect for somebody in authority, a law, a rule, etc.
defy.ex: I wouldn't have dared to defy my teachers.

degree.ex: Water freezes at 32 degrees Fahrenheit (32°F) or zero/nought degrees Celsius (0°C).

delay.def: a period of time when somebody/something has to wait because of a problem that makes something slow or late
delay.ex: Commuters will face long delays on the roads today.

delay.def: to make somebody/something late or force them to do something more slowly

delegate.def: a person who is chosen or elected to represent the views of a group of people and vote and make decisions for them

delegation.def: a group of people who represent the views of an organization, a country, etc.
delegation.ex: the Dutch delegation to the United Nations

delete.def: to remove something that has been written or printed, or that has been stored on a computer
delete.ex: Your name has been deleted from the list.

deliberate.def: done on purpose rather than by accident

deliberately.def: done in a way that was planned, not by chance
deliberately.ex: She's been deliberately ignoring him all day.

delicious.def: having a very pleasant taste or smell
delicious.ex: Who cooked this? It's absolutely delicious.

delight.def: to give somebody a lot of pleasure and joy
delight.ex: This news will delight his fans all over the world.

deliver.def: to take goods, letters, etc. to the person or people they have been sent to
deliver.ex: I get my food delivered from the supermarket to save time.

delivery.def: the act of taking goods, letters, etc. to the people they have been sent to

demand.def: a very strong request for something; something that somebody needs
demand.ex: demand for something, a demand for higher pay

demand.def: to make a very strong request for something
demand.ex: They are demanding the release of all political prisoners.

democracy.def: a system of government in which the people of a country can vote to elect their representatives

democratic.def: controlled by representatives who are elected by the people of a country; connected with this system

demon.ex: The people believed the girl was possessed by demons.

demonstrate.def: to show something clearly by giving proof or evidence
demonstrate.ex: New research convincingly demonstrates that age-related memory loss is not inevitable.

demonstration.def: a public meeting or a march (= an organized walk by many people) at which people show that they are protesting against or supporting somebody/something
demonstration.ex: to take part in/go on a demonstration

denial.def: a statement that something is not true or does not exist; the action of denying something
denial.ex: denial (of something), the prisoner’s repeated denials of the charges against him

denounce.def: to strongly criticize somebody/something that you think is wrong, illegal, etc.
denounce.ex: She publicly denounced the government's handling of the crisis.

dense.def: containing a lot of people, things, plants, etc. with little space between them

density.def: the quality of being dense; the degree to which something is dense
density.ex: The population density in this city is very high.

dentist.def: a person whose job is to take care of people’s teeth

deny.def: to say that something is not true
deny.ex: deny something, to deny an allegation/a charge/an accusation

depart.def: to leave a place, especially to start a trip
depart.ex: Flights for Rome depart from Terminal 3.

department.def: a section of a large organization such as a government, business, university, etc.
department.ex: department of something, the Department of Health

departure.def: an act of leaving a place
departure.ex: His sudden departure threw the office into chaos.

depend.ex: Starting salary varies from £26 000 to £30 500, depending on experience.

dependence.def: the state of needing the help and support of somebody/something in order to survive or be successful
dependence.ex: Our relationship was based on mutual dependence.

dependent.def: needing somebody/something in order to survive or be successful
dependent.ex: a woman with several dependent children

depict.def: to show an image of somebody/something in a picture
depict.ex: depict somebody/something (as somebody/something), a painting depicting the Virgin and Child

deploy.def: to move soldiers or weapons into a position where they are ready for military action
deploy.ex: 2 000 troops were deployed in the area.

deployment.def: the act of moving soldiers or weapons into a position where they are ready for military action

deposit.def: a sum of money that is given as the first part of a larger payment
deposit.ex: They normally ask you to pay $100 (as a) deposit.

deposit.def: to put money into a bank account
deposit.ex: Millions were deposited in Swiss bank accounts.

depressed.ex: You mustn't let yourself get depressed.

depressing.def: making you feel very sad and without enthusiasm

depression.def: a medical condition in which a person feels very sad, anxious and without hope and often has physical symptoms such as being unable to sleep, etc.
depression.ex: She was diagnosed as having clinical depression.

depth.def: the distance from the top or surface to the bottom of something; a particular distance down from the surface of something
depth.ex: What's the depth of the water here?

deputy.def: a person who is the next most important person below a business manager, a head of a school, a political leader, etc. and who does that person’s job when they are away
deputy.ex: I'm acting as deputy till the manager returns.

descend.def: to come or go down from a higher to a lower level

descent.def: an action of coming or going down
descent.ex: The plane began its descent to Heathrow.

describe.def: to say what somebody/something is like
describe.ex: The next section describes our findings in detail.

description.def: a piece of writing or speech that says what somebody/something is like; the act of writing or saying in words what somebody/something is like
description.ex: Follow the link below for a more detailed description.

desert.def: a large area of land that has very little water and very few plants growing on it. Many deserts are covered by sand.
to leave somebody without help or support
desert.ex: She was deserted by her husband.

deserve.def: to do something or show qualities that mean a particular reaction, reward or punishment is appropriate
deserve.ex: You deserve a rest after all that hard work.

design.def: the general arrangement of the different parts of something that is made, such as a building, book, machine, etc.
design.ex: The basic design of the car is very similar to that of earlier models.

design.def: to decide how something will look, work, etc., by drawing plans, making computer models, etc.
design.ex: He designed and built his own house.

designate.def: to say officially that somebody/something has a particular character or name; to describe somebody/something in a particular way
designate.ex: This area has been designated (as) a National Park.

designer.def: a person whose job is to decide how things such as clothes, furniture, tools, etc. will look or work by making drawings, plans or patterns
designer.ex: one of the country's top fashion designers

desirable.def: that you would like to have or do; worth having or doing
desirable.ex: She chatted for a few minutes about the qualities she considered desirable in a secretary.

desire.def: a strong wish to have or do something
desire.ex: He now had enough money to satisfy all his desires.

desire.def: to want something; to wish for something
desire.ex: We all desire health and happiness.

desk.def: a piece of furniture like a table, often with drawers (= parts like boxes in it with handles on the front for pulling them open), that you sit at to read, write, work, etc.
desk.ex: I spend all day sitting at a desk.

desktop.def: a screen on a computer that shows the icons of programs and files that can be used

desperate.def: feeling or showing that you have little hope and are ready to do anything without worrying about danger to yourself or others

desperately.def: in a way that shows you have little hope and are ready to do anything without worrying about danger to yourself or others
desperately.ex: She looked desperately around for a weapon.

despite.def: used to show that something happened or is true although something else might have happened to prevent it
despite.ex: Her voice was shaking despite all her efforts to control it.

destination.def: a place to which somebody/something is going or being sent
destination.ex: popular tourist/holiday destinations like the Bahamas

destroy.def: to damage something so badly that it no longer exists, works, etc.
destroy.ex: The building was completely destroyed by fire.

destruction.def: the act of destroying something; the process of being destroyed

destructive.ex: The war demonstrated the destructive power of modern weapons.

detail.def: a small individual fact or item; a less important fact or item
detail.ex: an expedition planned down to the last detail

detail.def: to give a list of facts or all the available information about something
detail.ex: The brochure details all the hotels in the area and their facilities.

detailed.def: giving many details and a lot of information; paying great attention to details
detailed.ex: a detailed description of the events

detain.def: to keep somebody in an official place, such as a police station, a prison or a hospital, and prevent them from leaving
detain.ex: One man has been detained for questioning.

detect.def: to discover or notice something, especially something that is not easy to see, hear, etc.
detect.ex: The tests are designed to detect the disease early.

detection.def: the process of discovering or noticing something, especially something that is not easy to see, hear, etc.; the fact of being discovered or noticed

detective.def: a person, especially a police officer, whose job is to investigate crimes and catch criminals

detention.def: the state of being kept in a place, especially a prison, and prevented from leaving
detention.ex: They were sentenced to 12 months' detention in a young offender institution.

deteriorate.ex: Her health deteriorated rapidly, and she died shortly afterwards.

determination.def: the quality that makes you continue trying to do something even when this is difficult

determine.def: to discover the facts about something; to calculate something exactly
determine.ex: An inquiry was set up to determine the cause of the accident.

determined.def: having made a definite decision to do something and not letting anyone prevent you
determined.ex: The opposition to her plan made her more determined than ever.

devastate.def: to completely destroy a place or an area
devastate.ex: The bomb devastated much of the old part of the city.

develop.def: to gradually grow or become bigger, more advanced, stronger, etc.; to make something do this
develop.ex: From birth to age 5, a child's brain develops rapidly.

development.def: the steady growth of something so that it becomes more advanced, stronger, etc.
development.ex: a baby’s development in the womb

device.def: an object or a piece of equipment that has been designed to do a particular job

devise.def: to invent something new or a new way of doing something
devise.ex: A new system has been devised to control traffic in the city.

diagnose.def: to say exactly what an illness or the cause of a problem is
diagnose.ex: The test is used to diagnose a variety of diseases.

diagnosis.def: the act of discovering or identifying the exact cause of an illness or a problem

diagram.def: a simple drawing using lines to explain where something is, how something works, etc.
diagram.ex: The results are shown in diagram 2.

dialogue.def: a conversation in a book, play or film or in language teaching materials
dialogue.ex: Learners are asked to listen to three short dialogues.

diamond.def: a clear precious stone of pure carbon, the hardest substance known. Diamonds are used in jewellery and also in industry, especially for cutting glass.

diary.def: a book in which you can write down the experiences you have each day, your private thoughts, etc.
diary.ex: Do you keep a diary (= write one regularly)?

dictate.def: to tell somebody what to do, especially in an annoying way
dictate.ex: They are in no position to dictate terms (= tell other people what to do).

dictator.def: a political leader who has complete power over a country, especially one who has gained it using military force
dictator.ex: The country suffered at the hands of a series of military dictators.

dictionary.def: a book or electronic resource that gives a list of the words of a language in alphabetical order and explains what they mean, or gives a word for them in a foreign language

die.ex: My father died suddenly at the age of 48.

diet.def: the food and drink that you eat and drink regularly
diet.ex: to eat a healthy, balanced diet

difference.def: the way in which two people or things are not like each other; the way in which somebody/something has changed
difference.ex: Americans and Brits speak the same language, but there are big cultural differences.

different.def: not the same as somebody/something; not like somebody/something else
different.ex: American English is significantly different from British English.

differentiate.def: to recognize or show that two things are not the same
differentiate.ex: It's difficult to differentiate between the two varieties.

differently.def: in a different way from somebody/something
differently.ex: Boys and girls may behave differently.

difficult.def: not easy; needing effort or skill to do or to understand
difficult.ex: The competition judges were given a very difficult task.

difficulty.def: a problem; a thing or situation that causes problems

dig.def: to make a hole in the ground or to move soil from one place to another using your hands, a tool or a machine
dig.ex: I think I'll do some digging in the garden.

digital.def: using a system of receiving and sending information as a series of the numbers one and zero, showing that an electronic signal is there or is not there

dignity.def: a calm and serious manner that deserves respect
dignity.ex: She accepted the criticism with quiet dignity.

dilemma.def: a situation that makes problems, often one in which you have to make a very difficult choice between things of equal importance
dilemma.ex: I could see no way of resolving this moral dilemma.

dimension.def: a measurement in space, for example how high, wide or long something is
dimension.ex: We measured the dimensions of the kitchen.

diminish.def: to become smaller, weaker, etc.; to make something become smaller, weaker, etc.
diminish.ex: The world's resources are rapidly diminishing.

dinner.def: the main meal of the day, eaten either in the middle of the day or in the evening

dip.def: to put something quickly into a liquid and take it out again
dip.ex: He dipped the brush into the paint.

diplomat.def: a person whose job is to represent his or her country in a foreign country, for example, in an embassy

diplomatic.def: connected with managing relations between countries (= diplomacy)

direct.def: going in the straightest line between two places without stopping or changing direction
without stopping or changing direction or vehicle
direct.ex: We flew direct to Hong Kong.

direct.def: to control or be in charge of somebody/something
direct.ex: He was asked to take command and direct operations.

direction.def: the general position a person or thing moves or points towards
direction.ex: They headed in the direction of the village.

directly.ex: The path leads directly to the river.

director.def: one of a group of senior managers who run a company

directory.def: a book or electronic resource containing lists of information, usually in alphabetical order, for example people’s phone numbers or the names and addresses of businesses in a particular area

dirt.def: any substance that makes something dirty, for example dust, soil or mud
dirt.ex: His clothes were covered in dirt.

disability.def: a physical or mental condition that makes it difficult for somebody to do some things that most other people can do

disabled.def: having a condition that makes it difficult for you to do some things that most other people can do

disadvantage.def: something that causes problems and tends to stop somebody/something from succeeding or making progress

disagree.def: if two people disagree or one person disagrees with another about something, they have a different opinion about it

disagreement.def: a situation where people have different opinions about something and often argue
disagreement.ex: Disagreement arose about exactly how to plan the show.

disappoint.def: to make somebody feel sad because something that they hope for or expect to happen does not happen or is not as good as they hoped
disappoint.ex: Her decision to cancel the concert is bound to disappoint her fans.

disappointed.def: upset because something you hoped for has not happened or been as good, successful, etc. as you expected
disappointed.ex: The singer has promised to refund any disappointed fans.

disappointing.def: not as good, successful, etc. as you had hoped; making you feel disappointed

disappointment.def: the feeling of being sad because something has not happened or been as good, successful, etc. as you expected or hoped
disappointment.ex: Book early for the show to avoid disappointment.

disaster.def: an unexpected event, such as a very bad accident, a flood or a fire, that kills a lot of people or causes a lot of damage

disc.ex: He wears an identity disc around his neck.

discard.def: to get rid of something that you no longer want or need
discard.ex: The room was littered with discarded newspapers.

discharge.def: to give somebody official permission to leave the police or the armed forces; to make somebody leave the police or the armed forces
discharge.ex: He was discharged from the army following his injury.

discipline.def: the practice of training people to obey rules and orders and punishing them if they do not; the controlled behaviour or situation that is the result of this training
discipline.ex: The school has a reputation for high standards of discipline.

disclose.def: to give somebody information about something, especially something that was previously secret
disclose.ex: The spokesman refused to disclose details of the takeover to the press.

disclosure.def: the act of making something known or public that was previously secret or private
disclosure.ex: the newspaper’s disclosure of defence secrets

discount.def: an amount of money that is taken off the usual cost of something
to take an amount of money off the usual cost of something; to sell something at a discount
discount.ex: Most of our stock has been discounted by up to 40 per cent.

discourage.def: to try to prevent something or to prevent somebody from doing something, especially by making it difficult to do or by showing that you do not approve of it
discourage.ex: discourage (doing) something, a campaign to discourage smoking among teenagers

discourse.def: a long and serious treatment or discussion of a subject in speech or writing
discourse.ex: discourse on something, a discourse on issues of gender and sexuality

discover.def: to be the first person to become aware that a particular place or thing exists
discover.ex: Cook is credited with discovering Hawaii.

discovery.def: an act or the process of finding somebody/something, or learning about something that was not known about before
discovery.ex: Researchers in this field have made some important new discoveries.

discretion.def: the freedom or power to decide what should be done in a particular situation
discretion.ex: I'll leave it up to you to use your discretion.

discrimination.def: the practice of treating somebody or a particular group in society less fairly than others
discrimination.ex: age/racial/gender/sex discrimination (= because of somebody’s age, race or sex)

discuss.def: to talk about something with somebody, especially in order to decide something
discuss.ex: Have you discussed the problem with anyone?

discussion.def: a conversation about somebody/something; the process of discussing somebody/something
discussion.ex: I discovered something interesting during a family discussion.

disease.def: an illness affecting humans, animals or plants, often caused by infection

dish.def: a flat shallow container for cooking food in or serving it from

dishonest.def: not honest; intending to trick people
dishonest.ex: Beware of dishonest traders in the tourist areas.

disk.ex: Red blood cells are roughly the shape of a disk.

dislike.def: a feeling of not liking somebody/something
dislike.ex: He did not try to hide his dislike of his boss.
Why do you dislike him so much?

dismiss.def: to decide that somebody/something is not important and not worth thinking or talking about
dismiss.ex: dismiss somebody/something, to dismiss a suggestion/a claim/an idea

dismissal.def: the act of dismissing somebody from their job; an example of this
dismissal.ex: He still hopes to win his claim against unfair dismissal.

disorder.def: a condition or illness that causes problems with the way part of the body or brain works

displace.def: to take the place of somebody/something
displace.ex: Gradually factory workers have been displaced by machines.

display.def: an arrangement of things in a public place to inform or entertain people or advertise something for sale
to put something in a place where people can see it easily; to show something to people
display.ex: The exhibition gives local artists an opportunity to display their work.

disposal.def: the act of getting rid of something
disposal.ex: The council is responsible for waste disposal and street cleaning.

dispose.def: to arrange things or people in a particular way or position
dispose.ex: The visitors disposed themselves in a circle round the statue.

dispute.def: an argument between two people, groups or countries; discussion about a subject on which people disagree
to question whether something is true or legally or officially acceptable

disrupt.def: to make it difficult for something to continue in the normal way
disrupt.ex: Demonstrators succeeded in disrupting the meeting.

disruption.def: a situation in which it is difficult for something to continue in the normal way; the act of stopping something from continuing in the normal way
disruption.ex: We aim to help you move house with minimum disruption to yourself.

dissolve.def: to mix with a liquid and become part of it

distance.def: the amount of space between two places or things

distant.def: far away in space or time

distinct.def: easily or clearly heard, seen, felt, etc.
distinct.ex: There was a distinct smell of gas.

distinction.def: a clear difference or contrast especially between people or things that are similar or related
distinction.ex: distinctions between traditional and modern societies

distinctive.def: having a quality or characteristic that makes something different and easily noticed

distinguish.def: to recognize the difference between two people or things
distinguish.ex: At what age are children able to distinguish between right and wrong?

distort.def: to change the shape, appearance or sound of something so that it is strange or not clear
distort.ex: a fairground mirror that distorts your shape

distract.def: to take somebody’s attention away from what they are trying to do
distract.ex: You're distracting me from my work.

distress.def: a feeling of great worry or unhappiness; great mental pain
distress.ex: The newspaper article caused the actor considerable distress.

distress.def: to make somebody feel very worried or unhappy
distress.ex: It was clear that the letter had deeply distressed her.

distribute.def: to give things to a large number of people; to share something between a number of people
distribute.ex: The leaflets have been widely distributed.

distribution.def: the way that something is spread or exists over a particular area or among a particular group of people
distribution.ex: They studied the geographical distribution of the disease.

district.def: an area of a country or town, especially one that has particular features
district.ex: the City of London’s financial district

disturb.def: to interrupt somebody when they are trying to work, sleep, etc.
disturb.ex: I'm sorry to disturb you, but can I talk to you for a moment?

disturbing.def: making you feel anxious and upset or shocked

dive.def: a jump into deep water with your head first and your arms in front of you
dive.ex: a spectacular high dive (= from high above the water)

dive.def: to jump into water with your head and arms going in first
dive.ex: We dived into the river to cool off.

diverse.def: very different from each other and of various kinds
diverse.ex: People from diverse cultures were invited to the event.

diversity.def: a range of many people or things that are very different from each other
diversity.ex: the biological diversity of the rainforests

divert.ex: The course of the stream has now been diverted.

divide.def: a difference between two groups of people or two things that separates them from each other
divide.ex: He offered advice on bridging cultural divides.

divide.def: to separate into parts; to make something separate into parts
divide.ex: The cells began to divide rapidly.

divine.def: coming from or connected with God or a god

division.def: the process or result of dividing into separate parts; the process or result of dividing something or sharing it out
division.ex: The organism begins as a single cell and grows by cell division.

divorce.def: the legal ending of a marriage
divorce.ex: The marriage ended in divorce in 1996.

divorce.def: to end your marriage to somebody legally

divorced.def: no longer married because your marriage has been legally ended

do.def: used to refer to actions that you do not mention by name or do not know about
do.ex: What are you doing this evening?

do.def: used before a full verb to form negative sentences and questions

doctor.def: a person who has been trained in medical science, whose job is to treat people who are ill or injured
doctor.ex: You'd better see a doctor about that cough.

doctrine.def: a belief or set of beliefs held and taught by a Church, a political party, etc.

document.def: an official paper, book or electronic file that gives information about something, or that can be used as evidence or proof of something
document.ex: Please read and sign the attached document.

document.def: to record something in the form of a written document, photograph, film, etc.
document.ex: Urban life in the nineteenth century is well documented.

documentary.def: a film or a radio or television programme giving facts about something
documentary.ex: There were some interesting interviews in the documentary.

documentation.def: the documents that are required for something, or that give evidence or proof of something
documentation.ex: I couldn't enter the country because I didn't have all the necessary documentation.

dog.def: an animal with four legs and a tail, often kept as a pet or trained for work, for example hunting or guarding buildings. There are many types of dog, some of which are wild.
dog.ex: I took the dog for a walk.

dollar.def: the unit of money in the US, Canada, Australia and several other countries
dollar.ex: You will be paid in American dollars.

domain.def: an area of knowledge or activity; especially one that somebody is responsible for

domestic.def: of or inside a particular country; not foreign or international

dominance.def: the fact of being more important, powerful or easy to notice than somebody/something else

dominant.def: more important, powerful or easy to notice than other things
dominant.ex: The firm has achieved a dominant position in the world market.

dominate.def: to control or have a lot of influence over somebody/something, especially in an unpleasant way
dominate.ex: She always says a lot in meetings, but she doesn't dominate.

donate.def: to give money, food, clothes, etc. to somebody/something, especially a charity
donate.ex: He donated thousands of pounds to charity.

donation.def: something that is given to a person or an organization such as a charity, in order to help them; the act of giving something in this way

donor.def: a person or an organization that makes a gift of money, clothes, food, etc. to a charity, etc.
donor.ex: international aid donors (= countries that give money, etc. to help other countries)

door.def: a piece of wood, glass, etc. that is opened and closed so that people can get in and out of a room, building, car, etc.; a similar thing in a cupboard

dose.def: an amount of a medicine or a drug that is taken once, or regularly over a period of time

dot.def: a small round mark, especially one that is printed
dot.ex: There are dots above the letters i and j.

double.def: twice as much or as many as usual
in twos or in two parts
double.ex: I thought I was seeing double (= seeing two of something).

double.def: twice as much or as many as
a number or amount that is twice as much or as many as another number or amount
double.ex: He gets paid double for doing the same job I do.

double.def: to become, or make something become, twice as much or as many
double.ex: Membership almost doubled in two years.

doubt.def: a feeling of being uncertain about something or not believing something
doubt.ex: a feeling of doubt and uncertainty

doubt.def: to feel uncertain about something; to feel that something is not true, will probably not happen, etc.
doubt.ex: There seems no reason to doubt her story.

down.def: to or at a lower place or position
down.ex: She jumped down off the chair.

down.def: from a high or higher point on something to a lower one
down.ex: The stone rolled down the hill.

download.def: data that is downloaded from another computer system
download.ex: A staggering 99.8 % of digital music downloads are to mobile handsets.

download.def: to get data from another computer, usually using the internet

downstairs.def: on a floor of a house or building lower than the one you are on, especially the one at ground level
down the stairs; on or to a floor of a house or building lower than the one you are on, especially the one at ground level
downstairs.ex: I couldn't sleep so I went downstairs and watched TV.

downtown.def: in, towards or typical of the centre of a city, especially its main business area
in or towards the centre of a city, especially its main business area
the centre of a city, especially its main business area
downtown.ex: a hotel in the heart of downtown

downwards.def: towards the ground; towards a lower place or position
downwards.ex: She was lying face downwards on the grass.

dozen.def: a group of twelve of the same thing

draft.def: a rough written version of something that is not yet in its final form
draft.ex: This is only the first draft of my speech.

draft.def: to write the first rough version of something such as a letter, speech, book or law
draft.ex: I'll draft a letter for you.

drag.def: to pull somebody/something along with effort and difficulty
drag.ex: The sack is too heavy to lift—you’ll have to drag it.

drain.def: to make something empty or dry by removing the liquid from it; to become empty or dry in this way

drama.def: a play for the theatre, television or radio

dramatic.def: sudden, very great and often surprising

dramatically.def: very suddenly and to a very great and often surprising degree

draw.def: to make pictures, or a picture of something, with a pencil, pen or chalk (but not paint)

drawing.def: a picture made using a pencil or pen rather than paint

dream.def: a series of images, events and feelings that happen in your mind while you are asleep
dream.ex: I had a really weird dream last night.

dream.def: to experience a series of images, events and feelings in your mind while you are asleep
dream.ex: Did I talk in my sleep? I must have been dreaming.

dress.def: a piece of clothing that is made in one piece and hangs down to cover the body as far as the legs, sometimes reaching to below the knees, or to the ankles

dressed.def: wearing clothes and not naked or wearing clothes for sleeping

drift.def: to move along smoothly and slowly in water or air

drink.def: a liquid for drinking; an amount of a liquid that you drink
to take liquid into your mouth and then down your throat into your stomach

drive.def: a journey in a car or other vehicle
to operate a vehicle so that it goes in a particular direction

driver.def: a person who drives a vehicle

driving.def: strong and powerful; having a strong influence in making something happen
driving.ex: Who was the driving force (= the person with the strongest influence) in the band?

driving.def: the way that somebody drives a vehicle; the act of driving
driving.ex: She was convicted of dangerous driving.

drop.def: a very small amount of liquid that forms a round shape
drop.ex: The first drops of rain fell.

drop.def: to allow something to fall by accident; to fall by accident
drop.ex: Be careful not to drop that plate.

drought.def: a long period of time when there is little or no rain
drought.ex: Farmers are facing ruin after two years of severe drought.

drown.def: to die because you have been underwater too long and you cannot breathe; to kill somebody by holding them underwater
drown.ex: Two children drowned after falling into the river.

drug.def: an illegal substance that some people smoke, inject, etc. for the physical and mental effects it has
drug.ex: He does not smoke or take drugs.

drum.def: a musical instrument made of a hollow round frame with plastic or skin stretched tightly across one or both ends. You play it by hitting it with sticks or with your hands.

drunk.def: having drunk so much alcohol that it is impossible to think or speak clearly
drunk.ex: They were clearly too drunk to drive.

dry.def: not wet or sticky; without water or moisture
to become dry; to make something dry
dry.ex: He did the laundry and hung it out to dry.

dual.ex: his dual role as composer and conductor

dub.def: to give somebody/something a particular name, often in a humorous or critical way
dub.ex: The media dubbed anorexia ‘the slimming disease’.

due.def: caused by somebody/something; because of somebody/something
due.ex: The team's success was largely due to her efforts.

dull.ex: Life in a small town could be deadly dull.

dumb.ex: That was a pretty dumb thing to do.

dump.def: to get rid of something you do not want, especially in a place that is not suitable
dump.ex: Too much toxic waste is being dumped at sea.

duo.def: two people who perform together or are often seen or thought of together
duo.ex: the comedy duo Laurel and Hardy

duration.def: the length of time that something lasts or continues
duration.ex: The school was used as a hospital for the duration of the war.

during.def: all through a period of time

dust.def: a fine powder that consists of very small pieces of sand, earth, etc.
dust.ex: A cloud of dust rose as the truck drove off.

duty.def: something that you feel you have to do because it is your moral or legal responsibility
duty.ex: It is my duty to report it to the police.

dvd.def: a disk on which large amounts of information, especially films, photographs and video, can be stored, for use on a DVD player or computer (the abbreviation for ‘digital versatile disc’ or ‘digital videodisc’)
dvd.ex: Let's just stay in and watch a DVD.

dynamic.def: having a lot of energy and a strong personality
the way in which people or things behave and react to each other in a particular situation

each.def: used to refer to every one of two or more people or things, when you are thinking about them separately

eager.def: very interested and excited by something that is going to happen or about something that you want to do; showing this

ear.def: either of the organs on the sides of the head that you hear with

early.def: near the beginning of a period of time, an event etc.
near the beginning of a period of time, an event, a piece of work, etc.
early.ex: We arrived early the next day.

earn.def: to get money for work that you do

earnings.def: the money that you earn for the work that you do

earth.def: the world; the planet that we live on

earthquake.def: a sudden, violent shaking of the earth’s surface

ease.ex: He passed the exam with ease.

ease.def: to become less unpleasant, painful or severe; to make something less unpleasant, etc.

easily.ex: The museum is easily accessible by car.

east.def: the direction that you look towards to see the sun rise; one of the four main points of the compass

eastern.def: located in the east or facing east

easy.def: not difficult; done or obtained without a lot of effort or problems
easy.ex: an easy way to make bread

eat.def: to put food in your mouth, bite it and swallow it
eat.ex: I was too nervous to eat.

echo.def: the reflecting of sound off a wall or inside a particular space so that a noise appears to be repeated; a sound that is reflected back in this way
echo.ex: There was an echo on the phone and I couldn't hear clearly.

echo.def: if a sound echoes, it is reflected off a wall, the side of a mountain, etc. so that you can hear it again
echo.ex: Her footsteps echoed in the empty room.

ecological.def: connected with the relation of plants and living creatures to each other and to their environment
ecological.ex: We risk upsetting the ecological balance of the area.

economic.def: connected with the trade, industry and development of wealth of a country, an area or a society
economic.ex: They discussed social, economic and political issues.

economics.def: the study of how a society organizes its money, trade and industry
economics.ex: He studied politics and economics at Yale.

economist.def: a person who studies or writes about economics

economy.def: the relationship between production, trade and the supply of money in a particular country or region

edge.def: the outside limit of an object, a surface or an area; the part furthest from the centre
edge.ex: I gripped the edge of my desk to steady myself.

edit.def: to prepare a piece of writing, a book, etc. to be published by correcting the mistakes, making improvements to it, etc.
edit.ex: This draft text will need to be edited.

edition.def: the form in which a book, newspaper, etc. is published

editor.def: a person who is in charge of a newspaper, magazine, etc., or part of one, and who decides what should be included
editor.ex: the editor of the Washington Post

editorial.def: connected with the task of preparing something such as a newspaper, a book or a television or radio programme, to be published or broadcast

educate.def: to teach somebody over a period of time at a school, university, etc.
educate.ex: She was educated in the US.

educated.def: having had the kind of education mentioned; having been to the school, college or university mentioned

education.def: a process of teaching, training and learning, especially in schools, colleges or universities, to improve knowledge and develop skills

educator.def: a person whose job is to teach or educate people

effect.def: a change that somebody/something causes in somebody/something else; a result

effective.def: producing the result that is wanted or intended; producing a successful result
effective.ex: Aspirin is a simple but highly effective treatment.

effectively.def: in a way that produces the intended result or a successful result
effectively.ex: The company must reduce costs to compete effectively.

effectiveness.def: the fact of producing the result that is wanted or intended; the fact of producing a successful result
effectiveness.ex: to check the effectiveness of the security system

efficiency.def: the quality of doing something well with no waste of time or money
efficiency.ex: improvements in efficiency at the factory

efficient.def: doing something in a good, careful and complete way with no waste of time, money or energy

efficiently.def: in a good and careful way, with no waste of time, money or energy

effort.def: an attempt to do something especially when it is difficult to do
effort.ex: The project was a team effort.

egg.def: a small oval object with a thin hard shell produced by a female bird and containing a young bird; a similar object (without a hard shell) produced by a female fish, insect, etc.
egg.ex: The female sits on the eggs until they hatch.

ego.def: your sense of your own value and importance
ego.ex: He has the biggest ego of anyone I've ever met.

either.def: used after negative phrases to state that a feeling or situation is similar to one already mentioned
either.ex: Pete can't go and I can't either.

either.def: one or the other of two; it does not matter which
either.ex: You can park on either side of the street.

elaborate.def: very complicated and detailed; carefully prepared and organized

elbow.def: the joint between the upper and lower parts of the arm where it bends in the middle
elbow.ex: She jabbed him with her elbow.

elderly.def: used as a polite word for ‘old’

elect.def: to choose somebody to do a particular job by voting for them
elect.ex: Voters will elect a new president on 30 March next year.

election.def: the process of choosing a person or a group of people for a position, especially a political position, by voting

electric.def: connected with electricity; using, produced by or producing electricity

electrical.def: connected with electricity; using or producing electricity
electrical.ex: an electrical fault in the engine

electricity.def: a form of energy from charged elementary particles, usually supplied as electric current through cables, wires, etc. for lighting, heating, driving machines, etc.

electronic.def: having or using many small parts, such as microchips, that control and direct a small electric current
electronic.ex: Airline passengers no longer have to turn off all electronic devices while on the plane.

electronics.def: the branch of science and technology that studies electric currents in electronic equipment

elegant.def: attractive and showing a good sense of style

element.def: a necessary or typical part of something; one of several parts that something contains
element.ex: There are three important elements to consider.

elementary.def: in or connected with the first stages of a course of study

elephant.def: a very large animal with thick grey skin, large ears, two curved outer teeth called tusks and a long nose called a trunk. There are two types of elephant, the African and the Asian.

elevate.def: to give somebody/something a higher position or rank, often more important than they deserve
elevate.ex: He elevated many of his friends to powerful positions within the government.

eleven.ex: There are only eleven of these rare animals left.

eligible.def: a person who is eligible for something or to do something, is able to have or do it because they have the right qualifications, are the right age, etc.
eligible.ex: Only those over 70 are eligible for the special payment.

eliminate.def: to remove or get rid of something
eliminate.ex: Credit cards eliminate the need to carry a lot of cash.

elite.def: a small group of people in a society, etc. who are powerful and have a lot of influence, because they are rich, intelligent, etc.
elite.ex: a member of the ruling/intellectual elite

else.def: in addition to something already mentioned

elsewhere.def: in, at or to another place or other places

email.def: a way of sending messages and data to other people by means of computers connected together in a network
email.ex: I will be on vacation for a week in November, with no access to email.

email.def: to send a message to somebody by email

embark.def: to get onto a ship or plane; to put somebody/something onto a ship or plane
embark.ex: We stood on the pier and watched as they embarked.

embarrassed.def: shy, uncomfortable or ashamed, especially in a social situation
embarrassed.ex: I've never felt so embarrassed in my life!

embarrassing.def: making you feel shy, uncomfortable or ashamed

embarrassment.def: shy, uncomfortable or guilty feelings; a feeling of being embarrassed
embarrassment.ex: I nearly died of embarrassment when he said that.

embassy.def: a group of officials led by an ambassador who represent their government in a foreign country

embed.def: to fix something in a substance or solid object
embed.ex: be embedded in something, an operation to remove glass that was embedded in his leg

embody.def: to express or represent an idea or a quality
embody.ex: embody something, a politician who embodied the hopes of black youth

embrace.def: to put your arms around somebody as a sign of love or friendship
embrace.ex: They embraced and promised to keep in touch.

emerge.def: to move out of or away from something and become possible to see
emerge.ex: The crabs emerge at low tide to look for food.

emergence.def: the fact of somebody/something moving out of or away from something and becoming possible to see
emergence.ex: the island’s emergence from the sea 3 000 years ago

emergency.def: a sudden serious and dangerous event or situation that needs immediate action to deal with it
emergency.ex: This is a medical emergency needing urgent treatment with antibiotics.

emission.def: the production or sending out of light, heat, gas, etc.
emission.ex: the emission of carbon dioxide into the atmosphere

emotion.def: a strong feeling such as love, fear or anger; the part of a person’s character that consists of feelings

emotional.def: connected with people’s feelings (= with the emotions)

emotionally.def: in a way that is connected with people's feelings (= with the emotions)

emphasis.def: special importance that is given to something
emphasis.ex: Since the elections there has been a greater emphasis on education.

emphasize.def: to give special importance to something
emphasize.ex: His speech emphasized the importance of attracting industry to the town.

empire.def: a group of countries or states that are controlled by one leader or government
empire.ex: These invasions almost led to the collapse of the Roman Empire.

empirical.def: based on experiments or experience rather than ideas or theories

employ.def: to give somebody a job to do for payment
employ.ex: How many people does the company employ?

employee.def: a person who is paid to work for somebody

employer.def: a person or company that pays people to work for them
employer.ex: They're very good employers (= they treat the people that work for them well).

employment.def: work, especially when it is done to earn money; the state of being employed

empower.def: to give somebody the power or authority to do something
empower.ex: The courts were empowered to impose the death sentence for certain crimes.

empty.def: with no people or things inside
to remove everything that is in a container, etc.
empty.ex: She emptied the bins, washed the glasses and went to bed.

enable.def: to make it possible for somebody/something to do something
enable.ex: This new programme will enable older people to study at college.

enact.ex: (be) enacted (by somebody/something), legislation enacted by parliament

encompass.def: to include a large number or range of things
encompass.ex: The job encompasses a wide range of responsibilities.

encounter.def: a meeting, especially one that is sudden, unexpected or violent
to experience something, especially something unpleasant or difficult, while you are trying to do something else
encounter.ex: We encountered a number of difficulties in the first week.

encourage.def: to give somebody support, courage or hope
encourage.ex: We were greatly encouraged by the positive response of the public.

encouragement.def: the act of encouraging somebody to do something; something that encourages somebody

encouraging.def: that gives somebody support, courage or hope
encouraging.ex: This month's unemployment figures are not very encouraging.

end.def: the final part of a period of time, an event, an activity or a story
end.ex: It's the end of an era.

end.def: to finish; to reach a point and go no further; to make something finish

endeavour.def: an attempt to do something, especially something new or difficult
endeavour.ex: There have been great advances in the field of scientific endeavour.

ending.def: the last part of a story, film, etc.
ending.ex: His stories usually have a happy ending.

endless.def: very large in size or amount and seeming to have no end

endorse.def: to say publicly that you support a person, statement or course of action

endorsement.def: a public statement or action showing that you support somebody/something
endorsement.ex: The election victory is a clear endorsement of their policies.

endure.def: to experience and deal with something that is painful or unpleasant without giving up
endure.ex: They had to endure a long wait before the case came to trial.

enemy.def: a person who hates somebody or who acts or speaks against somebody/something
enemy.ex: She didn't have an enemy in the world.

energy.def: the strength, effort and enthusiasm required for physical or mental activity, work, etc.
energy.ex: It's a waste of time and energy.

enforce.def: to make sure that people obey a particular law or rule
enforce.ex: It's the job of the police to enforce the law.

enforcement.def: the act of making people obey a particular law or rule

engage.def: to succeed in attracting and keeping somebody’s attention and interest
engage.ex: It is a movie that engages both the mind and the eye.

engagement.def: an agreement to marry somebody; the period during which two people are engaged
engagement.ex: Their engagement was announced in the local paper.

engaging.def: interesting or pleasant in a way that attracts your attention

engine.def: the part of a vehicle that produces power to make the vehicle move

engineer.def: a person whose job involves designing and building engines, machines, roads, bridges, etc.
engineer.ex: We worked with a team of scientists and engineers.

engineering.def: the activity of applying scientific knowledge to the design, building and control of machines, roads, bridges, electrical equipment, etc.
engineering.ex: The bridge is a triumph of modern engineering.

enhance.def: to increase or further improve the good quality, value or status of somebody/something
enhance.ex: This is an opportunity to enhance the reputation of the company.

enjoy.ex: We thoroughly enjoyed our time in New York.

enough.ex: I hadn't trained enough for the game.

enough.def: used before plural or uncountable nouns to mean ‘as many or as much as somebody needs or wants’
as many or as much as somebody needs or wants

enquire.def: to ask somebody for some information
enquire.ex: I called the station to enquire about train times.

enquiry.def: an official process to find out the cause of something or to find out information about something

enrich.def: to improve the quality of something, often by adding something to it
enrich.ex: The study of science has enriched all our lives.

enrol.def: to arrange for yourself or for somebody else to officially join a course, school, etc.
enrol.ex: You need to enrol before the end of August.

ensue.def: to happen after or as a result of another event

ensure.def: to make sure that something happens or is definite
ensure.ex: We are working to ensure the safety of people in the city.

enter.def: to come or go into something

enterprise.ex: He is in charge of an enterprise with a turnover of $26 billion.

entertain.def: to interest somebody or make somebody laugh in order to please them
entertain.ex: The aim of the series is both to entertain and inform.

entertaining.def: that you enjoy watching, listening to, doing or experiencing

entertainment.def: films, music, etc. used to entertain people; an example of this
entertainment.ex: radio, television and other forms of entertainment

enthusiasm.def: a strong feeling of excitement and interest in something and a desire to become involved in it
enthusiasm.ex: Her voice was full of enthusiasm.

enthusiast.def: a person who is very interested in something and spends a lot of time doing it

enthusiastic.def: feeling or showing a lot of excitement and interest about somebody/something

entire.def: including everything, everyone or every part

entirely.ex: The experience was entirely new to me.

entitle.def: to give somebody the right to have or to do something
entitle.ex: You will be entitled to your pension when you reach 65.

entity.def: something that exists separately from other things and has its own identity
entity.ex: The unit has become part of a larger department and no longer exists as a separate entity.

entrance.def: a door, gate, passage, etc. used for entering a room, building or place
entrance.ex: the front/back/side entrance of the house

entrepreneur.def: a person who makes money by starting or running businesses, especially when this involves taking financial risks
entrepreneur.ex: A creative entrepreneur, he was continually dreaming up new projects.

entry.def: an act of going into or getting into a place
entry.ex: She made her entry to the sound of thunderous applause.

envelope.def: a flat paper container used for sending letters in, with a part that you stick down to close it
envelope.ex: Writing paper and envelopes are provided in your room.

environment.def: the natural world in which people, animals and plants live
environment.ex: The government should do more to protect the environment.

environmental.def: connected with the natural conditions in which people, animals and plants live; connected with the environment

epidemic.def: a large number of cases of a particular disease or medical condition happening at the same time in a particular community
epidemic.ex: the outbreak of a flu epidemic

episode.def: one part of a story that is broadcast on television or radio in several parts
episode.ex: The next episode has not yet been filmed.

equal.def: the same in size, quantity, value, etc. as something else
equal.ex: There is an equal number of boys and girls in the class.

equal.def: a person or thing of the same quality or with the same status, rights, etc. as another
equal.ex: She treats the people who work for her as her equals.

equal.def: to be the same in size, quantity, value, etc. as something else

equality.def: the fact of being equal in rights, status, advantages, etc.

equally.def: to the same degree; in the same or in a similar way
equally.ex: Diet and exercise are equally important.

equation.def: a statement showing that two amounts or values are equal, for example 2x + y = 54
equation.ex: the numbers on the right-hand side of the equation

equip.def: to provide yourself/somebody/something with the things that are needed for a particular purpose or activity
equip.ex: equip something, to be fully/poorly equipped

equipment.def: the things that are needed for a particular purpose or activity
equipment.ex: a useful piece of equipment for the kitchen

equivalent.def: equal in value, amount, meaning, importance, etc.
equivalent.ex: 250 grams or an equivalent amount in ounces

equivalent.def: a thing, amount, word, etc. that is equal in value, meaning or purpose to something else
equivalent.ex: Send €20 or the equivalent in your own currency.

era.def: a period of time, usually in history, that is different from other periods because of particular characteristics or events

error.def: a mistake, especially one that causes problems or affects the result of something

erupt.def: when a volcano erupts or burning rocks, smoke, etc. erupt or are erupted, the burning rocks, etc. are thrown out from the volcano
erupt.ex: The volcano could erupt at any time.

escalate.def: to become greater, worse, more serious, etc.; to make something greater, worse, more serious, etc.

escape.def: the act or a method of escaping from a place or an unpleasant or dangerous situation
escape.ex: I had a narrow escape (= I was lucky to have escaped).

escape.def: to get away from a place where you have been kept as a prisoner or not allowed to leave

especially.def: more with one person, thing, etc. than with others, or more in particular circumstances than in others
especially.ex: Teenagers are very fashion conscious, especially girls.

essay.def: a short piece of writing by a student as part of a course of study
essay.ex: I have to write an essay this weekend.

essence.def: the most important quality or feature of something, that makes it what it is
essence.ex: His paintings capture the essence of France.

essential.def: completely necessary; extremely important in a particular situation or for a particular activity

essentially.def: when you think about the true, important or basic nature of somebody/something
essentially.ex: There are three essentially different ways of tackling the problem.

establish.def: to start or create an organization, a system, etc. that is meant to last for a long time

establishment.def: an organization, a large institution or a hotel
establishment.ex: The visa is for foreign nationals wishing to study at a university, college or similar educational establishment.

estate.def: an area of land with a lot of houses or factories of the same type on it
estate.ex: She lives in a tower block on an estate in London.

estimate.def: a judgement that you make without having the exact details or figures about the size, amount, cost, etc. of something
to form an idea of the cost, size, value etc. of something, but without calculating it exactly
estimate.ex: estimate something, to estimate the cost/value/number of something

eternal.def: without an end; existing or continuing forever
eternal.ex: the promise of eternal life in heaven

ethic.def: moral principles that control or influence a person’s behaviour

ethical.def: connected with beliefs and principles about what is right and wrong

ethnic.def: connected with or belonging to a group of people that share a cultural tradition

euro.def: the unit of money of some countries of the European Union
euro.ex: I paid five euros for it.

evacuate.def: to move people from a place of danger to a safer place

evaluate.def: to form an opinion of the amount, value or quality of something after thinking about it carefully
evaluate.ex: The trial will evaluate the effectiveness of the different drugs.

evaluation.def: the act of forming an opinion of the amount, value or quality of something after thinking about it carefully
evaluation.ex: an evaluation of the healthcare system

even.def: that can be divided exactly by two
used to emphasize something unexpected or surprising
even.ex: She didn't even call to say she wasn't coming.

evening.def: the part of the day between the afternoon and the time you go to bed
evening.ex: Remember to call me this evening.

event.def: a thing that happens, especially something important

eventually.def: at the end of a period of time or a series of events
eventually.ex: Our flight eventually left five hours late.

ever.def: used in negative sentences and questions, or sentences with if to mean ‘at any time’

every.def: used with singular nouns to refer to all the members of a group of things or people
every.ex: She knows every student in the school.

everyday.def: used or happening every day or regularly; ordinary
everyday.ex: Change is a part of everyday life in business.

everywhere.def: in, to or at every place; all places

evidence.def: the facts, signs or objects that make you believe that something is true
evidence.ex: Researchers have found clear scientific evidence of a link between exposure to sun and skin cancer.

evident.ex: The orchestra played with evident enjoyment.

evil.def: enjoying harming others; morally bad and cruel
evil.ex: Police described the killer as ‘a desperate and evil man’.

evil.def: a force that causes bad things to happen; morally bad behaviour
evil.ex: the eternal struggle between good and evil

evoke.def: to bring a feeling, a memory or an image into your mind
evoke.ex: The music evoked memories of her youth.

evolution.def: the slow steady development of plants, animals, etc. during the history of the earth, as they adapt to changes in their environment
evolution.ex: the evolution of the human species

evolutionary.def: connected with evolution; connected with slow steady development and change

evolve.def: to develop gradually, especially from a simple to a more complicated form; to develop something in this way
evolve.ex: The idea evolved from a drawing I discovered in the attic.

exact.ex: The new palace is an exact replica of the original building.

exactly.def: used to emphasize that something is correct in every way or in every detail

exaggerate.def: to make something seem larger, better, worse or more important than it really is
exaggerate.ex: The hotel was really filthy and I'm not exaggerating.

exam.def: a formal written, spoken or practical test, especially at school or college, to see how much you know about a subject, or what you can do

examination.def: a formal written, spoken or practical test, especially at school or college, to see how much you know about a subject, or what you can do

examine.def: to consider or study an idea, a subject, etc. very carefully
examine.ex: This study sets out to examine in detail the possible effects of climate change.

example.def: something such as an object, a fact or a situation that shows, explains or supports what you say
example.ex: Let me give you an example.

exceed.def: to be greater than a particular number or amount

excellence.def: the quality of being extremely good

except.def: used before you mention something that makes a statement not completely true
except.ex: I didn't tell him anything except that I needed the money.

except.def: used before you mention the only thing or person about which a statement is not true
except.ex: We work every day except Sunday.

exception.def: a person or thing that is not included in a general statement
exception.ex: Most of the buildings in the town are modern, but the church is an exception.

exceptional.ex: At the age of five he showed exceptional talent as a musician.

excess.def: in addition to an amount that is necessary, usual or legal
excess.ex: Excess food is stored as fat.

excess.def: more than is necessary, reasonable or acceptable
excess.ex: You can throw away any excess.

excessive.def: greater than what seems reasonable or appropriate
excessive.ex: They complained about the excessive noise coming from the upstairs flat.

exchange.def: an act of giving something to somebody or doing something for somebody and receiving something in return
exchange.ex: The exchange of prisoners took place this morning.

exchange.def: to give something to somebody and at the same time receive the same type of thing from them

excited.def: feeling or showing happiness and enthusiasm
excited.ex: Staff got excited when they heard they were getting a bonus.

excitement.def: the state of feeling or showing happiness and enthusiasm
excitement.ex: The news caused great excitement among her friends.

exciting.ex: This is an exciting opportunity for me.

exclude.def: to deliberately not include something in what you are doing or considering
exclude.ex: The cost of borrowing has been excluded from the inflation figures.

exclusion.def: the act of preventing somebody/something from entering a place or taking part in something
exclusion.ex: He was disappointed with his exclusion from the England squad.

exclusive.def: only to be used by one particular person or group; only given to one particular person or group
exclusive.ex: The hotel has exclusive access to the beach.

exclusively.def: for only one particular person, group or use
exclusively.ex: The resort caters almost exclusively for a high-society public.

excuse.def: a reason, either true or invented, that you give to explain or defend your behaviour
excuse.ex: Late again! What's your excuse this time?

excuse.def: to forgive somebody for something that they have done, for example not being polite or making a small mistake

execute.def: to kill somebody, especially as a legal punishment

execution.def: the act of killing somebody, especially as a legal punishment
execution.ex: He faced execution by hanging for murder.

executive.def: connected with managing a business or an organization, and with making plans and decisions
executive.ex: She has an executive position in a finance company.

executive.def: a person who has an important job as a manager of a company or an organization

exercise.def: physical or mental activity that you do to stay healthy or become stronger
to do sports or other physical activities in order to stay healthy or become stronger; to make an animal do this
exercise.ex: an hour’s class of exercising to music

exert.def: to use power or influence to affect somebody/something
exert.ex: He exerted all his authority to make them accept the plan.

exhibit.def: an object or a collection of objects put in a public place, for example a museum, so that people can see it
exhibit.ex: The museum contains some interesting exhibits on Spanish rural life.

exhibit.def: to show something in a public place for people to enjoy or to give them information
exhibit.ex: They will be exhibiting their new designs at the trade fairs.

exhibition.def: a collection of things, for example works of art, that are shown to the public
exhibition.ex: Have you seen the Picasso exhibition?

exile.def: the state of being sent to live in another country that is not your own, especially for political reasons or as a punishment

exist.def: to be real; to be present in a place or situation
exist.ex: Does life exist on other planets?

existence.def: the state or fact of being real or living or of being present
existence.ex: I was unaware of his existence until today.

exit.def: a way out of a public building or vehicle
to go out; to leave a building, stage, vehicle, etc.
exit.ex: The bullet entered her back and exited through her chest.

exotic.def: from or in another country, especially a tropical one; seeming exciting and unusual because it seems to be connected with foreign countries

expand.def: to become greater in size, number or importance; to make something greater in size, number or importance
expand.ex: Metals expand when they are heated.

expansion.def: an act of increasing or making something increase in size, amount or importance
expansion.ex: a period of rapid economic expansion

expect.def: to think or believe that something will happen or that somebody will do something
expect.ex: The company is expecting record sales this year.

expectation.def: a belief that something will happen because it is likely
expectation.ex: We are confident in our expectation of a full recovery.

expected.ex: Double the expected number of people came to the meeting.

expedition.def: an organized journey with a particular purpose, especially to find out about a place that is not well known

expenditure.def: the act of spending or using money; an amount of money spent

expense.def: the money that you spend on something
expense.ex: The garden was transformed at great expense.

experience.def: the knowledge and skill that you have gained through doing something for a period of time; the process of gaining this
experience.ex: My lack of practical experience was a disadvantage.

experience.def: to have a particular situation affect you or happen to you
experience.ex: Many people have never experienced these difficulties first-hand.

experienced.def: having knowledge or skill in a particular job or activity

experiment.def: a scientific test that is done in order to study what happens and to gain new knowledge
to do a scientific experiment or experiments
experiment.ex: After experimenting at Columbia University, he realized that fission was the key to releasing nuclear energy.

experimental.def: based on new ideas, forms or methods that are used to find out what effect they have
experimental.ex: The school's experimental teaching methods include letting the children decide what to study.

expert.def: done with, having or involving great knowledge or skill
a person with special knowledge, skill or training in something

expertise.def: expert knowledge or skill in a particular subject, activity or job

expire.def: to be no longer legally acceptable because the period of time for which it could be used has ended
expire.ex: When does your driving licence expire?

explain.def: to tell somebody about something in a way that makes it easy to understand
explain.ex: He was trying to explain the difference between hip hop and rap.

explanation.def: a statement, fact, or situation that tells you why something happened; a reason given for something
explanation.ex: The most likely explanation is that his plane was delayed.

explicit.def: clear and easy to understand, so that you have no doubt what is meant
explicit.ex: He gave me very explicit directions on how to get there.

explicitly.def: clearly or directly, so that the meaning is easy to understand
explicitly.ex: The report states explicitly that the system was to blame.

explode.def: to burst (= break apart) or make something burst loudly and violently, causing damage
explode.ex: A second bomb exploded in a crowded market.

exploit.def: to treat a person or situation as an opportunity to gain an advantage for yourself
exploit.ex: He exploited his father's name to get himself a job.

exploitation.def: a situation in which somebody treats somebody else in an unfair way, especially in order to make money from their work

exploration.def: the act of travelling through a place in order to find out about it or look for something in it
exploration.ex: Budgets for space exploration have been cut back.

explore.def: to travel to or around an area or a country in order to learn about it
explore.ex: As soon as we arrived on the island we were eager to explore.

explosion.def: the sudden violent bursting and loud noise of something such as a bomb exploding; the act of deliberately causing something to explode

explosive.def: easily able or likely to explode
a substance that is able or likely to cause an explosion

export.def: the selling and transporting of goods to another country
export.ex: a ban on the export of live cattle

export.def: to sell and send goods to another country
export.ex: The islands export sugar and fruit.

expose.def: to show something that is usually hidden
expose.ex: He smiled suddenly, exposing a set of amazingly white teeth.

exposure.def: the state of being in a place or situation where there is no protection from something harmful or unpleasant

express.def: to show or make known a feeling, an opinion, etc. by words, looks or actions
express.ex: Teachers have expressed concern about the changes.

expression.ex: What's the meaning of the expression ‘on cloud nine’?

extend.def: to make something longer or larger

extension.def: the act of increasing the area of activity, group of people, etc. that is affected by something
extension.ex: the extension of new technology into developing countries

extensive.def: covering a large area; great in amount

extensively.def: in a way that covers a large area

extent.def: how large, important, serious, etc. something is
extent.ex: It is difficult to assess the full extent of the damage.

external.def: connected with or located on the outside of something/somebody
external.ex: the external walls of the building

extra.def: more than is usual, expected, or than exists already
in addition; more than is usual, expected or exists already
a thing that is added to something that is not usual, standard or necessary and that costs more
extra.ex: The monthly fee is fixed and there are no hidden extras (= unexpected costs).

extract.def: a short passage from a book, piece of music, etc. that gives you an idea of what the whole thing is like
extract.ex: The following extract is taken from her new novel.

extract.def: to remove or obtain a substance from something, for example by using an industrial or a chemical process
extract.ex: a machine that extracts excess moisture from the air

extraordinary.ex: What an extraordinary thing to say!

extreme.ex: We are working under extreme pressure at the moment.

extreme.def: a feeling, situation, way of behaving, etc. that is as different as possible from another or is opposite to it

extremely.ex: It is extremely important to follow the directions exactly.

extremist.def: a person whose opinions, especially about religion or politics, are extreme, and who may do things that are violent or illegal for what they believe

eye.def: either of the two organs on the face that you see with
eye.ex: The suspect has dark hair and green eyes.

fabric.def: material made by weaving wool, cotton, silk, etc., used for making clothes, curtains, etc. and for covering furniture
fabric.ex: They sell a wide variety of printed cotton fabric.

fabulous.ex: They put on a fabulous performance.

face.def: the front part of the head, where the eyes, nose and mouth are
to be opposite somebody/something; to have your face or front pointing towards somebody/something or in a particular direction

facilitate.def: to make an action or a process possible or easier
facilitate.ex: The new trade agreement should facilitate more rapid economic growth.

facility.def: buildings, services, equipment, etc. that are provided for a particular purpose

fact.def: a thing that is known to be true, especially when it can be proved
fact.ex: fact about something, First, some basic facts about healthy eating.

faction.def: a small group of people within a larger one, whose members have some different aims and beliefs to those of the larger group
faction.ex: There are rival factions within the administration.

factor.def: one of several things that cause or influence something
factor.ex: Obesity is a major risk factor for heart disease.

factory.def: a building or group of buildings where goods are made, mainly by machine

faculty.def: a department or group of related departments in a college or university

fade.def: to become or to make something become paler or less bright
fade.ex: The curtains had faded in the sun.

fail.def: to not be successful in achieving something
fail.ex: Many diets fail because they are boring.

failure.def: lack of success in doing or achieving something
failure.ex: The success or failure of the plan depends on you.

fair.def: acceptable and appropriate in a particular situation

fairly.def: to some extent but not very

fairness.def: the quality of treating people equally or in a way that is reasonable
fairness.ex: The fairness of the judicial system is being questioned.

faith.def: trust in somebody’s ability or knowledge; trust that somebody/something will do what has been promised
faith.ex: If the company can retain its customers' faith, it could become the market leader.

fake.def: not what somebody claims it is; appearing to be something it is not
fake.ex: There were a few stalls selling fake designer clothing.

fall.ex: I had a bad fall and broke my arm.

fall.def: to drop down from a higher level to a lower level

false.ex: A whale is a fish. True or false?

fame.def: the state of being known and talked about by many people

familiar.def: well known to you; often seen or heard and therefore easy to recognize

family.def: connected with the family or a particular family
a group consisting of one or two parents and their children
family.ex: I introduced Neil to the other members of my family.

fan.def: a person who admires somebody/something or enjoys watching or listening to somebody/something very much

fancy.def: unusually complicated, often in an unnecessary way; intended to impress other people
fancy.ex: a kitchen full of fancy gadgets

fancy.def: to want something or want to do something

fantasy.def: a pleasant situation that you imagine but that is unlikely to happen
fantasy.ex: He spoke of his childhood fantasies about becoming a famous football player.

far.def: at a greater distance away from you
far.ex: I saw her on the far side of the road.

fare.def: the money that you pay to travel by bus, plane, taxi, etc.

farm.def: an area of land, and the buildings on it, used for growing crops and/or keeping animals
to use land for growing crops and/or keeping animals
farm.ex: The family has farmed in Kent for over two hundred years.

farmer.def: a person who owns or manages a farm
