farming.def: the business of managing or working on a farm

fashion.def: a popular style of clothes, hair, etc. at a particular time or place; the state of being popular

fashionable.def: following a style that is popular at a particular time

fast.def: moving or able to move quickly

fasten.def: to close or join together the two parts of something; to become closed or joined together

fat.def: having too much flesh on it and weighing too much
a white or yellow substance in the bodies of animals and humans, stored under the skin

fate.def: the things, especially bad things, that will happen or have happened to somebody/something
fate.ex: The fate of the three men is unknown.

father.def: a male parent of a child or an animal; a person who is acting as the father to a child

fault.def: the responsibility for something wrong that has happened or been done
fault.ex: Why should I say sorry when it's not my fault?

favour.def: a thing that you do to help somebody
favour.ex: Could you do me a favour and pick up Sam from school today?

favour.def: to prefer one system, plan, way of doing something, etc. to another
favour.ex: Many countries favour a presidential system of government.

favourable.def: making people have a good opinion of somebody/something
favourable.ex: She made a favourable impression on his parents.

favourite.def: liked more than others of the same kind
favourite.ex: It's one of my favourite movies.

favourite.def: a person or thing that you like more than the others of the same type

fear.def: the bad feeling that you have when you are in danger or when a particular thing frightens you
to be frightened of somebody/something or frightened of doing something

feat.def: an action or a piece of work that needs skill, strength or courage
feat.ex: The tunnel is a remarkable feat of engineering.

feather.def: one of the many soft light parts covering a bird’s body

feature.def: something important, interesting or typical of a place or thing
feature.ex: An interesting feature of the city is the old market.

feature.def: to include a particular person or thing as a special feature
feature.ex: The film features Cary Grant as a professor.

february.def: the 2nd month of the year, between January and March

federal.def: having a system of government in which the individual states of a country have control over their own affairs, but are controlled by a central government for national decisions, etc.

fee.def: an amount of money that you pay for professional advice or services
fee.ex: Employees are reimbursed for any legal fees incurred when they relocate.

feed.def: a meal of milk for a young baby; a meal for an animal
to give food to a person or an animal
feed.ex: Have you fed the cat yet?

feedback.def: advice, criticism or information about how good or useful something or somebody’s work is

feel.def: the feeling you get when you touch something or are touched
feel.ex: You can tell it's silk by the feel.

feel.def: to experience a particular feeling or emotion
feel.ex: The bus ride made me feel sick.

feeling.def: something that you feel through the mind or through the senses
feeling.ex: a feeling of something, a feeling of guilt/helplessness/anger/sadness

fellow.def: used to describe somebody who is the same as you in some way, or in the same situation

female.def: being a woman or a girl
an animal that can lay eggs or give birth to babies; a plant that can produce fruit
female.ex: One adult female can lay 400 to 500 eggs.

feminist.def: having or based on the belief that women should have the same rights and opportunities as men
a person who supports the belief that women should have the same rights and opportunities as men
feminist.ex: Nineteenth-century feminists demanded equal education and employment opportunities for single women.

fence.def: a structure made of wood or wire supported with posts that is put between two areas of land as a boundary, or around a garden, field, etc. to keep animals in, or to keep people and animals out
fence.ex: The two women chatted over the garden fence.

festival.def: a series of performances of music, plays, films, etc., usually organized in the same place once a year; a series of public events connected with a particular activity or idea

fever.def: a medical condition in which a person has a temperature that is higher than normal

few.def: used with plural nouns and a plural verb to mean ‘a small number’, ‘some’
a small number of people, things or places; some
few.ex: I recognized a few of the other people.

fibre.def: the part of food that helps to keep a person healthy by keeping the bowels working and moving other food quickly through the body

fiction.def: a type of literature that describes imaginary people and events, not real ones

field.def: an area of land in the country used for growing crops or keeping animals in, usually surrounded by a fence, etc.
field.ex: We had to walk across a ploughed field.

fierce.def: angry and aggressive in a way that is frightening

fifty.ex: She was born in the fifties.

fight.def: a struggle against somebody/something using physical force
to take part in a war or battle against an enemy

fighting.def: the activity of being involved in a battle against an enemy
fighting.ex: Fighting broke out in three districts of the city last night.

figure.def: a number representing a particular amount, especially one given in official information
to be part of a process, situation, etc. especially an important part
figure.ex: My feelings about the matter didn't seem to figure at all.

file.def: a box or folded piece of card for keeping loose papers together and in order
to put and keep paper documents in a particular place and in a particular order so that you can find them easily; to put a paper document in a box, file, etc.
file.ex: The forms should be filed alphabetically.

fill.def: to make something full of something; to become full of something
fill.ex: Please fill this glass for me.

film.def: a series of moving pictures recorded with sound that tells a story, watched at a cinema or on a television or other device
film.ex: Let's stay in and watch a film.

film.def: to make a film of a story or a real event; to record somebody/something on film
film.ex: They are filming in Moscow right now.

filter.def: a device containing paper, sand, chemicals, etc. that a liquid or gas is passed through in order to remove any materials that are not wanted
to pass liquid, light, etc. through a special device, especially to remove something that is not wanted
filter.ex: All drinking water must be filtered.

final.def: being or happening at the end of a series of events, actions, statements, etc.
final.ex: his final act as party leader

final.def: the last of a series of games or competitions, in which the winner is decided
final.ex: She reached the final of the 100m hurdles.

finally.def: after a long time, especially when there has been some difficulty or delay
finally.ex: The performance finally started half an hour late.

finance.def: money used to run a business, an activity or a project
finance.ex: The project will only go ahead if they can raise the necessary finance.

finance.def: to provide money for a project
finance.ex: He took a job to finance his stay in Germany.

find.def: to discover somebody/something unexpectedly or by chance

finding.def: information that is discovered as the result of research into something
finding.ex: Our research findings indicate that pregnant women benefit from this treatment.

fine.def: a sum of money that must be paid as punishment for breaking a law or rule
fine.ex: I got a parking fine for parking on double yellow lines.

fine.def: to make somebody pay money as an official punishment

finger.def: one of the four long thin parts that stick out from the hand (or five, if the thumb is included)
finger.ex: She ran her fingers through her hair.

finish.def: the last part or the end of something
finish.ex: It was a close finish as they had predicted.

finish.def: to stop doing something or making something because it is complete
finish.ex: Haven't you finished your homework yet?

fire.def: the flames, light and heat, and often smoke, that are produced when something burns
fire.ex: Most animals are afraid of fire.

fire.def: to shoot bullets, etc. from a gun or other weapon
fire.ex: The officer ordered his men to fire.

firearm.def: a gun that can be carried
firearm.ex: The police were issued with firearms.

firefighter.def: a person whose job is to put out fires
firefighter.ex: Firefighters were called to a house in Summertown.

firework.def: a small device containing powder that burns or explodes and produces bright coloured lights and loud noises, used especially at celebrations
firework.ex: (British English), to let off a few fireworks

firm.def: fairly hard; not easy to press into a different shape

firmly.def: in a strong or definite way

first.def: before anyone or anything else; at the beginning
first.ex: ‘Do you want a drink?’ ‘I'll finish my work first.’

first.def: happening or coming before all other similar things or people; 1st
the person or thing that comes or happens before all other similar people or things
first.ex: I was the first in my family to go to college.

firstly.def: used to introduce the first of a list of points you want to make in a speech or piece of writing
firstly.ex: There are two reasons for this decision: firstly…

fish.def: a creature that lives in water, breathes through gills, and uses fins and a tail for swimming
to try to catch fish with nets, a fishing line, etc.
fish.ex: The trawler was fishing off the coast of Iceland.

fishing.def: the sport or business of catching fish

fit.def: healthy and strong, especially because you do regular physical exercise
fit.ex: Top athletes have to be very fit.

fit.def: a sudden attack of an illness, such as epilepsy, in which somebody becomes unconscious and their body may make violent movements
to be the right shape and size for somebody/something
fit.ex: I tried the dress on but it didn't fit.

fitness.def: the state of being physically healthy and strong
fitness.ex: a magazine on health and fitness

five.ex: There are only five cookies left.

fix.def: a solution to a problem, especially an easy or temporary one
fix.ex: Luckily there is an easy fix.
The car won't start—can you fix it?

fixed.def: staying the same; not changing or able to be changed
fixed.ex: These fixed prices give farmers a degree of financial security.

fixture.def: a sports event that has been arranged to take place on a particular date and at a particular place
fixture.ex: There are plans to make the race an annual fixture.

flag.def: a piece of cloth with a special coloured design on it that may be the symbol of a particular country or organization, may be used to give a signal or may have a particular meaning. A flag can be attached to a pole (= a long thin straight piece of wood or metal) or held in the hand.

flame.def: a hot bright stream of burning gas that comes from something that is on fire
flame.ex: the tiny yellow flame of a match

flash.def: a sudden bright light that shines for a moment and then disappears
to shine very brightly for a short time; to make something shine in this way

flat.def: having a level surface, not curved or sloping, and without holes or any bits sticking out
a set of rooms for living in, usually on one floor of a building
flat.ex: They're renting a furnished flat on the third floor.

flavour.ex: The tomatoes give extra flavour to the sauce.

flaw.def: a mistake in something that means that it is not correct or does not work correctly
flaw.ex: The argument is full of fundamental flaws.

flawed.def: having a flaw; not perfect or correct

flee.def: to leave a person or place very quickly, especially because you are afraid of possible danger
flee.ex: She burst into tears and fled.

fleet.def: a group of military ships commanded by the same person

flesh.def: the soft substance between the skin and bones of animal or human bodies
flesh.ex: The trap had cut deeply into the rabbit's flesh.

flexibility.def: the ability to change to suit new conditions or situations
flexibility.ex: The new system offers a much greater degree of flexibility in the way work is organized.

flexible.def: able to change to suit new conditions or situations

flight.def: a journey made by air, especially in a plane

float.def: to move slowly on water or in the air
float.ex: A group of swans floated by.

flood.def: a large amount of water covering an area that is usually dry
flood.ex: The heavy rain has caused floods in many parts of the country.

flood.def: if a place floods or something floods it, it becomes filled or covered with water
flood.ex: The cellar floods whenever it rains heavily.

floor.def: the surface of a room that you walk on

flour.def: a fine white or brown powder made from grain, especially wheat, and used in cooking for making bread, cakes, etc.
flour.ex: Sift the flour and salt into a bowl.

flourish.def: to develop quickly and become successful or common
flourish.ex: Few businesses are flourishing in the present economic climate.

flow.def: the steady and continuous movement of something/somebody in one direction
flow.ex: the flow of an electric current

flow.def: to move steadily and continuously in one direction
flow.ex: She lost control and the tears began to flow.

flower.def: the coloured part of a plant from which the seed or fruit develops. Flowers usually grow at the end of a stem and last only a short time.
flower.ex: The plant has a beautiful bright red flower.

flu.def: a serious disease caused by a virus, that causes a high temperature, severe pains and weakness
flu.ex: The whole family has the flu.

fluid.def: a liquid; a substance that can flow

fly.def: a small flying insect with two wings. There are many different types of fly and many of them spread disease.
fly.ex: A fly was buzzing against the window.

fly.def: to move through the air, using wings

focus.def: the thing or person that people are most interested in; the act of paying special attention to something and making people interested in it
focus.ex: It was the main focus of attention at the meeting.

focus.def: to give attention, effort, etc. to one particular subject, situation or person rather than another
focus.ex: She was distracted and finding it hard to focus.

fold.def: a part of something, especially cloth, that is folded or hangs as if it had been folded
to bend something, especially paper or cloth, so that one part lies on top of another part
fold.ex: fold something, First, fold the paper in half/in two.

folding.def: that can be folded, so that it can be carried or stored in a small space

folk.def: traditional and typical of the ordinary people of a country or community
folk.ex: We visited an exhibition of folk art.

follow.def: to come or go after or behind somebody/something

following.def: that is/are going to be mentioned next
the thing or things that you will mention next; the person or people that you will mention next
following.ex: The following is a summary of events.

following.def: after or as a result of a particular event
following.ex: He took charge of the family business following his father's death.

fond.def: having warm or loving feelings for somebody, especially somebody you have known for a long time
fond.ex: I have grown quite fond of her.

food.def: things that people or animals eat
food.ex: Pat had prepared food and drink for the work party.

fool.def: a person who you think behaves or speaks in a way that lacks intelligence or good judgement

foot.def: the lowest part of the leg, below the ankle, on which a person or an animal stands

footage.def: part of a film showing a particular event
footage.ex: old film footage of the moon landing

football.def: a game played by two teams of 11 players, using a round ball that players kick up and down the playing field. Teams try to kick the ball into the other team’s goal.
football.ex: The kids were outside playing football.

for.def: used to show who is intended to have or use something or where something is intended to be put

forbid.def: to order somebody not to do something; to order that something must not be done
forbid.ex: He forbade them from mentioning the subject again.

force.def: violent physical action used to obtain or achieve something
force.ex: The release of the hostages could not be achieved without the use of force.

force.def: to make somebody do something that they do not want to do
force.ex: The President was forced into resigning.

forecast.def: a statement about what will happen in the future, based on information that is available now
to say what you think will happen in the future based on information that you have now
forecast.ex: Experts are forecasting a recovery in the economy.

foreign.def: in or from a country that is not your own
foreign.ex: What foreign languages do you speak?

foreigner.def: a person who comes from a different country
foreigner.ex: The fact that I was a foreigner was a big disadvantage.

forest.def: a large area of land that is thickly covered with trees

forever.def: used to say that a particular situation or state will always exist

forge.def: to put a lot of effort into making something successful or strong so that it will last
forge.ex: a move to forge new links between management and workers

forget.def: to not remember to do something that you ought to do, or to bring or buy something that you ought to bring or buy
forget.ex: ‘Why weren't you at the meeting?’ ‘Sorry—I forgot.’

forgive.def: to stop feeling angry with somebody who has done something to harm, annoy or upset you; to stop feeling angry with yourself

fork.def: a tool with a handle and three or four sharp points (called prongs), used for picking up and eating food
fork.ex: to eat with a knife and fork

form.def: a type or variety of something
form.ex: all the millions of different life forms on the planet today

form.def: to produce something in a particular way or make it have a particular shape
form.ex: Bend the wire so that it forms a ‘V’.

formal.def: very correct and suitable for official or important occasions

format.def: the general arrangement, plan, design, etc. of something
format.ex: The format of the new quiz show has proved popular.

formation.def: the action of forming something; the process of being formed
formation.ex: the formation of a new government

former.ex: This fine ruin was, in former times, a royal castle.

formerly.ex: Namibia, formerly known as South West Africa

formula.def: a series of letters, numbers or symbols that represent a rule or law
formula.ex: This formula is used to calculate the area of a circle.

formulate.def: to create or prepare something carefully, giving particular attention to the details
formulate.ex: formulate something, to formulate a policy/theory/plan/proposal

forthcoming.def: going to happen, be published, etc. very soon

fortunate.def: having or bringing an advantage, an opportunity, a piece of good luck, etc.
fortunate.ex: Remember those less fortunate than yourselves.

fortunately.ex: I was late, but fortunately the meeting hadn't started.

fortune.ex: He made a fortune in real estate.

forum.def: an event or medium where people can exchange opinions and ideas on a particular issue; a meeting organized for this purpose
forum.ex: forum (on something), to hold an international forum on drug abuse

forward.def: directed or moving towards the front
forward.ex: The door opened, blocking his forward movement.

forward.def: towards a place or position that is in front
forward.ex: She leaned forward and kissed him on the cheek.

fossil.def: the parts of a dead animal or a plant that have become hard and turned into rock
fossil.ex: fossils over two million years old

foster.ex: The club's aim is to foster better relations within the community.

found.def: to start something, such as an organization or an institution, especially by providing money

foundation.def: a principle, an idea or a fact that something is based on and that it grows from
foundation.ex: Respect and friendship provide a solid foundation for marriage.

founder.def: a person who starts an organization, institution, etc. or causes something to be built
founder.ex: the founder and president of the company

fraction.def: a small part or amount of something
fraction.ex: Only a small fraction of a bank's total deposits will be withdrawn at any one time.

fragment.def: a small part of something that has broken off or comes from something larger
fragment.ex: Police found fragments of glass near the scene.

frame.def: a strong border or structure of wood, metal, etc. that holds a picture, door, piece of glass, etc. in position
to put or make a frame or border around something

framework.def: the parts of a building or an object that support its weight and give it shape

franchise.def: formal permission given by a company to somebody who wants to sell its goods or services in a particular area; formal permission given by a government to somebody who wants to operate a public service as a business

frankly.def: in an honest and direct way that people might not like
frankly.ex: He spoke frankly about the ordeal.

fraud.def: the crime of cheating somebody in order to get money or goods illegally
fraud.ex: She was charged with credit card fraud.

free.def: without particular plans or arrangements; not busy
free.ex: Are you free on Saturday? We're having a barbecue.

free.def: to allow somebody to leave prison or somewhere they have been kept against their will
free.ex: The hijackers agreed to free a further ten hostages.

freedom.def: the power or right to do or say what you want without anyone stopping you

freely.def: without anyone trying to prevent or control something
freely.ex: the country’s first freely elected president

freeze.def: to become hard, and often turn to ice, as a result of extreme cold; to make something do this

frequency.def: the rate at which something happens or is repeated
frequency.ex: Fatal road accidents have decreased in frequency over recent years.

frequent.ex: He is a frequent visitor to this country.

frequently.ex: Buses run frequently between the city and the airport.

fresh.def: recently produced or picked and not frozen, dried or preserved in tins or cans

friday.def: the day of the week after Thursday and before Saturday

fridge.def: a piece of electrical equipment in which food is kept cold so that it stays fresh
fridge.ex: The kids tend to raid the fridge when they get home from school.

friend.def: a person you know well and like, and who is not usually a member of your family

friendly.def: behaving in a kind and pleasant way because you like somebody or want to help them

friendship.ex: They formed a close friendship at college.

frighten.def: to make somebody suddenly feel afraid
frighten.ex: I didn't mean to frighten you.

frog.def: a small animal with smooth skin, that lives both on land and in water (= is an amphibian). Frogs have very long back legs for jumping, and no tail.

from.def: used to show where somebody/something starts
from.ex: She began to walk away from him.

front.def: on or at the front of something
the part or side of something that faces forward; the side of something that you look at first
front.ex: The front of the building was covered with ivy.

frozen.def: kept at a very low temperature in order to preserve it

fruit.def: the part of a plant that consists of one or more seeds and a soft inner part, can be eaten as food and usually tastes sweet
fruit.ex: tropical fruits, such as bananas and pineapples

frustrated.def: feeling annoyed and impatient because you cannot do or achieve what you want
frustrated.ex: It's very easy to get frustrated in this job.

frustrating.def: causing you to feel annoyed and impatient because you cannot do or achieve what you want
frustrating.ex: It's frustrating to have to wait so long.

frustration.ex: Dave thumped the table in frustration.

fry.def: to cook something in hot fat or oil; to be cooked in hot fat or oil

fuel.def: any material that produces heat or power, usually when it is burnt
to supply something with material that can be burnt to produce heat or power
fuel.ex: Uranium is used to fuel nuclear plants.

fulfil.def: to do or achieve what was hoped for or expected

full.def: containing or holding as much or as many as possible; having no empty space

full-time.def: for all the hours of a week during which people normally work or study, rather than just for a part of it

fun.ex: She's really fun to be with.

fun.def: the feeling of enjoying yourself; activities that you enjoy
fun.ex: We had a lot of fun at Sarah's party.

function.def: a special activity or purpose of a person or thing
function.ex: The club serves a useful function as a meeting place.

function.def: to work in the correct way
function.ex: We now have a functioning shower.

functional.def: practical and useful; with little or no decoration
functional.ex: Bathrooms don't have to be purely functional.

fund.def: an amount of money that has been saved or has been made available for a particular purpose
to provide money for something, usually something official

fundamental.def: serious and very important; affecting the most central and important parts of something
fundamental.ex: the fundamental principles of scientific method

fundamentally.def: in every way that is important; completely
fundamentally.ex: The two approaches are fundamentally different.

funding.def: money for a particular purpose; the act of providing money for such a purpose

fundraising.def: the activity of collecting money for a charity or organization, often by organizing social events or entertainments
fundraising.ex: The hospice is planning a major fundraising event for June.

funeral.def: a ceremony, often a religious one, for burying or cremating (= burning) a dead person
funeral.ex: Hundreds of people attended the funeral.

fur.def: the soft thick mass of hair that grows on the body of some animals
fur.ex: The cat carefully licked its fur.

furniture.def: objects that can be moved, such as tables, chairs and beds, that are put into a house or an office to make it suitable for living or working in

further.def: at or to a greater distance
further.ex: We had walked a bit further than I had realized.

furthermore.def: in addition to what has just been stated. Furthermore is used especially to add a point to an argument.
furthermore.ex: He said he had not discussed the matter with her. Furthermore, he had not even contacted her.

future.def: taking place or existing at a time after the present
future.ex: The land will remain untouched for future generations to enjoy.

future.def: the time that will come after the present or the events that will happen then
future.ex: We need to plan for the future.

gain.def: an increase in the amount of something, especially in wealth or weight
gain.ex: The opposition made unexpected gains in the last election.

gain.def: to obtain or win something, especially something that you need or want
gain.ex: They managed to gain access to secret files.

gallery.def: a room or building for showing works of art, especially to the public

gallon.def: a unit for measuring liquid. In the UK, Canada and other countries it is equal to about 4.5 litres; in the US it is equal to about 3.8 litres. There are four quarts in a gallon.
gallon.ex: The tankers carried 130 000 gallons of fuel.

gambling.def: the activity of playing games of chance for money and of betting on horses, etc.

game.def: an activity that you do to have fun, often one that has rules and that you can win or lose; the equipment for a game
game.ex: The kids were playing a game with their balloons.

gap.def: a space where something is missing
gap.ex: Extra funding is needed to plug the gap.

garage.def: a building for keeping one or more cars or other vehicles in
garage.ex: a double garage (= one for two cars)

garden.def: a piece of land next to or around your house where you can grow flowers, fruit, vegetables, etc., usually with a lawn (= an area of grass)

gas.def: any substance like air that is neither a solid nor a liquid, for example hydrogen or oxygen
gas.ex: Air is a mixture of gases.

gate.def: a barrier like a door that is used to close an opening in a fence or a wall outside a building

gather.def: to come together, or bring people together, in one place to form a group

gathering.def: a meeting of people for a particular purpose

gay.def: sexually attracted to people of the same sex

gaze.def: a long, steady look at somebody/something
gaze.ex: He met her gaze (= looked at her while she looked at him).

gaze.def: to look steadily at somebody/something for a long time, either because you are very interested or surprised, or because you are thinking of something else
gaze.ex: She gazed at him in amazement.

gear.def: equipment in a vehicle that changes the relation between engine speed (or pedal speed on a bicycle) and the speed of the wheels moving forwards or backwards
gear.ex: Careless use of the clutch may damage the gears.

gender.def: the fact of being male or female, especially when considered with reference to social and cultural differences, rather than differences in biology; members of a particular gender as a group
gender.ex: issues of class, race and gender

gene.def: a unit inside a cell that controls a particular quality in a living thing that has been passed on from its parents

general.def: affecting all or most people, places or things
general.ex: books of general interest (= of interest to most people)

generally.ex: The initiative was generally considered a success.

generate.ex: The wind turbines are used to generate electricity.

generation.def: all the people who were born at about the same time

generic.def: shared by, including or typical of a whole group of things; not specific
generic.ex: ‘Vine fruit’ is the generic term for currants and raisins.

generous.def: giving or willing to give freely; given freely
generous.ex: The gallery was named after its most generous benefactor.

genetic.def: connected with genes (= the units in the cells of a living thing that control its physical characteristics) or genetics (= the study of genes)

genius.def: unusually great intelligence, skill or artistic ability

genocide.def: the murder of a large number of people from a particular nation or ethnic group, with the aim of destroying that nation or group
genocide.ex: Refugees gave accounts of the mass genocide.

genre.def: a particular type or style of literature, art, film or music that you can recognize because of its special features

gentle.def: calm and kind; doing things in a quiet and careful way

gentleman.def: a man who is polite and well educated, who has excellent manners and always behaves well
gentleman.ex: You acted like a true gentleman.

genuine.def: real; exactly what it appears to be; not artificial
genuine.ex: Is the painting a genuine Picasso?

genuinely.def: truly; in a way that is exactly what it appears to be and is not artificial
genuinely.ex: There are some genuinely funny moments in the film.

geography.def: the scientific study of the earth’s surface, physical features, divisions, products, population, etc.

gesture.def: a movement that you make with your hands, your head or your face to show a particular meaning
gesture.ex: He made a rude gesture at the driver of the other car.

get.ex: I got a call from Dave this morning.

ghost.def: the spirit of a dead person that a living person believes they can see or hear
ghost.ex: Do you believe in ghosts (= believe that they exist)?

giant.def: very large; much larger or more important than similar things usually are
giant.ex: The match was shown on a giant screen outside the town hall.

giant.def: (in stories) a very large strong person who is often cruel and stupid

gift.def: a thing that you give to somebody, especially on a special occasion or to say thank you

gig.def: a performance by musicians playing popular music or jazz in front of an audience; a similar performance by a comedian
gig.ex: They're doing a gig in Boston tonight.

girlfriend.def: a girl or a woman that somebody is having a romantic relationship with

give.def: to hand something to somebody so that they can look at it, use it or keep it for a time
give.ex: Give the letter to your mother when you've read it.

glance.ex: to take/have a glance at the newspaper headlines

glass.def: a hard, usually clear, substance used, for example, for making windows and bottles

glimpse.def: a sight of somebody/something for a very short time, when you do not see the person or thing completely
glimpse.ex: He caught a glimpse of her in the crowd.

global.def: covering or affecting the whole world
global.ex: This year the global economy will grow by about 4 per cent.

globe.def: the world (used especially to emphasize its size)
globe.ex: tourists from every corner of the globe

glorious.def: deserving or bringing great success and making somebody/something famous
glorious.ex: We congratulate you on this glorious victory.

glory.def: great success that brings somebody praise and honour and makes them famous

glove.def: a piece of clothing for the hand, made of wool, leather, etc. with separate parts for each finger and the thumb

go.def: a person’s turn to move or play in a game or an activity
to move or travel from one place to another
go.ex: She went into her room and shut the door behind her.

goal.def: a frame with a net into which players must kick or hit the ball in order to score a point
goal.ex: He headed the ball into an open goal (= one that had nobody defending it).

god.def: the being or spirit that is worshipped and is believed to have created the universe

gold.def: bright yellow in colour, like gold
gold.ex: The company name was spelled out in gold letters.

gold.def: a chemical element. Gold is a yellow precious metal used for making coins, jewellery, beautiful objects, etc.

golf.def: a game played over a large area of ground using sticks with a special shape to hit a small hard ball (a golf ball) into a series of 9 or 18 holes, using as few shots as possible

good.def: of high quality or an acceptable standard
behaviour that is morally right or acceptable
good.ex: Do they even know the difference between good and evil?

goodbye.def: used when you are leaving somebody or when somebody else is leaving
goodbye.ex: Goodbye! It was great to meet you.

goodness.ex: the essential goodness of human nature

goods.def: things that are produced to be sold

gorgeous.def: very beautiful and attractive; giving great pleasure

govern.def: to legally control a country or its people and be responsible for introducing new laws, organizing public services, etc.
govern.ex: The country is governed by elected representatives of the people.

governance.def: the activity of governing a country or controlling a company or an organization; the way in which a country is governed or a company or institution is controlled
governance.ex: He emphasized the company's commitment to high standards of corporate governance.

government.def: the group of people who are responsible for controlling a country or a state

governor.def: a person who is the official head of a country or region that is governed by another country
governor.ex: the former governor of the colony

grab.def: to take or hold somebody/something with your hand suddenly or roughly
grab.ex: She grabbed his arm as he got up to leave.

grace.def: an attractive quality of movement that is smooth and done with control; a simple and beautiful quality
grace.ex: She moves with the natural grace of a ballerina.

grade.def: a mark given in an exam or for a piece of school work
grade.ex: She got good grades in her exams.

grade.def: to give a grade to a student or to a piece of their written work
grade.ex: I spent all weekend grading papers.

gradually.def: slowly, over a long period of time

graduate.def: a person who has a university degree
to get a degree, especially your first degree, from a university or college
graduate.ex: She taught in France after she graduated.

grain.def: the small hard seeds of food plants such as wheat, rice, etc.; a single seed of such a plant
grain.ex: Russia sold 12 million tons of grain abroad last year.

grand.ex: It's not a very grand house.

grandfather.def: the father of your father or mother
grandfather.ex: The firm had been founded by his grandfather.

grandmother.def: the mother of your father or mother
grandmother.ex: I always loved going to visit my grandmother.

grandparent.def: the father or mother of your father or mother
grandparent.ex: The children are staying with their grandparents.

grant.def: a sum of money that is given by the government or by another organization to be used for a particular purpose
to agree to give somebody what they ask for, especially formal or legal permission to do something

graphic.def: connected with drawings and design, especially in the production of books, magazines, websites, etc.

graphics.def: designs, drawings or pictures that are used especially in the production of books, magazines, websites, etc.
graphics.ex: Text and graphics are prepared separately and then combined.

grasp.def: a strong hold of somebody/something or control over somebody/something
grasp.ex: I grabbed him, but he slipped from my grasp.

grasp.def: to take a strong hold of somebody/something
grasp.ex: He grasped my hand and shook it warmly.

grass.def: a common wild plant with narrow green leaves and stems that are eaten by cows, horses, sheep, etc.

grateful.def: feeling or showing thanks because somebody has done something kind for you or has done as you asked
grateful.ex: Thank you for doing this. I really am so grateful.

grave.def: very serious and important; giving you a reason to feel worried
grave.ex: The police have expressed grave concern about the missing child's safety.

grave.def: a place in the ground where a dead person is buried

gravity.def: the force that attracts objects in space towards each other, and that on the earth pulls them towards the centre of the planet, so that things fall to the ground when they are dropped

greatly.ex: People's reaction to the film has varied greatly.

green.def: having the colour of grass or the leaves of most plants and trees
the colour of grass and the leaves of most plants and trees

greet.def: to say hello to somebody or to welcome them
greet.ex: He greeted all the guests warmly as they arrived.

grey.def: having the colour of smoke or ashes
the colour of smoke or ashes
grey.ex: the dull grey of the sky

grid.def: a pattern of straight lines, usually crossing each other to form squares

grief.def: a very sad feeling, especially when somebody dies
grief.ex: She was overcome with grief when her husband died.

grin.ex: They grinned with delight when they heard our news.

grind.def: to break or press something into very small pieces between two hard surfaces or using a special machine

grip.def: an act of holding somebody/something tightly; a particular way of doing this
grip.ex: Keep a tight grip on the rope.
grip something, ‘Please don't go,’ he said, gripping her arm.

grocery.def: a shop that sells food and other things used in the home. In American English ‘grocery store’ is often used to mean ‘supermarket’.

gross.def: being the total amount of something before anything is taken away
gross.ex: gross weight (= including the container or wrapping)

ground.def: the solid surface of the earth
ground.ex: I found her lying on the ground.

group.def: a number of people or things that are together in the same place or that are connected in some way
group.ex: group of somebody/something, a group of people/students/friends

grow.def: to increase in size, number, strength or quality
grow.ex: The sector is growing at a phenomenal rate.

growth.def: the process in people, animals or plants of growing physically, mentally or emotionally
growth.ex: Lack of water will stunt the plant's growth.

guarantee.def: a formal promise that you will do something or that something will happen
guarantee.ex: They are demanding certain guarantees before they sign the treaty.

guarantee.def: to promise to do something; to promise something will happen
guarantee.ex: Basic human rights, including freedom of speech, are now guaranteed.

guard.def: a person, such as a soldier, a police officer or a prison officer, who protects a place or people, or prevents prisoners from escaping
to protect property, places or people from attack or danger

guerrilla.def: a member of a small group of soldiers who are not part of an official army and who fight against official soldiers, usually to try to change the government
guerrilla.ex: urban guerrillas (= those who fight in towns)

guess.def: an attempt to give an answer or an opinion when you cannot be certain if you are right
to try and give an answer or make a judgement about something without being sure of all the facts
guess.ex: I don't really know. I'm just guessing.

guest.def: a person that you have invited to your house or to a particular event that you are paying for
guest.ex: They only use the dining room when they have guests.

guidance.def: help or advice that is given to somebody, especially by somebody older or with more experience
guidance.ex: guidance for teachers on how to use video in the classroom

guide.def: a person who shows other people the way to a place, especially somebody employed to show tourists around interesting places
guide.ex: Our tour guide showed us around the old town.

guide.def: to show somebody the way to a place, often by going with them; to show somebody a place that you know well
guide.ex: It's a dangerous route—you'll need someone to guide you.

guideline.def: a set of rules or instructions that are given by an official organization telling you how to do something, especially something difficult
guideline.ex: The government has drawn up guidelines for schools during the pandemic.

guilt.def: the unhappy feelings caused by knowing or thinking that you have done something wrong
guilt.ex: She had feelings of guilt about leaving her children and going to work.

guilty.def: feeling ashamed because you have done something that you know is wrong or have not done something that you should have done
guilty.ex: Matt and Chrissy both looked equally guilty.

guitar.def: a musical instrument that usually has six strings and that you play with your fingers or with a plectrum

gun.def: a weapon that is used for firing bullets or shells
gun.ex: I have never fired a gun in my life.

gut.def: the tube in the body through which food passes when it leaves the stomach
gut.ex: It can take up to 72 hours for food to pass through the gut.

gym.def: a room or hall with equipment for doing physical exercise, for example in a school
gym.ex: The school has recently built a new gym.

habit.def: a thing that you do often and almost without thinking, especially something that is hard to stop doing
habit.ex: You need to change your eating habits.

habitat.def: the place where a particular type of animal or plant is normally found
habitat.ex: The panda's natural habitat is the bamboo forest.

hail.def: to describe somebody/something as being very good or special, especially in newspapers, etc.
hail.ex: The conference was hailed as a great success.

hair.def: the substance that looks like a mass of fine threads growing on a person's head

half.def: an amount equal to half of something/somebody
half.ex: I'll see you in half an hour.

half.def: either of two equal parts into which something is or can be divided

halfway.def: at an equal distance between two points; in the middle of a period of time
halfway.ex: It's about halfway between London and Bristol.

hall.def: a space or passage inside the entrance or front door of a building
hall.ex: Her brother was standing in the front hall.

halt.def: an act of stopping the movement or progress of somebody/something
halt.ex: Work came to a halt when the machine broke down.

halt.def: to stop; to make somebody/something stop
halt.ex: She walked towards him and then halted.

hand.def: the part of the body at the end of the arm, including the fingers and thumb
hand.ex: Ian placed a hand on her shoulder.

hand.def: to pass or give something to somebody
hand.ex: She handed the letter to me.

handful.def: the amount of something that can be held in one hand

handle.def: the part of a door, window, etc. that you use to open it
handle.ex: She turned the handle and opened the door.

handle.def: to deal with a situation, a person, an area of work or a strong emotion
handle.ex: A new man was appointed to handle the crisis.

handling.def: the way that somebody deals with or treats a situation, a person, an animal, etc.
handling.ex: I was impressed by his handling of the affair.

handy.def: easy to use or to do

hang.def: to attach something, or to be attached, at the top so that the lower part is free or loose
hang.ex: Hang your coat on the hook.

happen.def: to take place, especially without being planned

happily.def: in a cheerful way; with feelings of pleasure
happily.ex: children playing happily on the beach

happiness.def: the state of feeling or showing pleasure

harassment.def: the act of annoying or worrying somebody by putting pressure on them or saying or doing unpleasant things to them

harbour.def: an area of water on the coast, protected from the open sea by strong walls, where ships can shelter
harbour.ex: Several boats lay at anchor in the harbour.

hard.def: difficult to do, understand or answer

hardly.def: almost no; almost not; almost none

hardware.def: the machines and electronic parts in a computer or other electronic system
hardware.ex: We supply computer hardware to businesses.

harm.def: damage or injury that is caused by a person or an event
harm.ex: He would never frighten anyone or cause them any harm.

harm.def: to hurt or injure somebody or to damage something

harmful.def: causing damage or injury to somebody/something, especially to a person’s health or to the environment

harmony.def: a state of peaceful existence and agreement

harsh.ex: The punishment was harsh and unfair.

harvest.def: the time of year when the crops are gathered in on a farm, etc.; the act of cutting and gathering crops
to cut and gather a crop; to catch a number of animals or fish to eat
harvest.ex: The nuts are ready to harvest in September.

hat.def: a piece of clothing made to fit the head, often with a brim (= a flat edge that sticks out), usually worn out of doors

hate.def: a very strong feeling of dislike for somebody
hate.ex: She gave him a look of real hate.
The two boys hated each other.

hatred.def: a very strong feeling of dislike for somebody/something
hatred.ex: He looked at me with intense hatred.

haunt.def: if the ghost of a dead person haunts a place, people say that they have seen it there
haunt.ex: A headless rider haunts the country lanes.

have.def: to own, hold or possess something
have.ex: He had a new car and a boat.

have.def: used with the past participle to form perfect tenses

have to.def: used to show that you must do something

hazard.def: something that can be dangerous or cause damage

he.def: a male person or animal that has already been mentioned or is easily identified
he.ex: Everyone liked my father—he was the perfect gentleman.

head.def: the part of the body on top of the neck containing the eyes, nose, mouth and brain
head.ex: She nodded her head in agreement.

head.def: to move in a particular direction

headache.def: a continuous pain in the head

headline.def: the title of a news article printed in large letters, especially at the top of the front page on a newspaper or the home page on a news website

headquarters.def: a place from which an organization or a military operation is controlled; the people who work there
headquarters.ex: The firm’s headquarters is/are in London.

heal.def: to become healthy again; to make something healthy again
heal.ex: It took a long time for the wounds to heal.

health.def: the condition of a person’s body or mind
health.ex: to be good/bad for your health

healthcare.def: the service of providing medical care
healthcare.ex: the costs of healthcare for the elderly

healthy.def: having good health and not likely to become ill

hear.def: to be aware of sounds with your ears

heart.def: the organ in the chest that sends blood around the body, usually on the left in humans
heart.ex: The patient's heart stopped beating for a few seconds.

heat.def: to make something hot or warm; to become hot or warm
heat.ex: Heat the oil and add the onions.

heating.def: the process of supplying heat to a room or building; a system used to do this

heaven.def: the place believed to be the home of God where good people go when they die, sometimes imagined to be in the sky

heavily.def: to a great degree; in large amounts

heavy.def: weighing a lot; difficult to lift or move

heel.def: the back part of the foot below the ankle
heel.ex: Apply the cream to dry, cracked heels before bed.

height.def: the measurement of how tall a person or thing is
height.ex: Height: 210 mm. Width: 57 mm. Length: 170 mm.

heighten.def: if a feeling or an effect heightens, or something heightens it, it becomes stronger or increases
heighten.ex: Tension has heightened after the recent bomb attack.

helicopter.def: an aircraft without wings that has large blades (= long flat parts) on top that go round. It can fly straight up from the ground and can also stay in one position in the air.

hell.def: the place believed to be the home of devils and where bad people go after death
hell.ex: He was terrified of going to hell when he died.

hello.def: used as a greeting when you meet somebody, in an email, when you answer the phone or when you want to attract somebody’s attention

helmet.def: a type of hard hat that protects the head, worn, for example, by a police officer, a soldier or a person riding a bike or motorbike or playing some sports
helmet.ex: Police in riot helmets lined the streets.

help.def: the act of helping somebody to do something
help.ex: Thank you for all your help.

help.def: to make it easier or possible for somebody to do something by doing something for them or by giving them something that they need
help.ex: What can I do to help?

helpful.def: able to improve a particular situation

hence.ex: We suspect they are trying to hide something, hence the need for an independent inquiry.

her.def: of or belonging to a woman or girl who has already been mentioned or is easily identified
used as the object of a verb, after the verb be or after a preposition to refer to a woman or girl who has already been mentioned or is easily identified
her.ex: We're going to call her Sophie.

herb.def: a plant whose leaves, flowers or seeds are used to add taste to food, in medicines or for their pleasant smell. parsley, mint and oregano are all herbs.

here.def: used after a verb or preposition to mean ‘in, at or to this position or place’

heritage.def: the history, traditions, buildings and objects that a country or society has had for many years and that are considered an important part of its character

hero.def: a person who is admired by many people for doing something brave or good
hero.ex: a war hero (= somebody who was very brave during a war)

herself.def: used when the woman or girl who performs an action is also affected by it

hesitate.def: to be slow to speak or act because you feel uncertain or nervous

hey.def: used to attract somebody’s attention or to express interest, surprise or anger
hey.ex: Hey, can I just ask you something?

hidden.def: something that is hidden is kept or located in a place where it cannot be seen
hidden.ex: Hidden dangers lurk in the ocean depths.

hide.def: to put or keep somebody/something in a place where they/it cannot be seen or found
hide.ex: He hid the letter in a drawer.

hierarchy.def: a system, especially in a society or an organization, in which people are organized into different levels of importance from highest to lowest

high.def: measuring a long distance from the bottom to the top
high.ex: What's the highest mountain in the US?

high.def: at or to a position or level that is a long way up from the ground or from the bottom

high-profile.def: receiving or involving a lot of attention and discussion in the media

highlight.def: the best, most interesting or most exciting part of something
highlight.ex: One of the highlights of the trip was seeing the Taj Mahal.

highlight.def: to emphasize something, especially so that people give it more attention
highlight.ex: The report highlighted the importance of exercise to maintain a healthy body.

highly.ex: It is highly unlikely that she'll be late.

highway.def: a main road for travelling long distances, especially one connecting and going through cities and towns
highway.ex: Plans were being made for the construction of a new interstate highway system.

hill.def: an area of land that is higher than the land around it, but not as high as a mountain
hill.ex: a region of gently rolling hills

him.def: used as the object of a verb, after the verb be or after a preposition to refer to a male person or animal that has already been mentioned or is easily identified

himself.def: used when the man or boy who performs an action is also affected by it

hint.def: something that you say or do in an indirect way in order to show somebody what you are thinking
hint.ex: He gave a broad hint (= one that was obvious) that he was thinking of retiring.

hint.def: to suggest something in an indirect way

hip.def: the area at either side of the body between the top of the leg and the middle part of the body; the joint at the top of the leg that connects it with the top part of the body
hip.ex: She stood with her hands on her hips.

hire.def: the act of paying to use something for a short time

hire.ex: She was hired three years ago.

his.def: of or belonging to a man or boy who has already been mentioned or is easily identified

his.ex: He took my hand in his.

historian.def: a person who studies or writes about history; an expert in history
historian.ex: No serious historian today accepts this theory.

historic.def: important in history; likely to be thought of as important at some time in the future

historical.ex: You must place these events in their historical context.

history.def: all the events that happened in the past
history.ex: The battle was a turning point in human history.

hit.def: an act of hitting somebody/something with your hand or with an object held in your hand
to bring your hand, or an object you are holding, against somebody/something quickly and with force
hit.ex: I was afraid he was going to hit me.

hobby.def: an activity that you do for pleasure when you are not working

hockey.def: a game played on a field by two teams of 11 players, with curved sticks and a small hard ball. Teams try to hit the ball into the other team’s goal.

hold.def: the action of holding somebody/something; the way you are holding somebody/something
hold.ex: His hold on her arm tightened.

hold.def: to have somebody/something in your hand, arms, etc.
hold.ex: She was holding a large box.

hole.def: a hollow space in something solid or in the surface of something
hole.ex: He dug a deep hole in the garden.

holiday.def: a period of time when you are not at work or school

hollow.def: having a hole or empty space inside

holy.def: connected with God or a particular religion

home.def: connected with the place where you live
to or at the place where you live
home.ex: Come on, it's time to go home.

home.def: the house or flat that you live in, especially with your family
home.ex: Old people prefer to stay in their own homes.

homeland.def: the country where a person was born
homeland.ex: Many refugees have been forced to flee their homeland.

homeless.def: having no home, and therefore typically living on the streets
homeless.ex: The scheme has been set up to help homeless people.

homework.def: work that is given by teachers for students to do at home
homework.ex: I always do my homework on the bus.

honest.def: always telling the truth, and never stealing or cheating

honesty.ex: She answered all my questions with her usual honesty.

honour.ex: the guest of honour (= the most important one)

honour.def: to do something that shows great respect for somebody/something

hook.def: a curved piece of metal, plastic or wire for hanging things on, catching fish with, etc.
to fasten or hang something on something else using a hook; to be fastened or hanging in this way
hook.ex: We hooked the trailer to the back of the car.

hope.def: a feeling of wanting and expecting a particular thing to happen; something that you wish for
to want something to happen and think that it is possible
hope.ex: All we can do now is wait and hope.

hopeful.def: believing that something you want will happen
hopeful.ex: I feel hopeful that we'll find a suitable house very soon.

hopefully.def: used to express what you hope will happen

horizon.def: the furthest that you can see, where the sky seems to meet the land or the sea
horizon.ex: The sun sank below the horizon.

horn.def: a hard pointed part that grows, usually in pairs, on the heads of some animals, such as sheep and cows. Horns are often curved.
horn.ex: a large bull with curved horns

horrible.def: very bad or unpleasant; used to describe something that you do not like

horror.def: a feeling of great shock or fear
horror.ex: The thought of being left alone filled her with horror.

horse.def: a large animal with four legs, a mane (= long thick hair on its neck) and a tail. Horses are used for riding on, pulling carriages, etc.

hospital.def: a large building where people who are ill or injured are given medical treatment and care
hospital.ex: He had to go to/into hospital for treatment.

host.def: a person who invites guests to a meal, a party, etc. or who has people staying at their house
host.ex: Ian, our host, introduced us to the other guests.

host.def: to organize an event to which others are invited and make all the arrangements for them
host.ex: to host an event/a conference/a meeting

hostage.def: a person who is captured and held prisoner by a person or group, and who may be injured or killed if people do not do what the person or group is asking
hostage.ex: Three children were taken hostage during the bank robbery.

hostile.def: aggressive or unfriendly and ready to argue or fight
hostile.ex: The speaker got a hostile reception from the audience.

hostility.def: aggressive or unfriendly feelings or behaviour
hostility.ex: There was a barely veiled hostility in her tone.

hot.def: having a high temperature; producing heat

hotel.def: a building where people stay, usually for a short time, paying for their rooms and meals

hour.def: 60 minutes; one of the 24 parts that a day is divided into
hour.ex: I spent an hour on the phone.

house.def: a building for people to live in, usually for one family
house.ex: We live in a two-bedroom house.

house.def: to provide a place for a person or an animal to live
house.ex: The government is committed to housing the refugees.

household.def: all the people living together in a house or flat
household.ex: Most households now own at least one car.

housing.def: houses, flats, etc. that people live in, especially when referring to their type, price or condition

however.def: used to introduce a statement that contrasts with something that has just been said
however.ex: He was feeling bad. He went to work, however, and tried to concentrate.

huge.def: extremely large in size or amount; great in degree
huge.ex: The sums of money involved are potentially huge.

human.def: of or connected with people rather than animals, machines or gods
a person rather than an animal or a machine
human.ex: Dogs can hear much better than humans.

humanitarian.def: connected with helping people who are suffering and improving the conditions that they are living in
humanitarian.ex: to provide humanitarian aid to the war zone

humanity.ex: He was found guilty of crimes against humanity.

humble.def: showing you do not think that you are as important as other people
humble.ex: Be humble enough to learn from your mistakes.

humorous.def: funny; showing a sense of humour
humorous.ex: He gave a humorous account of their trip to Spain.

humour.def: the quality in something that makes it funny; the ability to laugh at things that are funny
humour.ex: It was a story full of gentle humour.

hundred.ex: One hundred (of the children) have already been placed with foster families.

hunger.def: the state of not having enough food to eat, especially when this causes illness or death
hunger.ex: Around fifty people die of hunger every day in the camp.

hungry.def: feeling that you want to eat something

hunt.def: an act of looking for somebody/something that is difficult to find
hunt.ex: The hunt is on for a suitable candidate.

hunt.def: to go after wild animals in order to catch or kill them for food, sport or to make money

hunting.def: going after and killing wild animals as a sport or for food

hurricane.def: a violent storm with very strong winds, especially in the western Atlantic Ocean
hurricane.ex: A powerful hurricane hit the Florida coast.

hurry.def: the need or wish to get something done quickly
to move or act quickly because there is not much time
hurry.ex: You'll have to hurry if you want to catch that train.

hurt.ex: None of the passengers were badly hurt.

hurt.def: a feeling of unhappiness because somebody has been unkind or unfair to you
hurt.ex: There was hurt and real anger in her voice.

hurt.def: to cause physical pain to somebody/yourself; to injure somebody/yourself
hurt.ex: He hurt his back playing squash.

husband.def: the man that somebody is married to; a married man

hypothesis.def: an idea or explanation of something that is based on a few known facts but that has not yet been proved to be true or correct

i.def: used as the subject of a verb when the speaker or writer is referring to himself/herself
i.ex: I think I'd better go now.

ice.def: water that has frozen and become solid
ice.ex: There was ice on the windows.

ice cream.def: a type of sweet frozen food made from milk fat, tasting of fruit, chocolate, etc. and often eaten as a dessert; a small amount of this food intended for one person, often served in a container made of biscuit that is like a cone in shape
ice cream.ex: Desserts are served with cream or ice cream.

icon.def: a small symbol on a computer or smartphone screen that represents a program or a file
icon.ex: Click on the printer icon with the mouse.

id.def: an official way of showing who you are, for example a document with your name, date of birth and often a photograph on it (the abbreviation for ‘identity’ or ‘identification’)
id.ex: You must carry ID at all times.

idea.def: a plan, thought or suggestion, especially about what to do in a particular situation
idea.ex: It would be a good idea to call before we leave.

ideal.def: an idea or standard that seems perfect, and worth trying to achieve or obtain
ideal.ex: He was accused of betraying his political ideals.

identification.def: the process of showing, proving or recognizing who or what somebody/something is
identification.ex: The identification of the crash victims was a long and difficult task.

identify.def: to recognize somebody/something and be able to say who or what they are
identify.ex: She was able to identify her attacker.

identity.ex: The police are trying to discover the identity of the killer.

ideological.def: based on or connected with an ideology

ideology.def: a set of ideas that an economic or political system is based on

idiot.def: a rude way to refer to somebody who you think is very stupid

if.def: used to say that one thing can, will or might happen or be true, depending on another thing happening or being true
if.ex: If you see him, give him this note.

ignorance.def: a lack of knowledge or information about something
ignorance.ex: They fought a long battle against prejudice and ignorance.

ignore.def: to pay no attention to something
ignore.ex: He ignored all the ‘No Smoking’ signs and lit up a cigarette.

ill.def: suffering from an illness or disease; not feeling well
ill.ex: Her father is seriously ill in St Luke's hospital.

illness.def: the state of being physically or mentally ill
illness.ex: The virus can cause illness in humans.

illusion.def: a false idea or belief, especially about somebody or about a situation
illusion.ex: She's under the illusion that (= believes wrongly that) she'll get the job.

illustrate.def: to use pictures, photographs, diagrams, etc. in a book, etc.

illustration.def: a drawing or picture in a book, magazine, for decoration or to explain something

image.def: a picture, photograph or statue that represents somebody/something
image.ex: Most simple leaflets will include text and images.

imagery.def: language that produces pictures in the minds of people reading or listening

imaginary.def: existing only in your mind or imagination
imaginary.ex: The equator is an imaginary line around the middle of the earth.

imagination.def: the ability to create pictures in your mind; the part of your mind that does this

imagine.def: to form a picture in your mind of what something might be like
imagine.ex: The house was just as she had imagined it.

immense.ex: There is still an immense amount of work to be done.

immigrant.def: a person who has come to live permanently in a different country from the one they were born in
immigrant.ex: immigrant from…, immigrants from other European countries

immigration.def: the process of coming to live permanently in a different country from the one you were born in; the number of people who do this

immune.def: that cannot catch or be affected by a particular disease or illness
immune.ex: Adults are often immune to German measles.

impact.def: the powerful effect that something has on somebody/something
to have an effect on somebody/something
impact.ex: Her father's death impacted greatly on her childhood years.

impatient.def: annoyed by somebody/something, especially because you have to wait for a long time
impatient.ex: I'd been waiting for twenty minutes and I was getting impatient.

implement.def: to make something that has been officially decided start to happen or be used

implementation.def: the act of making something that has been officially decided start to happen or be used
implementation.ex: the implementation of the new system

implication.def: a possible effect or result of an action or a decision
implication.ex: They failed to consider the wider implications of their actions.

imply.def: to suggest that something is true or that you feel or think something, without saying so directly
imply.ex: Are you implying (that) I am wrong?

import.def: a product or service that is brought into one country from another
import.ex: import from…, food imports from abroad

import.def: to bring a product, a service, an idea, etc. into one country from another
import.ex: The country has to import most of its raw materials.

importance.ex: She stressed the importance of careful preparation.

important.def: having a great effect on people or things; of great value

impose.def: to introduce a new law, rule, tax, etc.; to order that a rule, punishment, etc. be used
impose.ex: The UN Security Council imposed sanctions in 1992.

impossible.def: that cannot exist or be done; not possible

impress.def: if a person or thing impresses you, you admire them or it
impress.ex: We interviewed a number of candidates but none of them impressed us.

impressed.def: admiring somebody/something because you think they are particularly good, interesting, etc.
impressed.ex: I must admit I am impressed.

impression.def: an idea, a feeling or an opinion that you get about somebody/something, or that somebody/something gives you
impression.ex: My first impression of him was favourable.

impressive.def: making you admire them, because they are very large, good, skilful, etc.

imprison.def: to put somebody in a prison or another place from which they cannot escape
imprison.ex: They were imprisoned for possession of drugs.

imprisonment.def: the act of putting somebody in a prison or another place from which they cannot escape; the state of being there
imprisonment.ex: to be sentenced to life imprisonment for murder

improve.def: to become better than before; to make something/somebody better than before
improve.ex: Overall the situation has improved dramatically.

improvement.def: the act of making something better; the process of something becoming better
improvement.ex: The economy has shown significant improvement over the past 9 months.

in.def: into an object, an area or a substance
in.ex: She opened the door and went in.

in.def: at a point within an area or a space

inability.def: the fact of not being able to do something
inability.ex: the government’s inability to provide basic services

inappropriate.def: not suitable or appropriate in a particular situation

incentive.def: something that encourages you to do something
incentive.ex: There is no incentive for people to save fuel.

inch.def: a unit for measuring length or height, equal to 2.54 centimetres. There are 12 inches in a foot.
inch.ex: She's a few inches taller than me.

incidence.def: the extent to which something happens or has an effect
incidence.ex: an area with a high incidence of crime

incident.def: something that happens, especially something unusual or unpleasant
incident.ex: His bad behaviour was just an isolated incident.

inclined.ex: She was inclined to trust him.

include.def: if one thing includes another, it has the second thing as one of its parts
include.ex: The tour included a visit to the Science Museum.

including.def: having something as part of a group or set
including.ex: I've got three days' holiday including New Year's Day.

inclusion.def: the fact of including somebody/something; the fact of being included
inclusion.ex: His inclusion in the team is in doubt.

income.def: the money that a person, a region, a country, etc. earns from work, from investing money, from business, etc.

incorporate.def: to include something so that it forms a part of something
incorporate.ex: The new car design incorporates all the latest safety features.

increase.def: a rise in the amount, number or value of something
to become greater in amount, number, value, etc.; to make something greater in amount, number, value, etc.

increasingly.def: more and more all the time
increasingly.ex: Tourism is playing an increasingly important role in the region's economy

incredible.def: impossible or very difficult to believe

incur.def: if you incur something unpleasant, you are in a situation in which you have to deal with it
incur.ex: She had incurred the wrath of her father by marrying without his consent.

indeed.def: used after very and an adjective or adverb to emphasize a statement, description, etc.

independence.def: freedom from political control by other countries
independence.ex: Cuba gained independence from Spain in 1898.

index.def: a list of names or topics that are referred to in a book, etc., usually arranged at the end of a book in alphabetical order or listed in a separate file or book
index.ex: Look it up in the index.

indicate.def: to show that something is true or exists
indicate.ex: Initial tests indicate the presence of oxygen.

indication.def: a remark or sign that shows that something is happening or what somebody is thinking or feeling
indication.ex: They gave no indication of how the work should be done.

indicator.def: a sign that shows you what something is like or how a situation is changing
indicator.ex: The economic indicators are better than expected.

indictment.def: a sign that a system, society, etc. is very bad or very wrong
indictment.ex: The poverty in our cities is a damning indictment of modern society.

indigenous.def: coming from a particular place and having lived there for a long time before other people came there; relating to, belonging to or developed by these people
indigenous.ex: the indigenous peoples/languages of an area

indirect.def: happening not as the main aim, cause or result of a particular action, but in addition to it
indirect.ex: the indirect effects of the war

individual.def: considered separately rather than as part of a group
individual.ex: We interviewed each individual member of the community.

individual.def: a person considered separately rather than as part of a group
individual.ex: The competition is open to both teams and individuals.

indoor.def: located, done or used inside a building

indoors.ex: I prefer to stay indoors in this really hot weather.

induce.def: to persuade or influence somebody to do something
induce.ex: Nothing would induce me to take the job.

indulge.def: to allow yourself to have or do something that you like, especially something that is considered bad for you
indulge.ex: They went into town to indulge in some serious shopping.

industry.def: the production of goods from raw materials, especially in factories

inequality.def: the unfair difference between groups of people in society, when some have more wealth, status or opportunities than others
inequality.ex: We need to tackle inequality of opportunity wherever we find it.

inevitable.def: that you cannot avoid or prevent
inevitable.ex: It was an inevitable consequence of the decision.

inevitably.ex: Inevitably, the press exaggerated the story.

infamous.def: well known for being bad or evil
infamous.ex: a general who was infamous for his brutality

infant.def: a baby or very young child
infant.ex: a nursery for infants under two

infect.def: to make a disease or an illness spread to a person, an animal or a plant
infect.ex: It is not possible to infect another person through kissing.

infection.def: the act or process of causing or getting a disease

infer.def: to reach an opinion or decide that something is true on the basis of information that is available
infer.ex: Much of the meaning must be inferred from the context.

inflation.def: a general rise in the prices of services and goods in a particular country, resulting in a fall in the value of money; the rate at which this happens

inflict.def: to make somebody/something suffer something unpleasant
inflict.ex: They inflicted a humiliating defeat on the home team.

influence.def: the effect that somebody/something has on the way a person thinks or behaves or on the way that something works or develops
influence.ex: His early work shows the influence of Cézanne and Matisse.

influence.def: to have an effect on the way that somebody behaves or thinks, especially by giving them an example to follow
influence.ex: His writings have influenced the lives of millions.

influential.def: having a lot of influence on somebody/something

inform.def: to tell somebody about something, especially in an official way
inform.ex: The government took this decision without consulting Parliament or informing the public.

informal.def: relaxed and friendly; not following strict rules of how to behave or do something

ingredient.def: one of the things from which something is made, especially one of the foods that are used together to make a particular dish
ingredient.ex: Mix all the ingredients in a bowl.

inhabitant.def: a person or an animal that lives in a particular place
inhabitant.ex: the oldest inhabitant of the village

inherent.def: that is a basic or permanent part of somebody/something and that cannot be removed
inherent.ex: the difficulties inherent in a study of this type

inherit.def: to receive money, property, etc. from somebody when they die
inherit.ex: She inherited a fortune from her father.

inhibit.def: to prevent something from happening or make it happen more slowly or less frequently than normal
inhibit.ex: A lack of oxygen may inhibit brain development in the unborn child.

initial.ex: There is an initial payment of £60 followed by ten instalments of £25.

initiate.ex: to initiate legal proceedings against somebody

initiative.def: a new plan for dealing with a particular problem or for achieving a particular purpose

inject.def: to put a drug or other substance into a person’s or an animal’s body using a syringe
inject.ex: Adrenaline was injected into the muscle.

injection.def: an act of injecting somebody with a drug or other substance

injure.def: to harm yourself or somebody else physically, especially in an accident
injure.ex: He injured his knee playing hockey.

injured.ex: His injured leg prevented him from walking.

injury.def: harm done to a person’s or an animal’s body, for example in an accident

injustice.def: the fact of a situation being unfair and of people not being treated equally; an unfair act or an example of unfair treatment
injustice.ex: We are committed to fighting against poverty and injustice.

ink.def: coloured liquid for writing, drawing and printing

inmate.def: one of the people living in an institution such as a prison or a psychiatric hospital

inner.def: inside; towards or close to the centre of a place

innocent.def: not guilty of a crime, etc.; not having done something wrong
innocent.ex: They have imprisoned an innocent man.

innovation.def: the introduction of new things, ideas or ways of doing something

innovative.def: introducing or using new ideas, ways of doing something, etc.
innovative.ex: There will be a prize for the most innovative design.

input.def: time, knowledge, ideas, etc. that you put into work, a project, etc. in order to make it succeed; the act of putting something in
input.ex: I'd appreciate your input on this.

inquiry.def: an official process to find out the cause of something or to find out information about something

insect.def: any small creature with six legs and a body divided into three parts. Insects usually also have wings. Ants, bees and flies are all insects.
insect.ex: Most birds spend most of the day eating insects.

insert.def: to put something into something else or between two things
insert.ex: Insert coins into the slot and press for a ticket.

insertion.def: the act of putting something inside something else; a thing that is put inside something else
insertion.ex: An examination is carried out before the insertion of the tube.

inside.def: forming the inner part of something; not on the outside
inside.ex: the inside pages of a newspaper
She shook it to make sure there was nothing inside.

inside.def: the inner part, side or surface of something
inside.ex: The inside of the box was blue.

inside.def: on or to the inner part of something/somebody; within something/somebody

insider.def: a person who knows a lot about a group or an organization, because they are part of it
insider.ex: The situation was described by one insider as ‘absolute chaos’.

insight.def: an understanding of what something is like
insight.ex: There are many valuable insights in her book.

insist.def: to demand that something happen or that somebody agree to do something
insist.ex: I didn't really want to go but he insisted.

inspect.def: to look closely at something/somebody, especially to check that everything is as it should be
inspect.ex: The teacher walked around inspecting their work.

inspection.def: an official visit to a school, factory, etc. in order to check that rules are being obeyed and that standards are acceptable
inspection.ex: Regular inspections are carried out at the prison.

inspector.def: an officer of middle rank in the police force
