inspiration.def: the process that takes place when somebody sees or hears something that causes them to have exciting new ideas or makes them want to create something, especially in art, music or literature
inspiration.ex: Dreams can be a rich source of inspiration for an artist.

inspire.def: to give somebody the desire, confidence or enthusiasm to do something well
inspire.ex: The actors' enthusiasm inspired the kids.

install.def: to fix equipment or furniture into position so that it can be used
install.ex: They're planning to install a new drainage system.

installation.def: the act of fixing equipment or furniture in position so that it can be used

instance.def: a particular example or case of something
instance.ex: The report highlights a number of instances of injustice.

instant.ex: This account gives you instant access to your money.

instead.ex: Lee was ill so I went instead.

instinct.def: a natural quality that makes people and animals tend to behave in a particular way using the knowledge and abilities that they were born with rather than thought or training
instinct.ex: She did not seem to have any of the usual maternal instincts.

institute.def: an organization that has a particular purpose, especially one that is connected with education or a particular profession; the building used by this organization
institute.ex: The report was compiled by Germany's five leading economic research institutes.

institution.def: a large important organization that has a particular purpose, for example a university or bank
institution.ex: The deal is backed by one of the country's largest financial institutions.

institutional.def: connected with a large important organization, for example a university or bank

instruct.def: to tell somebody to do something, especially in a formal or official way
instruct.ex: The letter instructed him to report to headquarters immediately.

instruction.def: detailed information on how to do or use something
instruction.ex: Follow the instructions on the packet carefully.

instructor.def: a person whose job is to teach somebody a practical skill or sport

instrument.def: an object used for producing musical sounds, for example a piano or a drum

instrumental.ex: The Conservation Trust performs an instrumental role in the protection of rural environments.

insufficient.def: not large, strong or important enough for a particular purpose

insult.def: a remark or an action that is said or done in order to offend somebody
insult.ex: The crowd were shouting insults at the police.

insult.def: to say or do something that offends somebody
insult.ex: I have never been so insulted in my life!

insurance.def: an arrangement with a company in which you pay them regular amounts of money and they agree to pay the costs, for example, if you die or are ill, or if you lose or damage something

intact.ex: Most of the house remains intact even after two hundred years.

intake.def: the amount of food, drink, etc. that you take into your body

integral.def: being an essential part of something
integral.ex: Music is an integral part of the school's curriculum.

integrate.def: to combine two or more things so that they work together; to combine with something else in this way
integrate.ex: These programs will integrate with your existing software.

integrated.def: in which many different parts are closely connected and work successfully together
integrated.ex: an integrated programme of patient care

integration.def: the act or process of combining two or more things so that they work together
integration.ex: The aim is to promote closer economic integration.

integrity.def: the quality of being honest and having strong moral principles

intellectual.def: connected with or using a person’s ability to think in a logical way and understand things
intellectual.ex: Gifted children typically show great intellectual curiosity and a wide range of interests.

intellectual.def: a person who is well educated and enjoys activities in which they have to think seriously about things
intellectual.ex: He was a leading intellectual of his day.

intelligence.def: the ability to learn, understand and think in a logical way about things; the ability to do this well

intelligent.def: good at learning, understanding and thinking in a logical way about things; showing this ability

intend.def: to have a plan, result or purpose in your mind when you do something
intend.ex: We finished later than we had intended.

intended.def: that you are trying to achieve or reach

intense.ex: We were all suffering in the intense heat.

intensify.def: to increase in degree or strength; to make something increase in degree or strength

intensity.def: the state or quality of being intense

intensive.def: involving a lot of work or activity done in a short time

intent.ex: She denies possessing the drug with intent to supply.

intention.def: what you intend or plan to do; your aim
intention.ex: I have no intention of going to the wedding.

interact.def: to communicate with somebody, especially while you work, play or spend time with them
interact.ex: Teachers have a limited amount of time to interact with each child.

interaction.def: the act of communicating with somebody, especially while you work, play or spend time with them
interaction.ex: the interaction between performers and their audience

interactive.def: that allows information to be passed continuously and in both directions between a computer or other device and the person who uses it

interest.def: the feeling that you have when you want to know or learn more about somebody/something
interest.ex: Her research has generated interest around the world.

interest.def: to attract your attention and make you feel interested; to make yourself give your attention to something

interested.def: giving your attention to something because you enjoy finding out about it or doing it; showing interest in something and finding it exciting

interesting.def: attracting your attention because it is/they are special, exciting or unusual
interesting.ex: The article raises several interesting questions.

interface.def: the way a computer program presents information to a user or receives information from a user, in particular the layout of the screen and the menus

interfere.def: to get involved in and try to influence a situation that should not really involve you, in a way that annoys other people
interfere.ex: I wish my mother would stop interfering and let me make my own decisions.

interference.def: the act of getting involved in and trying to influence a situation that should not really involve you, in a way that annoys other people
interference.ex: They resent foreign interference in the internal affairs of their country.

interim.def: intended to last for only a short time until somebody/something more permanent is found

interior.def: connected with the inside part of something

interior.ex: the interior of a building/a car

intermediate.def: located between two places, things, states, etc.
intermediate.ex: an intermediate stage/step in a process

internal.def: connected with the inside of something
internal.ex: the internal structure of a building

international.def: connected with or involving two or more countries

internet.def: an international computer network connecting other networks and computers that allows people to share information around the world

interpret.def: to explain the meaning of something
interpret.ex: The students were asked to interpret the poem.

interpretation.def: the particular way in which something is understood or explained
interpretation.ex: Her evidence suggests a different interpretation of the events.

interrupt.def: to say or do something that makes somebody stop what they are saying or doing
interrupt.ex: Sorry to interrupt, but there's someone to see you.

interval.def: a period of time between two events
interval.ex: The interval between major earthquakes might be 200 years.

intervene.def: to become involved in a situation in order to improve or help it
intervene.ex: She might have been killed if the neighbours hadn't intervened.

intervention.def: action taken to improve or help a situation
intervention.ex: calls for government intervention to save the steel industry

interview.def: a formal meeting at which somebody is asked questions to see if they are suitable for a particular job, or for a course of study at a college, university, etc.
to talk to somebody and ask them questions at a formal meeting to find out if they are suitable for a job, course of study, etc.
interview.ex: The university interviews all potential candidates.

intimate.def: having a close and friendly relationship

into.def: to a position in or inside something

intriguing.def: very interesting because of being unusual or not having an obvious answer

introduce.def: to tell two or more people who have not met before what each other’s names are; to tell somebody what your name is
introduce.ex: Allow me to introduce my mother.

introduction.def: the first part of a book or speech that gives a general idea of what is to follow
introduction.ex: a book with an excellent introduction and notes

invade.def: to enter a country, town, etc. using military force in order to take control of it
invade.ex: Troops invaded on August 9th that year.

invasion.def: the act of an army entering another country by force in order to take control of it
invasion.ex: the German invasion of Poland in 1939

invent.def: to produce or design something that has not existed before

invention.def: a thing or an idea that has been invented
invention.ex: Fax machines were a wonderful invention at the time.

invest.def: to buy property, shares in a company, etc. in the hope of making a profit
invest.ex: Now is a good time to invest.

investigate.def: to carefully examine the facts of a situation, an event, a crime, etc. to find out the truth about it or how it happened
investigate.ex: The FBI has been called in to investigate.

investigation.def: an official examination of the facts about a situation, crime, etc.

investigator.def: a person who examines a situation such as an accident or a crime to find out the truth

investment.def: the act of investing money in something

investor.def: a person or an organization that invests money in something

invisible.ex: a wizard who could make himself invisible

invitation.def: a spoken or written request to somebody to do something or to go somewhere

invite.def: to ask somebody to come to a social event
invite.ex: Have you been invited to their party?

invoke.def: to mention or use a law, rule, etc. as a reason for doing something
invoke.ex: It is unlikely that libel laws will be invoked.

involve.def: if a situation, an event or an activity involves something, that thing is an important or necessary part or result of it
involve.ex: Any investment involves an element of risk.

involved.def: taking part in something; being part of something or connected with something
involved.ex: Some people tried to stop the fight but I didn't want to get involved.

involvement.def: the act of taking part in something or dealing with somebody

iron.def: a chemical element. Iron is a hard strong metal that is used to make steel and is also found in small quantities in blood and food.
to make clothes, etc. smooth by using an iron
iron.ex: He was ironing when I arrived.

ironic.def: showing that you really mean the opposite of what you are saying; expressing irony

ironically.def: in a way that shows that you really mean the opposite of what you are saying; in a way that expresses irony

irony.def: the funny or strange aspect of a situation that is very different from what you expect; a situation like this
irony.ex: It was one of life's little ironies.

irrelevant.def: not important to or connected with a situation

island.def: a piece of land that is completely surrounded by water

isolate.def: to separate somebody/something physically or socially from other people or things
isolate.ex: Patients with the disease should be isolated.

isolation.def: the act of separating somebody/something; the state of being separate

issue.def: an important topic that people are discussing or arguing about

issue.ex: They issued a joint statement denying the charges.

it.def: the study and use of electronic processes and equipment to store and send information of all kinds, including words, pictures and numbers (the abbreviation for ‘information technology’)
it.ex: the increasing use of IT in all aspects of today's society

it.def: used to refer to an animal or a thing that has already been mentioned or that is being talked about now

item.def: one thing on a list of things to buy, do, talk about, etc.

its.def: belonging to or connected with a thing, an animal or a baby
its.ex: Turn the box on its side.

itself.def: used when the animal or thing that does an action is also affected by it

jacket.def: a piece of clothing worn on the top half of the body over a shirt, etc. that has arms and fastens down the front; a short, light coat

jail.ex: She spent a year in jail.
He was jailed for life for murder.

jam.def: a thick sweet substance made by boiling fruit with sugar, often sold in jars and spread on bread

january.def: the 1st month of the year, between December and February

jazz.def: a type of music with strong rhythms, in which the players often improvise (= make up the music as they are playing), originally created by African American musicians at the beginning of the 20th century

jeans.def: trousers made of denim (= a type of strong cotton)

jet.def: a plane driven by jet engines

jewellery.def: objects such as rings and necklaces that people wear as decoration

job.def: work for which you receive regular payment
job.ex: I don't have a job at present.

join.def: to fix or connect two or more things together

joint.def: involving two or more people together
joint.ex: a joint account (= a bank account in the name of more than one person, for example shared by a couple)

joint.def: a place where two bones are joined together in the body in a way that enables them to bend and move

joke.def: something that you say or do to make people laugh, for example a funny story that you tell
to say something to make people laugh; to tell a funny story
joke.ex: She was laughing and joking with the children.

journal.def: a newspaper or magazine that deals with a particular subject or profession

journalism.def: the work of collecting and writing news stories for newspapers, magazines, radio, television or online news sites; the news stories that are written
journalism.ex: I'd like a career in journalism.

journalist.def: a person whose job is to collect and write news stories for newspapers, magazines, radio, television or online news sites

journey.def: an act of travelling from one place to another, especially when they are far apart
journey.ex: They went on a long train journey across India.

joy.ex: Her books have brought joy to millions.

judge.def: a person in a court who has the authority to decide how criminals should be punished or to make legal decisions
to form an opinion about somebody/something, based on the information you have
judge.ex: As far as I can judge, all of them are to blame.

judgement.def: the ability to make sensible decisions after carefully considering the best thing to do

judicial.def: connected with a court, a judge or legal judgement

juice.def: the liquid that comes from fruit or vegetables; a drink made from this

july.def: the 7th month of the year, between June and August

jump.def: to move quickly off the ground or away from a surface by pushing yourself with your legs and feet

junction.def: the place where two or more roads or railway lines meet
junction.ex: It was near the junction of City Road and Old Street.

june.def: the 6th month of the year, between May and July

junior.def: having a low rank in an organization or a profession

jurisdiction.def: the authority that an official organization has to make legal decisions about somebody/something

jury.def: a group of members of the public who listen to the facts of a case in a court and decide whether or not somebody is guilty of a crime, or whether a claim has been proved

just.def: that most people consider to be morally fair and reasonable

just.ex: There is just one method that might work.

justice.ex: She spent her life fighting for social justice and equality for women.

justification.def: a good reason why something exists or is done
justification.ex: I can see no possible justification for any further tax increases.

justify.def: to show that somebody/something is right or reasonable
justify.ex: How can they justify paying such huge salaries?

keen.def: wanting to do something or wanting something to happen very much
keen.ex: John was very keen to help.

keep.def: to continue to have something and not give it back or throw it away

key.def: a piece of metal with a special shape used for locking a door, starting a car, etc.

key.def: to put information into a computer using a keyboard

keyboard.def: the set of keys for operating a computer or typewriter, or the set of letters that you can touch to write on a smartphone or tablet

kick.def: a movement with the foot or the leg, usually to hit something with the foot

kick.def: to hit somebody/something with your foot

kidnap.def: to take somebody away illegally and keep them as a prisoner, especially in order to get money or something else for returning them
kidnap.ex: Two businessmen have been kidnapped by terrorists.

kidney.def: either of the two organs in the body that remove waste products from the blood and produce urine

kill.ex: Cancer kills thousands of people every year.

killing.def: an act of killing somebody deliberately
killing.ex: Their leader condemned the torture and killing of innocent civilians.

kilometre.def: a unit for measuring distance; 1 000 metres

kind.def: caring about others; gentle, friendly and generous
kind.ex: a very kind and helpful person

kind.def: a group of people or things that are the same in some way; a particular variety or type
kind.ex: kind of somebody/something, three kinds of cakes/cake

king.def: the male ruler of an independent state that has a royal family
king.ex: the kings and queens of England

kingdom.def: a country that has a king or queen as head of state (= official leader of the country)

kiss.ex: Come here and give me a kiss!

kiss.def: to touch somebody with your lips as a sign of love or sexual desire or when saying hello or goodbye
kiss.ex: They stood in a doorway kissing (= kissing each other).

kit.def: a set of tools or equipment that you use for a particular purpose

kitchen.def: a room in which meals are cooked or prepared
kitchen.ex: We ate at the kitchen table.

knee.def: the joint between the top and bottom parts of the leg where it bends in the middle

knife.def: a sharp blade (= metal edge) with a handle, used for cutting or as a weapon

knock.def: the sound of somebody hitting a door, window, etc. with their hand or with something hard to attract attention

knock.def: to hit a door, etc. in order to attract attention

know.def: to have information in your mind as a result of experience or because you have learned or been told it

knowledge.def: the information, understanding and skills that you gain through education or experience

label.def: a piece of paper, etc. that is attached to something and that gives information about it
to fix a label on something or write information on something
label.ex: Make sure that your luggage is clearly labelled.

laboratory.def: a room or building used for scientific research, experiments, testing, etc.

labour.ex: manual labour (= work using your hands)

lack.def: the state of not having something or not having enough of something
lack.ex: lack of something, a lack of understanding/knowledge

lack.def: to have none or not enough of something

lad.ex: Things have changed since I was a lad.

ladder.def: a piece of equipment for climbing up and down a wall, the side of a building, etc., consisting of two lengths of wood or metal that are joined together by steps or rungs
ladder.ex: to climb up/fall off a ladder

lady.def: a word used to mean ‘woman’ that some people, especially older people, consider is more polite
lady.ex: There's a lady waiting to see you.

lake.def: a large area of water that is surrounded by land

lamp.def: a device that uses electricity, oil or gas to produce light

land.def: the surface of the earth that is not sea

land.def: to come down through the air onto the ground or another surface

landing.def: an act of bringing an aircraft or a spacecraft down to the ground after a journey

landlord.def: a person or company from whom you rent a room, a house, an office, etc.

landmark.def: something, such as a large building, that you can see clearly from a distance and that will help you to know where you are

landscape.def: everything you can see when you look across a large area of land, especially in the country

lane.def: a narrow road in the country

language.def: the system of communication in speech and writing that is used by people of a particular country or area

lap.def: the top part of your legs that forms a flat surface when you are sitting down

laptop.def: a small computer that can work with a battery and be easily carried

last.def: after anyone or anything else; at the end

last.def: happening or coming after all other similar things or people

last.def: the person or thing that comes or happens after all other similar people or things

last.def: to continue for a particular period of time

late.def: arriving, happening or done after the expected, arranged or usual time
after the expected, arranged or usual time

later.def: coming after something else or at a time in the future
at a time in the future; after the time you are talking about

latest.def: the most recent or the newest thing or piece of news
latest.ex: This is the latest in robot technology.

latter.def: used to refer to the second of two things or people mentioned
the second of two things or people mentioned
latter.ex: He presented two solutions. The latter seems much better.

laugh.def: the sound you make when you think that something is funny or silly
to make the sounds and movements of your face that show you think something is funny or silly

laughter.def: the act or sound of laughing

launch.def: to start an activity, especially an organized one

law.def: a rule that deals with a particular crime, agreement, etc.

lawn.def: an area of ground covered in short grass in a garden or park, or used for playing a game on

lawyer.def: a person who is trained and qualified to advise people about the law and to represent them in court, and to write legal documents

lay.def: to put somebody/something in a particular position, especially when it is done gently or carefully
lay.ex: He laid a hand on my arm.

layer.def: a quantity or sheet of something that lies over a surface or between surfaces

layout.def: the way in which the parts of something such as the page of a book, a website, a garden or a building are arranged

lazy.def: unwilling to work or be active; doing as little as possible

lead.def: to go with or in front of a person or an animal to show the way or to make them go in the right direction

leader.def: a person who leads a group of people, especially the head of a country, an organization, etc.

leadership.def: the state or position of being a leader

leaf.def: a flat green part of a plant, growing from a stem or branch or from the root

league.def: a group of sports teams who all play each other to earn points and find which team is best

leak.def: a small hole that lets liquid or gas flow in or out of something by accident
to allow liquid or gas to get in or out through a small hole

lean.def: to bend or move from a straight position to a sloping position

leap.def: to jump high or a long way

learn.def: to gain knowledge or skill by studying, from experience, from being taught, etc.

least.ex: He always turns up just when you least expect him.

least.def: smallest in size, amount, degree, etc.

leather.def: material made by removing the hair or fur from animal skins and preserving the skins using special processes

leave.def: a period of time when you are allowed to be away from work for a holiday or for a special reason

leg.def: one of the long parts that connect the feet to the rest of the body

legacy.def: money or property that is given to you by somebody when they die

legend.def: a story from ancient times about people and events, that may or may not be true; this type of story

legendary.def: very famous and talked about a lot by people

legislation.def: a law or a set of laws passed by a parliament

legislative.def: connected with the act of making and passing laws

legislature.def: a group of people who have the power to make and change laws

legitimate.def: for which there is a fair and acceptable reason

leisure.def: time when you are not working or studying; free time

lemon.def: a yellow citrus fruit with juice that has a bitter, sharp taste. Slices of lemon and lemon juice are used in cooking and drinks.

lend.def: to give something to somebody or allow them to use something that belongs to you, which they have to return to you later

length.def: the size or measurement of something from one end to the other

lengthy.def: very long, and often too long, in time or size

lens.def: a curved piece of glass or plastic that makes things look larger, smaller or clearer when you look through it

lesbian.def: sexually attracted to other women; connected with lesbians

less.def: to a smaller degree; not so much
used with uncountable nouns to mean ‘a smaller amount of’

lesser.def: not as great in size, amount or importance as something/somebody else

lesson.def: a period of time in which somebody is taught something

let.def: used for making suggestions or as a polite way of telling people what you want them to do

lethal.def: causing or able to cause death

letter.def: a written, typed or printed message that is put in an envelope or attached to an email and sent to somebody

level.def: having a flat surface that does not slope

level.def: the height of something in relation to the ground or to what it used to be

level.def: to make something flat or smooth

liable.def: legally responsible for paying the cost of something
liable.ex: You will be liable for any damage caused.

liberal.def: willing to understand and respect other people’s behaviour, opinions, etc., especially when they are different from your own; believing people should be able to choose how they behave
a person who understands and respects other people’s opinions and behaviour, especially when they are different from their own

liberation.def: the act or process of freeing a country or a person from the control of somebody else

liberty.def: freedom to live as you choose without too many limits from government or authority
liberty.ex: the fight for justice and liberty

library.def: a building in which collections of books, newspapers, etc. and sometimes films and recorded music are kept for people to read, study or borrow

licence.def: an official document that shows that permission has been given to do, own or use something

license.def: to give somebody official permission to do, own, or use something

lie.def: a statement made by somebody knowing that it is not true
to be or put yourself in a flat position so that you are not standing or sitting

lie.def: to say or write something that you know is not true
lie.ex: You could see from his face that he was lying.

life.def: the ability to breathe, grow, produce young, etc. that people, animals and plants have before they die and that objects do not have

lifelong.def: lasting or existing all through your life

lifestyle.def: the way in which a person or a group of people lives and works

lifetime.def: the length of time that somebody lives or that something lasts

lift.def: a machine that carries people or goods up and down to different levels in a building or a mine

lift.def: to raise somebody/something or be raised to a higher position or level

light.def: the energy from the sun, a lamp, etc. that makes it possible to see things
to make something start to burn

lighting.def: the arrangement or type of light in a place

like.ex: We all have different likes and dislikes.
She's wearing a dress like mine.

like.def: to find somebody/something pleasant, attractive or of a good enough standard; to enjoy something

likelihood.def: the chance of something happening; how likely something is to happen

likely.def: having a good chance of happening or being something; probable or expected

likewise.def: the same; in a similar way

limb.def: an arm or a leg; a similar part of an animal, such as a wing

limit.def: the greatest or smallest amount of something that is allowed

limit.def: to stop something from increasing beyond a particular amount or level

limitation.def: the act or process of limiting or controlling somebody/something

limited.def: not very great in amount or extent

line.def: a long, thin mark on a surface

line.ex: Butter and line a 25 cm cake tin.

line-up.def: the people who are going to take part in a particular event

linear.ex: In his art he broke the laws of scientific linear perspective.

linger.def: to continue to exist for longer than expected

link.def: a connection between two or more people or things

lion.ex: The lion shook its mane and roared.

lip.def: either of the two soft edges at the opening to the mouth

liquid.def: in the form of a liquid; not a solid or a gas
a substance that flows freely and is not a solid or a gas, for example water or oil
liquid.ex: She poured the dark brown liquid down the sink.

list.def: a series of names, items, figures, etc., especially when they are written or printed
list.ex: The guest list includes numerous celebrities.

list.def: to write a list of things in a particular order
list.ex: We were asked to list our ten favourite songs.

listen.def: to pay attention to somebody/something that you can hear

listener.ex: a good listener (= somebody who you can rely on to listen with attention or sympathy)

listing.def: a list, especially an official or published list of people or things, often arranged in alphabetical order
listing.ex: a comprehensive listing of all airlines

literacy.def: the ability to read and write

literature.def: pieces of writing that are valued as works of art, especially novels, plays and poems (in contrast to technical books and newspapers, magazines, etc.)

litre.def: a unit for measuring volume, equal to 1.76 British pints or 2.11 American pints

litter.def: small pieces of rubbish such as paper, cans and bottles, that people have left lying in a public place
litter.ex: There will be fines for people who drop litter.

little.def: not big; small; smaller than others

little.def: used with uncountable nouns to mean ‘a small amount’, ‘some’

live.def: broadcast at the time of an actual event; played or recorded at an actual performance
live.ex: The show is going out live.

live.def: to have your home in a particular place
live.ex: to live in a house/a flat/an apartment

liver.def: a large organ in the body that cleans the blood and produces bile

living.def: money to buy the things that you need in life

load.def: something that is being carried (usually in large amounts) by a person, vehicle, etc.

load.ex: We loaded the car in ten minutes.

loan.def: money that an organization such as a bank lends and somebody borrows
loan.ex: to take out/repay a loan (= to borrow money/pay it back)

lobby.def: a large area inside the entrance of a public building where people can meet and wait
to try to influence a politician or the government and, for example, persuade them to support or oppose a change in the law
lobby.ex: Farmers will lobby Congress for higher subsidies.

local.def: belonging to or connected with the particular place or area that you are talking about or with the place where you live
a person who lives in a particular place or district

locate.def: to find the exact position of somebody/something
locate.ex: The mechanic located the fault immediately.

located.def: if something is located in a particular place, it exists there or has been put there
located.ex: a small town located 30 miles south of Chicago

location.def: a place where something happens or exists; the position of something
location.ex: a honeymoon in a secret location

lock.def: a device that keeps a door, window, box, etc. shut, usually needing a key to open it
lock.ex: She turned the key in the lock.

lock.def: to fasten something with a lock; to be fastened with a lock

log.def: a thick piece of wood that is cut from or has fallen from a tree
to put information in an official record or write a record of events
log.ex: The police log all phone calls.

logic.def: a way of thinking or explaining something
logic.ex: I fail to see the logic behind his argument.

logical.ex: It was a logical conclusion from the child's point of view.

logo.def: a printed design or symbol that a company or an organization uses as its special sign
logo.ex: All over the world there are red and white paper cups bearing the company logo.

lonely.def: unhappy because you have no friends or people to talk to
lonely.ex: She lives alone and often feels lonely.

long.def: measuring or covering a great length or distance, or a greater length or distance than usual

long-standing.def: that has existed or lasted for a long time

long-term.def: lasting or having an effect over a long period of time
long-term.ex: Our long-term goal is to lower operating costs by 10 per cent.

long-term.def: over a long period of time

long-time.def: having been the particular thing mentioned for a long time

look.def: an act of looking at somebody/something
look.ex: look at somebody/something, Here, have a look at this.

look.def: to turn your eyes in a particular direction
look.ex: Look closely and tell me what you see.

loom.def: to appear as a large shape that is not clear, especially in a frightening way
loom.ex: A dark shape loomed up ahead of us.

loop.def: a shape like a curve or circle made by a line curving right round
loop.ex: The road went in a huge loop around the lake.

loose.def: not securely fixed where it should be; able to become separated from something

lord.def: (in the UK) a man of high rank in the nobility (= people of high social class), or somebody who has been given the title ‘lord’ as an honour

lorry.def: a large vehicle for carrying heavy loads by road

lose.def: to be unable to find something/somebody

loss.def: the state of no longer having something or as much of something; the process that leads to this
loss.ex: I want to report the loss of a package.

lost.def: unable to find your way; not knowing where you are
lost.ex: We always get lost in London.

lot.def: used with verbs to mean ‘a great amount’
lot.ex: I care a lot about you.

lot.def: a large number or amount of somebody/something

lottery.def: a way of raising money for a government, charity, etc. by selling tickets that have different numbers on them that people have chosen. Numbers are then chosen by chance and the people who have those numbers on their tickets win prizes.

loud.def: in a way that makes a lot of noise or can be easily heard
loud.ex: Do you have to play that music so loud?

loudly.def: in a way that makes a lot of noise
loudly.ex: She screamed as loudly as she could.

love.def: a very strong feeling of liking and caring for somebody/something, especially a member of your family or a friend
love.ex: She has earned the love and respect of many people.

love.def: to have very strong feelings of liking and caring for somebody

lovely.ex: She looked particularly lovely that night.

low.def: not high or tall; not far above the ground
in or into a low position, not far above the ground
a low level or point; a low figure
low.ex: The temperature reached a record low in London last night.

lower.def: to reduce something or to become less in value, quality, etc.
lower.ex: He lowered his voice to a whisper.

loyal.def: remaining constant in your support of somebody/something

loyalty.def: the quality of being constant in your support of somebody/something
loyalty.ex: Can I count on your loyalty?

luck.def: good things that happen to you by chance, not because of your own efforts or abilities
luck.ex: with (any) luck, With any luck, we'll be home before dark.

lucky.ex: His friend was killed and he knows he is lucky to be alive.

lunch.def: a meal eaten in the middle of the day

lung.def: either of the two organs in the chest that you use for breathing
lung.ex: Her father died of lung cancer.

luxury.def: expensive and of very high quality
the fact of enjoying special and expensive things, particularly food and drink, clothes and places

lyric.def: the words of a popular song
lyric.ex: music and lyrics by Rodgers and Hart

machine.def: a piece of equipment with many parts that work together to do a particular task. The power used to work a machine may be electricity, steam, gas, etc. or human power.
machine.ex: Machines have replaced human labour in many industries.

machinery.def: machines as a group, especially large ones

mad.def: very stupid; not at all sensible
mad.ex: You must be mad to risk it.

magazine.def: a type of large thin book with a paper cover that you can buy every week or month, containing articles, photographs, etc., often on a particular topic; a similar collection of articles, etc. that appears regularly online

magic.def: having or using special powers to make impossible things happen or seem to happen
the secret power of appearing to make impossible things happen by saying special words or doing special things

magistrate.def: an official who acts as a judge in the lowest courts of law
magistrate.ex: to come up before the magistrates

magnetic.def: behaving like a magnet; that can be attracted by a magnet

magnificent.def: extremely attractive and impressive; deserving praise
magnificent.ex: The Taj Mahal is a magnificent building.

magnitude.def: the great size or importance of something; the degree to which something is large or important
magnitude.ex: We did not realize the magnitude of the problem.

mail.def: the official system used for sending and delivering letters, packages, etc.
to send something to somebody using the postal system
mail.ex: Don't forget to mail that letter to your mother.

main.def: being the largest or most important of its kind
main.ex: Be careful crossing the main road.

mainland.def: the main area of land of a country or region, not including any islands near to it
mainland.ex: to/from the mainland, a boat to/from the mainland

mainly.def: more than anything else; also used to talk about the most important reason for something
mainly.ex: They eat mainly fruit and nuts.

mainstream.def: considered normal because it reflects what is done or accepted by most people
the ideas and opinions that are thought to be normal because they are shared by most people; the people whose ideas and opinions are most accepted
mainstream.ex: His radical views place him outside the mainstream of American politics.

maintain.def: to make something continue at the same level, standard, etc.
maintain.ex: to maintain law and order/standards/a balance/control

maintenance.def: the act of keeping something in good condition by checking or repairing it regularly
maintenance.ex: The school pays for heating and the maintenance of the buildings.

majority.def: the largest part of a group of people or things
majority.ex: The majority of people interviewed prefer TV to radio.

make.def: the name or type of a machine, piece of equipment, etc. that is made by a particular company
make.ex: What make of car does he drive?

make.def: to create or prepare something by combining materials or putting parts together
make.ex: make something, to make a table/dress/cake

make-up.def: coloured substances used on the skin to make yourself look more attractive or to change your appearance

making.def: the act or process of making or producing something

male.def: a male person, animal or plant
male.ex: The male of the species has a white tail.

mall.def: a large building or covered area that has many shops, restaurants, etc. inside it

manage.def: to control or be in charge of a business, a team, an organization, land, etc.

management.def: the activity of running and controlling a business or similar organization

manager.def: a person who is in charge of running a business, a shop or a similar organization or part of one

mandate.def: the authority to do something, given to a government or other organization by the people who vote for it in an election
mandate.ex: It is undemocratic to govern an area without an electoral mandate.

mandatory.ex: The offence carries a mandatory life sentence.

manifest.def: to show something clearly, especially a feeling, an attitude or a quality
manifest.ex: Social tensions were manifested in the recent political crisis.

manipulate.def: to control or influence somebody/something, often in a dishonest way so that they do not realize it
manipulate.ex: She uses her charm to manipulate people.

manipulation.def: behaviour that controls or influences somebody/something, often in a dishonest way so that they do not realize it
manipulation.ex: Advertising like this is a cynical manipulation of the elderly.

manner.def: behaviour that is considered to be polite in a particular society or culture

manufacture.def: to make goods in large quantities, using machines

manufacturing.def: the business or industry of producing goods in large quantities in factories, etc.
manufacturing.ex: Many jobs in manufacturing were lost during the recession.

manuscript.def: a copy of a book, piece of music, etc. before it has been printed

many.def: used with plural nouns and verbs, especially in negative sentences or in more formal English, to mean ‘a large number of’. Also used in questions to ask about the size of a number, and with ‘as’, ‘so’ and ‘too’.
many.ex: We don't have very many copies left.

map.def: a drawing or plan of the earth’s surface or part of it, showing countries, towns, rivers, etc.
to make a map of an area
map.ex: an unexplored region that has not yet been mapped

marathon.def: a long running race of about 42 kilometres or 26 miles

march.def: the 3rd month of the year, between February and April
an organized walk by many people from one place to another, in order to protest about something, or to express their opinions
to walk with stiff regular steps like a soldier
march.ex: Quick march! (= the order to start marching)

margin.def: the empty space at the side of a written or printed page

marginal.ex: a marginal improvement in weather conditions

marine.def: connected with the sea and the creatures and plants that live there

mark.def: a written or printed symbol that is used as a sign of something, for example the quality of something or who made or owns it
to write or draw a symbol, line, etc. on something in order to give information about it
mark.ex: The flood level is marked by a white line on the wall.

marker.def: an object or a sign that shows the position of something

market.def: an occasion when people buy and sell goods; the open area or building where they meet to do this
to advertise a product in a particular way in order to make people want it
market.ex: The company utilizes every media tool available to market its products.

marketing.def: the activity of presenting, advertising and selling a company’s products or services in the best possible way

marketplace.def: the activity of competing with other companies to buy and sell goods, services, etc.
marketplace.ex: Companies must be able to survive in the marketplace.

marriage.def: the legal relationship between two people who are married to each other

marry.def: to become the husband or wife of somebody; to get married to somebody

mask.def: something that you wear over your face to hide it, or to frighten or entertain other people

mass.def: affecting or involving a large number of people or things
mass.ex: The world faces the tremendous problem of mass unemployment.

mass.def: a large amount of a substance that does not have a definite shape or form
mass.ex: She saw a large dark mass in the water.

massacre.def: the killing of a large number of people especially in a cruel way
massacre.ex: the bloody massacre of innocent civilians

master.def: a man who has people working for him, often as servants in his home
master.ex: They lived in fear of their master.

master.def: to learn or understand something completely
master.ex: She never completely mastered the art of lip-reading.

match.def: a sports event where people or teams compete against each other
to find somebody/something that goes together with or is connected with another person or thing

matching.def: having the same colour, pattern, style, etc. and therefore looking attractive together
matching.ex: The two sisters wore matching outfits.

mate.ex: They've been best mates since school.

mate.def: to have sex in order to produce young
mate.ex: Do foxes ever mate with dogs?

material.def: connected with money, possessions, etc. rather than with the needs of the mind or spirit
a substance that things can be made from
material.ex: building materials (= bricks, sand, glass, etc.)

mathematics.def: the science of numbers and shapes. Branches of mathematics include arithmetic, algebra, geometry and trigonometry.

maths.def: mathematics, especially as a subject in school
maths.ex: The core subjects are English, maths and science.

matter.def: used (to ask) if somebody is upset, unhappy, etc. or if there is a problem
matter.ex: What's the matter? Is there something wrong?

matter.def: to be important or have an important effect on somebody/something
matter.ex: ‘I'm afraid I forgot that book again.’ ‘It doesn't matter (= it is not important enough to worry about).’

mature.def: behaving in a sensible way, like an adult
mature.ex: Jane is very mature for her age.

mature.def: to become fully grown or developed
mature.ex: This particular breed of cattle matures early.

maximize.def: to increase something as much as possible

maximum.def: as large, fast, etc. as is possible, or the most that is possible or allowed
the greatest amount, size, speed, etc. that is possible, recorded or allowed
maximum.ex: a maximum of 30 children in a class

may.def: the fifth month of the year, between April and June
used to say that something is possible
may.ex: That may or may not be true.

maybe.def: used when you are not certain that something will happen or that something is true or is a correct number
maybe.ex: Maybe he'll come, maybe he won't.

mayor.def: the head of the government of a town or city, etc., elected by the public

me.def: the form of I that is used when the speaker or writer is the object of a verb or preposition, or after the verb be

meal.def: an occasion when people eat food, especially breakfast, lunch or dinner
meal.ex: Try not to eat between meals.

mean.def: to have something as a meaning in the same or another language

meaning.def: the thing or idea that a sound, word, sign, etc. represents
meaning.ex: What's the meaning of this word?

means.def: an action, an object or a system by which a result is achieved; a way of achieving or doing something
means.ex: Email is a highly effective means of communication.

meantime.def: for a short period of time but not permanently
meantime.ex: I'm changing my email address but for the meantime you can use the old one.

meanwhile.ex: Leave the cake to cool completely. Meanwhile, make the topping.

measure.def: an official action that is done in order to achieve a particular aim
to find the size, quantity, etc. of something in standard units
measure.ex: measure something/somebody, a device that measures the level of radiation in the atmosphere

measurement.def: the act or the process of finding the size, quantity or degree of something

meat.def: the soft part of an animal or a bird that can be eaten as food; a particular type of this

mechanic.def: a person whose job is repairing machines, especially the engines of vehicles

mechanical.def: operated by power from an engine

mechanism.def: a set of moving parts in a machine that performs a task

medal.def: a flat piece of metal, usually like a coin in shape, that is given to the winner of a competition or to somebody who has been brave, for example in war
medal.ex: to win a gold medal in the Olympics

media.def: the main ways that large numbers of people receive information and entertainment, that is television, radio, newspapers and the internet

medical.def: connected with illness and injury and their treatment

medication.def: a drug or another form of medicine that you take to prevent or to treat an illness; treatment involving drugs
medication.ex: Are you currently taking any medication?

medicine.def: the study and treatment of diseases and injuries

medieval.def: connected with the Middle Ages (about AD 1000 to AD 1450)

meditation.def: the practice of focusing your mind in silence, especially for religious reasons or in order to make your mind calm
meditation.ex: She found peace through yoga and meditation.

medium.def: in the middle between a larger and smaller size, amount, length, temperature, etc.
medium.ex: There are three sizes—small, medium and large.

medium.def: a way of communicating information, etc. to people

meet.def: to be in the same place as somebody by chance and talk to them
meet.ex: Maybe we'll meet again some time.

meeting.def: an occasion when people come together to discuss or decide something
meeting.ex: A hundred people attended the public meeting.

melody.def: a tune, especially the main tune in a piece of music written for several instruments or voices

melt.def: to become or make something become liquid as a result of heating
melt.ex: The snow showed no sign of melting.

member.def: a person, an animal or a plant that belongs to a particular group
member.ex: member of something, a member of the family/community

membership.def: the state of being a member of a group, a club, an organization, etc.
membership.ex: Who is eligible to apply for membership of the association?

memo.def: an official note from one person to another in the same organization

memoir.def: an account written by somebody, especially somebody famous, about their life and experiences

memorable.def: worth remembering or easy to remember, especially because of being special or unusual
memorable.ex: The holiday provided many memorable moments.

memorial.def: a statue, stone, etc. that is built in order to remind people of an important past event or of a famous person who has died
memorial.ex: a war memorial (= in memory of soldiers who died in a war)

memory.ex: I have a bad memory for names.

mental.def: connected with or happening in the mind; involving the process of thinking

mention.def: an act of referring to somebody/something in speech or writing
mention.ex: He made no mention of her work.

mention.def: to write or speak about something/somebody, especially without giving much information

mentor.def: an experienced person who advises and helps somebody with less experience over a period of time
mentor.ex: She was a friend and mentor to many young actors.

menu.def: a list of the food that is available at a restaurant or to be served at a meal

merchant.def: a person who buys and sells goods in large quantities, especially one who imports and exports goods

mercy.def: a kind or forgiving attitude towards somebody that you have the power to harm or right to punish

mere.def: used when you want to emphasize how small, unimportant, etc. somebody/something is
mere.ex: It took her a mere 20 minutes to win.

merely.def: used meaning ‘only’ or ‘simply’ to emphasize a fact or something that you are saying
merely.ex: It is not merely a job, but a way of life.

merge.def: to combine or make two or more things combine to form a single thing
merge.ex: The banks are set to merge next year.

merger.def: the act of joining two or more organizations or businesses into one
merger.ex: If the merger goes through, thousands of jobs will be lost.

merit.def: the quality of being good and of deserving praise or reward
merit.ex: a work of outstanding artistic merit

mess.ex: The room was in a mess.

message.def: a written or spoken piece of information, etc. that you send to somebody or leave for somebody when you cannot speak to them yourself
message.ex: There were no messages for me at the hotel.

metal.def: a type of solid mineral substance that is usually hard and shiny and that heat and electricity can travel through, for example tin, iron and gold

metaphor.def: a word or phrase used to describe somebody/something else, in a way that is different from its normal use, in order to show that the two things have the same qualities and to make the description more powerful, for example She has a heart of stone; the use of such words and phrases
metaphor.ex: a game of football used as a metaphor for the competitive struggle of life

method.def: a particular way of doing something
method.ex: Which method is the most effective?

methodology.def: a set of methods and principles used to perform a particular activity
methodology.ex: recent changes in the methodology of language teaching

metre.def: a unit for measuring length; a hundred centimetres

middle.def: in a position in the middle of an object, group of objects, people, etc.; between the beginning and the end of something
middle.ex: Pens are kept in the middle drawer.

middle.def: the part of something that is at an equal distance from all its edges or sides; a point or a period of time between the beginning and the end of something
middle.ex: in the middle, a lake with an island in the middle

midnight.ex: She heard the clock strike midnight.

midst.ex: Such beauty was unexpected in the midst of the city.

might.def: used when showing that something is or was possible
might.ex: He might get there in time, but I can't be sure.

migration.def: the movement every year of large numbers of birds or animals from one place to another
migration.ex: the seasonal migration of blue whales

mild.ex: a mild form of the disease

mile.def: a unit for measuring distance equal to 1 609 metres or 1 760 yards

militant.def: using, or willing to use, force or strong pressure to achieve your aims, especially to achieve social or political change
a person who uses, or is willing to use, force or strong pressure to achieve their aims, especially to achieve social or political change
militant.ex: Student militants were fighting with the police.

military.def: connected with soldiers or the armed forces
military.ex: We may have to take military action.
The military was/were called in to deal with the riot.

militia.def: a group of people who are not professional soldiers but who have had military training and can act as an army
militia.ex: He said he would call out the state militia if the rebels did not surrender.

milk.def: the white liquid produced by cows, goats and some other animals as food for their young and used as a drink by humans

mill.def: a building fitted with equipment for grinding grain into flour; a machine for grinding grain
mill.ex: The old mill has been converted into apartments.

million.ex: a population of half a million

mind.def: the part of a person that makes them able to be aware of things, to think and to feel
to be upset, annoyed or worried by something
mind.ex: I don't mind the cold—it's the rain I don't like.

mine.def: a deep hole or holes under the ground where minerals such as coal, gold, etc. are dug
of or belonging to the person writing or speaking

miner.def: a person who works in a mine taking out coal, gold, diamonds, etc.
miner.ex: Rescuers are trying to save miners trapped underground after a gas explosion.

mineral.def: a substance that is naturally present in the earth and is not formed from animal or vegetable matter, for example gold and salt. Some minerals are also present in food and drink and in the human body and are essential for good health.
mineral.ex: a country rich in mineral resources

minimal.def: very small in size or amount; as small as possible
minimal.ex: The work was carried out at minimal cost.

minimize.def: to reduce something, especially something bad, to the lowest possible level
minimize.ex: Good hygiene helps to minimize the risk of infection.

minimum.def: the smallest that is possible or allowed; extremely small
the smallest or lowest amount that is possible, required or recorded
minimum.ex: The class needs a minimum of six students to continue.

mining.def: the process of getting coal and other minerals from under the ground; the industry involved in this

minister.def: a senior member of the government who is in charge of a government department or a branch of one

ministry.def: a government department that has a particular area of responsibility
ministry.ex: The Ministry of Defence has issued the following statement.

minor.def: not very large, important or serious
minor.ex: The new plan involves widening a minor road through the valley.

minority.def: the smaller part of a group; less than half of the people or things in a large group
minority.ex: Only a small minority of students is/are interested in politics these days.

minute.ex: minute amounts of chemicals in the water

minute.def: each of the 60 parts of an hour, that are equal to 60 seconds

miracle.def: an act or event that does not follow the laws of nature and is believed to be caused by God
miracle.ex: the miracle of rising from the grave

mirror.def: a piece of special flat glass that reflects images, so that you can see yourself when you look in it
mirror.ex: He looked at himself in the mirror.

miserable.ex: We were cold, wet and thoroughly miserable.

misery.ex: Fame brought her nothing but misery.

misleading.def: giving the wrong idea or impression and making you believe something that is not true

miss.def: to be or arrive too late for something
miss.ex: If I don't leave now I'll miss my plane.

missile.def: a weapon that is sent through the air and that explodes when it hits the thing that it is aimed at

missing.def: that cannot be found or that is not in its usual place; that has been removed, lost or destroyed
missing.ex: I never found the missing piece.

mission.def: that cannot exist or be done; not possible

mistake.def: an action or an opinion that is not correct, or that produces a result that you did not want
mistake.ex: It's easy to make a mistake.

mistake.def: to not understand or judge somebody/something correctly
mistake.ex: I admit that I mistook his intentions.

mix.def: a combination of different people or things
mix.ex: It's a school with a good social and ethnic mix of children.

mix.def: if two or more substances or things mix or you mix them, they combine, usually in a way that means they cannot easily be separated
mix.ex: Oil and water do not mix.

mixed.def: consisting of different qualities or elements

mixture.ex: She was a curious mixture, part grand lady, part wild child.

mob.def: a large crowd of people, especially one that may become violent or cause trouble

mobile.def: connected with mobile phones, tablets, etc.

mobility.def: the ability to move easily from one place, social class or job to another

mobilize.def: to work together in order to achieve a particular aim; to organize a group of people to do this
mobilize.ex: The unions mobilized thousands of workers in a protest against the cuts.

mode.def: a particular way of doing something; a particular type of something

model.def: a copy of something, usually smaller than the original object
model.ex: a working model (= one in which the parts move) of a fire engine

model.def: to create a copy or description of an activity, a situation, etc. so that you can study it before dealing with the real thing
model.ex: The program can model a typical home page for you.

moderate.def: that is neither very good, large, hot, etc. nor very bad, small, cold, etc.

modern.def: of the present time or recent times

modest.def: not very large, expensive, important, etc.

modification.def: the act or process of changing something in order to improve it or make it more acceptable; a change that is made
modification.ex: Considerable modification of the existing system is needed.

modify.def: to change something slightly, especially in order to make it more suitable for a particular purpose
modify.ex: Patients are taught how to modify their diet.

moment.def: a very short period of time
moment.ex: Could you wait a moment, please?

momentum.def: the ability to keep increasing or developing
momentum.ex: The fight for his release gathers momentum each day.

monday.def: the day of the week after Sunday and before Tuesday, the first day of the working week

money.def: what you earn by working or selling things, and use to buy things

monitor.def: a screen that shows information from a computer; a television screen used to show particular kinds of information
monitor.ex: The details of today's flights are displayed on the monitor.

monitor.def: to watch and check something over a period of time in order to see how it develops, so that you can make any necessary changes
monitor.ex: Each student's progress is closely monitored.

monk.def: a member of a religious group of men who often live apart from other people in a monastery and who do not marry or have personal possessions

monkey.def: an animal with a long tail, that climbs trees and lives in hot countries. There are several types of monkey and they are related to apes and humans.
monkey.ex: Like humans, apes and monkeys live in complex social groupings.

monopoly.def: the complete control of trade in particular goods or the supply of a particular service; a type of goods or a service that is controlled in this way
monopoly.ex: In the past central government had a monopoly on television broadcasting.

monster.def: (in stories) an imaginary creature that is very large, ugly and frightening

month.def: any of the twelve periods of time into which the year is divided, for example May or June
month.ex: The months of July and August are the hottest.

monthly.def: happening once a month or every month

monument.def: a building, column, statue, etc. built to remind people of a famous person or event
monument.ex: A monument to him was erected in St Paul's Cathedral.

mood.def: the way you are feeling at a particular time
mood.ex: She's in a good mood today (= happy and friendly).

moon.def: the round object that moves around the earth once every 27½ days and shines at night by light reflected from the sun

moral.def: connected with principles of right and wrong behaviour
standards or principles of good behaviour, especially in matters of sexual relationships
moral.ex: Young people these days have no morals.

morality.def: principles relating to right and wrong or good and bad behaviour

more.def: used to form the comparative of most adjectives and adverbs with two or more syllables
more.ex: She was far more intelligent than her sister.

more.def: a larger number or amount of

used with plural nouns and verbs, especially in negative sentences or in more formal English, to mean ‘a large number of’. Also used in questions to ask about the size of a number, and with ‘as’, ‘so’ and ‘too’.
more.ex: We don't have very many copies left.

moreover.def: used to introduce some new information that adds to or supports what you have said previously
moreover.ex: A talented artist, he was, moreover, a writer of some note.

morning.def: the early part of the day from the time when people wake up until 12 o'clock in the middle of the day or before lunch
morning.ex: They left for Spain early this morning.

mortgage.def: a legal agreement by which a bank or similar organization lends you money to buy a house, etc., and you pay the money back over a particular number of years; the sum of money that you borrow
mortgage.ex: to apply for/take out/pay off a mortgage

mosque.def: a building where Muslims go to worship (= praise God)
mosque.ex: I used to pray at the local mosque with my father and grandfather.

most.def: used to form the superlative of most adjectives and adverbs of two or more syllables
the largest in number or amount
most.ex: Who do you think will get (the) most votes?

most.def: used with plural nouns and verbs, especially in negative sentences or in more formal English, to mean ‘a large number of’. Also used in questions to ask about the size of a number, and with ‘as’, ‘so’ and ‘too’.
most.ex: We don't have very many copies left.

mother.def: a female parent of a child or animal; a person who is acting as a mother to a child
mother.ex: I want to buy a present for my mother and father.

motion.def: the act or process of moving or the way something moves
motion.ex: What was Newton's first law of motion?

motivate.def: to be the reason why somebody does something or behaves in a particular way
motivate.ex: What motivates people to carry out such attacks?

motivation.def: the reason why somebody does something or behaves in a particular way
motivation.ex: What is the motivation behind this sudden change?

motive.ex: There seemed to be no motive for the murder.

motor.def: having an engine; using the power of an engine
motor.ex: The street is closed to motor vehicles.

motor.def: a device that uses electricity, petrol, etc. to produce movement and makes a machine, a vehicle, a boat, etc. work
motor.ex: An electric motor is used to pump the water.

motorcycle.def: a road vehicle with two wheels, driven by an engine, with one seat for the driver and often a seat for a passenger behind the driver
motorcycle.ex: He never learned to ride a motorcycle or to drive a car.

motorist.ex: The accident was reported by a passing motorist.

mount.ex: Residents mounted a campaign to fight the plans.

mountain.def: a very high hill, often with rocks near the top

mouse.def: a small animal that is covered in fur and has a long thin tail. Mice live in fields, in people’s houses or where food is stored.

mouth.def: the opening in the face used for speaking, eating, etc.; the area inside the head behind this opening
mouth.ex: She opened her mouth to say something.

move.def: a change of place or position
to change position or make somebody/something change position in a way that can be seen, heard or felt

movement.def: an act of moving the body or part of the body

movie.def: a series of moving pictures recorded with sound that tells a story, watched at a movie theater or on a television or other device

moving.def: causing strong, often sad, feelings about somebody/something

much.ex: Thank you very much for the flowers.

much.def: used with uncountable nouns, especially in negative sentences to mean ‘a large amount of something’, or after ‘how’ to ask about the amount of something. It is also used with ‘as’, ‘so’ and ‘too’.
much.ex: I don't have much money with me.

much.def: used with plural nouns and verbs, especially in negative sentences or in more formal English, to mean ‘a large number of’. Also used in questions to ask about the size of a number, and with ‘as’, ‘so’ and ‘too’.
much.ex: We don't have very many copies left.

mud.def: wet earth that is soft and sticky
mud.ex: The car wheels got stuck in the mud.

multiple.def: many in number; involving many different people or things
multiple.ex: The shape appears multiple times within each painting.

multiply.def: to add a number to itself a particular number of times
multiply.ex: The children are already learning to multiply and divide.

municipal.def: connected with or belonging to a town, city or district that has its own local government

murder.def: the crime of killing somebody deliberately
murder.ex: He was found guilty of murder.

murder.def: to kill somebody deliberately and illegally
murder.ex: He denies murdering his wife's lover.

muscle.def: a piece of body tissue that you make tight and relax in order to move a particular part of the body; the tissue that forms the muscles of the body

museum.def: a building in which objects of artistic, cultural, historical or scientific interest are kept and shown to the public

music.def: sounds that are arranged in a way that is pleasant or exciting to listen to. People sing music or play it on instruments.
music.ex: I like any kind of pop or dance music.

musical.def: a play or film in which part or all of the story is told using songs and often dancing

musician.def: a person who plays a musical instrument or writes music, especially as a job

must.def: used to say that something is necessary or very important (sometimes involving a rule or a law)
must.ex: All visitors must report to reception.

mutual.def: used to describe feelings that two or more people have for each other equally, or actions that affect two or more people equally

my.def: of or belonging to the speaker or writer

myself.def: used when the speaker or writer is also the person affected by an action
myself.ex: I cut myself on a knife.

mysterious.def: difficult to understand or explain; strange

mystery.def: something that is difficult to understand or to explain
mystery.ex: It is one of the great unsolved mysteries of this century.

myth.def: a story from ancient times, especially one that was told to explain natural events or to describe the early history of a people; this type of story

nail.def: the thin hard layer covering the outer tip of the fingers or toes

naked.ex: She was clutching the sheet around her naked body.

name.def: a word or words that a particular person, animal, place or thing is known by
to give a name to somebody/something
name.ex: He was named after his father (= given his father's first name).

namely.def: used to introduce more exact and detailed information about something that you have just mentioned
namely.ex: We need to concentrate on our target audience, namely women aged between 20 and 30.

narrative.def: describing events or telling a story

narrative.ex: a gripping narrative of their journey up the Amazon

narrow.def: measuring a short distance from one side to the other, especially in relation to length
narrow.ex: Stray dogs wander the steep narrow lanes of the old town.

narrow.def: to become or make something less wide
narrow.ex: This is where the river narrows.

nation.def: a country considered as a group of people with the same language, culture and history, who live in a particular area under one government

national.def: connected with a particular nation; shared by a whole nation
national.ex: Decide whether it would be better to advertise in a national or a local newspaper.

national.def: a citizen of a particular country

nationwide.def: happening or existing in all parts of a particular country

native.def: connected with the place where you were born and lived for the first years of your life
a person who was born in a particular country or area

natural.def: existing in nature; not made or caused by humans
natural.ex: the natural world (= of trees, rivers, animals and birds)

naturally.def: in a way that you would expect
naturally.ex: I get upset when things go wrong.

nature.def: all the plants, animals and things that exist in the universe that are not made by people
nature.ex: Take time to appreciate the beauties of nature.

naval.def: connected with the navy of a country

navigation.def: the skill or the process of planning a route for a ship or other vehicle and taking it there
navigation.ex: a maker of in-car navigation systems

near.def: at a short distance away from somebody/something

nearby.def: near in position; not far away
nearby.ex: Her mother lived in a nearby town.

nearby.def: a short distance from somebody/something; not far away

neat.def: tidy and in order; carefully done or arranged
neat.ex: She kept her desk extremely neat.

necessarily.def: used to say that something cannot be avoided
necessarily.ex: The number of places available is necessarily limited.

necessary.def: that is needed for a purpose or a reason
necessary.ex: It may be necessary to buy a new one.

necessity.def: the fact that something must happen or be done; the need for something
necessity.ex: We recognize the necessity for a written agreement.

neck.def: the part of the body between the head and the shoulders
neck.ex: He tied a scarf around his neck.

need.def: a situation when something is necessary or must be done
to require something/somebody because they are essential or very important, not just because you would like to have them
used to state that something is/was not necessary or that only very little is/was necessary; used to ask if something is/was necessary
need.ex: You needn't bother asking Rick—I know he's too busy.

needle.def: a small thin piece of steel that you use for sewing, with a point at one end and a hole for the thread at the other

negative.ex: The crisis had a negative effect on trade.

negative.def: a word or statement that means ‘no’; an act of refusing to do something or of denying something
negative.ex: She answered in the negative (= said ‘no’).

neglect.def: the fact of not giving enough care or attention to something/somebody; the state of not receiving enough care or attention
neglect.ex: The buildings are crumbling from years of neglect.

neglect.def: to fail to take care of somebody/something

negotiate.def: to try to reach an agreement by formal discussion
negotiate.ex: The government will not negotiate with terrorists.

negotiation.def: formal discussion between people who are trying to reach an agreement

neighbour.def: a person who lives next to you or near you
neighbour.ex: We've had a lot of support from all our friends and neighbours.

neighbourhood.def: a district or an area of a town; the people who live there
neighbourhood.ex: We grew up in the same neighbourhood.

neighbouring.def: located or living near or next to a place or person

neither.def: used to show that a negative statement is also true of somebody/something else
neither.ex: He didn't remember and neither did I.

neither.def: not one nor the other of two things or people

nerve.def: any of the long fibres that carry messages between the brain and parts of the body, enabling you to move, feel pain, etc.

nervous.def: anxious about something or afraid of something
nervous.ex: I felt really nervous before the interview.

nest.def: a hollow place or structure that a bird makes or chooses for laying its eggs in and sheltering its young
nest.ex: sparrows building a nest of twigs and dry grass

net.def: a net amount of money is the amount that remains when nothing more is to be taken away
material that is made of string, thread or wire twisted or tied together, with small spaces in between; a piece of this material used for a particular purpose
net.ex: Unfortunately the animals are often caught in fishing nets.

network.def: a complicated system of roads, lines, tubes, nerves, etc. that are connected to each other and operate together

neutral.def: not supporting or helping either side in a disagreement, competition, etc.
neutral.ex: Journalists are supposed to be politically neutral.

never.def: not at any time; not on any occasion

nevertheless.def: despite something that you have just mentioned
nevertheless.ex: There is little chance that we will succeed in changing the law. Nevertheless, it is important that we try.

new.def: not existing before; recently made, invented, introduced, etc.
new.ex: Have you read her new novel?

news.def: new information about something that has happened recently

newsletter.def: a report containing news of the activities of a club or organization that is sent regularly to all its members
newsletter.ex: Our sailing club produces a monthly newsletter.

newspaper.def: a set of large printed sheets of paper, or a website, containing news, articles, advertisements, etc. and published every day or every week; the organization responsible for producing this

next.def: coming straight after somebody/something in time, order or space
next.ex: The next train to Baltimore is at ten.

next.def: a person or thing that is next
next.ex: One moment he wasn't there, the next he was.

next to.def: in or into a position right by somebody/something
next to.ex: We sat next to each other.

niche.def: a comfortable or suitable role, job, way of life, etc.
niche.ex: He eventually found his niche in sports journalism.

night.def: the time between one day and the next when it is dark, when people usually sleep
night.ex: These animals only come out at night.

nightmare.def: a dream that is very frightening or unpleasant
nightmare.ex: He still has nightmares about the accident.

nine.ex: There are only nine of these rare animals left.

ninety.ex: The temperature must be in the nineties today.

no.def: not one; not any; not a
no.ex: No student is to leave the room.

no.def: used to give a negative reply or statement

noble.def: having or showing fine personal qualities that people admire, such as courage, honesty and care for others

nod.def: if you nod, nod your head or your head nods, you move your head up and down to show agreement, understanding, etc.
nod.ex: I asked him if he would help me and he nodded.

noise.def: a sound, especially when it is loud, unpleasant or frightening
noise.ex: There was a rattling noise coming from the back of the car.

nominate.def: to formally suggest that somebody/something should be chosen for an important role, prize, position, etc.
nominate.ex: She has been nominated for the presidency.

nomination.def: the act of suggesting or choosing somebody as a candidate in an election, or for a job or an award; the fact of being suggested for this
nomination.ex: Membership of the club is by nomination only.

nominee.def: a person who has been formally suggested for a job, a prize, etc.
nominee.ex: He was chosen as the party's presidential nominee.

non-profit.def: without the aim of making a profit

none.def: not one of a group of people or things; not any

nonetheless.ex: The book is too long but, nonetheless, informative and entertaining.

nonsense.def: ideas, statements or beliefs that you think are silly or not true
nonsense.ex: Reports that he has resigned are nonsense.

noon.def: 12 o’clock in the middle of the day
noon.ex: We should be there by noon.

nor.ex: She seemed neither surprised nor worried.

norm.def: a situation or a pattern of behaviour that is usual or expected
norm.ex: The new design is a departure from the norm.

normal.def: typical, usual or ordinary; what you would expect
the usual or average state, level or standard
normal.ex: The rainfall has been above normal for the time of year.

normally.ex: I would never normally discuss this.

north.def: the direction that is on your left when you watch the sun rise; one of the four main points of the compass

northern.def: located in the north or facing north; connected with or typical of the north part of the world or a region
northern.ex: the northern slopes of the mountains

nose.def: the part of the face that sticks out above the mouth, used for breathing and smelling things
nose.ex: She had dark eyes and a long narrow nose.

not.def: used with be, do or have to form the negative of verbs; used to form the negative of modal verbs like can or must

notable.def: deserving to be noticed or to receive attention; important

notably.def: used for giving a good or the most important example of something
notably.ex: The house had many drawbacks, most notably its price.

note.def: a short piece of writing to help you remember something
note.ex: Please make a note of the dates.

note.def: to notice or pay careful attention to something
