note.ex: Note the fine early Baroque altar inside the chapel.

notebook.def: a small book of plain paper for writing notes in
notebook.ex: The police officer wrote the details down in his notebook.

nothing.ex: There was nothing in her bag.

notice.def: a sheet of paper giving written or printed information, usually put in a public place
notice.ex: There was a notice on the board saying the class had been cancelled.

notice.def: to see or hear somebody/something; to become aware of somebody/something
notice.ex: People were making fun of him but he didn't seem to notice.

notify.def: to formally or officially tell somebody about something
notify.ex: Competition winners will be notified by post.

notion.def: an idea, a belief or an understanding of something
notion.ex: notion of something, a political system based on the notions of equality and liberty

novel.def: different from anything known before; new, interesting and often seeming slightly strange
a story long enough to fill a complete book, in which the characters and events are usually imaginary

november.def: the 11th month of the year, between October and December

now.def: because the thing mentioned is happening or has just happened
now.ex: Now that the kids have left home we've got a lot of extra space.

nowadays.def: at the present time, in contrast with the past
nowadays.ex: Nowadays most kids prefer going online to reading books.

nowhere.def: not in or to any place
nowhere.ex: I had nowhere to live, so I was sleeping on my sister's couch.

nuclear.def: using, producing or resulting from nuclear energy

number.def: a word or symbol that represents an amount or a quantity
number.ex: Think of a number and multiply it by two.

number.def: to give a number to something as part of a series or list
number.ex: All the seats in the stadium are numbered.

numerous.ex: He has been late on numerous occasions.

nurse.def: a person whose job is to take care of sick or injured people, usually in a hospital

nursery.def: a place where young children are cared for while their parents are at work; a day nursery or nursery school
nursery.ex: Her youngest child is at nursery now.

nursing.def: the job or skill of caring for people who are sick or injured

nut.def: a small hard fruit with a very hard shell that grows on some trees
nut.ex: to crack a nut (= open it)

nutrition.def: the process by which living things receive the food necessary for them to grow and be healthy

o'clock.def: used with the numbers 1 to 12 when telling the time, to mean an exact hour
o'clock.ex: He left between five and six o'clock.

obesity.def: the quality or fact of being very fat, in a way that is not healthy
obesity.ex: Obesity can increase the risk of heart disease.

obey.def: to do what you are told or expected to do
obey.ex: obey something, to obey a command/an order/rules/the law

object.def: a thing that can be seen and touched, but is not alive
object.ex: everyday objects such as cups and saucers

object.def: to say that you disagree with or oppose something
object.ex: If nobody objects, we'll postpone the meeting till next week.

objection.def: a reason why you do not like or are opposed to something; a statement about this
objection.ex: I'd like to come too, if you have no objection.

objective.def: not influenced by personal feelings or opinions; considering only facts
objective.ex: an objective assessment of the situation

objective.def: something that you are trying to achieve

obligation.def: the state of being forced to do something because it is your duty, or because of a law, etc.
obligation.ex: You are under no obligation to buy anything.

oblige.def: to force somebody to do something, by law, because it is a duty, etc.
oblige.ex: Parents are obliged by law to send their children to school.

observation.def: the act of watching somebody/something carefully for a period of time, especially to learn something
observation.ex: Most information was collected by direct observation of the animals' behaviour.

observe.ex: Have you observed any changes lately?

observer.ex: According to observers, the plane exploded shortly after take-off.

obsess.def: to completely fill your mind so that you cannot think of anything else, in a way that is not reasonable or normal

obsession.def: the state in which a person’s mind is completely filled with thoughts of one particular thing or person in a way that is not reasonable or normal
obsession.ex: Her fear of flying is bordering on obsession.

obstacle.def: a situation, an event, etc. that makes it difficult for you to do or achieve something
obstacle.ex: So far, we have managed to overcome all the obstacles that have been placed in our path.

obtain.def: to get something, especially by making an effort

obvious.ex: I know you don't like her but try not to make it so obvious.

obviously.def: used when giving information that you expect other people to know already or agree with
obviously.ex: Obviously, we don't want to spend too much money.

occasion.def: a particular time when something happens
occasion.ex: on an occasion, on this/that occasion

occasional.def: happening or done sometimes but not often
occasional.ex: He works for us on an occasional basis.

occasionally.ex: We occasionally meet for a drink after work.

occupation.ex: Please state your name, age and occupation below.

occupy.def: to fill or use a space, an area or an amount of time
occupy.ex: The bed seemed to occupy most of the room.

occur.ex: When exactly did the incident occur?

ocean.def: the mass of salt water that covers most of the earth’s surface
ocean.ex: The ship was dredged from the depths of the ocean.

october.def: the 10th month of the year, between September and November

odds.def: the degree to which something is likely to happen
odds.ex: The odds are very much in our favour (= we are likely to succeed).

of.def: belonging to somebody; relating to somebody

off.def: used to say that something has been removed or become separated
off.ex: He's had his beard shaved off.

off.def: down or away from a place or at a distance in space or time

offend.def: to make somebody feel upset because of something you say or do that is rude or embarrassing
offend.ex: They'll be offended if you don't go to their wedding.

offender.def: a person who commits a crime

offensive.def: rude in a way that causes somebody to feel upset or annoyed because it shows a lack of respect

offer.def: an act of saying that you are willing to do something for somebody or give something to somebody
to say that you are willing to give something to somebody
offer.ex: Josie had offered her services as a guide.

offering.def: something that is produced for other people to use, watch, enjoy, etc.
offering.ex: the latest offering from the Canadian-born writer

office.def: a room, set of rooms or building where people work, usually sitting at desks
office.ex: The company is moving to new offices on the other side of town.

officer.def: a person who is in a position of authority in the armed forces

official.def: agreed to, said, done, etc. by somebody who is in a position of authority
a person who is in a position of authority in a large organization
official.ex: She is the government official in charge of the project.

offspring.def: a child of a particular person or couple
offspring.ex: the problems parents have with their teenage offspring

oh.def: used when you are reacting to something that has been said, especially if you did not know it before
oh.ex: ‘I saw Ben yesterday.’ ‘Oh yes, how is he?’

oil.def: a thick liquid that is found in rock underground
oil.ex: Several companies are drilling for oil in the region.

ok.def: safe and well; in a calm or happy state

old.ex: The baby was only a few hours old.

on.def: in or into a position covering, touching or forming part of a surface

once.def: on one occasion only; one time

once.ex: We didn't know how we would cope once the money had gone.

one.ex: Do you want one or two?

one.def: used to avoid repeating a noun, when you are referring to somebody/something that has already been mentioned, or that the person you are speaking to knows about
one.ex: I'd like an ice cream. Are you having one, too?

onion.def: a round vegetable with many layers inside each other and a brown, red or white skin. Onions have a strong smell and taste.

online.def: available on or done using the internet or other computer network
online.ex: Online shopping is both cheap and convenient.

online.def: onto the internet; using the internet or other computer network
online.ex: Many children would rather go online than watch television.

only.def: used to say that no other or others of the same group exist or are there

only.ex: There are only a limited number of tickets available.

onto.def: used with verbs to express movement on or to a particular place or position
onto.ex: Move the books onto the second shelf.

open.def: allowing things or people to go through; not closed or blocked
open.ex: A wasp flew in the open window.

open.def: to move a door, window, lid, etc. into a position that is no longer closed; to get into this position
open.ex: Mr Chen opened the car door for his wife.

opening.def: a space or hole that somebody/something can pass through
opening.ex: We could see the stars through an opening in the roof.

openly.def: without hiding any feelings, opinions or information
openly.ex: Can you talk openly about sex with your parents?

opera.def: a dramatic work in which all or most of the words are sung to music; works of this type as an art form or entertainment

operate.def: to work in a particular way
operate.ex: Most domestic freezers operate at below −18°C.

operation.def: the process of cutting open a part of a person’s body in order to remove or repair a damaged part
operation.ex: Will I need to have an operation?

operational.def: connected with the way in which a business, machine, system, etc. works

operator.def: a person who operates equipment or a machine

opinion.def: your feelings or thoughts about somebody/something, rather than a fact
opinion.ex: He has very strong political opinions.

opponent.def: a person that you are playing or fighting against in a game, competition, argument, etc.

opportunity.def: a time when a particular situation makes it possible to do or achieve something

oppose.def: to disagree strongly with somebody’s plan, policy, etc. and try to change it or prevent it from succeeding
oppose.ex: This party would bitterly oppose the re-introduction of the death penalty.

opposed.def: disagreeing strongly with something and trying to stop it
opposed.ex: He was strongly opposed to modernism in art.

opposite.def: on the other side of a particular area from somebody/something and usually facing them
opposite.ex: Answers are given on the opposite page.

opposite.def: a person or thing that is as different as possible from somebody/something else
on the other side of a particular area from somebody/something, and usually facing them
opposite.ex: I sat opposite him during the meal (= on the other side of the table).

opposition.def: the act of strongly disagreeing with somebody/something, especially with the aim of preventing something from happening

opt.def: to choose to take or not to take a particular course of action
opt.ex: After graduating she opted for a career in music.

optical.def: connected with the sense of sight or the relationship between light and sight

optimism.def: a feeling that good things will happen and that something will be successful

optimistic.def: expecting good things to happen or something to be successful; showing this feeling
optimistic.ex: We are now taking a more optimistic view.

option.def: something that you can choose to have or do; the freedom to choose what you do
option.ex: As I see it, we have two options…

or.ex: Is your sister older or younger than you?

oral.ex: a test of both oral and written French

orange.def: between red and yellow in colour
a round citrus fruit with thick skin of a colour between red and yellow and a lot of sweet juice

orchestra.def: a large group of people who play various musical instruments together, led by a conductor
orchestra.ex: She plays the flute in the school orchestra.

order.def: the way in which people or things are placed or arranged in relation to each other
order.ex: The names are listed in alphabetical order.

order.def: to ask for something to eat or drink in a restaurant, bar, etc.
order.ex: I ordered a coffee and a sandwich.

ordinary.def: not unusual or different in any way

organ.def: a part of the body that has a particular purpose, such as the heart or the brain; part of a plant with a particular purpose

organic.def: produced or practised without using artificial chemicals

organization.def: a group of people who form a business, club, etc. together in order to achieve a particular aim
organization.ex: He's the president of a large international organization.

organizational.def: connected with the way in which the different parts of something are arranged; connected with an organization

organize.def: to make all the arrangements for something to happen or be provided
organize.ex: He helped to organize various events.

organized.def: involving large numbers of people who work together to do something in a way that has been carefully planned

organizer.def: a person who makes the arrangements for something

orientation.def: a person’s basic beliefs or feelings about a particular subject

origin.def: the point from which something starts; the cause of something
origin.ex: origin of something, the origins of life on earth

original.def: existing at the beginning of a particular period, process or activity
original.ex: The room still has many of its original features.

original.def: a document, work of art, etc. produced for the first time, from which copies are later made
original.ex: This painting is a copy; the original is in Madrid.

originally.def: used to describe the situation that existed at the beginning of a particular period or activity, especially before something was changed
originally.ex: The school was originally very small.

originate.def: to happen or appear for the first time in a particular place or situation
originate.ex: The disease is thought to have originated in the tropics.

other.def: used to refer to people or things that are additional or different to people or things that have been mentioned or are known about
other.ex: Mr Harris and Mrs Bate and three other teachers were there.

otherwise.def: used to state what the result would be if something did not happen or if the situation were different
otherwise.ex: I couldn't have afforded the trip.

ought.def: used to say what is the right thing to do

our.def: belonging to us; connected with us

ours.def: the one or ones that belong to us
ours.ex: Their house is very similar to ours, but ours is bigger.

ourselves.def: the reflexive form of we; used when you and another person or other people together cause and are affected by an action
ourselves.ex: We shouldn't blame ourselves for what happened.

out.def: away from the inside of a place or thing
out.ex: She ran out into the corridor.

outbreak.def: the sudden start of something unpleasant, especially violence or a disease

outcome.def: the result or effect of an action or event
outcome.ex: The likely outcome is a compromise.

outdoor.def: used, happening or located outside rather than in a building

outdoors.def: outside, rather than in a building
outdoors.ex: The rain prevented them from eating outdoors.

outer.ex: the outer layers of the skin

outfit.def: a set of clothes that you wear together, especially for a particular occasion or purpose
outfit.ex: She was wearing an expensive new outfit.

outing.def: a trip that you go on for pleasure or education, usually with a group of people and lasting no more than one day

outlet.def: a shop or an organization that sells goods made by a particular company or of a particular type
outlet.ex: The business has 34 retail outlets in this state alone.

outline.def: a description of the main facts or points involved in something
outline.ex: This is a brief outline of the events.

outline.def: to give a description of the main facts or points involved in something
outline.ex: He outlined his plan to leave St. Petersburg.

outlook.def: the attitude to life and the world of a particular person, group or culture

output.def: the amount of something that a person, a machine or an organization produces
output.ex: Manufacturing output has increased by 8 per cent.

outrage.def: a strong feeling of shock and anger
outrage.ex: The judge's remarks caused public outrage.

outrage.def: to make somebody very shocked and angry
outrage.ex: He was outraged at the way he had been treated.

outside.def: of, on or facing the outer side
not in a room, building or container but on or to the outside of it
outside.ex: I'm seeing a patient—please wait outside.

outside.def: the outer side or surface of something
outside.ex: The outside of the house needs painting.

outside.def: on or to a place on the outside of something
outside.ex: You can park your car outside our house.

outsider.def: a person who is not accepted as a member of a society, group, etc.
outsider.ex: Here she felt she would always be an outsider.

oven.def: the part of a cooker that is like a box with a door on the front, in which food is cooked or heated
oven.ex: Take the cake out of the oven.

over.def: across a street, an open space, etc.
resting on the surface of somebody/something and partly or completely covering them/it
over.ex: She put a blanket over the sleeping child.

overall.def: including all the things or people that are involved in a particular situation; general
overall.ex: There will be winners in each of three age groups, and one overall winner.

overall.def: including everything or everyone; in total
overall.ex: The company will invest $1.6m overall in new equipment.

overcome.def: to succeed in dealing with or controlling a problem that has been preventing you from achieving something
overcome.ex: She overcame injury to win the Olympic gold medal.

overlook.def: to fail to see or notice something
overlook.ex: He seems to have overlooked one important fact.

overly.ex: I'm not overly fond of pasta.

overnight.ex: We stayed overnight in London after the theatre.

overseas.def: connected with foreign countries, especially those separated from your country by the sea or ocean
overseas.ex: The firm is expanding into overseas markets.

overseas.def: to or in a foreign country, especially those separated from your country by the sea or ocean

oversee.def: to watch somebody/something and make sure that a job or an activity is done correctly
oversee.ex: United Nations observers oversaw the elections.

overturn.def: if something overturns, or if somebody overturns it, it turns into a position in which the top of it is where the bottom of it normally is or on its side

overwhelm.def: to have such a strong emotional effect on somebody that it is difficult for them to resist or know how to react
overwhelm.ex: She was overwhelmed by feelings of guilt.

overwhelming.def: very great or very strong; so powerful that you cannot resist it or decide how to react
overwhelming.ex: The evidence against him was overwhelming.

owe.def: to have to pay somebody for something that you have already received or return money that you have borrowed

own.def: used to emphasize that something belongs to or is connected with somebody
to have something that belongs to you, especially because you have bought it
own.ex: Do you own your house or do you rent it?

oxygen.def: a chemical element. Oxygen is a gas that is present in air and water and is necessary for people, animals and plants to live.
oxygen.ex: The patient didn't seem to be getting enough oxygen.

pace.def: the speed at which somebody/something walks, runs or moves
pace.ex: at a… pace, to set off at a steady/gentle/leisurely pace

pace.def: to walk up and down in a small area many times, especially because you are feeling nervous or angry
pace.ex: She paced up and down outside the room.

pack.def: a container, usually made of paper, that holds a number of the same thing or an amount of something, ready to be sold
pack.ex: pack of something, a pack of cigarettes/gum

pack.def: to put clothes, etc. into a bag in preparation for a trip away from home

package.def: something that is wrapped in paper or put into a thick envelope so that it can be sent by mail, carried easily, or given as a present
package.ex: A large package has arrived for you.

package.def: to put something into a box, bag, etc. to be sold or transported

packet.def: a small container made of paper or card in which goods are packed for selling

pad.def: a thick piece of soft material that is used, for example, for cleaning or protecting something or for holding liquid
pad.ex: medicated cleansing pads for sensitive skin

page.def: one side or both sides of a sheet of paper in a book, magazine, etc.

pain.def: the feelings that you have in your body when you have been hurt or when you are ill

paint.def: a liquid that is put on surfaces to give them a particular colour; a layer of this liquid when it has dried on a surface
to cover a surface or object with paint
paint.ex: Paint the shed with weather-resistant paint.

painter.def: a person whose job is painting buildings, walls, etc.
painter.ex: He works as a painter and decorator.

painting.def: a picture that has been painted

pair.def: two things of the same type, especially when they are used or worn together

palace.def: the official home of a king, queen, president, etc.

pale.def: having skin that is very light in colour; having skin that has less colour than usual because of illness, a strong emotion, etc.

palm.def: a straight tree with a mass of long leaves at the top, growing in tropical countries. There are several types of palm tree, some of which produce fruit.

panel.def: a square or rectangular piece of wood, glass or metal that forms part of a larger surface such as a door or wall
panel.ex: One of the glass panels in the front door was cracked.

panic.def: a sudden feeling of great fear that cannot be controlled and prevents you from thinking clearly

paper.def: the thin material that you write and draw on and that is also used for wrapping and packing things

parade.def: a public celebration of a special day or event, usually with bands in the streets and decorated vehicles

paragraph.def: a section of a piece of writing, usually consisting of several sentences dealing with a single subject. The first sentence of a paragraph starts on a new line.

parallel.def: two or more lines that are parallel to each other are the same distance apart at every point
a person, a situation, an event, etc. that is very similar to another, especially one in a different place or time
parallel.ex: These ideas have parallels in Freud's thought too.

parameter.def: something that decides or limits the way in which something can be done

parent.ex: He's still living with his parents.

parental.def: connected with a parent or parents

parish.def: an area that has its own church and that a priest is responsible for

park.def: an area of public land in a town or a city where people go to walk, play and relax
to leave a vehicle that you are driving in a particular place for a period of time

parking.def: the act of stopping a vehicle at a place and leaving it there for a period of time
parking.ex: There is no parking here between 9 a.m. and 6 p.m.

parliament.def: the group of people who are elected to make and change the laws of a country
parliament.ex: The German parliament is called the ‘Bundestag’.

parliamentary.def: connected with a parliament; having a parliament

part.def: some but not all of a thing
part.ex: We spent part of the time in the museum.

part-time.def: for part of the day or week in which people work
part-time.ex: She's looking for a part-time job.

partial.ex: It was only a partial solution to the problem.

partially.ex: The road was partially blocked by a fallen tree.

participant.def: a person who is taking part in an activity or event
participant.ex: The average age of study participants was 48 years.

participate.def: to take part in or become involved in an activity
participate.ex: Anyone who wishes to participate is welcome.

participation.def: the act of taking part in an activity or event
participation.ex: a show with lots of audience participation

particular.def: used to emphasize that you are referring to one individual person, thing or type of thing and not others
particular.ex: There is one particular patient I'd like you to see.

particularly.def: especially; more than usual or more than others

partly.ex: Some people are unwilling to attend the classes partly because of the cost involved.

partner.def: a person that you are doing an activity with, such as dancing or playing a game

partnership.def: the state of being a partner in business
partnership.ex: to be in/to go into partnership

party.def: a social occasion, often in a person’s home, at which people eat, drink, talk, dance and enjoy themselves

pass.def: an official document or ticket that shows that you have the right to enter or leave a place, to travel on a bus or train, etc.
pass.ex: a boarding pass (= for a plane)

pass.def: to achieve the required standard in an exam, a test, etc.
pass.ex: I'm not really expecting to pass first time.

passage.def: a long narrow area with walls on either side that connects one room or place with another
passage.ex: A dark narrow passage led to the main hall.

passenger.def: a person who is travelling in a car, bus, train, plane or ship and who is not driving it or working on it

passing.def: the process of time going by
passing.ex: The colour of the wood darkens with the passing of time.

passion.def: a very strong feeling of love, hate, anger, enthusiasm, etc.
passion.ex: I admire the passion and commitment shown by the players.

passionate.def: having or showing strong feelings of sexual love or of anger, etc.

passive.def: accepting what happens or what people do without trying to change anything or oppose them
passive.ex: He played a passive role in the relationship.

passport.def: an official document that identifies you as a citizen of a particular country, and that you may have to show when you enter or leave a country

password.def: a series of letters, numbers, etc. that you must type into a computer or computer system in order to be able to use it
password.ex: Enter a username and password to get into the system.

past.def: from one side of something to the other
past.ex: She smiled at me as she walked past.

past.def: the time that has gone by; things that happened in an earlier time
past.ex: I used to go there often in the past.

pastor.def: a minister in charge of a Christian church or group, especially in some Nonconformist churches
pastor.ex: He was ordained a pastor in the Lutheran Church.

patch.def: a small area of something, especially one which is different from the area around it
patch.ex: a black dog with a white patch on its back

patent.def: an official right to be the only person to make, use or sell a product or an invention; a document that proves this
patent.ex: patent on something, to apply for/obtain/take out a patent on an invention

path.def: a way or track that is built or is made by the action of people walking

pathway.def: a track that serves as a path
pathway.ex: They came out of the woods and onto a pathway.

patience.def: the ability to stay calm and accept a delay or something annoying without complaining
patience.ex: She has little patience with (= will not accept or consider) such views.

patient.def: able to wait for a long time or accept annoying behaviour or difficulties without becoming angry
patient.ex: You'll just have to be patient and wait till I'm finished.

patient.def: a person who is receiving medical treatment, especially in a hospital

patrol.def: the act of going to different parts of a building, an area, etc. to make sure that there is no trouble or crime
patrol.ex: Security guards make regular patrols at night.

patrol.def: to go around an area or a building at regular times to check that it is safe and that there is no trouble
patrol.ex: Troops patrolled the border day and night.

patron.def: a person who gives money and support to artists and writers
patron.ex: Frederick the Great was the patron of many artists.

pattern.def: the regular way in which something happens or is done

pause.def: a period of time during which somebody stops talking or stops what they are doing
pause.ex: There was a long pause before she answered.

pause.def: to stop talking or doing something for a short time before continuing
pause.ex: Anita paused for a moment, then said: ‘All right’.

pay.def: the money that somebody gets for doing regular work
pay.ex: Her job is hard work, but the pay is good.

pay.def: to give somebody money for work, goods, services, etc.

payment.def: the act of paying somebody/something or of being paid
payment.ex: What method of payment do you prefer?

peace.def: a situation or a period of time in which there is no war or violence in a country or an area

peaceful.def: not involving a war, violence or argument

peak.def: the point when somebody/something is best, most successful, strongest, etc.
peak.ex: Traffic reaches its peak between 8 and 9 in the morning.

peasant.def: (especially in the past, or in poorer countries) a farmer who owns or rents a small piece of land

peculiar.def: strange or unusual, especially in a way that is unpleasant or makes you worried

peer.def: a person who is the same age or who has the same social status as you
peer.ex: She enjoys the respect of her peers.

pen.def: an instrument made of plastic or metal used for writing with ink (= coloured liquid for writing, etc.)

penalty.def: a punishment for breaking a law, rule or contract

pencil.def: a narrow piece of wood, or a metal or plastic case, containing a black or coloured substance, used for drawing or writing
pencil.ex: I'll get a pencil and paper.

penny.def: a small British coin and unit of money. There are 100 pence in one pound (£1).
penny.ex: He had a few pennies in his pocket.

people.def: human beings; men, women and children
people.ex: At least ten people were killed in the crash.

pepper.def: a powder made from dried berries (called peppercorns), used to give a hot, spicy taste to food

per.def: used to express the cost or amount of something for each person, number used, distance travelled, etc.
per.ex: Rooms cost £50 per person, per night.

per cent.def: by, in or for every hundred
per cent.ex: a 15 per cent rise in price
Poor families spend about 80 to 90 per cent of their income on food.

perceive.def: to understand or think of somebody/something in a particular way
perceive.ex: This discovery was perceived as a major breakthrough.

percentage.def: the number, amount or rate of something, expressed as if it is part of a total that is 100; a part or share of a whole
percentage.ex: What percentage of the population is/are overweight?

perception.def: an idea, a belief or an image you have as a result of how you see or understand something
perception.ex: a campaign to change public perception of the police

perfect.def: having everything that is necessary; complete and without faults or weaknesses

perfectly.ex: It's perfectly normal to feel like this.

perform.def: to entertain an audience by playing a piece of music, acting in a play, etc.

performance.def: the act of performing a play, concert or some other form of entertainment

perhaps.ex: ‘Are you going to come?’ ‘Perhaps. I'll see how I feel.’

permanent.def: lasting for a long time or for all time in the future; existing all the time
permanent.ex: She was unable to find a permanent job.

permanently.def: in a way that lasts for a long time or for all time in the future; in a way that exists all the time
permanently.ex: The stroke left his right side permanently damaged.

permission.def: the act of allowing somebody to do something, especially when this is done by somebody in a position of authority
permission.ex: You must ask permission for all major expenditure.

permit.def: an official document that gives somebody the right to do something, especially for a limited period of time
to allow somebody to do something or to allow something to happen
permit.ex: Mobile phones are not permitted in the examination room.

persist.def: to continue to do something despite difficulties or opposition, in a way that can seem unreasonable
persist.ex: Why do you persist in blaming yourself for what happened?

persistent.def: determined to do something despite difficulties, especially when other people are against you and think that you are being annoying or unreasonable
persistent.ex: How do you deal with persistent salesmen who won't take no for an answer?

person.ex: What sort of person would do a thing like that?

personal.def: your own; not belonging to or connected with anyone else
personal.ex: The novel is written from personal experience.

personality.def: the various aspects of a person’s character that combine to make them different from other people
personality.ex: His wife has a strong personality.

personally.def: used to show that you are giving your own opinion about something

personnel.def: the people who work for an organization or one of the armed forces
personnel.ex: There is a severe shortage of skilled personnel.

perspective.def: a particular attitude towards something; a way of thinking about something

persuade.def: to make somebody do something by giving them good reasons for doing it
persuade.ex: Try to persuade him to come.

pet.def: an animal, a bird, etc. that you have at home for pleasure, rather than one that is kept for work or food

petition.def: a written document signed by a large number of people that asks somebody in a position of authority to do or change something
petition.ex: Would you like to sign our petition against experiments on animals?

petrol.def: a liquid obtained from petroleum, used as fuel in car engines, etc.
petrol.ex: to fill a car up with petrol

phase.def: a stage in a process of change or development

phenomenon.def: a fact or an event in nature or society, especially one that is not fully understood

philosopher.def: a person who studies or writes about philosophy
philosopher.ex: We studied the writings of the Greek philosopher Aristotle.

philosophy.def: the study of the nature and meaning of the universe and of human life
philosophy.ex: philosophy of something, the philosophy of science

phone.def: a piece of equipment for talking to people who are not in the same place as you
phone.ex: I have to make a phone call.

phone.def: to make a phone call to somebody
phone.ex: I was just phoning up for a chat.

photo.def: a picture that is made by using a camera that stores images in digital form or that has a film sensitive to light inside it

photograph.def: a picture that is made by using a camera that stores images in digital form or that has a film sensitive to light inside it
photograph.ex: aerial photographs of the crash site

photograph.def: to take a photograph of somebody/something
photograph.ex: photograph somebody/something, a workshop on photographing wildlife

photographer.def: a person who takes photographs, especially as a job

photography.def: the art, process or job of taking photographs or filming something
photography.ex: Her hobbies include hiking and photography.

phrase.def: a group of words that have a particular meaning when used together

physical.def: connected with a person’s body rather than their mind

physician.def: a doctor, especially one who is a specialist in general medicine and not surgery
physician.ex: Dr Dennett is a practicing family physician in Atlanta.

physics.def: the scientific study of matter and energy and the relationships between them, including the study of forces, heat, light, sound, electricity and the structure of atoms

piano.def: a large musical instrument played by pressing the black and white keys on the keyboard. The sound is produced by small hammers hitting the metal strings inside the piano.

pick.def: to choose somebody/something from a group of people or things
pick.ex: Pick a number from one to twenty.

picture.def: a painting or drawing, etc. that shows a scene, a person or thing
picture.ex: He likes books with lots of pictures in them.

picture.def: to imagine somebody/something; to create an image of somebody/something in your mind
picture.ex: I could picture the scene clearly.

piece.def: an amount of something that has been cut or separated from the rest of it; a standard amount of something
piece.ex: She wrote something on a small piece of paper.

pig.def: an animal with pink, black or brown skin, short legs, a broad nose and a short curly tail. Pigs are kept on farms for their meat (called pork) or live in the wild.

pile.def: a number of things that have been placed on top of each other
pile.ex: pile of something, a pile of clothes/paper

pile.def: to put things one on top of another; to form a pile
pile.ex: She piled the boxes one on top of the other.

pill.def: a small flat round piece of medicine that you swallow whole, without biting it

pilot.def: a person who operates the controls of an aircraft, especially as a job

pin.def: a short thin piece of stiff wire with a sharp point at one end and a round head at the other, used especially for fastening together pieces of cloth when sewing
pin.ex: Use pins to keep the patch in place while you sew it on.

pin.def: to attach something onto another thing or fasten things together with a pin, etc.
pin.ex: She pinned the badge onto her jacket.

pink.def: the colour that is produced when you mix red and white together

pioneer.def: a person who is the first to study and develop a particular area of knowledge, culture, etc. that other people then continue to develop
pioneer.ex: a pioneer in the field of microsurgery

pioneer.def: when somebody pioneers something, they are one of the first people to do, discover or use something new
pioneer.ex: a new technique pioneered by surgeons in a London hospital

pipe.def: a tube through which liquids and gases can flow
pipe.ex: Both hot and cold water pipes should be properly insulated.

pipeline.def: a series of pipes that are usually underground and are used for carrying oil, gas, etc. over long distances
pipeline.ex: There are plans to lay a gas pipeline through the region.

pirate.def: (especially in the past) a person on a ship who attacks other ships at sea in order to steal from them
pirate.ex: There were reports that a pirate ship had come looking for treasure in the cove.

pit.def: a large deep hole in the ground
pit.ex: We dug a deep pit in the yard.

pitch.def: an area of ground specially prepared and marked for playing a sports game

pity.def: used to show that you are disappointed about something
pity.ex: It's a pity that you can't stay longer.

place.def: a particular position, point or area
place.ex: Keep your purse in a safe place.

place.def: to put something in a particular place, especially when you do it carefully or deliberately
place.ex: He placed his hand on her shoulder.

placement.def: the act of finding somebody a suitable job or place to live

plan.def: something that you intend to do or achieve
plan.ex: Do you have any plans for the summer?

plan.def: to make detailed arrangements for something you want to do in the future
plan.ex: plan something, to plan a trip

plane.def: a flying vehicle with wings and one or more engines

planet.def: a large round object in space that moves around a star (such as the sun) and receives light from it
planet.ex: the planets of our solar system

planning.def: the act or process of making plans for something
planning.ex: The department is responsible for all financial planning.

plant.def: a living thing that grows in the earth and usually has a stem, leaves and roots, especially one that is smaller than a tree or bush
plant.ex: All plants need light and water.

plant.def: to put plants, seeds, etc. in the ground to grow

plastic.def: a light strong material that is produced by chemical processes and can be formed into shapes when heated. There are many different types of plastic, used to make different objects and fabrics.
plastic.ex: The pipes should be made of plastic.

plate.def: a flat, usually round, dish that you put food on

platform.def: the raised flat area next to the track at a train station where you get on or off the train
platform.ex: What platform does it go from?

play.def: a piece of writing performed by actors in a theatre or on television or radio
to do things for pleasure, as children do; to enjoy yourself, rather than work
play.ex: You'll have to play inside today.

player.def: a person who takes part in a game or sport

plea.def: a serious emotional request, especially for something needing action now
plea.ex: She made an impassioned plea for help.

plead.def: to ask somebody for something in a very strong and serious way
plead.ex: She pleaded with him not to go.

please.def: used as a polite way of asking for something or telling somebody to do something

pleasure.def: a state of feeling or being happy or satisfied
pleasure.ex: His eyes lit up with pleasure.

pledge.ex: pledge (of something), a pledge of support

pledge.def: to formally promise to give or do something
pledge.ex: Japan has pledged $100 million in humanitarian aid.

plenty.def: a large amount; as much or as many as you need

plot.def: the series of events that form the story of a novel, play, film, etc.
plot.ex: It's hard to follow the plot of the film.

plot.def: to make a secret plan to harm somebody, especially a government or its leader
plot.ex: It is claimed he plotted with the country's enemies.

plug.def: a small plastic object with two or three metal pins that connects a piece of electrical equipment to the main supply of electricity
plug.ex: The iron is fitted with a three-pin plug.

plug.def: to fill a hole with a substance or piece of material that fits tightly into it
plug.ex: He plugged the hole in the pipe with an old rag.

plunge.def: to move or make somebody/something move suddenly forwards and/or downwards
plunge.ex: She lost her balance and plunged 100 feet to her death.

plus.def: used after a number to show that the real number or amount is more than the one mentioned

plus.ex: I've got too much on at work. Plus my father is not well.
Knowledge of French is a plus in her job.

plus.def: used when the two numbers or amounts mentioned are being added together

pocket.def: a small piece of material like a small bag sewn into or onto a piece of clothing so that you can carry things in it

poem.def: a piece of writing in which the words are chosen for their sound and the images they suggest, not just for their obvious meanings. The words are arranged in separate lines, usually with a repeated rhythm, and often the lines rhyme at the end.
poem.ex: His collected poems were published after the war.

poetry.def: poems in general; poems as a genre of literature

point.def: a thing that somebody says or writes giving their opinion or stating a fact
point.ex: She made several interesting points in the article.

point.def: to stretch out your finger or something held in your hand towards somebody/something in order to show somebody where a person or thing is
point.ex: point at somebody/something, ‘What's your name?’ he asked, pointing at the child with his pen.

poison.def: a substance that causes death or harm if it gets into the body
poison.ex: Some mushrooms contain a deadly poison.

poison.def: to harm or kill a person or an animal by giving them poison
poison.ex: He was believed to poison his enemies.

poisonous.def: causing death or illness if swallowed or taken into the body

pole.def: a long thin straight piece of wood or metal, especially one with the end placed in the ground, used as a support

police.def: an official organization whose job is to make people obey the law and to prevent and solve crime; the people who work for this organization
police.ex: Get out of the house or I'll call the police.

policy.def: a plan of action agreed or chosen by a political party, a business, etc.
policy.ex: policy on something, the present government’s policy on education

polite.def: having or showing good manners and respect for the feelings of others
polite.ex: Please be polite to our guests.

political.def: connected with the state, government or public affairs

politician.def: a person whose job involves politics, especially as an elected member of parliament, etc.

politics.def: the activities involved in getting and using power in public life, and being able to influence decisions that affect a country or a society

poll.def: the process of questioning people who are representative of a larger group in order to get information about the general opinion

pollution.def: the process of making air, water, soil, etc. dirty; the state of being dirty

pond.def: a small area of still water, especially one that is artificial

pool.def: an area of water that has been created for people to swim in

poor.def: having very little money; not having enough money for basic needs
poor.ex: They were too poor to buy shoes for the kids.

pop.def: popular music of the sort that has been popular since the 1950s, usually with a strong rhythm and simple tunes, often contrasted with rock, soul and other forms of popular music
to make a short explosive sound; to cause something to make this sound

popular.def: liked or enjoyed by a large number of people
popular.ex: Andalucia is a popular tourist destination.

popularity.def: the state of being liked, enjoyed or supported by a large number of people

population.def: all the people who live in a particular area, city or country; the total number of people who live there
population.ex: One third of the world’s population consumes/consume two thirds of the world’s resources.

port.def: a town or city with a harbour, especially one where ships load and unload goods

portfolio.def: a thin flat case used for carrying documents, drawings, etc.
portfolio.ex: I left my portfolio on the train.

portion.ex: a substantial/significant portion of the population

portrait.def: a painting, drawing or photograph of a person, especially of the head and shoulders
portrait.ex: portrait of somebody, a portrait of his wife

portray.def: to show somebody/something in a picture; to describe somebody/something in a piece of writing
portray.ex: The painting portrays the duke’s third wife.

pose.def: to create a threat, problem, etc. that has to be dealt with

position.def: the place where somebody/something is located
position.ex: Where would be the best position for the lights?

position.def: to put somebody/something in a particular position
position.ex: She quickly positioned herself behind the desk.

positive.ex: The tests have so far yielded positive results.

positive.def: a good or useful quality or aspect
positive.ex: What are the positives and negatives of going this route?

possess.ex: He was charged with possessing a shotgun without a licence.

possession.def: something that you own or have with you at a particular time
possession.ex: Prisoners were allowed no personal possessions.

possibility.def: a thing that may happen or be true; the fact that something might happen or be true, but is not certain
possibility.ex: Bankruptcy is a real possibility if sales don't improve.

possible.def: that can be done or achieved
possible.ex: New technology has made it possible to communicate more easily.

possibly.def: used to say that something might exist, happen or be true, but you are not certain
possibly.ex: It was possibly their worst performance ever.

post.def: the official system used for sending and delivering letters, packages, etc.
post.ex: I'll send the original to you by post.

post.def: to send a letter, etc. to somebody by post
post.ex: The cost of posting a letter has gone up again.

post-war.def: existing, happening or made in the period after a war, especially the Second World War

poster.def: a large notice, often with a picture on it, that is put in a public place to advertise something

postpone.def: to arrange for an event, etc. to take place at a later time or date than originally planned
postpone.ex: The game has already been postponed three times.

pot.def: a deep round container used for cooking things in

potato.def: a round white vegetable with a brown or red skin that grows underground as part of a plant also called a potato
potato.ex: Will you peel the potatoes for me?

potential.def: that can develop into something or be developed in the future
the possibility of something happening or being developed or used
potential.ex: potential for something, the potential for change

potentially.def: used to say that something may develop into something

pound.def: the unit of money in the UK, worth 100 pence

pour.def: to make a liquid or other substance flow from a container in a continuous stream, especially by holding the container at an angle
pour.ex: Pour the sauce over the pasta.

powder.def: a dry mass of very small fine pieces or grains

power.def: the ability to control people or things
power.ex: He has the power to make things very unpleasant for us.

power.def: to supply a machine or vehicle with the energy that makes it work
power.ex: The aircraft is powered by a jet engine.

powerful.def: being able to control and influence people and events

practical.def: connected with real situations rather than with ideas or theories
practical.ex: to have gained practical experience of the work

practice.def: doing an activity or training regularly so that you can improve your skill; the time you spend doing this

practise.def: to do an activity or train regularly so that you can improve your skill
practise.ex: You need to practise every day.

practitioner.def: a person who works in a profession, especially medicine or law

praise.def: words that show that you approve of and admire somebody/something
praise.ex: The team coach singled out two players for special praise.

praise.def: to say that you approve of and admire somebody/something

pray.def: to speak to God, especially to give thanks or ask for help

prayer.def: words that you say to God giving thanks or asking for help

preach.def: to give a religious talk in a public place, especially in a church during a service
preach.ex: She preached to the congregation about forgiveness.

precede.def: to happen before something or come before something/somebody in order

precedent.def: an official action or decision that has happened in the past and that is seen as an example or a rule to be followed in a similar situation later
precedent.ex: The ruling set a precedent for future libel cases.

precious.def: rare and worth a lot of money

precisely.ex: They look precisely the same to me.

precision.def: the quality of being exact, accurate and careful
precision.ex: Her writing is imaginative but lacks precision.

predator.def: an animal that kills and eats other animals
predator.ex: Some animals have no natural predators.

predecessor.def: a person who did a job before somebody else
predecessor.ex: The new president reversed many of the policies of his predecessor.

predict.def: to say that something will happen in the future
predict.ex: predict something, a reliable method of predicting earthquakes

predictable.def: if something is predictable, you know in advance that it will happen or what it will be like

prediction.def: a statement that says what you think will happen; the act of making such a statement
prediction.ex: The results of the experiment confirmed our predictions.

predominantly.ex: She works in a predominantly male environment.

prefer.def: to like one thing or person better than another; to choose one thing rather than something else because you like it better
prefer.ex: prefer something, ‘Coffee or tea?’ ‘I'd prefer tea, thanks.’

preference.def: a greater interest in or desire for somebody/something than somebody/something else
preference.ex: It's a matter of personal preference.

pregnant.def: having a baby or young animal developing inside her/its body

prejudice.def: an unreasonable dislike of or preference for a person, group, custom, etc., especially when it is based on their race, religion, sex, etc.

preliminary.def: happening before a more important action or event
preliminary.ex: After a few preliminary remarks he announced the winners.

premier.ex: one of the country’s premier chefs

premise.def: a statement or an idea that forms the basis for a reasonable line of argument
premise.ex: the basic premise of her argument

premium.def: an amount of money that you pay once or regularly for an insurance policy

preparation.def: the act or process of getting ready for something or making something ready

prepare.def: to make something or somebody ready to be used or to do something
prepare.ex: prepare something/somebody, to prepare a report

prepared.def: ready and able to deal with something
prepared.ex: We'll be better prepared next time.

prescribe.def: to tell somebody to take a particular medicine or have a particular treatment; to write a prescription for a particular medicine, etc.
prescribe.ex: Valium is usually prescribed to treat anxiety.

prescription.def: an official piece of paper on which a doctor writes the type of medicine you should have, and which enables you to get it from a chemist's
prescription.ex: The doctor gave me a prescription for antibiotics.

presence.def: the fact of being in a particular place
presence.ex: He hardly seemed to notice my presence.

present.ex: I am not satisfied with the present situation.

present.def: a thing that you give to somebody as a gift
present.ex: What can I get him for a birthday present?

present.def: to show or offer something for other people to look at or consider
present.ex: Are you presenting a paper at the conference?

presentation.def: a meeting at which something, especially a new product or idea, or piece of work, is shown to a group of people
presentation.ex: The sales manager will give a presentation on the new products.

presently.def: at the time you are speaking or writing; now
presently.ex: The crime is presently being investigated by the police.

preservation.def: the act of keeping something in its original state or in good condition

preserve.def: to keep a particular quality, feature, etc.; to make sure that something is kept
preserve.ex: He was anxious to preserve his reputation.

preside.def: to lead or be in charge of a meeting, ceremony, etc.

presidency.def: the job of being president of a country or an organization; the period of time somebody holds this job
presidency.ex: the current holder of the EU presidency

president.def: the leader of a republic, for example the US

presidential.def: connected with the position or activities of a president

press.def: to push part of a device, etc. in order to make it work
press.ex: press something, to press a button/key

pressure.def: the act of trying to persuade or to force somebody to do something
pressure.ex: The pressure for change continued to mount.

prestigious.def: respected and admired as very important or of very high quality

presumably.def: used to say that you think that something is probably true
presumably.ex: Presumably this is where the accident happened.

presume.def: to suppose that something is true, although you do not have actual proof
presume.ex: They are very expensive, I presume?

pretend.def: to behave in a particular way, in order to make other people believe something that is not true
pretend.ex: I'm tired of having to pretend all the time.

prevail.def: to exist or be very common at a particular time or in a particular place
prevail.ex: We were horrified at the conditions prevailing in local prisons.

prevalence.def: the fact of existing or being very common at a particular time or in a particular place
prevalence.ex: an increase in the prevalence of smoking among young people

prevent.def: to stop somebody from doing something; to stop something from happening
prevent.ex: The accident could have been prevented.

prevention.def: the act of stopping something bad from happening

previous.def: happening or existing before the event or object that you are talking about
previous.ex: No previous experience is necessary for this job.

previously.def: at a time before the time that you are talking about
previously.ex: The building had previously been used as a hotel.

prey.def: an animal, a bird, etc. that is hunted, killed and eaten by another
prey.ex: The lion will often stalk its prey for hours.

price.def: the amount of money that you have to pay for something
to fix the price of something at a particular level
price.ex: The main courses are all reasonably priced.

pride.def: a feeling of being pleased or satisfied that you get when you or people who are connected with you have done something well or own something that other people admire
pride.ex: The sight of her son graduating filled her with pride.

priest.def: a person who is qualified to perform religious duties and ceremonies in the Roman Catholic, Anglican and Orthodox Churches

primarily.ex: a course designed primarily for specialists

primary.def: connected with the education of children between the ages of about five and eleven

prime.ex: My prime concern is to protect my property.

prince.def: a male member of a royal family who is not king, especially the son or grandson of the king or queen

princess.def: a female member of a royal family who is not a queen, especially the daughter or granddaughter of the king or queen

principal.ex: The principal reason for this omission is lack of time.

principal.def: the person who is in charge of a college or (in Scotland and Canada) a university
principal.ex: Peter Brown, principal of St John’s College

principle.def: a moral rule or a strong belief that influences your actions

print.def: letters, words, numbers, etc. that have been printed onto paper
print.ex: The tiny print was hard to read without my glasses.

print.def: to produce letters, pictures, etc. on paper using a machine that puts ink (= coloured liquid) on the surface
print.ex: I'm printing a copy of the document for you.

printer.def: a machine for printing text on paper, especially one connected to a computer

printing.def: the act of producing letters, pictures, patterns, etc. on something by pressing a surface covered with ink (= coloured liquid for printing, drawing, etc.) against it

prior.def: happening or existing before something else or before a particular time
prior.ex: Although not essential, some prior knowledge of statistics is desirable.

priority.def: something that you think is more important than other things and should be dealt with first

prison.def: a building where people are kept as a punishment for a crime they have committed, or while they are waiting for trial
prison.ex: She went to prison for tax evasion.

prisoner.def: a person who is kept in prison as a punishment, or while they are waiting for trial
prisoner.ex: The number of prisoners serving life sentences has fallen.

privacy.def: the state of being alone and not watched or interrupted by other people
privacy.ex: She was longing for some peace and privacy.

private.def: belonging to or for the use of a particular person or group; not for public use
private.ex: The sign said, ‘Private property. Keep out.’

privatization.def: the act of selling a business or an industry so that it is no longer owned by the government
privatization.ex: There were fears that privatization would lead to job losses.

privilege.def: a special right or advantage that a particular person or group of people has
privilege.ex: Education should be a universal right and not a privilege.

prize.def: an award that is given to a person who wins a competition, race, etc. or who does very good work
prize.ex: She was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize.

probability.def: how likely something is to happen
probability.ex: The probability is that prices will rise rapidly.

probable.def: likely to happen, to exist or to be true

probably.def: used to say that something is likely to happen or to be true

probe.def: a complete and careful investigation of something
probe.ex: a police probe into the financial affairs of the company

probe.def: to ask questions in order to find out secret or hidden information about somebody/something
probe.ex: He didn't like the media probing into his past.

problem.def: a thing that is difficult to deal with or to understand

problematic.def: difficult to deal with or to understand; full of problems; not certain to be successful
problematic.ex: The situation is more problematic than we first thought.

procedure.def: a way of doing something, especially the usual or correct way

proceed.def: to continue doing something that has already been started; to continue being done
proceed.ex: We're not sure whether we still want to proceed with the sale.

proceeding.def: the process of using a court to settle an argument or to deal with a complaint

proceeds.def: the money that you receive when you sell something or organize a performance, etc.; profits
proceeds.ex: She sold her car and bought a piano with the proceeds.

process.def: a series of things that are done in order to achieve a particular result

processing.def: the treatment of raw material, food, etc. in order to change it, preserve it, etc.

processor.def: a machine, person or company that processes things
processor.ex: The company is Ireland's largest dairy processor.

proclaim.def: to publicly and officially tell people about something important
proclaim.ex: The president proclaimed a state of emergency.

produce.def: things that have been made or grown, especially things connected with farming
produce.ex: The market is full of farm produce.

produce.def: to make things to be sold, especially in large quantities
produce.ex: Our company mainly produces goods for export.

producer.def: a person, a company or a country that grows or makes food, goods or materials

product.def: a thing that is grown, produced or created, usually for sale

production.def: the process of growing or making food, goods or materials, especially large quantities

productive.def: making goods or growing crops, especially in large quantities

productivity.def: the rate at which a worker, a company or a country produces goods, and the amount produced, compared with how much time, work and money is needed to produce them

profession.def: a type of job that needs special training or skill, especially one that needs a high level of education

professional.def: doing something as a paid job rather than as a hobby
a person who does a job that needs special training and a high level of education
professional.ex: You need a professional to sort out your finances.

professor.def: a university teacher of the highest rank

profile.def: a description of somebody/something that gives useful information
profile.ex: We first build up a detailed profile of our customers and their requirements.

profit.def: the money that you make in business or by selling things, especially after paying the costs involved

profitable.def: that makes or is likely to make money

profound.def: very great; felt or experienced very strongly
profound.ex: profound changes in the earth’s climate

program.def: a set of instructions in code that control the operations or functions of a computer
program.ex: You'll need to download and install the program on your computer.

program.def: to give a computer, etc. a set of instructions to make it perform a particular task
program.ex: In this class, students will learn how to program.

programme.def: something that people watch on television or listen to on the radio

programming.def: the process of writing and testing computer programs

progress.def: the process of improving or developing, or of getting nearer to achieving or completing something
to improve or develop over a period of time; to make progress
progress.ex: The course allows students to progress at their own speed.

progressive.def: in favour of new ideas, modern methods and change

prohibit.def: to stop something from being done or used especially by law
prohibit.ex: prohibit something, a law prohibiting the sale of alcohol

project.def: a piece of work involving careful study of a subject over a period of time, done by school or college students
to estimate what the size, cost or amount of something will be in the future based on what is happening now
project.ex: A growth rate of 4 per cent is projected for next year.

projection.def: an estimate or a statement of what figures, amounts, or events will be in the future, or what they were in the past, based on what is happening now
projection.ex: to make forward/backward projections of population figures

promise.def: a statement that tells somebody that you will definitely do or not do something
to tell somebody that you will definitely do or not do something, or that something will definitely happen
promise.ex: ‘We haven't got time to go to the park.’ ‘But you promised!’

promising.def: showing signs of being good or successful
promising.ex: He was voted the most promising new actor for his part in the movie.

promote.def: to help sell a product, service, etc. or make it more popular by advertising it or offering it at a special price
promote.ex: The band has gone on tour to promote their new album.

promotion.def: a move to a more important job or rank in a company or an organization
promotion.ex: The new job is a promotion for him.

prompt.def: to make somebody decide to do something; to cause something to happen
prompt.ex: The discovery of the bomb prompted an increase in security.

pronounce.def: to make the sound of a word or letter in a particular way

pronounced.def: very obvious, easy to notice or strongly expressed
pronounced.ex: He walked with a pronounced limp.

proof.def: information, documents, etc. that show that something is true

propaganda.def: ideas or statements that may be false or present only one side of an argument that are used in order to gain support for a political leader, party, etc.

proper.def: right, appropriate or correct; according to the rules
proper.ex: We should have had a proper discussion before voting.

properly.def: in a way that is correct and/or appropriate
properly.ex: How much money do we need to do the job properly?

property.def: a thing or things that are owned by somebody; a possession or possessions

proportion.def: a part or share of a whole
proportion.ex: Water covers a large proportion of the earth's surface.

proposal.def: a formal suggestion or plan; the act of making a suggestion

propose.def: to suggest a plan, an idea, etc. for people to think about and decide on
propose.ex: The government proposed changes to the voting system.

proposition.def: an idea or a plan of action that is suggested, especially in business
proposition.ex: I'd like to put a business proposition to you.

prosecute.def: to officially charge somebody with a crime in court
prosecute.ex: The police decided not to prosecute.

prosecution.def: the process of trying to prove in court that somebody is guilty of a crime (= of prosecuting them); the process of being officially charged with a crime in court
prosecution.ex: Prosecution for a first minor offence rarely leads to imprisonment.

prosecutor.def: a public official who charges somebody officially with a crime and prosecutes them in court

prospect.def: the possibility that something will happen
prospect.ex: There is no immediate prospect of peace.

prospective.def: expected to do something or to become something

prosperity.def: the state of being successful, especially in making money
prosperity.ex: Our future prosperity depends on economic growth.

protect.def: to make sure that somebody/something is not harmed, injured, damaged, etc.

protection.def: the act of protecting somebody/something; the state of being protected
protection.ex: Wear clothes that provide adequate protection against the wind and rain.

protective.def: providing or intended to provide protection
protective.ex: Workers should wear full protective clothing.

protein.def: a substance, found within all living things, that forms the structure of muscles, organs, etc. There are many different proteins and they are an essential part of what humans and animals eat to help them grow and stay healthy.

protest.def: the expression of strong dislike of or opposition to something; a statement or an action that shows this
to say or do something to show that you disagree with something or think it is bad, especially publicly

protester.def: a person who makes a public protest
protester.ex: Thousands of protesters marched through the city.

protocol.def: a system of fixed rules and formal behaviour used at official meetings, usually between governments

proud.def: feeling pleased and satisfied about something that you own or have done, or are connected with

prove.def: to use facts, evidence, etc. to show that something is true
prove.ex: They hope this new evidence will prove her innocence.

provide.def: to give something to somebody or make it available for them to use

province.def: one of the areas that some countries are divided into with its own local government

provincial.def: connected with one of the large areas that some countries are divided into, with its own local government

provision.def: the act of supplying somebody with something that they need or want; something that is supplied

provoke.def: to cause a particular reaction or have a particular effect
provoke.ex: The announcement provoked a storm of protest.

psychiatric.def: relating to psychiatry or to mental illnesses

psychological.def: connected with a person’s mind and the way in which it works

psychologist.def: a scientist who studies and is trained in psychology

psychology.def: the scientific study of the mind and how it influences behaviour

pub.def: a building where people go to drink and meet their friends. Pubs serve alcoholic and other drinks, and often also food.
pub.ex: We're all going to the pub after work.

public.def: connected with ordinary people in society in general
public.ex: The campaign is designed to increase public awareness of the issues.

public.def: ordinary people in society in general
public.ex: The palace is now open to the public.

publication.def: the act of printing a book, a magazine, etc. and making it available to the public; a book, a magazine, etc. that has been published

publicity.def: the attention that is given to somebody/something by newspapers, television, etc.

publish.def: to produce a book, magazine, CD-ROM, etc. and sell it to the public

publishing.def: the profession or business of preparing and printing books, magazines, CD-ROMs, etc. and selling or making them available to the public

pull.def: an act of trying to make something move by taking hold of it and using force to bring it towards you
pull.ex: I gave the door a sharp pull and it opened.

pull.def: to take hold of something and use force in order to move it or try to move it towards yourself

pulse.def: the regular beat of the heart as it sends blood around the body, that can be felt in different places, especially on the inside part of the wrist; the number of times the heart beats in a minute

pump.def: a machine that is used to force liquid, gas or air into or out of something
to make water, air, gas, etc. flow in a particular direction by using a pump or something that works like a pump
pump.ex: The engine is used for pumping water out of the mine.

punch.def: a hard hit made with the fist (= closed hand)
to hit somebody/something hard with your fist (= closed hand)
punch.ex: He was kicked and punched as he lay on the ground.

punish.def: to make somebody suffer because they have broken the law or done something wrong
punish.ex: Those responsible for this crime will be severely punished.

punishment.def: an act or a way of punishing somebody

punk.def: a type of loud and aggressive rock music popular in the late 1970s and early 1980s

pupil.def: a person who is being taught, especially a child in a school

purchase.def: the act or process of buying something
purchase.ex: to make a purchase (= buy something)
Please ensure that you purchase your ticket in advance.

pure.def: not mixed with anything else; with nothing added

purely.ex: I saw the letter purely by chance.

purple.def: having the colour of blue and red mixed together
the colour of blue and red mixed together
purple.ex: Her hair was dyed a bright shade of purple.

purpose.def: the intention, aim or function of something; the thing that something is supposed to achieve
purpose.ex: Our campaign's main purpose is to raise money.

pursue.def: to do something or try to achieve something over a period of time

pursuit.def: the act of looking for or trying to get something
pursuit.ex: pursuit of something, the pursuit of happiness/knowledge/profit

push.ex: She gave him a gentle push.

push.def: to use your hands, arms or body in order to make somebody/something move forward or away from you; to move part of your body into a particular position
push.ex: We pushed and pushed but the piano wouldn't move.

put.def: to move something into a particular place or position
put.ex: Put the cases down there, please.

puzzle.def: a game, etc. that you have to think about carefully in order to answer it or do it

qualification.def: an exam that you have passed or a course of study that you have successfully completed

qualified.def: having passed the exams or completed the training that are necessary in order to do a particular job; having the experience to do a particular job

qualify.def: to reach the standard of ability or knowledge needed to do a particular job, for example by completing a course of study or passing exams
qualify.ex: How long does it take to qualify?

quality.def: the standard of something when it is compared to other things like it; how good or bad something is
quality.ex: Improving the quality of care for nursing home residents is a priority.

quantity.def: an amount or a number of something
quantity.ex: quantity of something, a large/small quantity of something

quarter.def: one of four equal parts of something
quarter.ex: quarter of something, a quarter of an hour/a century

queen.def: the female ruler of an independent state that has a royal family
queen.ex: She was crowned queen at the age of fifteen.

query.def: a question, especially one asking for information or expressing a doubt about something
query.ex: Our assistants will be happy to answer your queries.

quest.def: a long search for something, especially for some quality such as happiness
quest.ex: quest for something, the quest for happiness/knowledge/truth

question.def: a sentence, phrase or word that asks for information
to ask somebody questions about something, especially officially
question.ex: Police are keen to question any witnesses.

questionnaire.def: a written list of questions that are answered by a number of people so that information can be collected from the answers
questionnaire.ex: (British English), to fill in a questionnaire

queue.def: a line of people, cars, etc. waiting for something or to do something
queue.ex: There were long queues at polling stations.

queue.def: to wait in a line of people, vehicles, etc. in order to do something, get something or go somewhere

quick.def: done with speed; taking or lasting a short time

quietly.def: in a way that makes very little noise

quit.def: to leave your job, school, etc.
quit.ex: If I don't get more money I'll quit.

quota.def: a limited number or amount of people or things that is officially allowed
quota.ex: to introduce a strict import quota on grain

quotation.def: a group of words or a short piece of writing taken from a book, play, speech, etc. and repeated because it is interesting or useful

quote.def: a group of words or a short piece of writing taken from a book, play, speech, etc. and repeated because it is interesting or useful
quote.ex: The essay was full of quotes.

quote.def: to repeat the exact words that another person has said or written

race.def: a competition between people, animals, vehicles, etc. to see which one is the faster or fastest
to compete against somebody/something to see who can go faster or the fastest, do something first, etc.; to take part in a race or races
race.ex: They raced to a thrilling victory in the relay.

racial.def: happening or existing between people of different races

racing.ex: He used to watch the racing on TV in the afternoons.

racism.def: the unfair treatment of people who belong to a different race; violent behaviour towards them

racist.def: having the belief that some races of people are better than others or having general beliefs about other people based only on their race; showing this through violent or unfair treatment of people of other races

a person who believes that some races of people are better than others or who has general beliefs about other people based only on their race, and may show this in violent or unfair treatment of people of other races

radar.def: a system that uses radio waves to find the position and movement of objects, for example planes and ships, when they cannot be seen
radar.ex: They located the ship by radar.

radiation.def: powerful and very dangerous rays that are sent out from radioactive substances
radiation.ex: high levels/doses of radiation that damage cells

radical.def: relating to the most basic and important parts of something; complete and detailed
radical.ex: the need for radical changes in education

radio.def: the activity of broadcasting programmes for people to listen to; the programmes that are broadcast
radio.ex: The play was written specially for radio.

rage.def: a feeling of violent anger that is difficult to control
rage.ex: His face was dark with rage.

raid.def: a short surprise attack on an enemy by soldiers, ships or aircraft
to visit a person or place without warning to look for criminals, illegal goods, drugs, etc.
raid.ex: The house was raided in the early hours.

rail.def: a wooden or metal bar placed around something as a barrier or to provide support
rail.ex: She leaned on the ship's rail and gazed out to sea.

railway.def: a track with rails on which trains run
railway.ex: The railway is still under construction.

rain.def: water that falls from the sky in separate drops
rain.ex: There will be rain in all parts tomorrow.

rain.def: when it rains, water falls from the sky in drops

raise.def: to lift or move something to a higher level
raise.ex: She raised the gun and fired.

rally.def: a large public meeting, especially one held to support a particular idea or political party
to come together or bring people together in order to help or support somebody/something
rally.ex: The cabinet rallied behind the Prime Minister.

random.def: done, chosen, etc. without somebody deciding in advance what is going to happen, or without any regular pattern
random.ex: the random killing of innocent people

range.def: a variety of things of a particular type
range.ex: The hotel offers a wide range of facilities.

range.def: to vary between two particular amounts, sizes, etc., including others between them
range.ex: Accommodation ranges from tourist class to luxury hotels.

rank.def: the position, especially a high position, that somebody has in a particular organization, society, etc.
rank.ex: She was not used to mixing with people of high social rank.
