rank.def: to give somebody/something a particular position on a scale according to quality, importance, success, etc.; to have a position of this kind
rank.ex: In most Australian elections, electors are required to rank all candidates.

ranking.def: the position of somebody/something on a scale that shows how good or important they are in relation to other similar people or things, especially in sport
ranking.ex: He has improved his ranking this season from 67th to 30th.

rape.def: the crime of forcing somebody to have sex when they do not want it or are not able to agree to it
to force somebody to have sex when they do not want it or are not able to agree to it

rapid.def: happening quickly or in a short period of time

rapidly.def: very quickly; at a great rate

rare.def: not done, seen, happening, etc. very often

rarely.ex: She is rarely seen in public nowadays.

rat.def: a small animal with a long tail, that looks like a large mouse, usually considered a pest (= an animal which is disliked because it destroys food or spreads disease)

rate.def: a measurement of the speed at which something happens
rate.ex: Figures published today show another fall in the rate of inflation.

rate.def: to have or think that somebody/something has a particular level of quality, value, etc.
rate.ex: The university is highly rated for its research.

rather.def: used to mean ‘fairly’ or ‘to some degree’, often when you are disappointed, surprised or expressing slight criticism

rating.def: a measurement of how good, popular, important, etc. somebody/something is, especially in relation to other people or things
rating.ex: The poll gave an approval rating of 39 per cent for the president.

ratio.def: the relationship between two groups of people or things that is represented by two numbers showing how much larger one group is than the other
ratio.ex: The school has a very high teacher-student ratio.

rational.def: based on reason rather than emotions

ray.def: a narrow line of light, heat or other energy
ray.ex: The last of the sun's rays shone on the grass.

reach.def: the distance over which you can stretch your arms to touch something; the distance over which a particular object can be used to touch something else
reach.ex: The shot was well beyond the reach of the goalkeeper.

reach.def: to arrive at the place that you have been travelling to
reach.ex: They didn't reach the border until after dark.

react.def: to change or behave in a particular way as a result of or in response to something
react.ex: I nudged her but she didn't react.

reaction.def: what you do, say or think as a result of something that has happened

read.def: to look at and understand the meaning of written or printed words or symbols

reader.def: a person who reads, especially one who reads a lot or in a particular way
reader.ex: an avid reader of science fiction

readily.ex: All ingredients are readily available from your local store.

reading.def: the activity of somebody who reads
reading.ex: My hobbies include reading and painting.

ready.def: fully prepared for what you are going to do and able to start it immediately

real.def: actually existing or happening and not imagined or pretended

realistic.def: accepting in a sensible way what it is actually possible to do or achieve in a particular situation

reality.def: the true situation and the problems that actually exist in life, in contrast to how you would like life to be

realization.def: the process of becoming aware of something
realization.ex: As realization dawned, he went pale.

realize.def: to understand or become aware of a particular fact or situation
realize.ex: I didn’t realize (that) you were so unhappy.

really.def: used to emphasize an adjective or adverb

realm.def: an area of activity, interest or knowledge
realm.ex: in the realm of something, in the realm of literature

rear.def: at or near the back of something

rear.ex: A trailer was attached to the rear of the truck.

reason.def: a cause or an explanation for something that has happened or that somebody has done
reason.ex: He said no but he didn't give a reason.

reasonable.ex: It is reasonable to assume that he knew beforehand that this would happen.

reasonably.def: to a degree that is fairly good but not very good

reasoning.def: the process of thinking about things in a logical way; opinions and ideas that are based on logical thinking
reasoning.ex: What is the reasoning behind this decision?

reassure.def: to say or do something that makes somebody less frightened or worried
reassure.ex: They tried to reassure her, but she still felt anxious.

rebel.def: a person who fights against the government of their country

rebellion.def: an attempt by some of the people in a country to change their government, using violence
rebellion.ex: The north of the country rose in rebellion against the government.

rebuild.def: to build or put something together again
rebuild.ex: After the earthquake, the people set about rebuilding their homes.

recall.ex: She could not recall his name.

receipt.def: a piece of paper or an electronic document that shows that goods or services have been paid for
receipt.ex: Can I have a receipt, please?

receive.def: to get or accept something that is sent or given to you

receiver.def: the part of a phone that you hold close to your mouth and ear
receiver.ex: to pick up/lift/put down/replace the receiver

recent.def: that happened or began only a short time ago

reception.def: the area inside the entrance of a hotel, an office building, etc. where guests or visitors go first when they arrive
reception.ex: Please report to reception on arrival.

recession.def: a difficult time for the economy of a country, when there is less trade and industrial activity than usual and more people are unemployed
recession.ex: How do you assess the impact of the current recession on manufacturing?

recipe.def: a set of instructions that tells you how to cook something and the ingredients (= items of food) you need for it
recipe.ex: recipe for something, a recipe for chicken soup

reckon.def: to think something or have an opinion about something
reckon.ex: I reckon (that) I’m going to get that job.

recognition.def: the act of remembering who somebody is when you see them, or of identifying what something is
recognition.ex: He glanced briefly towards her but there was no sign of recognition.

recognize.def: to know who somebody is or what something is when you see or hear them or it, because you have seen or heard them or it before
recognize.ex: I recognized him as soon as he came in the room.

recommend.def: to tell somebody that something is good or useful, or that somebody would be suitable for a particular job, etc.
recommend.ex: The hotel's new restaurant comes highly recommended (= a lot of people have praised it).

recommendation.def: an official suggestion about the best thing to do

reconstruction.def: the process of changing or improving the condition of something or the way it works; the process of putting something back into the state it was in before
reconstruction.ex: the reconstruction of the educational system

record.def: a written account of something that is kept so that it can be looked at and used in the future
record.ex: You should keep a record of your expenses.

record.def: to keep a permanent account of facts or events by writing them down, filming them, storing them in a computer, etc.
record.ex: Her childhood is recorded in the diaries of those years.

recording.def: sound or pictures that have been recorded on computer files or on CD, DVD, video, etc.

recount.def: sound or pictures that have been recorded on computer files or on CD, DVD, video, etc.

recover.def: to get well again after being ill, hurt, etc.
recover.ex: He's still recovering from his operation.

recovery.def: the process of becoming well again after an illness or injury
recovery.ex: My father has made a full recovery from the operation.

recruit.def: a person who has recently joined the armed forces or the police
to find new people to join a company, an organization, the armed forces, etc.
recruit.ex: We are trying to recruit officers from more diverse backgrounds.

recruitment.def: the act or process of finding new people to join a company, an organization, the armed forces, etc.

recycle.def: to treat things that have already been used so that they can be used again

red.def: having the colour of blood or fire
the colour of blood or fire

reduce.def: to make something less or smaller in size, quantity, price, etc.; to become less or smaller in size, quantity, etc.
reduce.ex: Reduce speed now (= on a sign).

reduction.def: an act of making something less or smaller; the state of being made less or smaller
reduction.ex: Some staff received wage rises, while others experienced a reduction.

refer.def: to send somebody/something to somebody/something for help, advice or a decision
refer.ex: My doctor referred me to a specialist.

referee.def: the official who controls the game in some sports
referee.ex: He was sent off for arguing with the referee.

reference.def: a thing you say or write that mentions somebody/something else; the act of mentioning somebody/something
reference.ex: She made no reference to her illness but only to her future plans.

referendum.def: an occasion when all the people of a country can vote on an important issue
referendum.ex: Switzerland decided to hold a referendum on joining the EU.

reflect.def: to show the image of somebody/something on the surface of something such as a mirror, water or glass
reflect.ex: His face was reflected in the mirror.

reflection.def: an image in a mirror, on a shiny surface, on water, etc.
reflection.ex: He admired his reflection in the mirror.

reform.def: change that is made to a social system, an organization, etc. in order to improve or correct it
to improve a system, an organization, a law, etc. by making changes to it
reform.ex: proposals to reform the social security system

refuge.def: shelter or protection from danger, trouble, etc.
refuge.ex: A further 300 people have taken refuge in the US embassy.

refugee.def: a person who has been forced to leave their country or home, because there is a war or for political, religious or social reasons
refugee.ex: There has been a steady flow of refugees from the war zone.

refusal.def: an act of saying or showing that you will not do, give or accept something
refusal.ex: the refusal of a request/an invitation/an offer

refuse.def: to say or show that you will not do something that somebody has asked you to do
refuse.ex: Go on, ask her; she can hardly refuse.

regain.def: to get back something you no longer have, especially an ability or a quality
regain.ex: I struggled to regain some dignity.

regard.def: attention to or thought and care for somebody/something
regard.ex: regard for somebody/something, to have scant/little/no regard for somebody/something

regard.def: to think about somebody/something in a particular way
regard.ex: They regarded people outside their own town with suspicion.

regardless.def: paying no attention, even if the situation is bad or there are difficulties
regardless.ex: The weather was terrible but we carried on regardless.

regime.def: a method or system of government, especially one that has not been elected in a fair way

region.def: a large area of land, usually without exact limits or borders

regional.def: of or relating to a region
regional.ex: services available at a local and regional level

register.def: an official list or record of names, items, etc.; a book that contains such a list
register.ex: The bride and groom signed the register.

register.def: to record your/somebody’s/something’s name on an official list

registration.def: the act of making an official record of something/somebody
registration.ex: the registration of letters and parcels

regret.def: a sad feeling because of something that has happened or something that you have done or not done
to feel sorry about something you have done or about something that you have not been able to do
regret.ex: If you don't do it now, you'll only regret it.

regular.def: following a pattern, especially with the same time or space in between each thing and the next

regularly.ex: We meet regularly to discuss the progress of the project.

regulate.def: to control something by means of rules
regulate.ex: The department is responsible for regulating the insurance industry.

regulation.def: an official rule made by a government or some other authority

regulator.def: a person or an organization that officially controls an area of business or industry and makes sure that it is operating fairly

regulatory.def: having the power to control an area of business or industry and make sure that it is operating fairly

rehabilitation.def: the process of helping somebody to return to a normal, healthy life after they have been in prison or very ill

reign.def: the period during which a king, queen, emperor, etc. rules
reign.ex: By the end of his reign, the vast empire was in decline.

reign.def: to rule as king, queen, emperor, etc.
reign.ex: It was the first visit by a British reigning monarch to Russia.

reinforce.def: to make a feeling, an idea, etc. stronger
reinforce.ex: The experience reinforced my sense of loss.

reject.def: to refuse to accept or consider something
reject.ex: to reject an argument/a hypothesis/a notion/a plan

rejection.def: the act of refusing to accept or consider something
rejection.ex: Her proposal met with unanimous rejection.

relate.def: show or make a connection between two or more things
relate.ex: I found it difficult to relate the two ideas in my mind.

related.def: connected with something/somebody in some way
related.ex: The amount of protein you need is directly related to your lifestyle.

relation.def: the way in which two people, groups or countries behave towards each other or deal with each other

relationship.def: the way in which two people, groups or countries behave towards each other or deal with each other

relative.def: considered and judged by being compared with something else
relative.ex: You must consider the relative merits of the two plans.

relative.def: a person who is in the same family as somebody else

relatively.def: to a fairly large degree, especially in comparison to something else

relax.def: to rest while you are doing something that you enjoy, especially after work or effort
relax.ex: Just relax and enjoy the movie.

relaxed.def: calm and not anxious or worried
relaxed.ex: He appeared relaxed and confident before the match.

relaxing.def: helping you to rest and become less anxious

release.def: the act of setting a person or an animal free; the state of being set free
release.ex: The government has been working to secure the release of the hostages.

release.def: to let somebody come out of a place where they have been kept or stuck and unable to leave or move
release.ex: release somebody, to release a prisoner

relevance.def: a close connection with the subject you are discussing or the situation you are in
relevance.ex: I don't see the relevance of your question.

relevant.def: closely connected with the subject you are discussing or the situation you are in

reliability.def: the quality of being able to be trusted to do what somebody wants or needs
reliability.ex: The incident cast doubt on her motives and reliability.

reliable.def: that can be trusted to do something well; that you can rely on
reliable.ex: We are looking for someone who is reliable and hard-working.

relief.def: the feeling of happiness that you have when something unpleasant stops or does not happen
relief.ex: I felt a huge sense of relief when I heard they were all OK.

relieve.def: to remove or reduce an unpleasant feeling or pain
relieve.ex: to relieve the symptoms of a cold

relieved.def: feeling happy because something unpleasant has stopped or has not happened; showing this

religion.def: the belief in the existence of a god or gods, and the activities that are connected with the worship of them, or in the teachings of a spiritual leader
religion.ex: Is there always a conflict between science and religion?

religious.def: connected with religion or with a particular religion

reluctant.def: hesitating before doing something because you do not want to do it or because you are not sure that it is the right thing to do
reluctant.ex: He finally gave a reluctant smile.

remain.def: to continue to be something; to be still in the same state or condition

remainder.def: the people, things or time that remain
remainder.ex: I kept some of his books and gave away the remainder.

remains.def: the parts of something that are left after the other parts have been used, eaten, removed, etc.
remains.ex: She fed the remains of her lunch to the dog.

remark.def: something that you say or write which expresses an opinion, a thought, etc. about somebody/something
to say or write a comment about something/somebody
remark.ex: George Orwell once remarked, is like war.

remarkable.def: unusual or surprising in a way that causes people to take notice

remarkably.def: in a way that is unusual or surprising and causes people to take notice
remarkably.ex: The car is in remarkably good condition for its age.

remedy.def: a way of dealing with or improving an unpleasant or difficult situation
remedy.ex: When the reservoir becomes blocked, the only remedy lies in cleaning the entire system.

remember.def: to have or keep an image in your memory of an event, a person, a place, etc. from the past
remember.ex: This is Carla. Do you remember her?

remind.def: to help somebody remember something, especially something important that they must do
remind.ex: I've forgotten your name. Can you remind me?

reminder.def: something that makes you think about or remember somebody/something, that you have forgotten or would like to forget
reminder.ex: The sheer size of the cathedral is a constant reminder of the power of religion.

remote.def: far away from places where other people live

removal.def: the act of taking somebody/something away from a particular place
removal.ex: Clearance of the site required the removal of a number of trees.

remove.def: to take somebody/something away from a place
remove.ex: Illegally parked vehicles will be removed.

render.def: to cause somebody/something to be in a particular state or condition

renew.def: to begin something again after it stopped or was interrupted
renew.ex: The army renewed its assault on the capital.

rent.def: an amount of money that you regularly pay so that you can use a house, room, etc.
rent.ex: I earn just about enough to pay the rent.

rent.def: to regularly pay money to somebody so that you can use something that they own, such as a house, a room, some land, etc.
rent.ex: Are you looking to buy or rent?

rental.def: the amount of money that you pay to use something for a particular period of time

repair.ex: They agreed to pay the costs of any repairs.

repair.def: to fix something that is broken, damaged or torn

repeat.def: an event that is very similar to something that happened before
repeat.ex: She didn't want a repeat performance of what had happened the night before.

repeat.def: to say or write something again or more than once
repeat.ex: repeat something, to repeat a question/a claim/an assertion/a warning

repeated.def: happening, said or done many times

replace.def: to be used instead of somebody/something else; to do something instead of somebody/something else
replace.ex: The new design will eventually replace all existing models.

replacement.def: the act of replacing one thing with another, especially something that is newer or better
replacement.ex: the replacement of worn car parts

reply.def: an act of replying to something/somebody in speech, writing or by some action
reply.ex: I asked her what her name was but she made no reply.

reply.def: to say or write something as an answer to somebody/something
reply.ex: She didn't even bother to reply.

report.def: a written or spoken account of an event, especially one that is published or broadcast
to give people information about something that you have heard, seen, done, etc.
report.ex: The crash happened seconds after the pilot reported engine trouble.

reportedly.def: according to what some people say
reportedly.ex: The band have reportedly decided to split up.

reporter.def: a person who collects and reports news for newspapers, radio or television
reporter.ex: He leaked the story to a news reporter.

reporting.def: the presenting of and writing about news on television and radio, and in newspapers

represent.def: to be a member of a group of people and act or speak for them at an event, a meeting, etc.
represent.ex: The competition attracted over 500 contestants representing eight different countries.

representation.def: the act of presenting somebody/something in a particular way; something that shows or describes something
representation.ex: the negative representation of single mothers in the media

representative.def: typical of a particular group of people; that is a typical example of something
representative.ex: Is a questionnaire answered by 500 people truly representative of the population as a whole?

representative.def: a person who has been chosen to speak or vote for somebody else or for a group of people, or to take the place of somebody else
representative.ex: Our elected representatives in government should be doing something about this.

reproduce.def: to make a copy of a picture, piece of text, etc.
reproduce.ex: It is illegal to reproduce these worksheets without permission from the publisher.

reproduction.def: the act or process of producing babies, young animals or plants

republic.def: a country that is governed by a president and politicians elected by the people and where there is no king or queen

reputation.def: the opinion that people have about what somebody/something is like, based on what has happened in the past

request.def: the action of asking for something formally and politely; a thing that you formally ask for
request.ex: request for something, a request for information

request.def: to ask for something or ask somebody to do something in a polite or formal way
request.ex: She requested permission to film at the White House.

require.def: to need something; to depend on somebody/something
require.ex: These pets require a lot of care and attention.

requirement.def: something that you need or want

rescue.def: the act of saving somebody/something from a dangerous or difficult situation; the fact of being saved
rescue.ex: We had given up hope of rescue.

rescue.def: to save somebody/something from a dangerous or harmful situation
rescue.ex: The coastguard rescued six people from the sinking boat.

research.def: a careful study of a subject, especially in order to discover new facts or information about it
to study something carefully and try to discover new facts about it

researcher.def: a person who studies something carefully and tries to discover new facts about it
researcher.ex: European researchers say olive oil could help prevent cancer.

resemble.def: to look like or be similar to another person or thing

reservation.def: an arrangement for a seat on a plane or train, a room in a hotel, etc. to be kept for you
reservation.ex: I'll call the restaurant and make a reservation.

reserve.def: a supply of something that is available to be used in the future or when it is needed
to ask for a seat, table, room, etc. to be available for you or somebody else at a future time
reserve.ex: I'd like to reserve a table for three for eight o'clock.

reside.def: to live in a particular place
reside.ex: He returned to Britain in 1939, having resided abroad for many years.

residence.def: a house, especially a large or impressive one
residence.ex: a desirable family residence for sale (= for example, in an advertisement)

resident.ex: the town’s resident population (= not tourists or visitors)

resident.def: a person who lives in a particular place or who has their home there
resident.ex: a resident of the United States

residential.def: suitable for living in; consisting of houses rather than factories or offices

residue.def: a small amount of something that remains at the end of a process
residue.ex: pesticide residues in fruit and vegetables

resign.def: to officially tell somebody that you are leaving your job, an organization, etc.
resign.ex: She was forced to resign due to ill health.

resignation.def: the act of giving up your job or position; the occasion when you do this

resist.def: to refuse to accept something and try to stop it from happening
resist.ex: They are determined to resist pressure to change the law.

resistance.def: dislike of or opposition to a plan, an idea, etc.; the act of refusing to obey
resistance.ex: As with all new ideas it met with resistance.

resolution.def: a definite decision to do or not to do something
resolution.ex: Have you made any New Year's resolutions (= for example, to give up smoking from 1 January)?

resolve.def: to find an acceptable solution to a problem or difficulty
resolve.ex: resolve something, to resolve an issue/a dispute/a conflict/a crisis

resort.def: a place where a lot of people go on holiday

resource.def: a supply of something that a country, an organization or a person has and can use, especially to increase their wealth
resource.ex: the exploitation of minerals and other natural resources

respect.def: a strong feeling of approval of somebody/something because of their good qualities or achievements
respect.ex: I have the utmost respect for her and her work.

respect.def: to have a very good opinion of somebody/something; to admire somebody/something
respect.ex: I respect Jack's opinion on most subjects.

respective.def: belonging or relating separately to each of the people or things already mentioned
respective.ex: They are each recognized specialists in their respective fields.

respectively.def: in the same order as the people or things already mentioned
respectively.ex: Julie and Mark, aged 17 and 19 respectively

respond.def: to give a spoken or written answer to somebody/something
respond.ex: I asked him his name, but he didn't respond.

responsibility.def: a duty to deal with or take care of somebody/something, so that you may be blamed if something goes wrong
responsibility.ex: to be in a position of responsibility

responsible.def: having the job or duty of doing something or taking care of somebody/something, so that you may be blamed if something goes wrong
responsible.ex: Mike is responsible for designing the entire project.

rest.def: the part of something that remains
rest.ex: Take what you want and throw the rest away.

rest.def: to relax, sleep or do nothing after a period of activity or illness; to not use a part of your body for some time
rest.ex: The doctor told me to rest.

restaurant.def: a place where you can buy and eat a meal
restaurant.ex: We're going to try this Italian restaurant that just opened.

restoration.def: the work of repairing and cleaning an old building, a painting, etc. so that its condition is as good as it originally was

restore.def: to bring back a situation or feeling that existed before
restore.ex: The measures are intended to restore public confidence in the economy.

restraint.def: a rule, a fact, an idea, etc. that limits or controls what people can do
restraint.ex: The government has imposed export restraints on some products.

restrict.def: to limit the size, amount or range of something
restrict.ex: Speed is restricted to 30 mph in towns.

restriction.def: a rule or law that limits what you can do or what can happen

result.def: a thing that is caused or produced because of something else
result.ex: And did your intervention produce the desired result?
A heavy frost could result in loss of the crop.

resume.def: if you resume an activity, or if it resumes, it begins again or continues after being interrupted

retail.def: the selling of goods to the public, usually through shops

retain.def: to keep something; to continue to have something

retire.def: to leave your job and stop working, especially because you have reached a particular age or because you are ill; to tell somebody they must stop doing their job
retire.ex: He is retiring next year after 30 years with the company.

retirement.def: the fact of leaving your job and stopping work, usually because you have reached a particular age; the time when you do this
retirement.ex: At 60, he was now approaching retirement.

retreat.def: a movement away from a place or an enemy because of danger or defeat
to move away from a place or an enemy because you are in danger or because you have been defeated
retreat.ex: The army was forced to retreat after suffering heavy losses.

retrieve.def: to bring or get something back, especially from a place where it should not be
retrieve.ex: She bent to retrieve her comb from the floor.

return.def: the action of arriving in or coming back to a place that you were in before
return.ex: A week had passed since their return to Geneva.

return.def: to come or go back from one place to another
return.ex: I waited a long time for him to return.

reveal.def: to make something known to somebody
reveal.ex: reveal something, to reveal a secret

revelation.def: a fact that people are made aware of, especially one that has been secret and is surprising
revelation.ex: revelation about/concerning something, startling/sensational revelations about her private life

revenge.def: something that you do in order to make somebody suffer because they have made you suffer
revenge.ex: She is seeking revenge for the murder of her husband.

revenue.def: the money that a government receives from taxes or that an organization, etc. receives from its business
revenue.ex: The government is currently facing a shortfall in tax revenue.

reverse.def: opposite to what has been mentioned
reverse.ex: to travel in the reverse direction

reverse.def: the opposite of what has just been mentioned
reverse.ex: This problem is the reverse of the previous one.

reverse.def: to change something completely so that it is the opposite of what it was before
reverse.ex: Falling birth rates may reverse the trend towards early retirement.

review.def: a report in a newspaper or magazine, or on the internet, television or radio, in which somebody gives their opinion of a book, play, film, product, etc.; the act of writing this kind of report
to write a report of a book, play, film, product, etc. in which you give your opinion of it
review.ex: Please rate and review your purchase on our website.

revise.def: to change your opinions or plans, for example because of something you have learned
revise.ex: I can see I will have to revise my opinions of his abilities now.

revision.def: a change or set of changes to something
revision.ex: He made some minor revisions to the report before printing it out.

revival.def: an improvement in the condition or strength of something

revive.def: to become, or to make somebody/something become, conscious or healthy and strong again
revive.ex: The flowers soon revived in water.

revolution.def: an attempt, by a large number of people, to change the government of a country, especially by violent action

reward.def: a thing that you are given because you have done something good, worked hard, etc.
to give something to somebody because they have done something good, worked hard, etc.
reward.ex: She was rewarded for her efforts with a cash bonus.

rhetoric.def: speech or writing that is intended to influence people, but that is not completely honest or sincere

rhythm.def: a strong regular repeated pattern of sounds or movements
rhythm.ex: to dance to the rhythm of the music

rice.def: short, narrow white or brown grain grown on wet land in hot countries as food; the plant that produces this grain

rich.def: having a lot of money or property
rich.ex: She's one of the richest women in the world.

rid.def: to be free of somebody/something that has been annoying you or that you do not want
rid.ex: She wanted to be rid of her parents and their authority.

ride.def: a short journey in a vehicle, on a bicycle, etc.
ride.ex: a train ride through beautiful countryside

ride.def: to sit on an animal, especially a horse, and control it as it moves
ride.ex: I learnt to ride as a child.

ridiculous.ex: I look ridiculous in this hat.

rifle.def: a gun with a long barrel which you hold to your shoulder to fire

right.def: true or correct as a fact
right.ex: Did you get the answer right?

right.def: on or to the right side
right.ex: Turn right at the end of the street.
Take the first street on the right.

ring.def: a piece of jewellery that you wear on your finger, consisting of a round band of gold, silver, etc., sometimes decorated with precious stones

ring.ex: Thousands of demonstrators ringed the building.

riot.def: a situation in which a group of people behave in a violent way in a public place, often as a protest
riot.ex: One prison guard was killed when a riot broke out in the jail.

rip.def: to tear something or to become torn, often suddenly or violently
rip.ex: I ripped my jeans on the fence.

rise.def: an increase in an amount, a number or a level
rise.ex: The industry is feeling the effects of recent price rises.

rise.def: to come or go upwards; to reach a higher level or position
rise.ex: The curtain rose to reveal an empty stage.

risk.def: the possibility of something bad happening at some time in the future; a situation that could be dangerous or have a bad result
risk.ex: The health risks are very low.

risk.def: to put something valuable or important in a dangerous situation, in which it could be lost or damaged
risk.ex: He risked his life to save her.

risky.def: involving the possibility of something bad happening
risky.ex: Life as an aid worker can be a risky business (= dangerous).

ritual.def: a series of actions that are always performed in the same way, especially as part of a religious ceremony

rival.def: competing with another person, company, thing, etc.
a person, company or thing that competes with another in sport, business, etc.
rival.ex: The two teams have always been rivals.

river.def: a natural flow of water that continues in a long line across land to the sea
river.ex: Eventually we came to the mouth of the River Thames.

road.def: a hard surface built for vehicles to travel on

rob.def: to steal money or property from a person or place

robbery.def: the crime of stealing money or goods from a bank, shop, person, etc., especially using violence or threats
robbery.ex: armed robbery (= using a gun, knife, etc.)

robot.def: a machine that can perform a complicated series of tasks by itself
robot.ex: These cars are built by robots.

robust.ex: She was almost 90, but still very robust.

rock.def: the hard solid material that forms part of the surface of the earth and some other planets
rock.ex: They drilled through several layers of rock to reach the oil.

rock.def: to move gently backwards and forwards or from side to side; to make somebody/something move in this way
rock.ex: The boat rocked from side to side in the waves.

rocket.def: a spacecraft in the shape of a tube that is driven by a stream of gases let out behind it when fuel is burned inside

rod.def: a long straight piece of wood, metal or glass
rod.ex: The concrete is reinforced with steel rods.

role.def: an actor’s part in a play, film, etc.
role.ex: She has landed the lead role (= the most important one) in a new play.

roll.def: a small loaf of bread for one person
to turn over and over and move in a particular direction; to make a round object do this
roll.ex: The ball rolled down the hill.

romance.def: an exciting, usually short, relationship between two people who are in love with each other

romantic.def: connected with or about love or a sexual relationship

roof.def: the structure that covers or forms the top of a building or vehicle

room.def: a part of a building that has its own walls, floor and ceiling and is usually used for a particular purpose
room.ex: I heard him enter the room.

root.def: the part of a plant that grows under the ground and takes in water and minerals that it sends to the rest of the plant

rope.def: very strong thick string made by twisting thinner strings, wires, etc. together
rope.ex: The rope broke and she fell 50 metres onto the rocks.

rose.def: a flower with a sweet smell that grows on a bush with thorns (= sharp points) on its stems

rotate.def: to move or turn around a central fixed point; to make something do this
rotate.ex: Stay well away from the helicopter when its blades start to rotate.

rotation.def: the action of an object moving in a circle around a central fixed point
rotation.ex: the daily rotation of the earth on its axis

rough.def: having a surface that is not even or regular

roughly.ex: Sales are up by roughly 10 per cent.

round.def: having a shape like a circle or a ball

round.ex: Everybody joins hands and dances round.

round.def: a stage in a competition or sports event
round.ex: the qualifying rounds of the National Championships
the first woman to sail round the world

route.def: a way that you follow to get from one place to another
route.ex: Which is the best route to take?

routine.def: done or happening as a normal part of a particular job, situation or process
the normal order and way in which you regularly do things

royal.def: connected with or belonging to the king or queen of a country

rub.def: to move your hand, a cloth, etc., backwards and forwards over a surface while pressing it
rub.ex: He sat up on the hard bunk and rubbed his eyes.

rubber.def: a strong substance that can be stretched and does not allow liquids to pass through it, used for making tyres, boots, etc. It is made from the liquid (= sap) inside a tropical plant or is produced using chemicals.

rubbish.def: things that you throw away because you no longer want or need them

rude.def: having or showing a lack of respect for other people and their feelings

rugby.def: a game played by two teams of 13 or 15 players, using an oval ball which may be kicked or carried. Teams try to put the ball over the other team’s line.
rugby.ex: to play a game of rugby

ruin.def: the state or process of being destroyed or severely damaged
ruin.ex: A large number of churches fell into ruin after the revolution.

ruin.def: to damage something so badly that it loses all its value, pleasure, etc.
ruin.ex: The bad weather ruined our trip.

rule.def: a statement of what may, must or must not be done in a particular situation or when playing a game
rule.ex: She laid down strict rules for her tenants, including prompt payment of rent.

rule.def: to control and have authority over a country, a group of people, etc.

ruling.def: an official decision made by somebody in a position of authority, especially a judge
ruling.ex: The court will make its ruling on the case next week.

rumour.def: a piece of information, or a story, that people talk about, but that may not be true

run.def: an act of running; a period of time spent running or the distance that somebody runs
run.ex: I go for a run every morning.

run.def: to move using your legs, going faster than when you walk
run.ex: Can you run as fast as Mike?

runner.def: a person or an animal that runs, especially one taking part in a race

running.def: the action or sport of running

rural.def: connected with or like the countryside

rush.def: a sudden strong movement or action
rush.ex: Shoppers made a rush for the exits.

rush.def: to move or to do something with great speed, often too fast
rush.ex: We've got plenty of time; there's no need to rush.

sack.def: to dismiss somebody from a job
sack.ex: She was sacked for refusing to work on Sundays.

sacred.def: connected with God or a god; considered to be holy

sacrifice.def: the fact of giving up something important or valuable to you in order to get or do something that seems more important; something that you give up in this way
sacrifice.ex: The makers of the product assured us that there had been no sacrifice of quality.

sacrifice.def: to give up something that is important or valuable to you in order to get or do something that seems more important for yourself or for another person
sacrifice.ex: She sacrificed everything for her children.

sad.ex: We are very sad to hear that you are leaving.

safe.def: protected from any danger, harm or loss
safe.ex: The children are quite safe here.

safety.def: the state of being safe and protected from danger or harm
safety.ex: in safety, a place where children can play in safety

sail.def: a sheet of strong cloth which the wind blows against to make a boat or ship travel through the water
sail.ex: As the boat moved down the river the wind began to fill the sails.

sail.def: to travel on water using sails or an engine

sailing.def: the sport or activity of travelling in a boat with sails

sailor.def: a person who works on a ship as a member of the crew
sailor.ex: a crew of two officers and 13 sailors

saint.def: a person that the Christian Church recognizes as being very holy, because of the way they have lived or died

salad.def: a mixture of raw vegetables such as lettuce, tomato and cucumber, usually served with other food as part of a meal
salad.ex: All main courses come with salad or vegetables.

salary.def: money that employees receive for doing their job, especially professional employees or people working in an office, usually paid every month

sale.def: an act or the process of selling something
sale.ex: regulations governing the sale of alcoholic beverages

salt.def: a white substance that is added to food to make it taste better or to preserve it. Salt is obtained from mines and is also found in seawater. It is sometimes called common salt to show that it is different from other chemical salts. Its chemical name is sodium chloride.

same.def: exactly the one or ones referred to or mentioned; not different
same.ex: We have lived in the same house for twenty years.
We treat boys exactly the same as girls.
I would do the same again.

sample.def: a number of people or things taken from a larger group and used in tests to provide information about the group
sample.ex: The interviews were given to a random sample of students.

sample.def: to try a small amount of a particular food to see what it is like; to experience something for a short time to see what it is like
sample.ex: I sampled the delights of Greek cooking for the first time.

sanction.def: an official order that limits trade, contact, etc. with a particular country, in order to make it do something, such as obeying international law
sanction.ex: Trade sanctions were imposed against any country that refused to sign the agreement.

sand.def: a substance that consists of very small fine grains of rock. Sand is found on beaches, in deserts, etc.

sandwich.def: two slices of bread, often spread with butter, with a layer of meat, cheese, etc. between them

satellite.def: an electronic device that is sent into space and moves around the earth or another planet. It is used for communicating by radio, television, etc. and for gathering information.

satisfaction.def: the good feeling that you have when you have achieved something or when something that you wanted to happen does happen; something that gives you this feeling

satisfied.def: pleased because you have achieved something or because something that you wanted to happen has happened

satisfy.def: to make somebody pleased by doing or giving them what they want

saturday.def: the day of the week after Friday and before Sunday

sauce.def: a thick liquid that is eaten with food to add taste to it

save.def: to keep somebody/something safe from death, harm, loss, etc.
save.ex: save somebody/something, to save somebody’s life

saving.def: an amount of something such as time or money that you do not need to use or spend
saving.ex: Buy three and make a saving of 55p.

say.def: the right to influence something by giving your opinion before a decision is made
say.ex: We had no say in the decision to sell the company.

say.def: to speak or tell somebody something, using words

scale.def: the size or extent of something, especially when compared with something else
scale.ex: They entertain on a large scale (= they hold expensive parties with a lot of guests).

scan.def: to look quickly but not very carefully at a document, etc.
scan.ex: I scanned the list quickly for my name.

scandal.def: behaviour or an event that people think is morally or legally wrong and causes public feelings of shock or anger

scare.def: a situation in which a lot of people are anxious or frightened about something

scared.def: frightened of something or afraid that something bad might happen
scared.ex: The thieves got scared and ran away.

scary.ex: It was a really scary moment.

scattered.def: spread far apart over a wide area or over a long period of time

scenario.def: a description of how things might happen in the future
scenario.ex: Let me suggest a possible scenario.

scene.def: the place where something happens, especially something unpleasant

sceptical.def: having doubts that a claim or statement is true or that something will happen

schedule.def: a plan that lists all the work that you have to do and when you must do each thing
schedule.ex: I have a hectic schedule for the next few days.

schedule.def: to arrange for something to happen at a particular time
schedule.ex: The meeting is scheduled for Friday afternoon.

scheme.def: a plan or system for doing or organizing something

scholar.def: a person who knows a lot about a particular subject because they have studied it in detail

scholarship.def: an amount of money given to somebody by an organization to help pay for their education
scholarship.ex: She won a scholarship to study at Stanford.

school.def: a place where children go to be educated
school.ex: My brother and I went to the same school.

science.def: knowledge about the structure and behaviour of the natural and physical world, based on facts that you can prove, for example by experiments
science.ex: new developments in science and technology

scientist.def: a person who studies one or more of the natural sciences (= for example, physics, chemistry or biology)

scope.def: the opportunity or ability to do or achieve something
scope.ex: There's still plenty of scope for improvement.

score.def: the number of points, goals, etc. scored by each player or team in a game or competition
to win points, goals, etc. in a game or competition
score.ex: Fraser scored again in the second half.

scratch.def: a mark, a cut or an injury made by scratching somebody’s skin or the surface of something
scratch.ex: Her hands were covered in scratches from the brambles.

scratch.def: to rub your skin with your nails, usually because it is itching
scratch.ex: John yawned and scratched his chin.

scream.def: a loud high shout made by somebody who is hurt, frightened, excited, etc.; a loud high noise
scream.ex: She let out a scream of pain.

scream.def: to give a loud, high shout, because you are hurt, frightened, excited, etc.
scream.ex: There was a bang and I heard someone scream.

screen.def: the flat surface at the front of a television, computer, or other electronic device, on which you see pictures or information
to show a film, etc. in a cinema or on television
screen.ex: a list of films to be screened as part of the festival

screening.def: the act of showing a film or television programme
screening.ex: This will be the movie's first screening in this country.

screw.def: a thin pointed piece of metal like a nail with a raised spiral line (called a thread) along it and a line or cross cut into its head. Screws are turned and pressed into wood, metal, etc. with a screwdriver in order to fasten two things together.
screw.ex: One of the screws is loose.

screw.def: to fasten one thing to another or make something tight with a screw or screws
screw.ex: The bookcase is screwed to the wall.

script.def: a written text of a play, film, broadcast, talk, etc.
script.ex: That line isn't in the original script.

scrutiny.ex: Her argument doesn't really stand up to scrutiny.

sculpture.def: a work of art that is a solid figure or object made by carving or shaping wood, stone, clay, metal, etc.

sea.def: the salt water that covers most of the earth’s surface and surrounds its continents and islands
sea.ex: The waste was dumped in the sea.

seal.def: an official design or mark, stamped on a document to show that it is real and carries the authority of a particular person or organization
seal.ex: The letter bore the president's seal.

seal.def: to close an envelope, etc. by sticking the edges of the opening together
seal.ex: Make sure you've signed the cheque before sealing the envelope.

search.def: an attempt to find somebody/something, especially by looking carefully for them/it
search.ex: search for somebody/something, a long search for the murder weapon

search.def: to look carefully for something/somebody; to examine a particular place when looking for something/somebody
search.ex: She searched in vain for her passport.

season.def: any of the four main periods of the year: spring, summer, autumn and winter

seat.def: a place where you can sit, for example a chair
seat.ex: She sat back in her seat.

seat.def: to give somebody a place to sit; to sit down in a place
seat.ex: Please wait to be seated (= in a restaurant, etc.).

second.def: after one other person or thing in order or importance
second.ex: She came second in the marathon.

second.def: happening or coming next after the first in a series of similar things or people; 2nd
second.ex: This is the second time it's happened.

second.def: a unit for measuring time. There are 60 seconds in one minute.
second.ex: She can run 100 metres in just over 11 seconds.

secondary.def: connected with teaching children of 11–18 years

secondly.def: used to introduce the second of a list of points you want to make in a speech or piece of writing
secondly.ex: Firstly, it's expensive, and secondly, it's too slow.

secret.def: known about by only a few people; kept hidden from others
something that is known about by only a few people and not told to others

secretary.def: a person who works in an office, working for another person, dealing with mail and phone calls, keeping records, arranging meetings with people, etc.

section.def: any of the parts into which something is divided
section.ex: That section of the road is still closed.

sector.def: a part of an area of activity, especially of a country’s economy

secular.def: not connected with spiritual or religious matters

secure.def: likely to continue or be successful for a long time
to obtain or achieve something, especially when this means using a lot of effort
secure.ex: secure something, to secure a contract/deal/funding

security.def: the activities involved in protecting a country, building or person against attack, danger, etc.
security.ex: national/homeland security (= the defence of a country)

see.def: to become aware of somebody/something by using your eyes
see.ex: She looked for him but couldn't see him in the crowd.

seed.def: the small hard part produced by a plant, from which a new plant can grow
seed.ex: a packet of wild flower seeds

seek.ex: Drivers are advised to seek alternative routes.

seeker.def: a person who is trying to find or get the thing mentioned

seem.def: to give the impression of being or doing something

seemingly.def: in a way that appears to be true but may in fact not be

segment.def: a part of something that is separate from the other parts or can be considered separately
segment.ex: She cleaned a small segment of the painting.

seize.def: to take somebody/something in your hand suddenly and using force
seize.ex: She tried to seize the gun from him.

seldom.ex: He had seldom seen a child with so much talent.

select.def: to choose somebody/something from a group of people or things, usually according to a system
select.ex: He hasn't been selected for the team.

selection.def: the process of choosing somebody/something from a group of people or things, usually according to a system
selection.ex: She took a long time to make her selection.

selective.def: affecting or involving only a small number of people or things from a larger group

self.def: the type of person you are, especially the way you normally behave, look or feel
self.ex: You'll soon be feeling your old self again (= feeling well or happy again).

sell.def: to give something to somebody in exchange for money

seminar.def: a class at a university or college when a small group of students and a teacher discuss or study a particular topic
seminar.ex: Teaching is by lectures and seminars.

send.def: to make something go or be taken to a place, especially by post, email, radio, etc.
send.ex: She sent a letter of support.

senior.def: high in rank or status; higher in rank or status than others

sensation.def: a feeling that you get when something affects your body

sense.def: an understanding about something; an ability to judge something
sense.ex: One of the most important things in a partner is a sense of humour (= the ability to find things funny or make people laugh).

sense.def: to become aware of something even though you cannot see it, hear it, etc.
sense.ex: sense something, Sensing danger, they started to run.

sensible.def: able to make good judgements based on reason and experience rather than emotion; practical
sensible.ex: She's a sensible sort of person.

sensitive.def: aware of and able to understand other people and their feelings

sensitivity.def: the ability to understand other people’s feelings
sensitivity.ex: sensitivity to the needs of children

sentence.def: a set of words expressing a statement, a question or an order, usually containing a subject and a verb. In written English sentences begin with a capital letter and end with a full stop/period (.), a question mark (?) or an exclamation mark (!).
sentence.ex: Does the sentence contain an adverb?

sentence.def: to say officially in court that somebody is to receive a particular punishment
sentence.ex: He sentenced the defendant to life in prison.

sentiment.def: a feeling or an opinion, especially one based on emotions

separate.def: forming a unit by itself; not joined to something else
separate.ex: They have begun to sleep in separate rooms.

separate.def: to divide into different parts or groups; to divide things into different parts or groups
separate.ex: Stir the sauce constantly so that it does not separate.

separation.def: the act of separating people or things; the state of being separate
separation.ex: separation from somebody/something, the state’s eventual separation from the federation

september.def: the 9th month of the year, between August and October

sequence.def: a set of events, actions, numbers, etc. which have a particular order and which lead to a particular result
sequence.ex: He described the sequence of events leading up to the robbery.

serial.def: doing the same thing in the same way several times; done in the same way several times

series.def: a set of television or radio programmes or podcasts that deal with the same subject or that have the same characters
series.ex: The first episode of the new series is on Saturday.

servant.def: a person who works in another person’s house, and cooks, cleans, etc. for them

serve.def: to give somebody food or drink, for example at a restaurant or during a meal
serve.ex: Breakfast is served between 7 and 10 a.m.

service.def: a system that provides something that the public needs, organized by the government or a private company

session.def: a period of time that is spent doing a particular activity

set.def: a group of similar things that belong together in some way
to prepare or arrange something so that it is ready for use or in position
set.ex: Have you set the alarm clock?

set-up.def: a way of organizing something; a system
set-up.ex: I've only been here a couple of weeks and I don't really know the set-up.

setting.def: an environment where something is located; the place at which something happens
setting.ex: a rural/an ideal/a beautiful/an idyllic setting

settle.def: to put an end to an argument or a disagreement
settle.ex: settle something, to settle a dispute/a matter/an issue

settlement.def: an official agreement that ends an argument between two people or groups

settler.def: a person who goes to live in a new country or region
settler.ex: Most of the settlers came from England.

several.def: more than two but not very many

sex.def: the state of being male or female
sex.ex: How can you tell what sex a fish is?

sexual.def: connected with the physical activity of sex

sexuality.def: the feelings and activities connected with a person’s sexual desires
sexuality.ex: He was confused about his sexuality.

shade.def: an area that is dark and cool under or behind something, for example a tree or building, because the sun’s light does not get to it
shade.ex: The shade of the pine tree provided some protection from the sun.

shadow.def: the dark shape that somebody/something’s form makes on a surface, for example on the ground, when they are between the light and the surface
shadow.ex: The children were having fun, chasing each other's shadows.

shake.ex: She gave him a shake to wake him.

shake.def: to move or make somebody/something move with short quick movements from side to side or up and down
shake.ex: The whole house shakes when a train goes past.

shall.def: used with I and we for talking about or predicting the future
shall.ex: This time next week I shall be in Scotland.

shallow.def: not having much distance between the top or surface and the bottom

shame.def: used to say that something is a cause for feeling sad or disappointed
shame.ex: She's retiring because of ill health, which is a great shame.

shape.def: the form of the outer edges or surfaces of something; an example of something that has a particular form
to make something into a particular shape
shape.ex: Shape the dough into a ball.

shaped.def: having the type of shape mentioned
shaped.ex: a huge balloon shaped like a giant cow

share.def: one part of something that is divided between two or more people
share.ex: Next year we hope to have a greater share of the market.

share.def: to have, use or experience something at the same time as somebody else
share.ex: There isn't an empty table. Would you mind sharing?

shareholder.def: an owner of shares in a company or business
shareholder.ex: the major shareholders in the company

sharp.def: having a fine edge or point, especially of something that can cut or make a hole in something

shatter.def: to suddenly break into small pieces; to make something suddenly break into small pieces
shatter.ex: He dropped the vase and it shattered into pieces on the floor.

she.def: a female person or animal that has already been mentioned or is easily identified
she.ex: ‘What does your sister do?’ ‘She's a dentist.’

shed.def: to get rid of something that is no longer wanted
shed.ex: The factory is shedding a large number of jobs.

sheep.def: an animal with a thick coat, kept on farms for its meat (called mutton or lamb) or its wool

sheer.def: used to emphasize the size, degree or amount of something
sheer.ex: The area is under threat from the sheer number of tourists using it.

sheet.def: a large piece of thin cloth used on a bed to lie on or lie under
sheet.ex: Have you changed the sheets (= put clean sheets on the bed)?

shelf.def: a flat board, made of wood, metal, glass, etc., fixed to the wall or forming part of a cupboard, bookcase, etc., for things to be placed on
shelf.ex: I helped him put up some shelves in his bedroom.

shell.def: the hard outer part of eggs, nuts, some seeds, and some animals
shell.ex: We collected shells on the beach.

shelter.def: the fact of having a place to live or stay, considered as a basic human need
shelter.ex: Human beings need food, clothing and shelter.

shelter.def: to give somebody/something a place where they are protected from the weather or from danger; to protect somebody/something
shelter.ex: Trees shelter the house from the wind.

shift.def: a period of time worked by a group of workers who start work as another group finishes
to move, or move something, from one position or place to another
shift.ex: Lydia shifted uncomfortably in her chair.

shine.def: to produce or reflect light; to be bright
shine.ex: The sun shone brightly in a cloudless sky.

shiny.def: smooth and bright; reflecting the light

ship.def: a large boat that carries people or goods by sea
ship.ex: The yard builds ships for the Navy.

ship.def: to send or transport somebody/something by ship or by another means of transport
ship.ex: The company ships its goods all over the world.

shipping.def: ships in general or considered as a group
shipping.ex: The canal is open to shipping.

shirt.def: a piece of clothing worn on the upper part of the body, made of light cloth, with sleeves and usually with a collar and buttons down the front

shock.def: a strong feeling of surprise as a result of something happening, especially something unpleasant; the event that causes this feeling
shock.ex: I got a terrible shock the other day.
President Kennedy's assassination in Dallas on November 22, 1963, shocked the world.

shocked.def: surprised and upset; showing that somebody feels surprised and upset
shocked.ex: I was quite shocked when I found out what he'd done.

shocking.def: that offends or upsets people; that is morally wrong

shoe.def: one of a pair of objects usually made of leather or plastic that you wear on your feet

shoot.def: the part that grows up from the ground when a plant starts to grow; a new part that grows on plants or trees
to fire a gun or other weapon; to fire something from a weapon

shooting.def: a situation in which a person is shot with a gun
shooting.ex: Terrorist groups claimed responsibility for the shootings and bomb attacks.

shop.def: a building or part of a building where you can buy goods or services

shop.ex: I shop there from time to time.

shopping.def: the activity of going to shops and buying things or ordering them online

shore.def: the land along the edge of the sea, the ocean or a lake

short.def: measuring or covering a small length or distance, or a smaller length or distance than usual

short-term.def: lasting a short time; designed only for a short period of time in the future

shortage.def: a situation when there is not enough of the people or things that are needed

shot.def: the act of firing a gun; the sound this makes
shot.ex: We heard some shots in the distance.

should.def: used to show what is right, appropriate, etc., especially when criticizing somebody’s actions

shoulder.def: either of the two parts of the body between the top of each arm and the neck

shout.def: a loud cry of anger, fear, excitement, etc.
to say something in a loud voice; to speak loudly/angrily to somebody

show.def: a programme on television or the radio
show.ex: Millions of people watch the show.

show.def: to make something clear; to prove something
show.ex: The figures clearly show that her claims are false.

shower.def: a piece of equipment producing a flow of water that you stand under to wash yourself; the small room or part of a room that contains a shower

shrink.def: to become smaller, especially when washed in water that is too hot; to make clothes, cloth, etc. smaller in this way
shrink.ex: My sweater shrank in the wash.

shrug.def: to raise your shoulders and then drop them to show that you do not know or care about something

shut.def: to close something; to become closed

shy.def: nervous or embarrassed about meeting and speaking to other people
shy.ex: He is not exactly the shy and retiring type.

side.def: either of the two halves of a surface, an object or an area that is divided by an imaginary central line
side.ex: the right side of the brain

sigh.def: an act or the sound of sighing
to take and then let out a long deep breath that can be heard, to show that you are disappointed, sad, tired, etc.
sigh.ex: He sighed deeply at the thought.

sight.ex: to lose your sight (= to become blind)

sign.def: an event, an action, a fact, etc. that shows that something exists, is happening or may happen in the future
sign.ex: I should have seen the signs.

sign.def: to write your name on a document, letter, etc. to show that you have written it, that you agree with what it says, or that it is real

signal.def: a movement or sound that you make to give somebody information, instructions, a warning, etc.
to make a movement or sound to give somebody a message, an order, etc.

signature.def: your name as you usually write it, for example at the end of a letter
signature.ex: Someone had forged her signature on the cheque.

significance.def: the importance of something, especially when this has an effect on what happens in the future
significance.ex: a decision of major political significance

significant.def: large or important enough to have an effect or to be noticed
significant.ex: There are no significant differences between the two groups of students.

significantly.def: in a way that is large or important enough to have an effect on something or to be noticed

silence.def: a complete lack of noise or sound
silence.ex: Their footsteps echoed in the silence.

silent.def: where there is little or no sound; making little or no sound
silent.ex: At last the traffic fell silent.

silk.def: fine soft thread produced by silkworms
silk.ex: The caterpillar spins the silk around its entire body.

silly.def: showing a lack of thought, understanding, or judgement

silver.def: a chemical element. Silver is a shiny, grey-white precious metal used for making coins, jewellery, beautiful objects, etc.

similar.def: like somebody/something but not exactly the same

similarity.def: the state of being similar to somebody/something but not exactly the same
similarity.ex: The report highlights the similarity between the two groups.

similarly.ex: It is a little cheaper than other similarly sized cars.

simple.def: not complicated; easy to understand or do

simply.def: used to emphasize how easy or basic something is

simulate.def: to create particular conditions that exist in real life using computers, models, etc., usually for study or training purposes
simulate.ex: Computer software can be used to simulate conditions on the seabed.

simulation.def: a situation in which a particular set of conditions is created artificially in order to study or experience something that could exist in reality
simulation.ex: a computer simulation of how the planet functions

simultaneously.def: at the same time as something else
simultaneously.ex: The game will be broadcast simultaneously on TV and radio.

sin.def: an offence against God or against a religious or moral law

since.def: from a time in the past until a later past time, or until now
since.ex: She went for a run on Monday and has not been seen since.

since.def: from an event in the past until a later past event, or until now
since.ex: Cath hasn't phoned since she went to Berlin.

sincere.def: showing what you really think or feel

sing.def: to make musical sounds with your voice in the form of a song or tune
sing.ex: I just can't sing in tune!

singer.def: a person who sings, or whose job is singing, especially in public

singing.def: the activity of making musical sounds with your voice

single.ex: He sent her a single red rose.

single.def: a ticket that allows you to travel to a place but not back again
single.ex: How much is a single to York?

sink.def: to go down below the surface or towards the bottom of a liquid or soft substance
sink.ex: The ship sank to the bottom of the sea.

sir.def: used as a polite way of addressing a man whose name you do not know, for example in a shop or restaurant
sir.ex: Good morning, sir. Can I help you?

sister.def: a girl or woman who has the same mother and father as another person

sit.def: to rest your weight on your bottom with your back straight, for example on/in a chair
sit.ex: She sat and stared at the letter in front of her.

site.def: a place where a building, town, etc. was, is or will be located
site.ex: the site of a sixteenth-century abbey

situated.def: in a particular place or position
situated.ex: My bedroom was situated on the top floor of the house.

situation.def: all the circumstances and things that are happening at a particular time and in a particular place

size.def: how large or small a person or thing is

sketch.def: a simple picture that is drawn quickly and does not have many details
sketch.ex: The artist is making sketches for his next painting.

ski.def: connected with the sport of skiing
one of a pair of long narrow pieces of wood, metal or plastic that you attach to boots so that you can move smoothly over snow
to move over snow on skis, especially as a sport
ski.ex: Our children want to learn to ski.

skiing.def: the sport or activity of moving over snow on skis
skiing.ex: He also enjoys skiing and mountain biking.

skill.def: the ability to do something well
skill.ex: The job requires skill and an eye for detail.

skilled.def: having enough ability, experience and knowledge to be able to do something well

skin.def: the layer of tissue that covers the body

skip.def: to move forwards lightly and quickly making a little jump with each step
skip.ex: She skipped happily along beside me.

skirt.def: a piece of clothing that hangs from the middle part of the body

skull.def: the bone structure that forms the head and surrounds and protects the brain

sky.def: the space above the earth that you can see when you look up, where clouds and the sun, moon and stars appear
sky.ex: The sky suddenly went dark and it started to rain.

slam.def: to shut, or to make something shut, with a lot of force, making a loud noise
slam.ex: I heard the door slam behind him.

slap.def: to hit somebody/something with the flat part of your hand

slash.def: to make a long cut with a sharp object, especially in a violent way
slash.ex: Someone had slashed the tyres on my car.

slave.def: a person who is owned by another person and is forced to work for and obey them
slave.ex: A former slave, he graduated from Clafflin University in South Carolina.

slavery.def: the state of being forced to work as a slave

sleep.def: the natural state of rest in which your eyes are closed, your body is not active, and your mind is not conscious
sleep.ex: I need to get some sleep.

sleep.def: to rest with your eyes closed and your mind and body not active
sleep.ex: Let her sleep—it'll do her good.

slice.def: a thin flat piece of bread, meat, cheese, etc. that has been cut off a larger piece; a piece of cake that has been cut from a larger cake
slice.ex: Cut the meat into thin slices.

slide.def: one page of an electronic presentation, that may contain text and images, that is usually viewed on a computer screen or projected onto a larger screen
slide.ex: Here's a slide showing target markets.

slide.def: to move easily over a smooth or wet surface; to make something move in this way
slide.ex: As I turned left on a bend, my car started to slide.

slip.def: to slide a short distance by accident so that you fall or nearly fall
slip.ex: She slipped and landed flat on her back.

slogan.def: a word or phrase that is easy to remember, used for example by a political party or in advertising to attract people’s attention or to suggest an idea quickly

slope.def: a surface or piece of land that slopes (= is higher at one end than the other)
slope.ex: The town is built on a slope.

slope.def: to be at an angle so that it is higher at one end than the other

slot.def: a long, narrow opening, into which you put or fit something
slot.ex: to put some coins in the slot

slow.def: not moving, acting or done quickly; taking a long time; not fast
to go or to make something/somebody go at a slower speed or be less active
slow.ex: Economic growth has slowed a little.

slowly.def: at a slow speed; not quickly

small.def: not large in size, number, degree, amount, etc.

smart.def: looking clean and neat; well dressed in fashionable and/or formal clothes
smart.ex: I have to be smart for work.

smartphone.def: a mobile phone that also has some of the functions of a computer, for example the facility to use apps and the internet
smartphone.ex: You can use your smartphone to access the internet.

smash.def: to break something, or to be broken, violently and noisily into many pieces

smell.def: the quality of something that people and animals sense through their noses

smile.def: the expression that you have on your face when you are happy or you think something is funny, in which the corners of your mouth turn upwards
to make a smile appear on your face

smoke.def: the grey, white or black gas that is produced by something burning
to take smoke from a cigarette, pipe, etc. into your mouth and let it out again
smoke.ex: Do you mind if I smoke?

smoking.def: the activity or habit of smoking cigarettes, etc.
smoking.ex: No Smoking (= for example, on a notice)

smooth.def: completely flat and even, without any rough areas or holes
smooth.ex: the smooth surface of the metal

snake.def: a reptile with a very long thin body and no legs. There are many types of snake, some of which are poisonous.
snake.ex: a snake coiled up in the grass

snap.def: to break something suddenly with a sharp noise; to be broken in this way
snap.ex: The wind had snapped the tree in two.

snow.def: small soft white pieces (called flakes) of frozen water that fall from the sky in cold weather; this substance when it is lying on the ground
when it snows, snow falls from the sky
snow.ex: It's been snowing heavily all day.

so.def: used to show the reason for something
so.ex: It was still painful, so I went to see a doctor.

so-called.def: used to show that you do not think that the word or phrase that is being used to describe somebody/something is appropriate

soak.def: to put something in liquid for a time so that it becomes completely wet; to become completely wet in this way
soak.ex: I usually soak the beans overnight.

soap.def: a substance that you use with water for washing your body
soap.ex: Wash the affected area with soap and water.

soar.def: if the value, amount or level of something soars, it rises very quickly

soccer.def: a game played by two teams of 11 players, using a round ball which players kick up and down the playing field. Teams try to kick the ball into the other team’s goal.
soccer.ex: I played soccer when I was younger.

social.def: connected with activities in which people meet each other for pleasure
social.ex: She has a busy social life.

society.def: people in general, living together in communities
society.ex: She believes that the arts benefit society as a whole.

sock.def: a piece of clothing that is worn over the foot, ankle and lower part of the leg, especially inside a shoe

soft.def: changing shape easily when pressed; not stiff or hard

software.def: the programs used by a computer for doing particular jobs

soil.def: the top layer of the earth in which plants, trees, etc. grow

solar.def: of or connected with the sun

soldier.def: a member of an army, especially one who is not an officer
soldier.ex: They visited the graves of US soldiers killed in the First World War.

sole.ex: the sole surviving member of the family

solely.ex: She was motivated solely by self-interest.

solicitor.def: a lawyer who prepares legal documents, for example for the sale of land or buildings, advises people on legal matters, and can speak for them in some courts of law
solicitor.ex: Her first step was to contact a solicitor for advice.

solid.def: hard; not in the form of a liquid or gas
solid.ex: The planet Jupiter may have no solid surface at all.

solid.def: a substance or an object that is solid, not a liquid or a gas

solidarity.def: support by one person or group of people for another because they share feelings, opinions, aims, etc.

solo.def: done by one person alone, without anyone helping them
a piece of music, dance or entertainment performed by only one person
solo.ex: The song features an extended guitar solo.

solution.def: a way of solving a problem or dealing with a difficult situation
solution.ex: Attempts to find a solution have failed.

solve.def: to find a way of dealing with a problem or difficult situation
solve.ex: Attempts are being made to solve the problem of waste disposal.

some.def: used with uncountable nouns or plural countable nouns to mean ‘an amount of’ or ‘a number of’, when the amount or number is not given
some.ex: There's some milk in the fridge.

some.def: used to refer to an amount of something or a number of people or things when the amount or number is not given

somebody.def: a person who is not known or mentioned by name

somehow.def: in a way that is not known or certain
somehow.ex: We must stop him from seeing her somehow.

someone.def: a person who is not known or mentioned by name

something.def: a thing that is not known or mentioned by name
something.ex: We stopped for something to eat.

sometime.def: at a time that you do not know exactly or has not yet been decided
sometime.ex: I saw him sometime last summer.

sometimes.def: occasionally rather than all of the time

somewhere.def: in, at or to a place that you do not know or do not mention by name
somewhere.ex: I read somewhere that she refuses to do interviews.

somewhere.def: a place that you do not know or do not mention by name
somewhere.ex: We need to find somewhere to live.

song.def: a short piece of music with words that you sing

soon.def: in a short time from now; a short time after something else has happened
soon.ex: She sold the house soon after her husband died.

sophisticated.def: clever and complicated in the way that it works or is presented

sorry.def: feeling sad and ashamed about something that has been done
sorry.ex: We're very sorry about the damage to your car.

sorry.def: used when you are apologizing for something

sort.def: a group or type of people or things that are similar in a particular way
sort.ex: sort of somebody/something, ‘What sort of music do you like?’ ‘Oh, all sorts.’

sort.def: to arrange things in groups or in a particular order according to their type, etc.; to separate things of one type from others
sort.ex: I started at the bottom, answering phones and sorting the mail.

soul.def: the spiritual part of a person, believed to exist after death
soul.ex: He believed his immortal soul was in peril.

sound.def: sensible; that you can rely on and that will probably give good results
sound.ex: He's a person of very sound judgement.

sound.def: to give a particular impression when heard or read about

soup.def: a liquid food made by boiling meat, vegetables, etc. in water, often eaten as the first course of a meal

source.def: a place, person or thing that you get something from

south.def: the direction that is on your right when you watch the sun rise; one of the four main points of the compass

southern.def: located in the south or facing south; connected with or typical of the south part of the world or a region
southern.ex: the southern slopes of the mountains

sovereignty.def: complete power to govern a country
sovereignty.ex: The country claimed sovereignty over the island.

space.def: the area outside the earth’s atmosphere where all the other planets and stars are
space.ex: Valentina Tereshkova was the first woman in space.
