spam.def: advertising material sent by email to people who have not asked for it; advertising material on the internet that is not wanted

span.def: the length of time that something lasts or is able to continue
span.ex: I worked with him over a span of six years.

span.def: to last all through a period of time or to cover the whole of it

spare.def: available to do what you want with rather than work
spare.ex: He's studying music in his spare time.

spare.def: to make something such as time or money available to somebody or for something, especially when it requires an effort for you to do this
spare.ex: I'd love to have a break, but I can't spare the time just now.

spark.def: to cause something to start or develop, especially suddenly
spark.ex: The proposal would spark a storm of protest around the country.

speak.def: to talk to somebody about something; to have a conversation with somebody
speak.ex: The President refused to speak to reporters.

speaker.def: a person who gives a talk or makes a speech
speaker.ex: He was a guest speaker at the conference.

special.def: not ordinary or usual; different from what is normal
special.ex: The school will only allow this in special circumstances.

specialist.def: having or involving expert knowledge of a particular area of work, study or medicine
a person who is an expert in a particular area of work or study
specialist.ex: specialist in something, a specialist in Japanese history

specialize.def: to become an expert in a particular area of work, study or business; to spend more time on one area of work, etc. than on others
specialize.ex: Many students prefer not to specialize too soon.

specialized.def: designed or developed for a particular purpose or area of knowledge

species.def: a group into which animals, plants, etc. that are able to have sex with each other and produce healthy young are divided, smaller than a genus and identified by a Latin name
species.ex: a conservation area for endangered species

specific.def: connected with one particular thing only

specifically.def: connected with or intended for one particular thing only
specifically.ex: liquid vitamins specifically designed for children

specification.def: a detailed description of how something is, or should be, designed or made
specification.ex: the technical specifications of the new model (= of car)

specify.def: to state something, especially by giving an exact measurement, time, exact instructions, etc.
specify.ex: Remember to specify your size when ordering clothes.

specimen.def: a small amount of something that shows what the rest of it is like
specimen.ex: Astronauts have brought back specimens of rock from the moon.

spectacle.def: two lenses in a frame that rests on the nose and ears. People wear spectacles in order to be able to see better or to protect their eyes from bright light.

spectacular.ex: The coastal road has spectacular scenery.

spectator.def: a person who is watching an event, especially a sports event
spectator.ex: The new football stadium will hold 75 000 spectators.

spectrum.def: a band of coloured lights in order of their wavelengths, as seen in a rainbow and into which light may be separated
spectrum.ex: A spectrum is formed by a ray of light passing through a prism.

speculate.def: to form an opinion about something without knowing all the details or facts
speculate.ex: We all speculated about the reasons for her resignation.

speculation.def: the act of forming opinions about what has happened or what might happen without knowing all the facts
speculation.ex: His private life is the subject of much speculation.

speech.def: a formal talk that a person gives to an audience
speech.ex: Several people made speeches at the wedding.

speed.def: the rate at which somebody/something moves or travels

speed.ex: They sped off to get help.

spell.def: a short period of time during which something lasts
spell.ex: We had a spell of warm weather in April.

spell.def: to say or write the letters of a word in the correct order
spell.ex: How do you spell your surname?

spelling.def: the act of forming words correctly from individual letters; the ability to do this
spelling.ex: the differences between British and American spelling

spend.def: to give money to pay for goods, services, etc.
spend.ex: I've spent all my money already.

spending.def: the amount of money that is spent by a government, an organization or a person

sphere.def: a solid figure that is completely round, with every point on its surface at an equal distance from the centre
sphere.ex: The Earth is not a perfect sphere.

spice.def: one of the various types of powder or seed that come from plants and are used in cooking. Spices have a strong taste and smell.
spice.ex: common spices such as ginger and cinnamon

spicy.def: having a strong taste because spices have been added to it

spider.def: a small creature with eight thin legs. Many spiders spin webs to catch insects for food.

spill.def: to flow over the edge of a container by accident; to make liquid do this
spill.ex: Water had spilled out of the bucket onto the floor.

spin.ex: Only in the last 50 years have we had clocks accurate enough to measure changes in the earth's spin.

spin.def: to turn round and round quickly; to make something do this
spin.ex: The plane was spinning out of control.

spine.def: the row of small bones that are connected together down the middle of the back
spine.ex: A shiver went down my spine.

spirit.def: the part of a person that includes their mind, feelings and character rather than their body
spirit.ex: You are underestimating the power of the human spirit to overcome difficulties.

spiritual.def: connected with the human spirit, rather than the body or physical things

spite.def: a feeling of wanting to hurt or upset somebody
spite.ex: I'm sure he only said it out of spite.

split.def: an argument or disagreement that divides a group of people or makes somebody separate from somebody else
split.ex: Ten years after their acrimonious split, the band has reformed.

split.def: to divide, or to make something divide, into two or more parts
split.ex: He was a member of the team that split the atom in 1932.

spoil.def: to change something good into something bad, unpleasant, etc.
spoil.ex: Our camping trip was spoilt by bad weather.

spoken.def: involving speaking rather than writing; expressed in speech rather than in writing

spokesman.def: a person who speaks for a group or an organization

spokesperson.def: a person who speaks for a group or an organization

spokeswoman.def: a woman who speaks for a group or an organization

sponsor.def: a person or company that pays for a radio or television programme, or for a concert or sporting event, usually in return for advertising
sponsor.ex: The programme is funded by a number of corporate sponsors.

sponsor.def: to pay the costs of a particular event, programme, etc. as a way of advertising
sponsor.ex: Sports events are no longer sponsored by the tobacco industry.

spoon.def: a tool that has a handle with a shallow bowl at the end, used for mixing, serving and eating food

sport.def: activity that you do for pleasure and that needs physical effort or skill, usually done in a special area and according to fixed rules
sport.ex: There are excellent facilities for sport and recreation.

spot.def: a small round area that has a different colour or feels different from the surface it is on
spot.ex: Which has spots, the leopard or the tiger?

spot.def: to see or notice a person or thing, especially suddenly or when it is not easy to do so
spot.ex: Can you spot the difference between these two pictures?

spotlight.def: a light with a single, very bright beam that can be directed at a particular place or person, especially a performer on the stage
spotlight.ex: The room was lit by spotlights.

spouse.ex: Fill in your spouse’s name here.

spread.def: an increase in the amount or number of something that there is, or in the area that is affected by something
spread.ex: measures to halt the spread of the disease

spread.def: to affect or make something affect, be known by, or be used by more and more people
spread.ex: The news had spread and was causing great excitement.

spring.def: the season between winter and summer when plants begin to grow
spring.ex: The following spring, the three artists travelled to California.

spring.def: to move suddenly and with one quick movement in a particular direction
spring.ex: The cat crouched ready to spring.

spy.def: a person who tries to get secret information about another country, organization or person, especially somebody who is employed by a government or the police
spy.ex: He was denounced as a foreign spy.

spy.def: to collect secret information about another country, organization or person
spy.ex: He spied for his government for more than ten years.

squad.def: a section of a police force that deals with a particular type of crime

square.def: having four straight equal sides and four angles of 90°
a shape with four straight sides of equal length and four angles of 90°; a piece of something that has this shape
square.ex: First break the chocolate into squares.

squeeze.def: to press something, especially with your fingers
squeeze.ex: to squeeze a tube of toothpaste

stab.def: to push a sharp, pointed object, especially a knife, into somebody, killing or injuring them
stab.ex: He was stabbed to death in a racist attack.

stability.def: the quality or state of being steady and not changing or being upset in any way (= the quality of being stable)

stabilize.def: to become or to make something become steady and unlikely to change; to make something stable

stable.def: fixed or steady; not likely to move, change or fail
stable.ex: The patient's condition is stable (= it is not getting worse).

stadium.def: a large sports ground surrounded by rows of seats and usually other buildings

staff.def: all the workers employed in an organization considered as a group

stage.def: a period or state that something/somebody passes through while developing or making progress
stage.ex: I can't make a decision at this stage.

stage.def: to organize and present a play or an event for people to see
stage.ex: to stage a play/an event/an exhibition

stair.def: a set of steps built between two floors inside a building
stair.ex: We had to carry the piano up three flights of stairs.

stake.def: money that somebody invests in a company
stake.ex: a 20 per cent stake in the business

stall.def: a table or small shop with an open front that people sell things from, especially at a market
stall.ex: He works on a market stall in the Square.

stamp.def: a small piece of paper with a design on it that you buy and stick on an envelope or a package before you post it

stance.def: the opinions that somebody has about something and expresses publicly
stance.ex: What is the newspaper's stance on the war?

stand.def: an attitude towards something or an opinion that you make clear to people
stand.ex: He has avoided taking a firm stand.

stand.def: to be on your feet; to be in a vertical position
stand.ex: She was too weak to stand.

standard.def: average or normal rather than having special or unusual features
standard.ex: the standard rate of tax (= paid by everyone)

standard.def: a level of quality, especially one that people think is acceptable
standard.ex: There has been a fall in living standards.

standing.def: done from a position in which you are standing rather than sitting or running

star.def: a large ball of burning gas in space that we see as a point of light in the sky at night
star.ex: There was a big moon and hundreds of stars were shining overhead.

star.def: to have one of the main parts in a film, play, etc.
star.ex: He starred in that movie about the crazy professor.

stare.def: to look at somebody/something for a long time

stark.def: unpleasant; real, and impossible to avoid
stark.ex: The author paints a stark picture of life in a prison camp.

start.def: the point at which something begins
start.ex: If we don't hurry, we'll miss the start.

start.def: to begin doing or using something

starve.def: to suffer or die because you do not have enough food to eat; to make somebody suffer or die in this way
starve.ex: The animals were left to starve to death.

state.def: provided or controlled by the government of a country
a country considered as an organized political community controlled by one government
to formally write or say something, especially in a careful and clear way
state.ex: He has already stated his intention to run for election.

statement.def: something that you say or write that gives information or an opinion
statement.ex: Are the following statements true or false?

station.def: a place where trains stop so that passengers can get on and off; the buildings connected with this

statistic.def: a collection of information shown in numbers
statistic.ex: Analysis of crime statistics showed high levels of violent crime within the area.

statistical.def: connected with or based on statistics

statue.def: a figure of a person or an animal in stone, metal, etc., usually the same size as in real life or larger

status.def: the legal position of a person, group or country

stay.def: a period of staying; a visit
stay.ex: I enjoyed my stay in Prague.

stay.def: to continue to be in a particular place for a period of time without moving away

steadily.def: gradually and in an even and regular way
steadily.ex: The company's exports have been increasing steadily.

steady.def: developing, growing, etc. gradually and in an even and regular way
steady.ex: We've had five years of steady economic growth.

steal.def: to take something from a person, shop, etc. without permission and without intending to return it or pay for it
steal.ex: I'll report you to the police if I catch you stealing again.

steam.def: the hot gas that water changes into when it boils
steam.ex: Steam rose from the boiling kettle.

steel.def: a strong, hard metal that is made of a mixture of iron and carbon

steep.def: rising or falling quickly, not gradually

steer.def: to control the direction in which a boat, car, etc. moves
steer.ex: He steered the boat into the harbour.

stem.def: the main long, thin part of a plant above the ground from which the leaves or flowers grow; a smaller part that grows from this and supports flowers or leaves
to stop something that is flowing from spreading or increasing
stem.ex: The cut was bandaged to stem the bleeding.

step.def: one of a series of things that you do in order to achieve something
step.ex: We are taking steps to prevent pollution.

step.def: to lift your foot and move it in a particular direction or put it on or in something; to move a short distance

stereotype.def: a fixed idea or image that many people have of a particular type of person or thing, but which is often not true in reality and may cause hurt and offence

stick.def: a thin piece of wood that has fallen or been broken from a tree
stick.ex: We collected dry sticks to start a fire.

stick.def: to fix something to something else, usually with a sticky substance; to become fixed to something in this way
stick.ex: He stuck a stamp on the envelope.

sticky.def: made of or covered in a substance that sticks to things that touch it

stiff.ex: a sheet of stiff black cardboard

still.ex: A fallen tree floated in the still water.

still.def: continuing until a particular point in time and not finishing
still.ex: I am very happy that you all are still alive and well.

stimulate.def: to make something develop or become more active; to encourage something
stimulate.ex: The exhibition has stimulated interest in her work.

stimulus.def: something that helps somebody/something to develop better or more quickly
stimulus.ex: Books provide children with ideas and a stimulus for play.

stir.def: to move a liquid or substance around, using a spoon or something similar, in order to mix it completely

stock.def: a supply of goods that is available for sale in a shop
stock.ex: We have a fast turnover of stock.

stomach.def: the organ inside the body where food goes when you eat it

stone.def: a hard solid mineral substance that is found in the ground, often used for building
stone.ex: Most of the houses are built of stone.

stop.def: a place where a bus or train stops regularly for passengers to get on or off
stop.ex: I get off at the next stop.

stop.def: to no longer move; to make somebody/something no longer move
stop.ex: The car stopped at the traffic lights.

storage.def: the process of keeping something in a particular place until it is needed; the space where things can be kept
storage.ex: tables that fold flat for storage

store.def: a large shop that sells many different types of goods
store.ex: It's available at London's three biggest stores.

store.def: to put something somewhere and keep it there to use later
store.ex: We do not have adequate space to store these documents.

storm.def: very bad weather with strong winds and rain, and often thunder and lightning

story.def: a description of events and people that the writer or speaker has invented in order to entertain people

straight.def: without a bend or curve; going in one direction only
not in a curve or at an angle; in a straight line
straight.ex: Keep straight on for two miles.

straightforward.def: easy to do or to understand; not complicated

strain.def: pressure on a system or relationship because great demands are being placed on it
strain.ex: The transport service cannot cope with the strain of so many additional passengers.

strand.def: a single thin piece of thread, wire, hair, etc.
strand.ex: He pulled at a loose strand of wool in his sweater.

strange.def: unusual or surprising, especially in a way that is difficult to understand
strange.ex: A strange thing happened this morning.

stranger.def: a person that you do not know
stranger.ex: There was a complete stranger sitting at my desk.

strategic.def: done as part of a plan that is meant to achieve a particular purpose or to gain an advantage

strategy.def: a plan that is intended to achieve a particular purpose

street.def: a public road in a city or town that has houses and buildings on one side or both sides
street.ex: I was just walking along the street when it happened.

strength.def: the quality of being physically strong
strength.ex: He pushed against the rock with all his strength.

strengthen.def: to become more powerful or effective; to make somebody/something more powerful or effective
strengthen.ex: Her position in the party has strengthened in recent weeks.

stress.def: pressure or worry caused by problems in somebody’s life or by having too much to do
to give extra force to a word or syllable when saying it
stress.ex: You stress the first syllable in ‘happiness’.

stretch.def: an area of land or water, especially a long one
stretch.ex: a particularly dangerous stretch of road

stretch.def: to make something longer, wider or looser, for example by pulling it; to become longer, etc. in this way
stretch.ex: Is there any way of stretching shoes?

strictly.def: with a lot of control and rules that must be obeyed
strictly.ex: She was brought up very strictly.

strike.def: a period of time when an organized group of employees of a company stops working because of an argument over pay or conditions
to hit somebody/something hard or with force

striking.def: interesting and unusual enough to attract attention

string.def: long, thin material used for tying things together, made of several threads that have been twisted together; a piece of string used to fasten or pull something or keep something in place

strip.def: a long narrow piece of paper, metal, cloth, etc.
to take off all or most of your clothes or another person’s clothes
strip.ex: I stripped and washed myself all over.

strive.def: to try very hard to achieve something
strive.ex: We encourage all members to strive for the highest standards.

stroke.def: an act of hitting a ball, for example with a bat or racket

strong.def: having a lot of physical power so that you can lift heavy weights, do hard physical work, etc.
strong.ex: He's strong enough to lift a car!

strongly.def: in a way that shows definite and serious opinions or beliefs

structural.def: connected with the way in which something is built or organized
structural.ex: Storms have caused structural damage to hundreds of homes.

structure.def: the way in which the parts of something are connected together, arranged or organized; a particular arrangement of parts
to arrange or organize something into a system or pattern
structure.ex: How well does the teacher structure the lessons?

struggle.def: a hard fight in which people try to obtain or achieve something, especially something that somebody else does not want them to have
struggle.ex: Marx wrote about the class struggle.

struggle.def: to try very hard to do something when it is difficult or when there are a lot of problems
struggle.ex: life as a struggling artist (= one who is very poor)

student.def: a person who is studying at a university or college

studio.def: a room where radio or television programmes are recorded and broadcast from, or where music is recorded

study.def: the activity of learning or gaining knowledge, either from books or by examining things in the world
study.ex: a room set aside for private study

study.def: to spend time learning about a subject by reading, going to college, etc.
study.ex: He sat up very late that night, studying.

stuff.def: used to refer to a substance, material, group of objects, etc. when you do not know the name, when the name is not important or when it is obvious what you are talking about
stuff.ex: What's all that sticky stuff on the carpet?

stuff.def: to fill a space or container tightly with something
stuff.ex: She had 500 envelopes to stuff with leaflets.

stumble.def: to hit your foot against something while you are walking or running and almost fall

stun.def: to make a person or an animal unconscious for a short time, especially by hitting them on the head
stun.ex: The fall stunned me for a moment.

stupid.def: showing a lack of thought or good judgement

style.def: the particular way in which something is done
style.ex: a wide range of musical styles

subject.def: likely to be affected by something, especially something bad
subject.ex: At this stage these are proposals and are still subject to change.

subject.def: a thing or person that is being discussed, described or dealt with
subject.ex: Walker's work has been the subject of much debate.

submission.def: the act of accepting that somebody has defeated you and that you must obey them

submit.def: to give a document, proposal, etc. to somebody in authority so that they can study or consider it
submit.ex: submit something, to submit an application/a claim/a proposal

subscriber.def: a person who pays money, usually once a year, to receive regular copies of a magazine or newspaper or have access to it online

subscription.def: an amount of money that you pay regularly to receive a service, be a member of a club, support a charity or receive regular copies of a newspaper or magazine; the act of paying this money

subsequent.def: happening or coming after something else

subsequently.def: afterwards; later; after something else has happened
subsequently.ex: The original interview notes were subsequently lost.

subsidy.def: money that is paid by a government or an organization to reduce the costs of services or of producing goods so that their prices can be kept low

substance.def: a type of solid, liquid or gas that has particular qualities

substantial.def: large in amount, value or importance

substitute.def: a person or thing that you use or have instead of the one you normally use or have
to take the place of somebody/something else; to use somebody/something instead of somebody/something else
substitute.ex: Nothing can substitute for the advice your doctor is able to give you.

substitution.def: an act of using one person or thing in the place of another
substitution.ex: Two substitutions were made during the game.

subtle.def: not very obvious or easy to notice

suburb.def: an area where people live that is outside the centre of a city

suburban.def: in or connected with a suburb

succeed.def: to achieve something that you have been trying to do or get; to have the result or effect that was intended

success.def: the fact that you have achieved something that you want and have been trying to do or get
success.ex: The company has achieved considerable success in this market.

successful.def: achieving your aims or what was intended

successfully.def: in a way that achieves your aims or what was intended
successfully.ex: She had already successfully completed these courses.

succession.def: a number of people or things that follow each other in time or order

successive.def: following immediately one after the other
successive.ex: This was their fourth successive win.

successor.def: a person or thing that comes after somebody/something else and takes their/its place
successor.ex: Who's the likely successor to him as party leader?

such.def: used to emphasize the great degree of something
such.ex: This issue was of such importance that we could not afford to ignore it.

suck.def: to take liquid, air, etc. into your mouth by using the muscles of your lips
suck.ex: to suck the juice from an orange

sudden.def: happening or done quickly and unexpectedly
sudden.ex: News of his sudden and unexpected death came as a great shock.

suddenly.ex: I suddenly realized what I had to do.

sue.def: to make a claim against a person or an organization in court about something that they have said or done to harm you
sue.ex: They threatened to sue if the work was not completed.

suffer.def: to be badly affected by a disease, pain, sad feelings, a lack of something, etc.
suffer.ex: I hate to see animals suffering.

suffering.ex: Death finally brought an end to her suffering.

sufficient.def: enough for a particular purpose; as much as you need
sufficient.ex: Allow sufficient time to get there.

sufficiently.def: enough for a particular purpose; as much as you need
sufficiently.ex: The following day she felt sufficiently well to go to work.

sugar.def: a sweet substance, often in the form of white or brown crystals, made from the juices of various plants, used in cooking or to make tea, coffee, etc. sweeter
sugar.ex: Do you take sugar (= have it in your tea, coffee, etc.)?

suggest.def: to put forward an idea or a plan for other people to think about
suggest.ex: I'd like to suggest a different explanation for the company's decline.

suggestion.def: an idea or a plan that you mention for somebody else to think about

suicide.def: the act of killing yourself deliberately

suit.def: a set of clothes made of the same cloth, including a jacket and trousers or a skirt
suit.ex: She was wearing a grey business suit.

suit.def: to be convenient or useful for somebody
suit.ex: If we met at two, would that suit you?

suitable.def: right or appropriate for a particular purpose or occasion

suite.def: a set of rooms, especially in a hotel

sum.ex: You will be fined the sum of £200.

summarize.def: to give a summary of something
summarize.ex: This essay briefly summarizes some of our approaches.

summary.def: a short statement that gives only the main points of something, not the details

summer.def: the warmest season of the year, coming between spring and autumn
summer.ex: We're going away in the summer.

summit.def: the highest point of something, especially the top of a mountain
summit.ex: We reached the summit at noon.

sun.def: the star that shines in the sky during the day and gives the earth heat and light
sun.ex: The sun was shining and birds were singing.

sunday.def: the day of the week after Saturday and before Monday, thought of as either the first or the last day of the week

superior.def: better in quality than somebody/something else; greater than somebody/something else

supermarket.def: a large shop that sells food, drinks and goods used in the home. People choose what they want from the shelves and pay for it as they leave.
supermarket.ex: I only shop at this supermarket.

supervise.def: to be in charge of somebody/something and make sure that everything is done correctly, safely, etc.

supervision.def: the work or activity involved in being in charge of somebody/something and making sure that everything is done correctly, safely, etc.
supervision.ex: Very young children should not be left to play without supervision.

supervisor.ex: I have a meeting with my supervisor about my research topic.

supplement.def: a thing that is added to something else to improve or complete it
supplement.ex: vitamin/dietary supplements (= vitamins and other foods eaten in addition to what you usually eat)

supplement.def: to add something to something in order to improve it or make it more complete
supplement.ex: supplement something with something, a diet supplemented with vitamin pills

supply.def: an amount of something that is provided or available to be used
supply.ex: Advances in agriculture increased the food supply.

supply.def: to provide somebody/something with something that they need or want, especially in large quantities
supply.ex: The company supplied sports equipment to schools.

support.def: approval that you give to somebody/something because you want them to be successful
support.ex: There is strong public support for the policy.

support.def: to help or encourage somebody/something by saying or showing that you agree with them/it
support.ex: support somebody/something, to support an idea/a view

supporter.def: a person who supports a political party, an idea, etc.

supportive.def: encouraging somebody or giving them help or sympathy

suppose.def: to think or believe that something is true or possible (based on the knowledge that you have)
suppose.ex: Getting a visa isn't as simple as you might suppose.

supposedly.def: according to what is generally thought or believed but not known for certain
supposedly.ex: The novel is supposedly based on a true story.

suppress.def: to put an end, often by force, to a group or an activity that is believed to threaten authority

supreme.ex: the Supreme Commander of the armed forces

sure.def: confident that you know something or that you are right
sure.ex: ‘Is that John over there?’ ‘I'm not sure’.
‘Will you get me my coat?’ ‘Sure, where is it?’

surely.def: used to show that you are almost certain of what you are saying and want other people to agree with you
surely.ex: Surely we should do something about it?

surface.def: the outside or top layer of something
surface.ex: We need a flat, smooth surface to play the game on.

surge.def: a sudden increase of a strong feeling
surge.ex: She felt a sudden surge of anger.

surge.def: to move quickly and with force in a particular direction
surge.ex: The gates opened and the crowd surged forward.

surgeon.def: a doctor who is trained to perform surgery (= medical operations that involve cutting open a person’s body)

surgery.def: medical treatment of injuries or diseases that involves cutting open a person’s body and often removing or replacing some parts; the branch of medicine connected with this treatment

surgical.def: used in or connected with surgery

surplus.def: an amount that is extra or more than you need

surprise.def: an event, a piece of news, etc. that is unexpected or that happens suddenly

surprise.ex: It wouldn't surprise me if they got married soon.

surrender.def: to admit that you have been defeated and want to stop fighting; to allow yourself to be caught, taken prisoner, etc.
surrender.ex: The rebel soldiers were forced to surrender.

surrounding.def: that is near or around something

surveillance.def: the act of carefully watching a person suspected of a crime or a place where a crime may be committed
surveillance.ex: The police are keeping the suspects under constant surveillance.

survey.def: an investigation of the opinions, behaviour, etc. of a particular group of people, which is usually done by asking them questions
survey.ex: A recent survey showed 75 per cent of those questioned were in favour of the plan.

survey.def: to look carefully at the whole of something, especially in order to get a general impression of it
survey.ex: The next morning we surveyed the damage caused by the fire.

survival.def: the state of continuing to live or exist, often despite difficulty or danger

survive.def: to continue to live or exist
survive.ex: She was the last surviving member of the family.

survivor.def: a person who continues to live, especially despite being nearly killed or experiencing great danger or difficulty
survivor.ex: the sole/only survivor of the massacre

suspect.def: a person who is suspected of a crime or of having done something wrong
to have an idea that something is probably true or likely to happen, especially something bad, but without having definite proof
suspect.ex: As I had suspected all along, he was not a real policeman.

suspend.def: to hang something from something else
suspend.ex: A lamp was suspended from the ceiling.

suspension.def: the act of officially removing somebody from their job, school, team, etc. for a period of time, usually as a punishment

suspicion.def: a feeling that somebody has done something wrong, illegal or dishonest, even though you have no proof
suspicion.ex: They drove away slowly to avoid arousing suspicion.

suspicious.def: feeling that somebody has done something wrong, illegal or dishonest, without having any proof
suspicious.ex: They became suspicious of his behaviour and contacted the police.

sustain.def: to provide enough of what somebody/something needs in order to live or exist

sustainable.def: involving the use of natural products and energy in a way that does not harm the environment

swallow.def: to make food, drink, etc. go down your throat into your stomach
swallow.ex: Always chew food well before swallowing it.

swear.def: to use rude or offensive language, usually because you are angry
swear.ex: She fell over and swore loudly.

sweater.def: a piece of clothing for the upper part of the body, made of wool or cotton, with long sleeves
sweater.ex: She wore jeans and a sweater.

sweep.def: to clean a room, surface, etc. using a broom (= a type of brush on a long handle)

sweet.def: containing, or tasting as if it contains, a lot of sugar
sweet.ex: a cup of hot sweet tea

sweet.def: a small piece of sweet food, usually made with sugar and/or chocolate and eaten between meals

swim.def: a period of time during which you swim
to move through water by moving your arms and legs, without touching the bottom

swimming.def: the sport or activity of swimming
swimming.ex: Swimming is a good form of exercise.

swing.ex: He took a wild swing at the ball.

swing.def: to move backwards or forwards or from side to side while hanging from a fixed point; to make something do this
swing.ex: His arms swung as he walked.

switch.def: a small device that you press or move up and down in order to turn a light or piece of electrical equipment on and off
to change or make something change from one thing to another
switch.ex: We're in the process of switching over to a new system of invoicing.

sword.def: a weapon with a long metal blade (= sharp cutting part) and a handle
sword.ex: to draw/sheathe a sword (= to take it out of/put it into its cover)

symbol.def: a person, an object, an event, etc. that represents a more general quality or situation
symbol.ex: The vase is decorated with religious symbols.

symbolic.def: containing symbols, or being used as a symbol
symbolic.ex: He shook his fist in a symbolic gesture of defiance.

sympathetic.def: kind to somebody who is hurt or sad; showing that you understand and care about their problems

sympathy.def: the feeling of being sorry for somebody; showing that you understand and care about somebody’s problems
sympathy.ex: sympathy for somebody, to express/feel sympathy for somebody

symptom.def: a change in your body or mind that shows that you are not healthy
symptom.ex: Symptoms include a headache and sore throat.

syndrome.def: a set of physical conditions that show you have a particular disease or medical problem

synthesis.def: the act of combining separate ideas, beliefs, styles, etc.; a mixture or combination of ideas, beliefs, styles, etc.
synthesis.ex: synthesis of A with B, the synthesis of art with everyday life

system.def: an organized set of ideas or theories or a particular way of doing something
system.ex: reform of the country's education system

systematic.def: done according to a system or plan, in a complete, efficient or determined way
systematic.ex: a systematic approach to solving the problem

t-shirt.def: an informal shirt, usually with short sleeves (= arms) no collar or buttons, or just a few buttons at the top
t-shirt.ex: He was wearing a faded pair of blue jeans and an old T-shirt.

table.def: a piece of furniture that consists of a flat top supported by legs
table.ex: We sat at a round table in the corner.

tablet.def: a small computer that is easy to carry, with a large touch screen and usually without a physical keyboard
tablet.ex: The company has launched its latest 10-inch tablet.

tackle.def: an act of trying to take the ball from an opponent in football (soccer), hockey, etc.; an act of knocking somebody to the ground, for example in rugby or American football
tackle.ex: He was booked for a late tackle on Torres.

tackle.def: to make a determined effort to deal with a difficult problem or situation
tackle.ex: The government is determined to tackle inflation.

tactic.def: the particular method you use to achieve something
tactic.ex: They tried all kinds of tactics to get us to go.

tactical.def: connected with the particular method you use to achieve something

tag.def: a small piece of paper, cloth, plastic, etc. attached to something to identify it or give information about it
tag.ex: He put name tags on all his shirts.

tag.def: to fasten a tag onto something/somebody
tag.ex: Each animal was tagged with a number for identification.

tail.def: the part that sticks out at the back of the body of a bird, an animal or a fish, which the animal can move from side to side or up and down
tail.ex: The dog ran up, wagging its tail.

take.def: to carry or move something from one place to another
take.ex: Remember to take your coat when you leave.

tale.def: a story created using the imagination, especially one that is full of action and adventure

talent.def: a natural ability to do something well
talent.ex: The festival showcases the talent of young musicians.

talented.def: having a natural ability to do something well

talk.def: a speech or lecture on a particular subject
talk.ex: I went to several interesting talks at the conference.

talk.def: to say things; to speak in order to give information or to express feelings, ideas, etc.

tall.def: having a greater than average height

tank.def: a large container for holding liquid or gas

tap.def: a device for controlling the flow of water from a pipe into a bath or sink
to hit somebody/something quickly and lightly

tape.def: a long, narrow piece of material with a sticky substance on one side that is used for sticking things together

target.def: a result that you try to achieve
to aim an attack or a criticism at somebody/something
target.ex: He accused the group of deliberately targeting civilians.

task.def: a piece of work that somebody has to do, especially a hard or unpleasant one

taste.def: the particular quality that different foods and drinks have that allows you to recognize them when you put them in your mouth

tax.def: money that you have to pay to the government so that it can pay for public services. People pay tax according to their income and businesses pay tax according to their profits. Tax is also often paid on goods and services.
to put a tax on somebody/something; to make somebody pay tax
tax.ex: His declared aim was to tax the rich.

taxi.def: a car with a driver that you pay to take you somewhere. Taxis usually have meters that show how much money you have to pay.
taxi.ex: We took a taxi to the airport.

taxpayer.def: a person who pays tax to the government, especially on the money that they earn
taxpayer.ex: Hundreds of thousands of pounds of taxpayers' money (= money paid in taxes) have been spent on the project.

tea.def: the dried leaves (called tea leaves) of the tea bush

teach.def: to give lessons to students in a school, college, university, etc.; to help somebody learn something by giving information about it
teach.ex: She teaches at our local school.

teacher.def: a person whose job is teaching, especially in a school

teaching.ex: She wants to go into teaching (= make it a career).

team.def: a group of people who play a particular game or sport against another group of people

tear.def: a hole that has been made in something by tearing
tear.ex: This sheet has a tear in it.

tear.def: to damage something by pulling it apart or into pieces or by cutting it on something sharp; to become damaged in this way
tear.ex: I tore my jeans on the fence.

technical.def: connected with the practical use of machines, methods, etc. in science and industry
technical.ex: We offer free technical support for those buying our software.

technique.def: a particular way of doing something, especially one in which you have to learn special skills

technology.def: scientific knowledge used in practical ways in industry, for example in designing new machines

teenage.def: between 13 and 19 years old; connected with people of this age

teenager.def: a person who is between 13 and 19 years old

teens.def: the years of a person’s life when they are between 13 and 19 years old
teens.ex: She began writing poetry in her teens.

telephone.def: a system for talking to somebody else over long distances, using wires or radio; a machine used for this
telephone.ex: The telephone rang and Pat answered it.

telephone.def: to speak to somebody by phone
telephone.ex: Please write or telephone for details.

television.def: a piece of electrical equipment with a screen on which you can watch programmes with moving pictures and sounds

tell.def: to give information to somebody by speaking or writing
tell.ex: He told the news to everybody he saw.

temperature.def: the measurement in degrees of how hot or cold a thing or place is

temple.def: a building used for religious worship, especially in religions other than Christianity
temple.ex: the Temple of Diana at Ephesus

temporarily.def: in a way that lasts or is intended to last or be used only for a short time; in a way that is not permanent
temporarily.ex: We regret this service is temporarily unavailable.

temporary.def: lasting or intended to last or be used only for a short time; not permanent

tempt.def: to attract somebody or make somebody want to do or have something, even if they know it is wrong
tempt.ex: I was tempted by the dessert menu.

ten.ex: There are only ten of these rare animals left.

tenant.def: a person who pays rent for the use of a room, building, land, etc. to the person who owns it
tenant.ex: They had evicted their tenants for non-payment of rent.

tend.def: to be likely to do something or to happen in a particular way because this is what often or usually happens
tend.ex: Women tend to live longer than men.

tendency.def: if somebody/something has a particular tendency, they are likely to behave or act in a particular way

tennis.def: a game in which two or four players use rackets to hit a ball backwards and forwards across a net on a specially marked court

tension.def: a situation in which people do not trust each other, or feel unfriendly towards each other, and that may cause them to attack each other
tension.ex: There is mounting tension along the border.

tent.def: a shelter made of a large sheet of canvas, nylon, etc. that is supported by poles and ropes fixed to the ground, and is used especially for camping
tent.ex: to put up/take down a tent

tenure.def: the period of time when somebody holds an important job, especially a political one; the act of holding an important job

term.def: a word or phrase used as the name of something, especially one connected with a particular type of language
to use a particular name or word to describe somebody/something
term.ex: term somebody/something + adj., At his age, he can hardly be termed young.

terminal.def: that cannot be cured and will lead to death, often slowly
a building or set of buildings at an airport where passengers arrive and leave
terminal.ex: A second terminal was opened last year.

terminate.def: to end; to make something end
terminate.ex: Your contract of employment terminates in December.

terms.def: the conditions that people offer, demand or accept when they make an agreement, an arrangement or a contract

terrain.def: used to refer to an area of land when you are mentioning its natural features, for example, if it is rough, flat, etc.

terrible.def: very unpleasant; making you feel very unhappy, upset or frightened

terribly.ex: I'm terribly sorry—did I hurt you?

terrify.def: to make somebody feel extremely frightened

territory.def: land that is under the control of a particular country or political leader

terrorism.def: the use of violent action in order to achieve political aims or to force a government to act

terrorist.def: a person who takes part in terrorism
terrorist.ex: The terrorists are threatening to blow up the plane.

test.def: an examination of somebody’s knowledge or ability, consisting of questions for them to answer or activities for them to perform
to find out how much somebody knows, or what they can do by asking them questions or giving them activities to perform
test.ex: Students will be tested at the end of each module.

testify.def: to make a statement that something happened or that something is true, especially as a witness in court
testify.ex: She refused to testify against her husband.

testimony.def: a thing that shows that something else exists or is true
testimony.ex: This increase in exports bears testimony to the successes of industry.

testing.def: the activity of testing somebody/something in order to find something out, see if it works, etc.

text.ex: a computer that can process text

textbook.def: a book that teaches a particular subject and that is used especially in schools and colleges

texture.def: the way a surface, substance or piece of cloth feels when you touch it, for example how rough, smooth, hard or soft it is

than.def: used to introduce the second part of a comparison

thank.def: to tell somebody that you are grateful for something
thank.ex: I must write and thank Mary for the present.

thankfully.def: used to show that you are pleased that something good has happened or that something bad has been avoided
thankfully.ex: There was a fire in the building, but thankfully no one was hurt.

thanks.def: used to show that you are grateful to somebody for something they have done
thanks.ex: ‘How are you?’ ‘Fine, thanks (= thanks for asking).’

thanks.def: words or actions that show that you are grateful to somebody for something
thanks.ex: How can I ever express my thanks to you for all you've done?

that.ex: I can't walk that far (= as far as that).

that.def: used after some verbs, adjectives and nouns to introduce a new part of the sentence
that.ex: She said (that) the story was true.

that.def: used for referring to a person or thing that is not near the speaker or as near to the speaker as another
that.ex: Look at that man over there.

that.def: used for referring to a person or thing that is not near the speaker, or not as near to the speaker as another

the.def: used to refer to somebody/something that has already been mentioned or is easily understood
the.ex: There were three questions. The first two were relatively easy but the third one was hard.

theatre.def: a building or an outdoor area where plays and similar types of entertainment are performed

theft.def: the crime of stealing something from a person or place

their.def: of or belonging to people, animals or things that have already been mentioned or are easily identified

theirs.ex: Theirs are the children with very fair hair.

them.def: used when referring to people, animals or things as the object of a verb or preposition, or after the verb be

theme.def: the subject or main idea in a talk, piece of writing or work of art

themselves.def: used when people or animals performing an action are also affected by it
themselves.ex: They seemed to be enjoying themselves.

then.def: used to refer to a particular time in the past or future
then.ex: Life was harder then because neither of us had a job.

theology.def: the study of religion and beliefs

theoretical.def: connected with the ideas and principles on which a particular subject is based, rather than with practice and experiment

theory.def: a formal set of ideas that is intended to explain why something happens or exists
theory.ex: theory of something, the theory of evolution/relativity

therapist.def: a specialist who treats a particular type of illness or problem, or who uses a particular type of treatment

therapy.def: the treatment of a physical problem or an illness
therapy.ex: He is receiving therapy for cancer.

there.def: used to show that something exists or happens
there.ex: There's a restaurant around the corner.

thereafter.def: after the time or event mentioned
thereafter.ex: She married at 17 and gave birth to her first child shortly thereafter.

thereby.def: used to introduce the result of the action or situation mentioned
thereby.ex: Regular exercise strengthens the heart, thereby reducing the risk of heart attack.

therefore.def: used to introduce the logical result of something that has just been mentioned
therefore.ex: He's only 17 and therefore not eligible to vote.

thesis.def: a long piece of writing completed by a student as part of a university degree, based on their own research
thesis.ex: Students must submit a thesis on an agreed subject within four years.

they.def: people, animals or things that have already been mentioned or are easily identified
they.ex: ‘Where are John and Liz?’ ‘They went for a walk.’

thick.def: having a larger distance between opposite sides or surfaces than other similar objects or than normal

thief.def: a person who steals something from another person or place

thin.def: having a smaller distance between opposite sides or surfaces than other similar objects or than normal
thin.ex: Cut the vegetables into thin strips.

thing.def: an object whose name you do not use because you do not need to or want to, or because you do not know it
thing.ex: Can you pass me that thing over there?

think.def: to have a particular idea or opinion about something/somebody; to believe something
think.ex: Do you really think (that) he'll win?

thinking.def: the process of thinking about something
thinking.ex: I had to do some quick thinking.

third.def: each of three equal parts of something
third.ex: He divided the money into thirds.

this.ex: It's about this high (= as high as I am showing you with my hands).

this.def: used to refer to a particular person, thing or event that is close to you, especially compared with another
this.ex: How long have you been living in this country?

thorough.def: done completely; with great attention to detail
thorough.ex: a thorough knowledge of the subject

though.def: used especially at the end of a sentence or clause to add a fact or an opinion that makes the previous statement less strong or less important
though.ex: Our team lost. It was a good game though.
Anne was fond of Tim, though he often annoyed her.

thought.def: something that you think of or remember
thought.ex: I couldn't bear the thought of waiting any longer.

thought-provoking.def: making people think seriously about a particular subject or issue

thousand.ex: There were thousands of people there.

thread.def: a thin string of cotton, wool, silk, etc. used for sewing or making cloth

threat.def: a statement in which you tell somebody that you will punish or harm them, especially if they do not do what you want
threat.ex: threat against somebody/something, to make threats against somebody

threaten.def: to say that you will cause trouble, hurt somebody, etc. if you do not get what you want
threaten.ex: They broke my windows and threatened me.

three.ex: There are only three cookies left.

threshold.def: the floor or ground at the bottom of a doorway, considered as the entrance to a building or room

thrive.def: to become, and continue to be, successful, strong, healthy, etc.
thrive.ex: New businesses thrive in this area.

throat.def: a passage in the neck through which food and air pass on their way into the body; the front part of the neck

through.def: from one end or side of something to the other
through.ex: Put the coffee in the filter and let the water run through.

through.def: from one end or side of something/somebody to the other
through.ex: The burglar got in through the window.

throughout.def: in or into every part of something
throughout.ex: They export their products to markets throughout the world.

throw.def: to send something from your hand through the air by moving your hand or arm quickly

thumb.def: the short, thick finger at the side of the hand, slightly apart from the other four
thumb.ex: She still sucks her thumb when she's worried.

thursday.def: the day of the week after Wednesday and before Friday

ticket.def: a printed piece of paper, or a message or image received on your phone or computer, that gives you the right to travel on a particular bus, train, etc. or to go into a theatre, etc.

tide.def: a regular rise and fall in the level of the sea, caused by the pull of the moon and sun; the flow of water that happens as the sea rises and falls
tide.ex: the ebb and flow of the tide

tidy.def: arranged neatly and with everything in order
to make something look neat by putting things in the place where they belong
tidy.ex: I spent all morning cleaning and tidying.

tie.def: a long, narrow piece of cloth worn around the neck, especially by men, with a knot in front
tie.ex: He was dressed impeccably in a suit and tie.

tie.def: to attach or hold two or more things together using string, rope, etc.; to fasten somebody/something with string, rope, etc.
tie.ex: She tied the newspapers in a bundle.

tight.def: held or fixed in position securely; difficult to move, open or separate
tight.ex: He kept a tight grip on her arm.

tighten.def: to hold or fix something more securely in position; to make something or become more difficult to move, open or separate
tighten.ex: tighten something, to tighten a lid/screw/rope/knot

timber.def: trees that are grown to be used in building or for making things

time.def: what is measured in minutes, hours, days, etc.
time.ex: The world exists in time and space.

time.def: to measure how long it takes for something to happen or for somebody to do something

timely.def: happening at exactly the right time
timely.ex: A nasty incident was prevented by the timely arrival of the police.

timing.def: the act of choosing when something happens; a particular point or period of time when something happens or is planned
timing.ex: The timing of the decision was a complete surprise.

tin.def: a metal container in which food is sold; the contents of one of these containers
tin.ex: Open the tin and drain the tuna.

tiny.def: very small in size or amount

tip.def: a small piece of advice about something practical
tip.ex: tip on/for doing something, handy tips for buying a computer

tip.def: to give somebody an extra amount of money to thank them for something they have done for you as part of their job
tip.ex: Americans were always welcome because they tended to tip heavily.

tired.def: feeling that you would like to sleep or rest; needing rest

tissue.def: a piece of soft paper, used especially as a handkerchief

title.def: the name of a book, poem, painting, piece of music, etc.
title.ex: What's title of her new book?

title.def: to give a book, piece of music, etc. a particular name
title.ex: Their first album was titled ‘Made in Valmez’.

to.def: used to show purpose or intention
to.ex: I set out to buy food.

to.def: in the direction of something; towards something

tobacco.def: the dried leaves of the tobacco plant that are used for making cigarettes, smoking in a pipe, etc.

today.ex: I've got a piano lesson later today.

toe.def: one of the five small parts that stick out from the foot; any similar part on the foot of an animal or bird
toe.ex: the big/little toe (= the largest/smallest toe)

together.def: with or near to somebody/something else; with each other

toilet.def: a large bowl attached to a pipe that you sit on or stand over when you get rid of waste matter from your body

tolerance.def: the quality of being willing to accept or tolerate somebody/something, especially opinions or behaviour that you may not agree with, or people who are not like you
tolerance.ex: She had no tolerance for jokes of any kind.

tolerate.def: to allow somebody to do something that you do not agree with or like
tolerate.ex: Their relationship was tolerated but not encouraged.

toll.def: money that you pay to use a particular road or bridge

tomato.def: a soft fruit with a lot of juice and shiny red skin that is eaten as a vegetable either raw or cooked
tomato.ex: a bacon, lettuce, and tomato sandwich

tomorrow.def: on or during the day after today
tomorrow.ex: I'm off now. See you tomorrow.
Today is Tuesday, so tomorrow is Wednesday.

ton.def: a unit for measuring weight, in the UK 2 240 pounds (long ton) and in the US 2 000 pounds (short ton)
ton.ex: What have you got in this bag? It weighs a ton (= is very heavy).

tone.def: the quality of somebody’s voice, especially expressing a particular emotion

tongue.def: the soft part in the mouth that moves around, used for tasting, swallowing, speaking, etc.
tongue.ex: He clicked his tongue to attract their attention.

tonight.def: on or during the evening or night of today
tonight.ex: Will you have dinner with me tonight?

tonight.def: the evening or night of today

tonne.def: a unit for measuring weight, equal to 1 000 kilograms
tonne.ex: a record grain harvest of 236m tonnes

too.def: used before adjectives and adverbs to say that something is more than is good, necessary, possible, etc.
too.ex: He's far too young to go on his own.

tool.def: an instrument such as a hammer, screwdriver, saw, etc. that you hold in your hand and use for making things, repairing things, etc.

tooth.def: any of the hard white structures in the mouth used for biting food
tooth.ex: I've just had a tooth out at the dentist's.

top.ex: He lives on the top floor.

top.def: the highest part or point of something
top.ex: She was standing at the top of the stairs.

top.def: to be higher than a particular amount
top.ex: Worldwide sales look set to top $1 billion.

topic.def: a subject that you talk, write or learn about
topic.ex: The main topic of conversation was Tom's new girlfriend.

torture.def: the act of causing somebody severe pain in order to punish them or make them say or do something
torture.ex: Many of the refugees have suffered torture.

torture.def: to hurt somebody physically or mentally in order to punish them or make them tell you something
torture.ex: Many of the rebels were captured and tortured by secret police.

toss.def: to throw something lightly or carelessly
toss.ex: I tossed the book aside and got up.

total.def: being the amount or number after everyone or everything is counted or added together
the amount you get when you add several numbers or amounts together; the final number of people or things when they have all been counted
total.ex: You got 47 points on the written examination and 18 on the oral, making a total of 65.

totally.ex: They come from totally different cultures.

touch.def: the sense that enables you to be aware of things and what they are like when you put your hands and fingers on them
to put your hand or another part of your body onto somebody/something

tough.def: having or causing problems or difficulties

tour.def: a journey made for pleasure during which several different towns, countries, etc. are visited
to travel around a place, for example on holiday, or to perform, to advertise something, etc.
tour.ex: He toured America with his one-man show.

tourism.def: the business activity connected with providing accommodation, services and entertainment for people who are visiting a place for pleasure
tourism.ex: The area is heavily dependent on tourism.

tourist.def: a person who is travelling or visiting a place for pleasure

tournament.def: a sports competition involving a number of teams or players who take part in different games and must leave the competition if they lose. The competition continues until there is only the winner left.

towards.ex: They were heading towards the German border.

towel.def: a piece of cloth or paper used for drying things, especially your body
towel.ex: Help yourself to a clean towel.

tower.def: a tall narrow building or part of a building, especially of a church or castle

town.def: a place with many houses, shops, etc. where people live and work. It is larger than a village but smaller than a city.
town.ex: The nearest town is ten miles away.

toy.def: made as a copy of a particular thing and used for playing with
an object for children to play with

trace.def: a mark, an object or a sign that shows that somebody/something existed or was present
trace.ex: It's exciting to discover traces of earlier civilizations.

trace.def: to find or discover somebody/something by looking carefully for them/it
trace.ex: We finally traced him to an address in Chicago.

track.def: rails (= metal bars) that a train moves along
to find somebody/something by following the marks, signs, information, etc., that they have left behind them
track.ex: track somebody/something, hunters tracking and shooting bears

trade.def: the activity of buying and selling or of exchanging goods or services between people or countries

trade.ex: The firm openly traded in arms.

trademark.def: a name, symbol or design that a company uses for its products and that cannot be used by anyone else

trading.def: the activity of buying and selling things
trading.ex: new laws on Sunday trading (= shops being open on Sundays)

tradition.def: a belief, custom or way of doing something that has existed for a long time among a particular group of people; a set of these beliefs or customs

traditional.def: being part of the beliefs, customs or way of life of a particular group of people, that have not changed for a long time

traffic.def: the vehicles that are on a road at a particular time
traffic.ex: There's always a lot of traffic at this time of day.

tragedy.def: a very sad event or situation, especially one that involves death
tragedy.ex: It's a tragedy that she died so young.

tragic.def: making you feel very sad, usually because somebody has died or suffered a lot
tragic.ex: He was killed in a tragic accident at the age of 24.

trail.def: a long line or series of marks that is left by somebody/something
to pull something behind somebody/something, usually along the ground; to be pulled along in this way
trail.ex: A jeep trailing a cloud of dust was speeding in my direction.

trailer.def: a truck, or a container with wheels, that is pulled by another vehicle
trailer.ex: a car towing a trailer with a boat on it

train.def: a number of connected coaches or trucks, pulled by an engine or powered by a motor in each one, taking people and goods from one place to another
to teach a person or an animal the skills for a particular job or activity; to be taught in this way

trainer.def: a shoe that you wear for sports or as informal clothing

training.def: the process of learning the skills that you need to do a job

trait.def: a particular quality in your personality

transaction.def: a piece of business that is done between people, especially an act of buying or selling

transcript.def: a written or printed copy of words that have been spoken

transfer.def: the act of moving somebody/something from one place, group or job to another; an occasion when this happens
to move from one place to another; to move something/somebody from one place to another
transfer.ex: The film studio is transferring to Hollywood.

transform.def: to change the form of something; to change in form
transform.ex: The photochemical reactions transform the light into electrical impulses.

transformation.ex: The way in which we work has undergone a complete transformation in the past decade.

transit.def: the process of being moved or carried from one place to another

transition.def: the process or a period of changing from one state or condition to another
transition.ex: transition from something to something, the transition from school to full-time work

translate.def: to express the meaning of speech or writing in a different language
translate.ex: He translated the letter into English.

translation.def: the process of changing something that is written or spoken into another language

transmission.def: the act or process of passing something from one person, place or thing to another

transmit.def: to send an electronic signal, radio or television broadcast, etc.

transparency.def: the quality of something, such as a situation or an argument, that makes it easy to understand
transparency.ex: a need for greater transparency in legal documents

transparent.def: allowing you to see through it
transparent.ex: The insect's wings are almost transparent.

transport.def: a system for carrying people or goods from one place to another using vehicles, roads, etc.
to take something/somebody from one place to another in a vehicle

transportation.def: a system for carrying people or goods from one place to another using vehicles, roads, etc.
transportation.ex: public transportation (= the system of buses, trains, etc. provided for people to travel from one place to another)

trap.def: a piece of equipment for catching animals
trap.ex: a fox with its leg in a trap

trap.def: to put somebody in a dangerous place that they cannot get out of

trauma.def: a mental condition caused by severe shock, stress or fear, especially when the harmful effects last for a long time
trauma.ex: the effects of trauma and stress on the body

travel.def: the act or activity of travelling
to go from one place to another, especially over a long distance
travel.ex: I go to bed early if I'm travelling the next day.

traveller.def: a person who is travelling or who often travels
traveller.ex: She is a frequent traveller to Belgium.

treasure.def: a collection of valuable things such as gold, silver and jewellery

treat.def: to behave in a particular way towards somebody/something
treat.ex: treat somebody/something with something, to treat people with respect

treatment.def: something that is done to cure an illness or injury, or to make somebody look and feel good
treatment.ex: The drug is used in the treatment of depression.

treaty.def: a formal agreement between two or more countries

tree.def: a tall plant that can live a long time. Trees have a thick central wooden trunk from which branches grow, usually with leaves on them

trend.def: a general direction in which a situation is changing or developing

trial.def: a formal examination of evidence in court by a judge and often a jury, to decide if somebody accused of a crime is guilty or not

tribal.def: connected with a tribe or tribes

tribe.def: a social group in a traditional society consisting of people with the same language, culture, religion, etc., living in a particular area and often having one leader known as a chief
tribe.ex: They were a nomadic horse-riding tribe.

tribunal.def: a type of court with the authority to deal with a particular problem or disagreement

tribute.def: an act, a statement or a gift that is intended to show your love or respect, especially for a dead person
tribute.ex: At her funeral her oldest friend paid tribute to her life and work.

trick.def: something that you do to make somebody believe something that is not true, or to annoy somebody as a joke
trick.ex: They had to think of a trick to get past the guards.

trick.def: to make somebody believe something which is not true, especially in order to cheat them
trick.ex: I'd been tricked and I felt stupid.

trigger.def: the part of a gun that you press in order to fire it

trigger.ex: Nuts can trigger off a violent allergic reaction.

trio.def: a group of three people or things
trio.ex: A trio of English runners featured in the women’s 1 500 metres.

trip.def: a journey to a place and back again, especially a short one for pleasure or a particular purpose
trip.ex: Did you have a good trip?

trip.def: to catch your foot on something and fall or almost fall

triumph.def: a great success, achievement or victory
triumph.ex: one of the greatest triumphs of modern science

troop.ex: They announced the withdrawal of 12 000 troops from the area.

trophy.def: an object such as a silver cup that is given as a prize for winning a competition

tropical.def: coming from, found in or typical of the tropics

trouble.def: a problem, worry, difficulty, etc. or a situation causing this
to make somebody worried or upset
trouble.ex: What is it that's troubling you?

troubled.ex: She looked into his troubled face.

trousers.def: a piece of clothing that covers the lower body and is divided into two parts to cover each leg separately

truck.def: a large vehicle for carrying heavy loads by road

true.def: connected with facts rather than things that have been invented or guessed
true.ex: Indicate whether the following statements are true or false.

truly.def: used to emphasize that a particular statement, feeling, etc. is sincere or real
truly.ex: She truly believes that none of this is her fault.

trust.def: the belief that somebody/something is good, sincere, honest, etc. and will not try to harm or trick you
trust.ex: Our partnership is based on trust.

trust.def: to have confidence in somebody; to believe that somebody is good, sincere, honest, etc.

trustee.def: a person or an organization that has control of money or property that has been put into a trust for somebody
trustee.ex: The bank will act as trustees for the estate until the child is 18.

truth.def: the true facts about something, rather than the things that have been invented or guessed
truth.ex: Do you think she's telling the truth?

try.def: an act of trying to do something
try.ex: I doubt they'll be able to help but it's worth a try (= worth asking them).

try.def: to make an attempt or effort to do or get something
try.ex: I don't know if I can come but I'll try.

tsunami.def: an extremely large wave in the sea caused, for example, by an earthquake
tsunami.ex: A tsunami early warning system was set up in Hawaii.

tube.def: a long, hollow pipe made of metal, plastic, rubber, etc., through which liquids or gases move from one place to another
tube.ex: He had to be fed through a feeding tube for several months.

tuesday.def: the day of the week after Monday and before Wednesday

tuition.def: the act of teaching something, especially to one person or to people in small groups
tuition.ex: She received private tuition in French.

tune.def: a series of musical notes that are sung or played in a particular order to form a piece of music

tunnel.def: a passage built underground, for example to allow a road or railway to go through a hill, under a river, etc.

turn.def: the time when somebody in a group of people should or is allowed to do something
turn.ex: When it's your turn, take another card.

turn.def: to move or make something move around a central point
turn.ex: The wheels of the car began to turn.

turnout.def: the number of people who attend a particular event
turnout.ex: This year's festival attracted a record turnout.

turnover.def: the total amount of goods or services sold by a company during a particular period of time

tv.ex: We spent the evening watching TV.

twelve.ex: There are only twelve of these rare animals left.

twice.ex: I don't know him well; I've only met him twice.

twin.def: used to describe one of a pair of children who are twins
one of two children born at the same time to the same mother

twist.def: the action of turning something with your hand, or of turning a part of your body
twist.ex: She gave the lid another twist and it came off.

twist.def: to bend or turn something into a particular shape
twist.ex: Twist the wire to form a circle.

two.ex: There are only two cookies left.

type.def: a class or group of people or things that share particular qualities or features and are part of a larger group; a kind or sort
to write something using a computer keyboard or typewriter

typical.def: having the usual qualities or features of a particular type of person, thing or group

typically.def: used to say that something usually happens in the way that you are stating
typically.ex: The standard chips are typically used for databases and other business software.

tyre.def: a thick rubber ring that fits around the edge of a wheel of a car, bicycle, etc.

ultimate.def: happening at the end of a long process

ultimately.ex: A poor diet will ultimately lead to illness.

umbrella.def: an object with a round folding frame of long, straight pieces of metal covered with material, that you use to protect yourself from the rain or from hot sun

unable.def: not having the skill, strength, time, knowledge, etc. to do something
unable.ex: a former soldier who has been unable to find work since the war ended

unacceptable.def: so bad that you think it should not be allowed
unacceptable.ex: Such behaviour is totally unacceptable in a civilized society.

uncertainty.ex: There is considerable uncertainty about the company's future.

uncle.def: the brother of your mother or father; the husband of your aunt or uncle

uncomfortable.def: not letting you feel physically comfortable; unpleasant to wear, sit on, etc.

unconscious.def: in a state like sleep because of an injury or illness, and not able to use your senses
unconscious.ex: They found him lying unconscious on the floor.

under.ex: He pulled up the covers and crawled under.

under.def: in, to or through a position that is below something
under.ex: Have you looked under the bed?

undergo.def: to experience something, especially a change or something unpleasant

undergraduate.def: a university or college student who is studying for their first degree

underground.def: under the surface of the ground

underlying.def: important in a situation but not always easily noticed or stated clearly
underlying.ex: The underlying assumption is that the amount of money available is limited.

undermine.def: to make something, especially somebody’s confidence or authority, gradually weaker or less effective
undermine.ex: Our confidence in the team has been seriously undermined by their recent defeats.

understand.def: to know or realize the meaning of words, a language, what somebody says, etc.

understanding.def: the knowledge that somebody has about a particular subject or situation
understanding.ex: Students will gain a broad understanding of the workings of Parliament.

undertake.def: to make yourself responsible for something and start doing it

underwear.def: clothes that you wear under other clothes and next to the skin

undoubtedly.def: used to emphasize that something exists or is definitely true
undoubtedly.ex: There is undoubtedly a great deal of truth in what he says.

unemployed.def: without a job although able to work
unemployed.ex: How long have you been unemployed?

unemployment.def: the fact of a number of people not having a job; the number of people without a job

unexpected.def: if something is unexpected, it surprises you because you were not expecting it

unfair.def: not right or fair according to a set of rules or principles; not treating people equally
unfair.ex: They had been given an unfair advantage.

unfold.def: to spread open or flat something that has previously been folded; to become open and flat

unfortunate.def: having bad luck; caused by bad luck
unfortunate.ex: He was unfortunate to lose in the final round.

unfortunately.def: used to say that a particular situation or fact makes you sad or disappointed, or gets you into a difficult position
unfortunately.ex: I won't be able to attend the meeting.

uniform.def: the special set of clothes worn by all members of an organization or a group at work, or by children at school
uniform.ex: The hat is part of the school uniform.

unify.def: to join people, things, parts of a country, etc. together so that they form a single unit
unify.ex: The new leader hopes to unify the country.

union.def: an organization of workers, usually in a particular industry, that exists to protect their interests, improve conditions of work, etc.

unique.def: being the only one of its kind

unit.def: a single thing, person or group that is complete by itself but can also form part of something larger
unit.ex: After 1946 the British Government treated the four territorial divisions as a single unit.

unite.def: to join together with other people in order to do something as a group
unite.ex: Nationalist parties united to oppose the government's plans.

united.def: joined together as a political unit or by shared aims

unity.def: the state of being in agreement and working together; the state of being joined together to form one unit

universal.def: done by or involving all the people in the world or in a particular group
universal.ex: Such problems are a universal feature of old age.

universe.def: the whole of space and everything in it, including the earth, the planets and the stars
universe.ex: Could there be intelligent life elsewhere in the universe?

university.def: an institution at the highest level of education where you can study for a degree or do research
university.ex: Is there a university in this town?

unknown.ex: A previously unknown group claimed responsibility for the bombing.

unless.def: used to say that something can only happen or be true in a particular situation
unless.ex: You won't get paid for time off unless you have a doctor's note.

unlike.def: different from a particular person or thing
unlike.ex: Music is quite unlike any other art form.

unlikely.def: not likely to happen; not probable
unlikely.ex: The project seemed unlikely to succeed.

unnecessary.def: not needed; more than is needed
unnecessary.ex: They were found guilty of causing unnecessary suffering to animals.

unprecedented.def: that has never happened, been done or been known before
unprecedented.ex: The situation is unprecedented in modern times.

until.def: up to the point in time or the event mentioned
until.ex: Let's wait until the rain stops.

unusual.def: different from what is usual or normal

unveil.def: to remove a cover or curtain from a painting, statue, etc. so that it can be seen in public for the first time
unveil.ex: The Queen unveiled a plaque to mark the official opening of the hospital.

up.def: towards or in a higher position
up.ex: He jumped up from his chair.

up.def: to or in a higher position somewhere
up.ex: She climbed up the flight of steps.

update.def: a report or broadcast that gives the most recent information about something; a new version of something containing the most recent information
update.ex: They will send you regular updates by email.

update.def: to make something more modern by adding new parts, etc.
