update.ex: an updated version of the app

upgrade.def: the act of making a machine, computer system, etc. more powerful and efficient; the more powerful and efficient machine, computer system, etc.
upgrade.ex: instructions for installing an upgrade to the existing system

upgrade.def: to make a machine, computer system, etc. more powerful and efficient; to start using a new and better version of a machine, system, etc.
upgrade.ex: We are constantly upgrading our software to meet customers' needs.

uphold.def: to support something that you think is right and make sure that it continues to exist
uphold.ex: We have a duty to uphold the law.

upper.def: located above something else, especially something of the same type or the other of a pair

upset.def: unhappy or disappointed because of something unpleasant that has happened
upset.ex: I understand how upset you must be feeling.

upset.def: to make somebody/yourself feel unhappy, anxious or annoyed
upset.ex: This decision is likely to upset a lot of people.

upstairs.def: on a floor of a house or other building that is higher than the one that you are on
up the stairs; on or to a floor of a house or other building higher than the one that you are on

upwards.def: towards a higher place or position
upwards.ex: Place your hands on the table with the palms facing upwards.

urban.def: connected with a town or city

urge.def: to advise or try hard to persuade somebody to do something
urge.ex: If you ever get the chance to visit this place, I strongly urge you to do so.

urgent.def: that needs to be dealt with or happen immediately
urgent.ex: The police have issued an urgent appeal for information.

us.def: used when the speaker or writer and another or others are the object of a verb or preposition, or after the verb be
us.ex: She gave us a picture as a wedding present.

usage.def: the way in which words are used in a language
usage.ex: a book on current English usage

use.def: the act of using something; the state of being used
use.ex: A ban was imposed on the use of chemical weapons.

use.def: to do something with a machine, a method, an object, etc. for a particular purpose

used.def: familiar with something because you do it or experience it often
used.ex: I'm not used to eating so much at lunchtime.

used to.def: used to say that something happened continuously or frequently during a period in the past
used to.ex: I used to live in London.

useful.def: that can help you to do or achieve what you want

useless.def: not useful; not doing or achieving what is needed or wanted

user.def: a person or thing that uses something

usual.def: that happens or is done most of the time or in most cases
usual.ex: This is the usual way of doing it.

usually.def: in the way that is usual or normal; most often
usually.ex: How long does the journey usually take?

utility.def: a service provided for the public, for example an electricity, water or gas supply

utilize.def: to use something, especially for a practical purpose
utilize.ex: The Romans were the first to utilize concrete as a building material.

utterly.ex: We're so utterly different from each other.

vacation.def: a period of time spent travelling or resting away from home
vacation.ex: They're on vacation in Hawaii right now.

vacuum.def: a space that is completely empty of all substances, including all air or other gas
vacuum.ex: a vacuum pump (= one that creates a vacuum)

vague.def: not clear in a person’s mind
vague.ex: to have a vague impression/memory/recollection of something

valid.def: that is legally or officially acceptable
valid.ex: Do you have a valid passport?

validity.def: the state of being legally or officially acceptable
validity.ex: The period of validity of the agreement has expired.

valley.def: an area of low land between hills or mountains, often with a river flowing through it; the land that a river flows through
valley.ex: In the valley below cows were grazing peacefully.

valuable.ex: My home is my most valuable asset.

value.def: how much something is worth in money or other goods for which it can be exchanged
to think that somebody/something is important
value.ex: He has come to value her advice and support.

van.def: a covered vehicle with no side windows in its back half, usually smaller than a lorry, used for carrying goods or people
van.ex: the driver of a white van

vanish.def: to disappear suddenly and/or in a way that you cannot explain
vanish.ex: He turned around and vanished into the house.

variable.def: a situation, number or quantity that can vary or be varied
variable.ex: With so many variables, it is difficult to calculate the cost.

variation.def: a change, especially in the amount or level of something
variation.ex: The dial records very slight variations in pressure.

variety.def: several different sorts of the same thing
variety.ex: There is a wide variety of patterns to choose from.

vary.def: to be different from each other in size, shape, etc.
vary.ex: New techniques were introduced with varying degrees of success.

vast.def: extremely large in area, size, amount, etc.

vegetable.def: a plant or part of a plant that is eaten as food. Potatoes, beans and onions are all vegetables.
vegetable.ex: The children don't eat enough fresh vegetables.

vehicle.def: a thing that is used for transporting people or goods from one place to another, such as a car or lorry
vehicle.ex: motor vehicles (= cars, buses, lorries, etc.)

vein.def: any of the tubes that carry blood from all parts of the body towards the heart

venture.def: a business project or activity, especially one that involves taking risks
venture.ex: A disastrous business venture lost him thousands of dollars.

venture.def: to go somewhere even though you know that it might be dangerous or unpleasant
venture.ex: They ventured nervously into the water.

venue.def: a place where people meet for an organized event, for example a concert, sporting event or conference
venue.ex: The band will be playing at 20 different venues on their UK tour.

verbal.ex: The job applicant must have good verbal skills.

verdict.def: an official judgement made in court or at an inquest (= an official investigation into somebody's death)
verdict.ex: Has the jury reached a verdict?

verify.def: to check that something is true or accurate
verify.ex: We have no way of verifying his story.

verse.def: writing that is arranged in lines, often with a regular rhythm or pattern of rhyme
verse.ex: Most of the play is written in verse, but some of it is in prose.

version.def: a form of something that is slightly different from an earlier form or from other forms of the same thing
version.ex: version of something, the latest version of the software package

versus.def: used to show that two teams or sides are against each other
versus.ex: It is France versus Brazil in the final.

vertical.def: going straight up or down from a level surface or from top to bottom in a picture, etc.
vertical.ex: the vertical axis of the graph

very.def: used to emphasize that you are talking about a particular thing or person and not about another
very.ex: He might be phoning her at this very moment.

very.def: used before adjectives, adverbs and determiners to mean ‘in a high degree’ or ‘extremely’

vessel.def: a tube that carries blood through the body of a person or an animal, or liquid through the parts of a plant

veteran.def: a person who has a lot of experience in a particular area or activity
veteran.ex: the veteran American actor, Clint Eastwood

viable.def: that can be done; that will be successful

vice.def: criminal activities that involve sex or drugs
vice.ex: At the door were two plain-clothes detectives from the vice squad.

victim.def: a person who has been attacked, injured or killed as the result of a crime, a disease, an accident, etc.

victory.def: success in a game, an election, a war, etc.

video.def: a system of recording moving pictures and sound, either using a digital method of storing data or (in the past) using videotape
video.ex: A wedding is the perfect subject for video.

view.def: what you can see from a particular place or position, especially beautiful countryside
view.ex: There were magnificent views of the surrounding countryside.

view.def: to think about somebody/something in a particular way
view.ex: How do you view your position within the company?

viewer.def: a person watching television or a video on the internet
viewer.ex: The programme attracted millions of viewers.

viewpoint.def: a way of thinking about a subject
viewpoint.ex: Try looking at things from a different viewpoint.

village.def: a very small town located in a country area
village.ex: We visited towns and villages all over Spain.

villager.def: a person who lives in a village
villager.ex: Some of the villagers have lived here all their lives.

violate.def: to go against or refuse to obey a law, an agreement, etc.

violation.def: the act of going against or refusing to obey a law, an agreement, etc.
violation.ex: They were in open violation of the treaty.

violence.def: violent behaviour that is intended to hurt or kill somebody
violence.ex: Police do not think this killing was a random act of violence.

violent.def: involving or caused by physical force that is intended to hurt or kill somebody

virtual.def: made to appear to exist by the use of computer software, for example on the internet
virtual.ex: a system to help programmers create virtual environments

virtue.def: behaviour or attitudes that show high moral standards
virtue.ex: He led a life of virtue.

virus.def: a living thing, too small to be seen without a microscope, that causes disease in people, animals and plants

visa.def: a stamp or mark put in your passport by officials of a foreign country that gives you permission to enter, pass through or leave their country

visible.ex: The house is clearly visible from the beach.

vision.def: the ability to see; the area that you can see from a particular position

visit.def: an occasion or a period of time when somebody goes to see a place or person and spends time there
to go to see a person or a place for a period of time
visit.ex: My parents are coming to visit me next week.

visitor.def: a person who visits a person or place
visitor.ex: We've got visitors coming this weekend.

visual.def: of or connected with seeing or sight

vital.def: necessary or essential in order for something to succeed or exist
vital.ex: vital for somebody/something, the vitamins that are vital for health

vitamin.def: a natural substance found in food that is an essential part of what humans and animals need to help them grow and stay healthy. There are many different vitamins.

voice.def: the sound or sounds produced through the mouth by a person speaking or singing
voice.ex: I could hear voices in the next room.

volume.def: the amount of space that an object or a substance fills; the amount of space that a container has
volume.ex: How do you measure the volume of a gas?

voluntary.def: done willingly, not because you are forced

volunteer.def: a person who does a job without being paid for it
volunteer.ex: Schools need volunteers to help children to read.

volunteer.def: to offer to do something without being forced to do it or without getting paid for it
volunteer.ex: He has been volunteering for 11 years now.

vote.def: a formal choice that you make in an election or at a meeting in order to choose somebody or decide something
to show formally by marking a paper, raising your hand, using a voting machine, etc. which person you want to win an election, or which plan or idea you support
vote.ex: How did you vote at the last election?

voting.def: the action of choosing somebody/something in an election or at a meeting
voting.ex: He was eliminated in the first round of voting.

vow.def: to make a formal and serious promise to do something or a formal statement that is true
vow.ex: She vowed never to speak to him again.

vulnerability.def: the fact of being weak and easily hurt physically or emotionally
vulnerability.ex: the vulnerability of newborn babies to disease

vulnerable.def: weak and easily hurt physically or emotionally
vulnerable.ex: These offices are highly vulnerable to terrorist attack.

wage.def: a regular amount of money that you earn, usually every week or every month, for work or services
wage.ex: wage/wages of something, wages of £300 a week

wait.def: an act of waiting; an amount of time waited
to stay where you are or delay doing something until somebody/something comes or something happens
wait.ex: She rang the bell and waited.

waiter.def: a man whose job is to serve customers at their tables in a restaurant, etc.
waiter.ex: I'll ask the waiter for the bill.

wake.def: to stop sleeping; to make somebody stop sleeping
wake.ex: I always wake early in the summer.

walk.def: a journey on foot, usually for pleasure or exercise
to move or go somewhere by putting one foot in front of the other on the ground, but without running
walk.ex: The baby is just learning to walk.

wall.def: a long, solid structure that rises straight up from the ground, made of stone, brick or concrete, that surrounds, divides or protects an area of land

wander.def: to walk slowly around or to a place, often without any particular sense of purpose or direction
wander.ex: She wandered aimlessly around the streets.

want.def: to have a desire or a wish for something/somebody
want.ex: Do you want some more tea?

war.def: a situation in which two or more countries or groups of people fight against each other over a period of time

ward.def: a separate room or area in a hospital for people with the same type of medical condition

warehouse.def: a building where large quantities of goods are stored, especially before they are sent to shops to be sold
warehouse.ex: Police are investigating a fire at a furniture warehouse.

warfare.def: the activity of fighting a war, especially using particular weapons or methods

warm.def: at a fairly high temperature in a way that is pleasant, rather than being hot or cold
to make something/somebody warm or warmer; to become warm or warmer
warm.ex: Come in and warm yourself by the fire.

warming.def: the process of making something, or of becoming, warm or warmer

warn.def: to tell somebody about something, especially something dangerous or unpleasant that is likely to happen, so that they can avoid it
warn.ex: I tried to warn him, but he wouldn't listen.

warning.def: a statement, an event, etc. telling somebody that something bad or unpleasant may happen in the future so that they can try to avoid it

warrant.def: a legal document that is signed by a judge and gives the police authority to do something
to make something necessary or appropriate in a particular situation

warrior.def: (especially in the past) a person who fights in a battle or war
warrior.ex: a warrior nation (= whose people are skilled in fighting)

wash.def: an act of cleaning somebody/something using water and usually soap
wash.ex: These towels are ready for a wash.

wash.def: to make something/somebody clean using water and usually soap

washing.def: the act of cleaning something using water and usually soap
washing.ex: a gentle shampoo for frequent washing

waste.def: no longer needed for a particular process and therefore thrown away
the act of using something in a careless or unnecessary way, causing it to be lost or destroyed
to use more of something than is necessary or useful
waste.ex: Stop wasting time and just get on with it!

watch.def: a type of small clock that you wear on your wrist, or (in the past) carried in your pocket
watch.ex: She kept looking anxiously at her watch.

watch.def: to look at somebody/something for a time, paying attention to what happens
watch.ex: I was in the living room, watching TV.

water.def: a liquid without colour, smell or taste that falls as rain, is in lakes, rivers and seas, and is used for drinking, washing, etc.
to pour water on plants, etc.

wave.def: a raised line of water that moves across the surface of the sea, ocean, etc.
wave.ex: Huge waves were breaking on the shore.

wave.def: to move your hand or arm from side to side in the air in order to attract attention, say hello, etc.
wave.ex: The people on the bus waved and we waved back.

way.def: very far; by a large amount
way.ex: She finished the race way ahead of the other runners.

way.def: a method, style or manner of doing something
way.ex: I prefer to do things the easy way.

we.def: I and another person or other people; I and you

weak.ex: She is still weak after her illness.

weaken.def: to make somebody/something less strong or powerful; to become less strong or powerful
weaken.ex: The team has been weakened by injury.

weakness.ex: The disease causes progressive muscle weakness.

wealth.def: a large amount of money, property, etc. that a person or country owns
wealth.ex: the desire to gain wealth and power

wealthy.def: having a lot of money, possessions, etc.

weapon.def: an object such as a knife, gun, bomb, etc. that is used for fighting or attacking somebody
weapon.ex: Modern nuclear weapons are much more destructive than either biological or chemical weapons.

wear.def: to have something on your body as a piece of clothing, a decoration, etc.
wear.ex: He was wearing a new suit.

weather.def: the condition of the atmosphere at a particular place and time, such as the temperature, and if there is wind, rain, sun, etc.

weave.def: to make cloth, a carpet, a basket, etc. by crossing threads or narrow pieces of material across, over and under each other by hand or on a machine called a loom
weave.ex: The baskets are woven from strips of willow.

web.def: a system for finding information on the internet, in which documents are connected to other documents

website.def: a set of pages on the internet, where a company or an organization, or an individual person, puts information
website.ex: For current prices please visit our website.

wedding.def: a marriage ceremony, and the meal or party that usually follows it
wedding.ex: I dreamed of having a big wedding, with all my family and friends.

wednesday.def: the day of the week after Tuesday and before Thursday

weed.def: a wild plant growing where it is not wanted, especially among crops or garden plants
weed.ex: The yard was overgrown with weeds.

week.def: a period of seven days, either from Monday to Sunday or from Sunday to Saturday

weekly.def: happening, done or published once a week or every week

weigh.ex: How much do you weigh (= how heavy are you)?

weight.def: how heavy somebody/something is, which can be measured in, for example, kilograms or pounds
weight.ex: It is about 76 kilos in weight.

weird.def: very strange or unusual and difficult to explain
weird.ex: I had a really weird dream last night.

welcome.ex: I'll go, then. I know when I'm not welcome.

welcome.def: used as a greeting to tell somebody that you are pleased that they are there
something that you do or say to somebody when they arrive, especially something that makes them feel you are happy to see them
to say hello to somebody in a friendly way when they arrive somewhere
welcome.ex: They were at the door to welcome us.

welfare.def: the general health, happiness and safety of a person, an animal or a group
welfare.ex: We are concerned about the child's welfare.

well.def: in a good, right or acceptable way
well.ex: They played well in the tournament.

well.def: used to express surprise, anger or relief
well.ex: Well, well—I would never have guessed it!

well.def: a deep hole in the ground from which people obtain water. The sides of wells are usually covered with brick or stone and there is usually a cover or a small wall at the top of the well.

west.def: the direction that you look towards to see the sun go down; one of the four main points of the compass

western.def: located in the west or facing west

wet.def: covered with or containing liquid, especially water

what.def: used in questions to ask for particular information about somebody/something

whatever.def: not at all; not of any kind
any or every; anything or everything

whatsoever.def: not at all; not of any kind

wheat.def: a plant grown for its grain that is used to produce the flour for bread, cakes, pasta, etc.; the grain of this plant

wheel.def: one of the round objects under a car, bicycle, bus, etc. that turns when it moves
wheel.ex: He braked suddenly, causing the front wheels to skid.

when.def: at what time; on what occasion
when.ex: When did you last see him?

when.def: at or during the time that
when.ex: I loved history when I was at school.

whenever.def: at any time that; on any occasion that
whenever.ex: You can ask for help whenever you need it.

where.def: in or to what place or situation
(in) the place or situation in which

whereas.def: used to compare or contrast two facts
whereas.ex: Some of the studies show positive results, whereas others do not.

whereby.ex: They have introduced a new system whereby all employees must undergo regular training.

whether.def: used to express a doubt or choice between two possibilities
whether.ex: He seemed undecided whether to go or stay.

which.def: used in questions to ask somebody to be exact about one or more people or things from a limited number
which.ex: Which is better exercise—swimming or tennis?

while.def: during the time that something is happening; at the same time as something else is happening
while.ex: We must have been burgled while we were asleep.
I only stayed for a short while.

whilst.def: during the time that something is happening; at the same time as something else is happening
whilst.ex: In the UK it is illegal to drive whilst holding a mobile phone.

whip.def: to hit a person or an animal hard with a whip, as a punishment or to make them go faster or work harder
whip.ex: He was taken back to the jail and soundly whipped.

whisper.def: a low, quiet voice or the sound it makes
whisper.ex: in a whisper, ‘I love you,’ he said in a whisper.

whisper.def: to speak very quietly to somebody so that other people cannot hear what you are saying
whisper.ex: Don't you know it's rude to whisper?

white.def: having the colour of fresh snow or of milk
the colour of fresh snow or of milk

who.def: used in questions to ask about the name, identity or function of one or more people

whoever.def: the person or people who; any person who
whoever.ex: Whoever says that is a liar.

whole.def: all that there is of something
whole.ex: The scheme would cover the whole of the UK.

whom.def: used instead of ‘who’ as the object of a verb or preposition

whose.def: used in questions to ask who something belongs to

why.def: used in questions to ask the reason for or purpose of something

wide.def: measuring a large distance from one side to the other

widely.def: by a lot of people; in or to many places
widely.ex: The term is widely used in everyday speech.

widen.def: to become wider; to make something wider

widespread.def: existing or happening over a large area or among many people

widow.def: a woman whose husband or wife has died and who has not married again

width.def: the measurement from one side of something to the other; how wide something is
width.ex: The terrace runs the full width of the house.

wife.def: the woman that somebody is married to; a married woman
wife.ex: I met my wife at university.

wild.def: living or growing in natural conditions; not kept in a house or on a farm

wildlife.def: animals, birds, insects, etc. that are wild and live in a natural environment

will.def: the ability to control your thoughts and actions in order to achieve what you want to do; a strong and determined desire to do something that you want to do
used for talking about or predicting the future
will.ex: You'll be in time if you hurry.

willing.def: not objecting to doing something; having no reason for not doing something
willing.ex: Many consumers are willing to pay more for organic food

willingness.def: the quality of being happy and ready to do something
willingness.ex: Success in studying depends on a willingness to learn.

win.def: a victory in a game, contest, etc.
to be the most successful in a competition, race, battle, etc.

wind.def: to have many bends and twists
wind.ex: The path wound down to the beach.

window.def: an opening in the wall or roof of a building, car, etc., usually covered with glass, that allows light and air to come in and people to see out; the glass in a window
window.ex: She looked out of the window.

wine.def: an alcoholic drink made from the juice of grapes that has been left to ferment. There are many different kinds of wine.

wing.def: one of the parts of the body of a bird, insect or bat that it uses for flying
wing.ex: The swan flapped its wings noisily.

winner.def: a person, a team, an animal, etc. that wins something
winner.ex: The lucky winner gets an all-expenses-paid trip to Sydney.

winter.def: the coldest season of the year, between autumn and spring

wipe.def: to rub something against a surface, in order to remove dirt or liquid from it; to rub a surface with a cloth, etc. in order to clean it
wipe.ex: Please wipe your feet on the mat.

wire.def: metal in the form of thin thread; a piece of this

wisdom.def: the ability to make sensible decisions and give good advice because of the experience and knowledge that you have
wisdom.ex: She was known to be a woman of great wisdom.

wise.def: able to make sensible decisions and give good advice because of the experience and knowledge that you have

wish.def: used especially in a letter, email or card to say that you hope that somebody will be happy, well or successful
wish.ex: Give my good wishes to the family.

wish.def: to want something to happen or to be true even though it is unlikely or impossible

wit.def: the ability to say or write things that are both clever and humorous

with.def: in the company or presence of somebody/something

withdraw.def: to take money out of a bank account
withdraw.ex: With this account, you can withdraw up to £300 a day.

withdrawal.def: the act of taking an amount of money out of your bank account
withdrawal.ex: You can make withdrawals of up to $250 a day.

within.def: before a particular period of time has passed; during a particular period of time
within.ex: You should receive a reply within seven days.

without.def: not having, experiencing or showing something
without.ex: They had gone two days without food.

witness.def: a person who sees something happen and is able to describe it to other people
witness.ex: He failed to interview a key witness.

witness.def: to see something happen (typically a crime or an accident)
witness.ex: to witness an accident/a murder/an attack

wonder.def: a feeling of surprise and pleasure that you have when you see or experience something beautiful, unusual or unexpected
wonder.ex: He retained a childlike sense of wonder.

wonder.def: to think about something and try to decide what is true, what will happen, what you should do, etc.
wonder.ex: ‘Why do you want to know?’ ‘No particular reason. I was just wondering.’

wonderful.def: very good, pleasant or a lot of fun
wonderful.ex: This is a wonderful opportunity to invest in new markets.

wood.def: the hard material that the trunk and branches of a tree are made of; this material when it is used to build or make things with, or as a fuel
wood.ex: He chopped some wood for the fire.

wool.def: the soft hair that covers the body of sheep and some other animals
wool.ex: Sheep were kept for their wool and meat.

word.def: a single unit of language that means something and can be spoken or written
word.ex: Do not write more than 200 words.

work.def: the job that a person does especially in order to earn money
work.ex: He started work as a security guard.

work.def: to do something that involves physical or mental effort, especially as part of a job
work.ex: I can't work if I'm cold.

worker.def: a person who works, especially one who does a particular kind of work

workforce.def: all the people who work for a particular company, organization, etc.

working.def: having a job for which you are paid

workout.def: a period of physical exercise that you do to keep fit
workout.ex: She does a 20-minute workout every morning.

workplace.def: the office, factory, etc. where people work
workplace.ex: the introduction of new technology into the workplace

workshop.def: a period of discussion and practical work on a particular subject, in which a group of people share their knowledge and experience
workshop.ex: There will be the opportunity for practical theatre work in drama workshops.

world.def: the earth, with all its countries, peoples and natural features

worldwide.def: affecting all parts of the world

worm.def: a long, thin creature with a soft body and no bones or legs

worried.def: thinking about unpleasant things that have happened or that might happen and therefore feeling unhappy and afraid

worry.def: the state of worrying about something
worry.ex: The threat of losing their jobs is a constant source of worry to them.

worry.def: to keep thinking about unpleasant things that might happen or about problems that you have
worry.ex: Don't worry. We have plenty of time.

worse.def: of poorer quality or lower standard; more unpleasant
worse.ex: The rooms were awful and the food was worse.
I'm afraid there is worse to come.

worship.def: the practice of showing respect for God or a god, by saying prayers, singing with others, etc.; a ceremony for this
to show respect for God or a god, especially by saying prayers, singing, etc. with other people in a religious building
worship.ex: The Mayans built jungle pyramids to worship their gods.

worst.def: of the poorest quality or lowest standard; worse than any other person or thing of a similar kind
worst.ex: It was by far the worst speech he had ever made.
He was voted the worst dressed celebrity.

worst.def: the most serious or unpleasant thing that could happen; the part, situation, possibility, etc. that is worse than any other
worst.ex: When they did not hear from her, they feared the worst.

worth.def: having a value in money, etc.
an amount of something that has the value mentioned
worth.ex: The winner will receive fifty pounds' worth of books.

worthwhile.def: important, pleasant, interesting, etc.; worth spending time, money or effort on
worthwhile.ex: It was in aid of a worthwhile cause (= a charity, etc.).

worthy.def: having the qualities that deserve somebody/something
worthy.ex: Very few of his ideas are worthy of further attention.

would.def: used in polite offers or invitations

wound.def: an injury to part of the body, especially one in which a hole is made in the skin using a weapon
to injure part of the body, especially by making a hole in the skin using a weapon
wound.ex: He had been wounded in the arm.

wow.def: used to show that you are very surprised or impressed by somebody/something

wrap.def: to cover something completely in paper or other material, for example when you are giving it as a present
wrap.ex: He spent the evening wrapping up the Christmas presents.

wrist.def: the joint between the hand and the arm

write.def: to make letters or numbers on a surface, especially using a pen or a pencil
write.ex: In some countries children don't start learning to read and write until they are six.

writer.def: a person whose job is writing books, stories, articles, etc.

writing.def: the activity of writing, in contrast to reading, speaking, etc.
writing.ex: Our son’s having problems with his reading and writing (= at school)

written.def: expressed in writing rather than in speech
written.ex: Having a written record of what I've done is very valuable.

wrong.ex: I got all the answers wrong.

wrong.def: in a way that produces a result that is not correct or that you do not want
behaviour that is not honest or morally acceptable
wrong.ex: Children must be taught the difference between right and wrong.

yard.def: a piece of land next to or around your house where you can grow flowers, fruit, vegetables, etc., usually with a lawn (= an area of grass)
yard.ex: They have a gorgeous old oak tree in their front yard.

year.def: the period from 1 January to 31 December, that is 365 or 366 days, divided into 12 months

yell.def: to shout loudly, for example because you are angry, excited, frightened or in pain
yell.ex: He yelled at the other driver.

yellow.def: having the colour of lemons or butter
the colour of lemons or butter

yes.def: used to answer a question and say that something is correct or true

yesterday.ex: A company spokeswoman said yesterday that no final decision had been made yet.

yet.def: used in negative sentences and questions to talk about something that has not happened but that you expect to happen
yet.ex: I haven't received a letter from him yet.

yet.def: despite what has just been said
yet.ex: It's a small car, yet it's surprisingly spacious.

yield.def: the total amount of crops, profits, etc. that are produced

you.def: used as the subject or object of a verb or after a preposition to refer to the person or people being spoken or written to
you.ex: You said you knew the way.

young.def: having lived or existed for only a short time; not fully developed
young.ex: Young babies need to be wrapped up warmly.

young.def: young people considered as a group
young.ex: It's a movie that will appeal to the young.

youngster.def: a young person or a child
youngster.ex: The camp is for youngsters aged 8 to 14.

your.def: of or belonging to the person or people being spoken or written to

yourself.def: used when the person or people being spoken to both cause and are affected by an action

youth.def: the time of life when a person is young, especially the time before a child becomes an adult
youth.ex: He had been a talented musician in his youth.

zero.ex: Five, four, three, two, one, zero… We have lift-off.

zone.def: an area or a region with a particular feature or use
